reviews.comment: Material is manageable, but the professor is  is dismissive of student concerns and often redirects questions to ChatGPT rather than providing instruction. Lectures frequently end in 20 mins, making commutes feel wasted.
Bro just read off the slides, I'd rather open my YouTube watch someone else explain the concepts, or let the TA do the job. Yes he has accent, be prepare for that

I took professor Ayala in the winter of 2016. Class was straight forward.  2 page paper, midterm, and final. This class is very dense. A lot of reading but it's sociological theory so it's bound to be dense. I would take him again.

He speaks really fast and has an accent, but you will get use to it by week 3. Very knowledgeable with his stuff and is honest if he feels ambivalent with a concept. All readings are available on class website so didn't have to buy anything! Except an iclicker used during lecture for questions. & no surprises on tests! Cool guy!

He talks really fast, can barely understand him. But, he posts his readings/lecture notes online - very helpful. If it wasn't for the T.A. though, I'd probably do horribly.. so go to discussion! Tests are hard so study hard! Fill in the blanks and short answer.. fill in the blanks are very hard.
Best professor I have ever had. Easy, clear and he saves you a lot of money by posting all readings on his website.

This class was so much harder than it should have been (worser than actual upper lvl in person classes) ! There were no clear guidelines ever and lectures were so incredibly dry. Tests were worth so much with each question being worth like 9 points each as a mcq, and every question was an application question with multiple parts and pathways.

I absolutely loved Professor Saba Aliyari. She is an amazing teacher who encouraged me every step of the way, offered TA hours on the weekends, stayed on after lectures to answer questions, and was generally excellent in every possible way.
Nice the nicest professor and the class is a lot of work, but if you keep up with the homework and do the extra credit, you'll be OK and you'll learn a lot
Very intense, lots of assignments and tough grader.

There's a ton of work to do, but you learn a lot- you do a lot of the work outside of class and come to lectures for solidifying the learning through participation. She gives questions that are thought-provoking w/ irl context which I think would be helpful for the MCAT. Gives extra credit opportunities that are useful. Took LS7A.
Very confusing class and the tests are much more complex than the actual stuff that is taught in the class don't take it.

Professor Aliyari is very interactive with students, is approachable (via email/zoom), and is passionate about cell biology. Exams are based off of her lectures, discussion worksheets, and readings. One is able to succeed in this class if they have preexisting knowledge in basic biology and are able to dissect through large amounts of information.
I would not choose this class. It was confusing content on the exams at times and you need to study so much the whole textbook. Don't go by her slides and the lectures only.

She often confuses herself during lectures and never clarifies about them. She literally doesn't provide any assistance outside of class! No email responds to lecture questions, and no office hours She rushes through most of her slides and often tells us to read them ourselves Her teaching isn't worth 995 $. The book only costs 80
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: EXTREMELY HARD TO LISTEN TO AND UNDERSTAND! YOU ARE LITERALLY MESSING UP YOUR GRADE IF YOU ONLY COMPLETELY STUDY OFF FROM THE LECTURES ONLY! The book would save my grade! Her pronunciation is bad, and her explanation doesn't make sense due to poor grammar speaking

She is smart and very passionate on what she does, but she is not a good lecturer. It was hard to understand - her accent was strong, and lecture is not organized. Hard to focus on lecture. She wants your participation but she wants you to explain what you didn't learn yet. This was 100 - but you have to review science paper. HUH??!?

Great professor and very passionate about the subject. There are 2 papers & exams along with weekly discussion posts based on the readings. The class is mainly highlighting main points of the reading and this is what will be on the quiz. I recommend going to office hours as that will give you more insight into what will be on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Alim is an amazing individual. He is not only passionate about his students and their success but also their roles and perspectives on current issues. His lectures were always so engaging and you could see the passion for his work. There were 2 papers (supposed to be 3 but things changed because of the blm movement) and based on readings.

Despite the other reviews, I really enjoyed this class. Professor Alim is very passionate when he's teaching. I thought the class was very easy and pretty interesting. I also didn't bother buying the book we were supposed to get, and we didn't even end up using it. He also gives EC opportunities which is nice.
I thought this class would be hard, but because of Alim I was able to do really well and he gave a lot of extra credit along the way to help boost grades, great guy

At first I hated this course but overall it end as bad as it started. There was an immense amount of reading and a hard mid-term but after that the class kinda got easier. Very nice professor, very approachable, and engaging, I liked him but did not find the material overly exciting.

He's not that bad as the people below said. Yes it is true the readings end up being a waste of time, hard to understand, and so long, but he makes up for it on his exams. I barely glanced over my notes the night before the midterm and still got an A on it (all you have to do is throw in terms and your answers will be fine)
most UNORGANIZED class I've ever taken in college.  reading is assigned as if its the only class you're taking.

This class sucks!!! I have never read so much in my life only to retain such a minimal amount of information. Dont take this class unless you want to persue a PdD in Linguistics which is pretty much what you need in order to understand the material.
Unless you love stupid pointless linguistic theory, stay away. Worst class I ever took. Do not take this class. It's absolutely horrible.

Do not take this class for a GE. Trust me. It is not worth your time. Take your GE's at a CC and transfer them over. Figuring out how to drive is easier than this class. Unnecessarily rude professor and TA's, extreme amount of reading, balancing this class is not possible. Get ready to read and get ready to stress out. AVOID THIS CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Clear and interesting lectures. No textbook, all pdf articles. 1 written midterm (short answers) and same with final. Cumulative. Weekly reflection papers based on the lectures and "key terms" the student creates from them. Once I stopped using the articles for the basis of my reflection papers and solely used lecture notes, I got better grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's a descent guy and a descent professor. 3 papers overall, midterm, & final exam. Reading outside material helps a lot and office hours with TA. Definitely a doable class. Crammed for class the night before the midterm and pulled off a 92, not knowing any of the info when I started.
reviews.rating_tags: SO MANY PAPERS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: His lectures aren't terrible but it definitely feels like it's missing something. Most of the information I get from the course comes from my TA, Jacob, or from reading over the extra material- not so much the actual textbook. There is one midterm and a final. Also, two 3 page papers and one 7 page paper.
reviews.rating_tags: SO MANY PAPERS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this course! It was focused mainly on Tableau, and we learned a lot of hands-on skills that I can actually use. Amini was fair and organized, grading was always done quickly, and there were plenty of opportunities to earn extra. Overall, a great class for anyone interested in learning data visualization in a practical way.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Navid is by far my favorite professor at CSULA! His lectures are extremely thorough and interesting, and projects are actually fun instead of the boring things that are usually of the book. I am having no problems with homework, but the quizzes are HARD! You will have to study up for them. After this class, you will know your stuff.

Professor Amini is a great instructor, he clearly explains the subject well. If you attend lectures and labs and review them you should be good. This class is more about how much you practice and understand Java.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I dropped this class. This class was taken online and was supposed to be on Tuesdays and Thursdays. However, professor Amini only showed up on Tuesdays and did not teach anything, he only went over what slides, videos, and textbook material we should be learning. No teaching was done, expectations are high, expect to hand write code &teach yourself

Professor Amini is an amazing lecturer, and he explains concepts in a clear and concise manner. For my semester, there were eleven quizzes in total, which was somewhat inconvenient. However, I learned a lot from this class, and it was overall a highly enjoyable experience. I would definitely recommend taking this class with this professor.

Structure of class is really good. Course grade is based on exams, quizzes, exercises, and a group project. Really helpful. Clear instructions and expectations. Lectures were interesting. Exams were a little challenging but material is manageable. Overall, I think he is one of the top tier professors CSULA has for CS.
Prof. Amini really cares that we learn. The material was very well organized and the lectures were amazing. I would definitely take any course he teaches!
researcher - not a teacher; limited experience running an entire course/real life experience.
:) I love this prof so mush. He teaches sooooo well. And I think his lectures are very interesting.

The most terrible professor in UCLA. No idea what he is trying to teach in this course. He always talks to himself instead of to students. Tough grader and TAs. Deduction point without no reason even though that your homework are same as the homework who got the full point. When you try to appeal, good luck, since no one will reply.
He is okay teacher. Not easy, not hard. So, if you study at least sometime, you'll do fine in his classes.
He may be a good professor for graduate students, but he tries not to "waste" his time with undergraduate students. His lecture is okay.

he is a very interesting and funny teacher, he likes to make fun of himself. he is good in connecting real-life happenings in economics in class. and therefore, he makes it more interesting.  good teacher and i recommend him. the only part that he could increase a little is his knowledge of engli
No Comments

She waited until the very end of the quarter to accuse me of using AI on most of my assignments - I NEVER used AI and also had Google Docs histories for all of my assignments. I went through an official investigation in which they ruled me innocent. She did not gaf and still deducted points, so I got a B+ in the class when I earned an A.

She's the worst. Forces students through lockdown browser quizzes riddled with ultra-specific gotcha questions. Hides behind vague grading criteria. L professor. Deducts points at random with zero explanation. Overrides TAs grades and fights with them. btw she hides in the TA GroupMe
TLDR: TAs made the class- professors meh.
Very bad experience for this professor, the lectures are interesting but I believe she could handle student concerns in a much more supportive and effective manner.

I'm writing this review because I don't want others to go through what I did. She lacks basic compassion and treats students with an unjustified level of suspicion. I hope this helps the next student make a better choice.
WORSE CLASS I'VE EVER TAKEN!!!!! Sociology has always been an interesting class for me however this professor is truly disappointed.

The other comments aren't lying this class is hard because there's too many assignments due every week. Theres a lot of reading and the weekly quizzes are one attempt only and not specific enough to what you read. The hardest class I've taken at ucla solely because of the workload. I got really burnt out in the first four weeks.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Avoid this class. Prof is strict and unapproachable, with random grade deductions and no feedback. Requires Doc link history for assignments and checks for AI usage. Weekly quizzes are overly specific. No chance to challenge grades or get help via email. Heavy reading and videos. Midterm essay and Oral Final. Weekly discussion, quiz, and perusal.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors ever

Work-heavy, tedious class. Each week we had a reading to leave 5 comments on & 2 500-word discussions/2 replies that we had to incorporate lecture/readings into. Also had a midterm + 2 7-8 papers. If you follow the rubric for the papers + fill out her midterm study guide q's you can do well. She's strict on deadlines. I enjoyed the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I took two online courses this summer with this instructor and received an A- in both. However, I am leaving this review to help other students maintain their excitement to learn and retain information. This course load was so dense without any guidance from the instructor. I taught myself and learning became a chore for the first time ever.

This class was a lot of work, you will get out what you put in. My main reason for not recommending her is because she is not helpful or accommodating of her students. I sent her a few emails throughout the year to check in on my grade as well as to ask for accommodation for a trip, and I was referred to the syllabus with my questions unaddressed.

Honestly one of the worst professors at UCLA. The amount of work she gives is insane. Initially, I was very interested in the subject and she made me despise learning about it. We had 2 discussion posts (500-600 words each), 2 quizzes, and had to annotate 2 readings every week. We also had 2 papers, each being 8 pages long. Totally not worth it!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Terribly strict grader for such a simple bullshit class, save yourself some time and find a different Professor.
Awful teacher. Does not care about her student's learning. Expects only the regurgitation of facts, no room for original thoughts or creativity. Terri Anderson Unesisarily strict grader.

She was extremely insensitive to the circumstances students were going thru during the pandemic. She repeatedly told us that she was in her "cabin finally catching up on Netflix shows" while we were literally dying here in LA due to wildfires & surge in covid cases. She admitted to recycling lectures she's used in the past. Extremely disappointed!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Do yourself a favor and DO NOT TAKE HER!! She would post prerecorded lectures that gave the bare minimum, assign a lot of reading that you basically have to do in order to do the essays and weekly homework since you're mostly teaching yourself everything, and is just overall insensitive to everything and everyone. The work wasn't worth the credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The worst. The class material was wonderful, but my passion for it waned after every paper. Toughest paper grader ever- which was probably a tactic to make us work harder. I realized at the end of the term I could see the "mean avg" for every paper, which was typically around a D or C-. Vague feedback, but she curved my grade massively at the end.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: lol. relatively easy class just discussion posts and three papers, however, very harsh grader with little to no feedback. also is just REALLY rude

This professor is super insightful and is clearly an expert in their field. I learned so much about gender constructs and am more conscious of how gender is enacted everyday. The class is super dense and is reading-intensive, but hey, it's an upper div class at UCLA, so it's to be expected.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She's kinda sorta okay. The readings are really long. Some classes are really interesting. Others are really boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This is an amazing professor. Hope you dont pass her by bc of her bad reviews. I took her for the Soc of emotions and this was by far, the best class and professor Ive taken at UCLA. She does assign an insane amount of reading and work, and she is also a tough grader but her class is well worth it! U will actually learn something valuable!

The material was very interesting but she seems to believe that the times have not changed. If you are working regular 30-40 hours a week (my experience) You can not give 200 pages of reading a week like let's say when college was cheaper and your only worry was reading for class. If you have no life she is the professor you need.
She cares about the subject.
I took her intro to sociology class at Occidental College. The things she teaches are pretty easy. Essay grading can be pretty harsh. Not a difficult class.

I had prof. Anderson for a sociology class that she taught at Occidental College (not UCLA) and I thought that she was great. She was very accessible and presented her subject in a way that really grabbed my attention.

?30% for a 7-pg essay focusing on social change in music, 10% for a 3-pg LA Art Walk report, & 32% on a research project where you integrate an interview w/artist in a 10-pg paper regarding what is necessary for there to be art. NO MIDTERMS NOR FINAL for this class. There were even special guests who visit in person or communicate via videochat.

I love professor Anderson, she is interesting and informative. I really enjoyed the material in the class. People who think doing a 1 page journal per week is too much obviously shouldn't be in college in the first place. Highly recommend this professor.
she doesnt seem to care about her students much. she's not a easy grader! I dont like her class, boring.

I just finished a summer class with her. She gives WAY too much work. For this (6-week) class you were supposed to turn in one journal per week, plus there were two papers, and one group paper that included field work plus a presentation. On the last day of class one of each of those 3 things was due. If you have a life don't take her classes. ANAL
Awesome!
I love Professor Anderson
a lot of work, but easy grading. teacher is obnoxious. doesn't allow laptops, phones, etc. i only attended lecture a couple of times and got an A.

Super easy class if you pay attention to what she says, I had the best TA so this class really was easy... she is crystal clear in lecture, and there is not test just research paper which is divided into little assignments. Recommended!

This class is a lot of work but if you do the work and the extra credit there is no excuse not to get an A.  There is a lot of reading but Generally you just need to read one or two o the ten things she assigns per week to write your essays.

She was terrible.  The class was super easy and a complete joke, but the entire time we just had to sit around and listen to her opinion, and any time anyone had an opinion of their own, she just shut them down.  I learned nothing.
She is an extremely interesting woman and professor! I highly suggest this class and professor to all my friends!
She's a little flighty but in a good way. She loves what she teaches and makes you comparably interested!
She is amazing! I love her so much. Her assignments are unbelievable. I will take her again. =)

She is an amazing teacher.  A teacher who actually teaches practical material.  The topics are a bit abstract but it's your life! Her teaching style along with the course material made her classes the best I had taken at UCLA

Such an amazing professor. Her lectures/assigned readings made me think critically about the social constuction of gender in our society.I loved doing the journal entries because I learned so much about myself through many of the articles, films, and lectures.  At her office hrs., she's approachable and will talk about her life experience. LOVE HER

I love prof. Anderson. She is what made me finish school. She gives you live lessons rather then just preach. 10 times better the LVL. I wish I did not graduate so that I could take more classes with there. Easy A too if you do all the work.
I really liked her, she makes class fun. At the end, i came out feeling like i actually learned something, not only from class but from myself also!
Anderson is so cool and hip. She talks to you like a person and not a student. Her projects are really out there but you really learn.
Do not take him, rude professor; homework does not help at all with tests; extremely hard tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Corbin excels in physics teaching. His dedication shines in office hours, ensuring deep understanding over memorization. Inspiring and thorough, he makes physics engaging and accessible. A true gem for students seeking to truly grasp the subject. The funniest parts is when he cracks jokes. For example, when deriving planar symmetry - old mcdonald.
Great lecturer, exams extremely difficult, make sure to do all the homework and practice problems from previous exams
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: In a nutshell, lectures are very engaging but exams are very tough.
Great teacher.  Great person.  Great lectures.  Probably the best lower division professor you'll have for science majors.

Great lecturer: organized and entertaining. The book is a reference, exams are based on lecture material. His tests are impossible to finish within 50min, but the final is way more doable. Just go to his office hours as much as you can because he goes over problems that help your solving skills during exams. Great person outside the classroom too!
Best professor I've ever had. Explains everything really well and actually cares for his students to learn something.
Amazing professor, tests are really hard, but he explains the material very well, and is always available if you need help. Just go to office hours and show him that you care and want to learn.
His tests are all symbolic, so his homework is not very useful for preparing. Go to his workshops if you really want to be ready for the tests.

Brent was a great guy both in and out of the classroom.  He would be willing to change the subject to something non-class related if he could tell you were getting stressed in order to help you get your focus back.
He is very funny professor.

I liked Prof. Corbin (he prefers his students call him brent) because he is very down to earth with his class. Although he admits during the first lecture that the class will be hard, he makes physics as fun as he can.
No Comments

He is a nice guy but is disorganized. He will change assigned readings or turn papers into projects. Syllabus is highly subjected to change so if you like to get ahead on your work for the week, this will suck. If you show up to class and raise your hand he won't be a pain. His personality is annoying, he complains, and can be snarky and petty.

Blake Allmendinger is a great professor who really cares about student's understanding and development. If you have any issues he is very easy to get in touch with either via email or during office hours. The texts are easy to read within the allotted time and Blake is available to discuss them throughout the week. Would highly recommend.

Allmendinger is my favorite professor I've had in my two years at UCLA, and definitely the one that I've had the most personal contact with. It was easy to talk to him outside of class and joke around, but he was also really helpful with information. His lectures are funny and compelling, just like him. Take his class!

Detective Fiction was one of the best courses I've taken in my college career.  The subject matter is fascinating and entertaining and the lectures are interesting.  However, the tests are extremely difficult and require creativity and a mastery of the concepts discussed in class in addition to your own theories and ideas.  Tough class but worth it

Prof. Allmendinger is professional but also funny & relaxed; you will look forward to each class if you read & appreciate witty humor -lectures are interesting & relate to real-life.  Smart, helps you to become a better writer instead of simply going through the motions. TRULY CARES, there for students, responds promptly to emails even on weekends.

Blake is absolutely amazing, especially if you're an English major! Prepare to read important texts and engage in intellectually stimulating discussions. He's always there for you whenever you need him and will ensure you succeed if you want to. He is straightforward in what he expects, and is overall a wonderful professor.

Don't take the class unless you are an eloquent and fast writer. The professor grades on in class essays. You could learn more about the subject on a weekend by reading professional essays  than you will learn in his class. Again, his class is a writing class.

Hands down the best professor I've ever had.  Incredibly kind, funny, and accessible.  On top of all that, he puts together really interesting classes.  I learned a lot, and had a blast doing it....he genuinely cares about his students.  Take his class.  Any of them.  You'll thank yourself later, it's an absolute treat.

Overall, Professor Allmendinger is very nice and extremely funny, however, he is not organized especially when it comes to explaining what he expects for papers and exams! Almost 20% of my class failed the midterm and it seems to be because he did not explain what he expected! If you take his class, be VERY careful and go to office hours!!

Obsessed with Professor Allmendinger.  He is THE most genuinely concerned and approachable professor I have ever had.  Ever.  Period.  He cares deeply about his students.  Plus, this class was outstanding...doing the "homework" (reading detective fiction novels) made me feel guilty because I enjoyed myself so much.  Take this course. It's perfect.

Fantastic teacher. Funny, insightful, and helpful. Responds to emails very quickly. Lots of discussion in class but will never pressure or cold call you into speaking in front of class. Also, very clear about breakdown of work and grades. Outstanding teacher. Did not do as well in the class than I wanted but would absolutely take him again.

Allmendinger is my all time favorite professor! I have taken two classes with him and I would not hesitate in taking more! He is funny and makes the class truly enjoyable.  He is very clear about how your grade breaks down and is always willing to help! He is also understanding if you don't want to participate in discussion. Love him!

Prof. Allmendinger (most students just call him Blake) is an amazingly devoted, highly approachable teacher and mentor. His teaching style is very casual and he rarely, if ever, merely lectures. Some students, especially those who are not very independent thinkers and who require strict guidance, may not care for this style. I, however, love it!
Best English professor at UCLA hands down. Smart, witty, and extremely dedicated. Hilarious and insightful lectures. Take his classes. You won't regret a second.

He is an excellent professor. My favorite so far. He made class fun and interesting. I would suggest his class to anyone. He is very knowledgeable about his subject and is open to feedback. He is enthusiastic in his lectures and clear on his expectations. You know what to expect on tests and can always go to him if you need help.

He is by far the best professor I have had at UCLA. He makes class fun and entertaining. He is very reasonable and approachable. He is clear on what he wants and what he tests on. Participation is not mandatory, but it can help if you are on the border of a better grade.

I disagree that you have to speak up in his class in order to do well.  I got an "A" and never participated in discussion at all. He says on the first day that participating helps if you are on the borderline for a certain grade but is by no means mandatory.  This is by far the most entertaining class I have ever had and one of the best teachers!

I just finished taking a course with him in Spring 2012.  I haven't received my final grade yet, but I definitely have to that it was the most enjoyable course I've ever taken.  It was extremely thought provoking, and I appreciated the different avenues of thoughts he offered.  He was very helpful, and extremely funny and enjoyable.

If you are quiet, I don't recommend this professor, and in fact would not recommend discussion-enriched courses in general. Allmendinger has been the most open-minded professor I have had as an English major at UCLA, and I appreciate having a professor who does not pander to his students, but raises them up to be equals at the table.
Professor Allmendinger is a great, funny, and engaging teacher. I always looked forward to his lectures. I highly recommend taking him.

He seems like a nice enough guy and is very available to give assistance. However, if you have difficulty participating in discussion, this is not the class for you. If you are very talkative, you will be happy with your grade.

Professor Allmendinger's selection of novels for my American Literature class was very diverse and interesting. His lectures are insightful and he's very articulate. He expects a lot from his students and their writing. Professor Allmendinger is very approachable and I will highly consider taking him for another course in the future.

i took allmendinger almost a decade ago. to this day, i still think back on the experience with great fondness. this guy is smart, funny, engaging and serious about scholarship. it's an honor to have the opp. to learn from him! show up, do the work and engage in the material at the level any self-respecting bruin should, and you will do well.

Your grade is set from your look. Seemed to anger him if you are cute & smart;he played "games" giving instructions then grading harshly for power "play".He likes All-American,UCLA types(Teaches the West!).If you are European or Ethnic,safely use convention and get low B, C, or D. It is hard to fail you if always use Standard writing conventions.

He is so utterly useless. I can't believe this guys is still teaching here. One of the pitfalls of tenure. I know what I'm talking about. I got an A so it's not about the grade. He gives the English Dept. a bad rep.
xyz

Barely lectures and rarely puts anything on the board. Mentions things in passing and then grades very harshly and unfairly. Appears as though he has not re the books he has assigned in quite some time which makes it difficult for him to recall facts, judge and grade fairly or add to and answer questions in class. Don't really think he has a clue.
Unfair grader.  Gives vague assignments, then grades them harshly.
I took 1 class with her and she was I really liked how she spoked clearly.

I want to warn everyone do not take any methods class from her. I took psych188. I went to see her during office hours three times, she was never there. Her assignments are extremely long and vague. half or more of class dropped, and I wish I had. I'm terrified I won't pass.

Professor Goff is great! You really learn valuable things in the class and she makes the material easy to understand. The class is pretty structured so you have to work hard but it's very doable. Highly recommend her!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: One of the best psych professors at ucla. She makes her lectures very interesting funny and easy to follow.

Taking Professor Goff was probably my best choice I had made in UCLA. Extremely funny, clear, flexible, and understanding professor. During the finals week where WHO declared Covid 19 Pandemic, she was extremely quick and flexible with final even with such short notice. There is no required material and I personally think she grades fairly.

Amazing professor i took her at Cerritos, if you're intimidated by chemistry, Professor Bauer will be there for you. She makes Science fun. Attendance is required but I promise you wouldn't want to miss her class. Enjoy

Professor Bauer is amazing! Highly recommend taking her class! She is always there when you need help or have questions. She makes the class easy! She does give a lot of work throughout the week but it is not hard at all. If you want a good chemistry lesson please don't hesitate to take her class!

I can't give Dr. Bauer enough praise, she is kind, funny, passionate about Chemistry and puts the students before any grade. She is the definition of a Professor who cares about her students and making sure we actually learn the material instead of docking points and making University even harder than it is. Her love for science radiates

She was really nice.  She wanted her students to learn the material and made the grade less important, which was nice.  The midterms are more difficult than the final and she is there to help you understand the material.  The only thing, was her study guides weren't very comprehensive of the test, so it was a bit difficult at first.

She's a really nice professor. Did demonstrations in class a lot to try and excite people. Her tests were challenging though. I am pretty awful with the 20B focus on g-chem, and I tried really hard and failed the midterms and final, and got a C+, so yay. She says her class isn't meant to be a weeder class, so try and you will pass and learn.

If you like seminars and find the topic interesting DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. To call this a seminar is a joke, she conducts the "seminar" like verbal quizzes, checking if you remember what author said on pg3 and gushing about her reading list. She has ZERO tolerance to students having their own take, insights, or ideas. Her interpretation is law.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Her accent is really heavy, can not understand what is she actually saying. Really bad professor since she doesn't care about the student.

The TAs in this class are the MVP. It's difficult to understand what is going on in class because the outline isn't organized. However, if you ask questions during office hours and do the readings, you'll be fine in the class. You also have many extra credit opportunities. There's only 100 points total for your grade, so you can't mess up. GG

I've learned a lot from her.  If you take the Honors Seminar, she is incredibly supportive, but don' expect sugar coating. You don't always have to agree with her, but she tries really hard to be honest with her students, which is preferable to vague feedback. Also, she makes herself really available, so I would recommend going to office hours.

Her accent is not that bad. No term paper. Midterm and final(30 points each). 100 to earn.  4 quizzes (5 each). Reaction papers (5 each). Her lectures are interesting some times. She provides a lecture outline. It's a pretty easy class. Just do the readings and make sure you go to office hours. She likes to know that you're trying. Really nice!

No one can understand what the professor is saying. She seems to be going on and on about nothing in particular. At the beginning of each lecture, the TA's pass out outlines for the class, but you can still never understand where we're at in the lecture because you can't tell what she's saying. I was really excited to take this class, but hate it!

The professor is a poor communicator; she goes off tangent frequently. During several lectures, she literally stammered and babbled for 30 minutes about how conservatives are wrong.  UCLA deserves to be out of money if they grant tenure to people who can't teach.
Awful teacher. Incoherent and disorganized. Avoid at all costs.

Easy class just insanely boring. The whole thing consists of weekly reflections of 200 words, 3 quizzes 30 mins with 20 qs, and one final paper that you can choose what to write. The first 5 weeks were ok but I think as the class gets more complicated = he rambles more. It feels like I want to fall asleep through this class.

lectures interesting. Still remember very  academic. too much crammed in 10 wks multip choice test not clear cut.  prefer cut n dry. felt like he wants to trick you. Didn't understand why he during mid n final went around helping people  some way too long. cmon its a test. know it or dont but no help nevr got to ask him why he did that.  not cool.

These lectures were amazing- I even saved the podcasts onto my computer to listen to again. Prof Barrett is speaks with incredible clarity, is kind, respectful and helpful in office hours, and posts all his slideshows before class and podcasts after class so you have everything you could possibly need to understand the concepts. The exams are fair.
There is no discussion section. All lectures are podcasted. Overall, his lectures are pretty boring, but his tests are fair. Be sure to study his lecture slides and you'll do fine.

He is super smart, and the material is interesting. I don't think the tests are too hard - they are just really good at assessing how well you know the material. Considering how light the reading, and the load is; there is no excuse for you not to.

Prof Barrett is really nice. He posts all his lectures online so you theoretically don't ever have to go to the class. Exams are hard though, questions are worded in a confusing matter so make sure to do all the reading and either attend or watch the lectures. Don't get behind just because they're online!
No Comments
sticks to book material for lectures
I took her courses from LACC and UCLA. Even though it's been years, she's still the best teacher I've ever had.

She is so nice and teaches at the perfect pace. Korean seems difficult at first, however she makes it really fun to learn. I recommend her 100% of the time! As long as you're interested in the class and practice, you'll get an A
awesome
Great Teacher! Fantastic! Very down to earth and approachable! She does extremely well in teaching Korean. She also teaches at L.A.C.C. so she always well prepares non-heritage speakers! A+
She is so nice!!!I loved her class!!The quizes were little bit hard for non-heritage speakers but she was very helpful to those students, too.
He is a very talented lecturer. He emphasizes practice problems, which is very helpful.

He was the substitute for a mse statistics and modeling course and was very good at explaining concepts and was engaging. I very much enjoyed his class and hopes he gets to teach more mat sci classes.
Very example oriented, which is helpful with understanding statistics materials.
It's a nice and clear lecture. Totally recommended.
Makes hard concepts easy to understand using daily life examples.
Great teacher. Lots of examples.

Genuinely might be the best professor I've learned from at UCLA. He subbed in for a class that I was pretty lost in and I was basically completely caught back up after the 3 lectures he taught. He's really good at explaining engineering concepts without just throwing endless formulas at you. This guy makes me wanna become a teacher someday.

French 2 with Wadie was an outstanding experience. He was unbelievably nice and you can tell he really cares about his students' learning experiences. French 2 is a hard class but Wadie makes it manageable.

I really enjoyed taking French 2 with Wadie at UCLA. He is very nice and was really accessible outside of class over email and in person. You could really tell that he genuinely cared about students' learning. He's a great lecturer and encourages class participation to ensure comprehension. Overall, I had a great experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Wadie is a great TA for french! He really cares about how we're doing in the class and whether we improve or not. He tries to help as much as he can in any situation. The lecture slides are great too, you can tell he takes a lot of time to prepare them. They are entertaining and so are the games we will play to prepare for tests.

Wadie is amazing. He is seriously one of my favourite teachers I've had at UCLA so far. I had him as a French professor ( TA led french classes). He really cares about everyone and wants everyone to do well. He also does his best to add fun videos and activities as supplement to the mandatory slideshows.
Super awesome professor. Very caring and hopes the best for his students learning. He is a must take because he is willing to go the extra distance to help you make the most of your time.

Wadie is truly the best TA (not only in French but at UCLA). As a senior, this is the first time I've ever posted on this site and I think that speaks to just how gifted Wadie is. I had rough quarter when I took French 2 and in many cases, my day was uplifted by just knowing that I had French with Wadie! An absolute gift to UCLA!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: Best French teacher; hope I can take French 3 with him too!

DO NOT TAKE HER COURSE NO MATTER WHAT YOUR NSA OR PEOPLE TRY TO CONVINCE YOU!!! She is not new student-friendly and will not care if you're failing the class. This course is so lecture-heavy that if you're not in ethnomusicology or anything similar, you will NOT understand (even those within the major struggled). Please heed the ratings.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Offers little to nothing of substance in lectures and talks about random figures in history with no context to them. She expects you to remember some random name she threw out that won't be in any reading materials as it will likely be on a test. incredibly disorganized, doesn't take criticism well, hardly will even work with the TA's to help you
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I don't think I've ever seen an ostensible professional, in a professional setting, act so unprofessionally. At the end of this farce of a class, when we were allowed to do evals and the TA attempted to rush that process so as to smother the anticipated negative reviews, the class came together as one to complete them and tell UCLA to dump her.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Goes on tangents so you don't know what's important. Also tough grader; on the midterm the highest grade was a C-. Luckily she grades on a curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: If you go to lecture, you do not need to read most of the readings she posts. Just go to class and you will get a good grade. Also, be active in class. She loves it when people engage in conversation during lecture.

Professor Pires is just an alright professor. The information given in her lectures is very informative but the way she presents it just makes you want to stand up and walk out. She expects you to understand hard concepts of biology in a week and gets upset if you explain that you don't understand something. I would be wary of taking her class.
Make sure you complete all assignments and take advantage of the extra credit opportunities. Also, make sure to pay attention to the Practice AoLs.

Professor Pires was not a good professor. During lectures she acts as if she cares and wants you to go to office hours, but once you do go to her office hours, she makes you feel stupid with any questions asked and has a hostile approach. Would not recommend her as a professor. 0/10
ok

Cares about her students. Her exams are some of the hardest I've ever taken. Do the homework and reading before class. Class consisted purely of clicker questions for practice, you must come in already knowing the material. Discussion is boring, all simulations and a quick walk through the botanical garden.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: Her lecture was really good, she explains it better than the book. But the exams are tricky, she doesn't give questions that is straight forward. There are online quiz each week due by tuesday. You must read the book ahead in order to do those quizzes. She wants the student to succeed, and helps a lot during office hour.

Good lecturer. I never slept in class (it's a 9:30 am class). Notes are important, book helps to clarify. Loves Q's on lab techniques, since she's been doing that since forever (as a grad student). Tests were MC, and not the worst kind where all Q's had choices as all of the above or A&B. Clickers used (T.P.) Lots of OHs a wk. She's good for LS 3.
her tests are very tricky and go into the slightest detail of the book. You have to read and understand/memorize the entire book and graphs. test averages were midterm 1 73% midterm 2 63%. Final?
She was tough, but if you put your nose to the book and study hard, you will earn your grade. She has great study guides as well.

Debbie Pires rocks.  She is extremely helpful, tech savvy, patient, helpful and beautiful too.  She challenges you to really understand and learn the concepts and retain them on a long term basis. I wish more teachers would aspire to be more like Debbie Pires. Of course, except for the physical beauty.

This is for LS 1. Class is fast-paced but the workload is reasonable. Definitely need to take good lecture notes AND read the textbook. She's a great lecturer and one of the few profs in the UC system that actually like teaching.

She's a good teacher, but you definitely need to read the book and listen to her lecture pod casts. It is a difficult class - you should spend at least four hours per lecture studying. It is very interesting though and she tells cool stories about the subject.
Really interesting class and, I'm a social sciences major.
She's very encouraging, helpful, OH always packed with students because during lecture she talks way too fast. exams are conceptual and covers a lot of material. class in bruincasted

Prof Smallberg makes you watch two-hour-long lectures online three times per week. While he is certainly great at explaining C++, the tests are hard and often focus on weird rules of C++. The MCQs have 20+ options. I would suggest strengthening your coding skills before taking CS31, especially if you don't have any prior experience.
Not good. Old-fassioned.

Very good instructor, super nerdy, I highly recommend him, very hard class, projects take all of your time, but such an important subject. It was a huge shock to go from community college to this level of classes.

I came into the course with 0 experience with C++. Lectures can be dry but I had to stay on my toes. Coding projects can't and should not be procrastinated. During exams, if you payed attention in lecture, did well on the projects, and go through the problems carefully, you should be fine. He was great for my first CS professor.
If you have no programming experience (like me), his class is very hard. If you do (like many of the people in the class), you probably won't need much effort in this class.
He is Great

Prof. Smallberg will go out of his way to help you, even looking back at code you've already submitted and asking you about bugs in your program, so in the future you don't make the mistakes.  Will respond to questions posed at 2 in the morning

I had Professor Smallberg for CS31 and CS32. He's an excellent instructor but the course is very challenging because of the length of the projects, which take most people double digit hours. They're graded by a program so it's easy to get a 0. If you want to be a programmer this course will prepare you for the real world, which is the intention.

This teacher is not too bad, he only has a problem with his personality. He wants you to think exactly like him, if you don't, you will be punished getting very low grades even when your proyects are OK. However there is a way to escape to his punishment: go to his office hours because he loves to be worshiped. Do it if you want at least a B.
Don't count on a curve, there isn't one.
Okay prof for teaching, but if you really want a good lecturer, take Nachenburg, but you can go to w/e com sci teacher, the hw and basic material for the classes are the same

Extremely helpful! I was in actually both his CS31 and CS32 classes, and Dr. Smallberg was very clear and precise about everything he taught. I emailed him on average 10 emails per proj/hw, and he would patiently answer all of them, and I'm just ONE of about 200 of his students! The only prof that would top his teaching style is probably Nachenberg

I guess he's an ok lecturer although his lectures were pretty boring.  I found the class very difficult due to having no prior programming experience whereas most of the class did...I would not recommend taking this class without trying to learn some C++ first because otherwise you will find it HARD.

He is a great teacher, goes very much into depth and really brings all the details to the foreground, and he does it all very clearly. Tests are difficult (long), and homeworks/projects are also really large (and if you make a single mistake you can get 0 points in some cases). Overall hard but very good teacher.
One of the best CS lecturers at UCLA by far.
Very helpful, he conveys ideas very well and makes concepts easier to understand.

I had this professor in the 80's.  He turned me on to Computer Science, and programming, in particular.  He has the unique talent of being able to take very complex concepts and break them down so that any student can take it and run!  I will always remember Dr. Smallberg.  Thank you!!
Excellent. Sort of corny at times, but he's a really good teacher.

Only 3 opportunities to get a grade. The breakdown is 15% for a quiz, 30% midterm and 35% final. He never posts the grades so you are kind of just blind the whole time never knowing your grade until the final day. He does not really engage with the students either, just a monotone lecture and a study guide with unclear guidelines. just dont
It's difficult to understand what he says.

I took Professor Phillips Fall 2020 & loved his class. His class is Asynchronous & lecture heavy. The good thing is that you could rewatch the lectures & download the transcript to highlight the necessary content. He always gives a study guide for midterm and final which is very useful. You don't really need to read bc everything is in the lectures
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Midterm, paper and final. Best lecturer by far at UCLA. He is straight forward and very clear. Reading will reinforce his lectures. Go to class take good notes and you will be fine, even though he is intimidating.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I loved professor Phillips' class. He is a fantastic teacher and gives great lectures. I would take every class with him if I could. One midterm, a paper, and final. VERY LECTURE HEAVY. As long as you come to class and take good notes you will do fine. You only really need one of the books he assigns as a supplement to help reinforce the lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: There were about 40 pages of one book that you actually needed. The rest of the test/paper material can be done with just lecture notes. Take good notes, he has no power point and lectures like a stream of consciousness. Write fast and study hard to pass. Tough on grading, you really need to know the answers. Very interesting and funny lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: The greatest lecturer I have ever had!
Fairly easy class as long as you attend lecture and take notes. Phillips is great, very entertaining and clear.
The class isn't as difficult as some would have you believe. If you're the type of student that attends class regularly and takes good notes you should be fine.

I had Phillips for Hist. of Rome and all in all I would recommend him. He delivers crystal clear lectures, and while you really have to study hard, like others say, it's well worth it. The only downside is his personality. He comes across as very arrogant and unapproachable. Try to talk to him and he'll brush you off quickly. Needs to learn respect

I loved Roman History with Phillips!Yes, the class is difficult, but he is an excellent professor! Like others have said,you will learn in his class whether you want to or not. Many students were intimidated by him but he wasn't all that bad. Very handsome professor which can be distracting during lectures. Take Phillips for any class!

This guy is hilarious! When he gives his lectures, he is extremely scrupulous and straight-forward, but then he'll randomly crack a joke, and get the entire class laughing. He's pretty good looking too. Only downside is that you spend the ENTIRE class taking notes. There isn't even enough time to zone out. :(

Phillips is a VERY hard professor. Lectures extremely fast, and really expects students to know every aspect of Roman history. He is not very approachable, however, his notes comprise his exams. On the plus side, he's very CUTE, so that could either be good or bad depending on if you're easily distracted. Overall, Phillips is a great professor!

Awesome class, great prof. Hist of Rome is not a piece of cake, but he makes the subject very interesting. Take good notes and you're golden! Phillips is a great prof, but don't expect him to be your best friend. More friendly in office hours than in class. Take this class, you will learn prob more than you wanted to about Roman History.
His lectures comprise his exams. If you take half-way decent notes there is no NEED to read the texts (although it is helpful if you live in a world of perfect time management).

Phillips is a very serious teacher, but he is not boring. he is very straight forward midterm, final and one paper. if you mess up on the paper and the midterm but do good in the final you could still earned an A.

He is not very approachable after his lectures, but he does know his greek history.  His classes were the best notes I have ever taken.  I didnt really do the reading cause he covers it during lectures.  You just have to know your stuff and know details!!!
He is boring and hard. Don't take him unless you want to read at least 100 pages a night!

Doctor P is an amazing person. My sister died at the end of the term so I reached out for an extension on an essay and to try to find an alternative date to take the final. He called me 5 minutes later to tell me he closed out my Grade and exempted me from the remaining work. I doubt he realized just  how much that kindness meant to me.

I took BIB1001 Old Testament History with Dr Patrick in spring term 2024
 ( PLNU ).He is a fast speaker but his lectures are recorded so you can listen a couple times if you need to.The downside of his class was that you only have 4 items that make up the entire grade so you can't afford to have a bad midterm or final and expect to pass .
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Equal parts Arrogance & Ego + visibly bored in every lecture. That is what you get with this guy.His expectations are unrealistic&the grading scale is built around the assumption everyone taking this class is already an expert.
I took this class with Dr P through USC last year he was a cool dude. His lectures are recorded so you can watch them when you have time + the entire class is graded on two tests & one paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great professor! Very clear expectations from his students. Is more concerned about students understanding the material than assigning a bunch of busy work.

Dr P is one of the most real teachers I have had. I took this class in 2022 with him.The man is an unapologetic Conservative Gay Jew & he changed my view on all 3 of those groups. He taught diversity in a way that erased the identity politics & threw out the PC Garbage.
Very engaging lectures, pretty fair grading . The class covers a lot of ground that you are expected to be responsible for.

Doctor Patrick is that rare breed of professor where he is equally  as obnoxious as he is intellectually gifted. The man literally quoted HIMSELF in a lecture about his own published material . YES he pulls sections of the material from HIS OWN text. Unfortunately he is the only professor who teaches this course most years.
Dr Patrick is a NO nonsense professor. The exie

Thankful I switched sections & ended up with Doctor Patrick.He is what I call a no frills professor.There are 2 tests + 2 essays which make up the entire course. He doesn't care if you attend the lectures , he doesn't care if you watch the pre recorded lectures , he doesn't assign homework .

Doctor Patrick has moments of clarity but his lectures tend to turn into him rambling.If you think you are going to pull source material or quotes from CNN or other left leaning media sources you can expect him to reject it on the grounds of credibility issues.Clear conservative slant to his world view which is reflected in his grading.

WATCH THE LECTURES.That is all you have to do to get an A.Every single question on the two exams he gives all term are pulled word for word from his lectures.I think I went to class twice in person & just watched the rest of the lectures on the portal since they were recorded.He was slow to respond to emails but that's the only issue I had.

The course itself was great with information packed lectures he records ahead of time which lets you watch them multiple times so that's cool. Dr.Patrick has a reputation for keeping a tight grading scale and its well deserved.I barely swung a B+ and I am in the top 2% of my class.Only 13 people in a class of 125 got A's.

(Taken at USC 2020) The only real downside to Professor Patricks class is that he can be extremely long winded when he's lecturing. The grading scale is tough but clear.There are only a couple of tests all term.

I enjoyed this class and professor patricks energy all semester.I was nervous taking a DEI class taught by a vocal conservative but I sure was glad I made the choice to enroll in his section.He was loud and energetic at times but in that way people get when they are passionate about the material.

Dr Patrick was one of my favorite lecture professors at USC & it was sad to see him leave. He took a course based on the concepts of Equity & Inclusion yet he kept the material unbiased and non political. The lectures were clear .The Grading rubric was tough but easy to understand.All around a great course .

Dr. Patrick teaches across multiple departments (Law , Education ,Business) and a pretty wide array of courses.I took LAW105 with him back in 2020 & EDUC523 in 2021.He taught the Law class with a sterile efficiency that bored me to death.Then I went into the Education class expecting the same and got a super happy high energy lecture instead.
Doctor Patrick has cultivated one of my favorite courses with this one.The pace is pretty fast but well worth it in my opinion.Very informative and eye opening.
He is a bit long winded with his lectures but easy to understand.Tough grading scale on the final paper.Overall Decent guy.

The guy is a run of the mill CA republican you will often find teaching at the business and law colleges here in SoCal.He is obviously over qualified to teach a basic policy class which is made abundantly clear by how bored he looked the entire semester.Hard to stay engaged in a class when the professor is checked out from day 1.

I took EDUC 523 Equity & Inclusion In Organizational Contexts with Dr Patrick at USC back in 2020.He is still one of my favorite lecture professors from my time there.The lectures are recorded and correspond with the textbook even though its more like a teaching aid as he only pulls about half the lecture content from it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Be prepared to do a ton of reading & watch the video lectures multiple times.Dr Patrick teaches this class at warp speed under the assumption that you've come in with a basic understanding of individual constitutional protections & the landmark Supreme Court decisions from the 1960-1980s.Dont take this class if you don't or you will not keep up.

Law 675: HIV/AIDS Law and Public Policy (Taken Fall 2019)
Dr. Patrick has an insane amount of knowledge on the subject matter which he somehow squeezes into a single term.I learned more from this course about the history of HIV-AIDS & LGBTQ Public-Judicial Policy than I have learned in 3 years of academic courses on the same subject.

Law 675:HIV-AIDS Law and Public Policy(2019)

Dr.Patrick has an insane amount of knowledge on the subject matter which he somehow squeezes into a single term.I learned more from this course about the history of HIV-AIDS & LGBTQ Public-Judicial Policy than I have learned in 3 years of academic courses on the same subject.This was a fantastic class.

I had my first class with Dr. Patrick in 2020 & he was one of the kindest most flexible instructors I have ever had. My mother had passed away & we were about 2/3 through the term.He let me submit my term paper early and counted it as my entire grade allowing me to skip the midterm and final so I could go home early to be with my family.

Prof. Patrick is a nice guy. He talks with everyone and tries to engage people in class. The bad part is he ends up selecting his favorite students then spends the entire term asking the same 5 students questions. The class isn't too difficult but it felt like I was watching a podcast with the same 5 guests not attending a university lecture.

I first took a course he taught at USC on censorship & the 1st amendment in 2018. So I jumped on this class when I saw he was teaching. He speaks loudly & is bold with the way he presents controversial topics. He is a very passionate educator who wont shy away from discussing the darker parts of society. Highly Recommend taking his classes.
Professor Patrick is one of the best professors you will ever have.His resume is impressive and he is incredibly accessible.His lectures can be long but they are full of information.

His entire class was built on extensive recorded lectures that make up almost all of the material you need to learn.The textbook wasn't really necessary to buy since the tests are taken directly word for word from his lectures.Almost zero classwork to turn in.I would take him again
*Clear Grading * Clear Expectations * Minimal Work *Exceptionally Tough Grading on the Term Paper

This was easily the hardest course I have taken so far in my time at UCLA.This course is a carbon copy of one taught at Harvard, he teaches it exactly how it was designed.There was a significant amount of reading from multiple texts that resulted in the need for major dedicated time to study.I learned a ton but it was a rough ride.

I had Dr Patrick a few years ago at USC, it was one of my favorite classes.I went back to school in 2022 and had him again.He is easy to understand and his assignments are straight forward.If you study for the tests you will do great in any of his classes.

Doctor Patrick is an extremely knowledgable professor but the man pulls no punches.He will have an all out argument with a student if they get disrespectful and drag the hell out of them while he's at it.Lectures felt like a combination of high value learning + a gay comedy hour.If you are easily offended take a different class.I LOVED it.

Boring Class
He was easy to communicate with
He offered virtual office hours twice a week
I would take him again

Doctor Patrick is a hilarious man with clear conservative leanings teaching a course previously taught by a super liberal professor.This was the most entertaining & information packed class I have taken.If you need a professor whose going to pander to sensitive students feelings this isn't the class for you.
Great Professor.He is a no BS kind of guy & that's clear right off.If you watch the lectures you will ace this class.Minimal tasks to complete & no busy work.
Lectures are long but recorded so you can watch multiple times.He talks fast so I found myself having to watch multiple times to be able to take notes.
Professor Patrick is tough but fair.If you study you will do well.

I also took LAW105 with him but this was night and day.He seems extremely passionate about this class and his lectures had an excited energy.He had live Q&A sessions scheduled with live lecture hours that went along with the recorded stuff.I left this class with a totally different view of him.I had a fantastic experience this time around.
Middle of the road instructor who doesn't seem to care about this particular course.Everything is cookie cutter
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is the first college teacher I have had who actually went out of his way to encourage students to fill out campus reviews of his course and rate him online at the end of the quarter.I had a good experience with him ended up pulling off an A.His office hours are virtual and he responds too his own emails pretty fast.

His lectures lean right and have a clear conservative bend to them.He is extremely particular about what he will consider to be "Reliable Source Material " and anything that veers too far to the left will likely be deemed unreliable for the purpose of citation. Great Instructor overall as long as you color within the lines.

I had previously taken Law105 with him so I had a decent idea of what to expect.I was a bit surprised that he stuck to only assigning 1 paper and 2 exams all term for a much more complicated course.The lectures are  recorded and available online.He is pretty hands off but easy to reach during office hours if you need to.

Professor Patrick is a nice guy & easy to get ahold of during virtual office hours.The tests are manageable if you watch the lectures a couple of times each.The grading on the term paper seemed to be on a whole different level than the rest of the class which felt inconsistent.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was the second time I have taken a lecture class with Doctor Patrick & it was run the same way.Pre recorded lectures that stay available all semester, two midterm tests pulled directly from his lectures, and one term paper.He grades all of the papers himself and is pretty open with how tough of a grader he is.

Doctor Patrick uses previously recorded lectures and then also sets specific dates for live lectures but you dont need to do both.All the tests are pulled from the lectures.I didn't use my textbook once.

Professor Patrick runs his classes on a super simple system.There is zero homework throughout the term.The lectures are recorded online starting on day one and stay up for the entire term.The test questions are taken word for word from his lectures.Put your best energy into the one term paper he assigns as he takes no prisoners with his grading.

I took DPI 122:Politics and American Public Policy with Dr Patrick this past term.This is a course borrowed directly from Harvard and he graded the class as if it were being taught at Harvard.There was very little homework or classwork required so most of your final grade comes from two exams which are based off of his lectures not the textbook.

This was my second class with Dr Patrick and his teaching style is consistent.He assigns a limited amount of work and leaves the recorded lectures open all term.Its up to you if you watch them or join in on the handful of live lecture Q&A sessions.The exam questions are taken directly from his lectures not the text book.Easy course if you study.

I have taken three different courses with Professor Patrick since 2020 and he frames his classes the same exact way every term .There will be one large essay assignment which will be graded on a bell curve with very little room for mistakes and then two mid term exams.Those 3 items make up your final grade in the class.

Dr Patrick is one of the coolest teachers I have had since transferring to UCLA.He sets you up for success by making the classwork extremely light and the lectures are available anytime you want to watch them online.I didn't do well on the one big essay but after speaking with him during office hours he gave me a week to redo it and I got an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Prof P is AWESOME

This guy is hilarious and always in a good mood.He loves what he teaches & if you show an interest he will bend over backward to help you succeed.The entire class is graded on one paper and a couple exams which is fantastic.This class had zero busywork which I loved.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would not take a course taught by Professor Patrick again.The material is clear and the lectures are pre recorded but that's where the good stuff ends.His expectations are unrealistic and his grading is downright unfair.He gave me a C on a paper other professors looked at and said they would have given A's.

I have mixed feelings as I write this review.He has a VERY strong personality and doesn't take being challenged well.He makes it clear he isn't paid to be liked.The material is straight forward and the lectures are recorded so overall the class itself is a 5/5 you just have to deal with his ego.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Patrick is one of those professors that you will love or hate.His expectations are high but clear from the first day of class.The entire class is made up of a couple of essays and the course exams.All of his lectures are recorded & available on the student portal so you can watch them multiple times which was great for studying.

Professor Patrick is tough .My entire grade was made up of 1 Essay + Midterm+ Final Exam with each being worth 1/3 of the points available for the entire course.If you are a high performer he's great but if you struggle or are not a great test taker you should find a section taught by someone else.Very Little Flexibility.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Unwilling to give second chances. Extremely smart but obviously more focused on her research. If you're a bad test-taker, don't take her class. Majority of the grade is impacted by exam scores. Blunt and rude to her students. We're all people.. reflecting on what the point of all this is because of her. Compassion/ discourse is nowhere to be found.

Professor Diaconecsu is an amazing woman. She is not only a profound researcher, but she is probably the most generous professors I have met. She is always willing to help. The content of this course is very abstract and elusive, making it extremely difficult to teach, but Paula overcomes this by jumping right into endless, helpful examples.

It is very hard to understand what she says in class. Not just because of her accent, but of the awful way she teaches. You'll just find out that you learn nothing from her. Extremely vague explanations on the course materials. Don't bother to go to her class although she will give pop quizzes during lecture. Office hours are much helpful.

She should be avoided at all costs.  Knowledgable, but inarticulate, unclear, and incredibly difficult. She has ridiculously high expectations of student performance.  There is no way to feel that you have done well in this class.

Took DC1 with this professor and overall had a really awesome experience. Was extremely accommodating with deadlines and very helpful for feedback. The instructor is not very tech savvy, however. There was often repeated lecture videos within the same module from different years, wrong assignment deadlines, and incorrect turning in instructions.

This was asynchronous DC1 class. Overall great—I signed up for DCII with him. Gives good feedback and chance to correct work. Is encouraging. Written assignments don't match what's said to do in the videos, so there's some figuring out to do. Lots of the dates are wrong in the assignments too. And it's a lot of work, but is 6 credits, so…

Larry is such a good professor! He explains everything so thoroughly in his lessons and is very clear about grading criteria and feedback. I never had any questions about how to complete assignments or what was expected. My class was remote/asynchronous but he recorded individualized videos reviewing our work every week which was so helpful.

Larry is great, he is very helpful and thorough. He will constantly remind you that this is a technical course, not an art class so we should focus on perfection of our technical abilities. I love that because I get caught up with details and waste time a lot so time management is key here. I've developed so many skills, I hope to take his DC2&DC3.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Larry is very direct, even blunt. But I definitely got a lot out of the course which is more than I could say about DC I. Take notes, he likes you to take notes. I did not get the impression that he was handing out grades. I feel I'm in the minority getting an A+.
he is so nice! you are pretty much guarenteed an A if you do the work! TAKE him if you have the chance!

He is incredibly smart and this class was very interesting but he is just horrible. Yes, he did provide the questions before the midterm and final but the answers provided were not good enough for him. Thats what he told me at least. He is very old-fashioned and could not care less about how well you do in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took this class because I needed the req but it sucked for me. Half the class failed. I got 10/40 on the midterm (which is an in person 3 HR pen written exam) when asked why he said "Your answers are right but arent what Im looking for." He, under any circumstance, does not give out extra credit even if you are a point off from a passing grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Convinced these good reviews are either him and his TA army, NPCs, or just the Bio students that have these concepts down to a T. If you are new to Bio Anthro and have no photographic memory I am warning you to steer clear of this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Worst class possibly ever. Passionate teacher. The lectures based solely on what he says in class so no slides or anything be prepared to keep up with what he says. random pop quizzes. Exams are worth the most of class but are extremley hard and he has said that he does not hand out A's to everyone but only the poeple whos anwsers are perfect.

Acknowledge the professor's passion in this topic Midterm (35%) and final (45%) are in-person challenging essay questions. Lecture content necessitates thorough note-taking and memorization due to the absence of recordings or PowerPoint slides. Heavy reading load as topics frequently change, TAs grade like everyone can afford F's. F this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was the worst. He is very very smart which is great but he spends most of lecture ranting. His exams are incredibly hard and there's only two of them. He expects you to be able to master the material in the way that he has mastered it and I feel like it is very unrealistic. He is a nice guy but I would never take him again.

I hate leaving poor reviews on Profs., however... His lectures are interesting but he spend most of the time ranting about nothing. The TA's are helpful. He only has 2 exams which is entirely ridiculous. I was CONSTANTLY stressed about my grade and less focused on the topics. If you can, I suggest avoiding.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just a heads up, Professor Fessler posts all of the course evaluations (completed before we get our grades back) from students on his personal website (danielmtfessler dot com) to help students out. There are a ton more reviews there than here.

Most intelligent professor on UCLA campus. If you didn't take advantage of the information provided or the hours he set side for meeting with students, then your loss. Hardest A I have ever worked for, but totally worth it. He's tough, but not unapproachable as some previous students have alluded to.

Super hard class. Yes he gives you the questions ahead of time, but no they are not easy to answer by any means even if you have a week to prepare. This class NEEDS a discussion section. While prof. Fessler is indeed extremely smart, he demands a lot from his students in this class so be prepared to work your hardest.

Fessler leads an extremely informative class based on lectures and readings. He recites lectures from memory with 1 or 2 slides per class. The readings he assigns are absolutely required as there's a quiz on them every morning AT 8am when the class starts. It requires work, but he's extremely intelligent and helpful and the information is awesome
great class but very difficult and pop quizzes almost every class. So much for a class. If i wasn't working fulltime then i would have done much better. Did enjoy the class alot

Best class I took at UCLA, straight up! Fessler is very hard but if you do the work you will definitely take a lot out of this class. Material is extremely interesting and relevant to everyday life. Highly recommend!
Seriously, this guys gives you all the questions for the exams. How can you possibly do bad in this class?
great class. just pay attention once in a while. this isn't high school
