His lectures are very interesting but the material that he presents is so hard to understand. Based of that material or the reading he gives "pop" quizzes" pretty much everyday. I would not advise to take his class..very intense
wow! he was so rude! dont think about asking for help because the chance that you will get it is slim to none... his class is very hard... the average score on one of our exams was a 60%
he may seem strict and arrogant but he has good intentions, and has many interesting things to say; is very knowledgable and lectures basically from memory
If you don't have a background in medieval Russian history or aren't ready to dedicate the next few months of your life to it, don't take her classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She's a really nice lady. If you're having a difficult time understanding what she wants in your papers and tests, go to her office hours. She's very helpful and will do what she can to help you understand. Be sure to do the reading and bring your textbooks to class.
Class material is really interesting, too bad the teacher isn't. She is a horrible grader, and the lectures are horribly boring. Don't take this class unless you have too.

Excellent professor with lucid teaching methods in her classes! It truly made me love learning Russian History and the Russian culture! Exams are all essay style. Midterm consists of 5 id terms with an essay. Final it's just like an essay(she changed the format at last). Final essay on a selected topic! Class is very engaging! Just love it!

lots of cool guest speakers.  class isnt hard but prof has very specific guidelines for essays and assignments.  there are no exams. good lectures but attendance is required. interesting lecture. prof responds quickly to emails very helpful. great class definitely take.

The study guides are a nightmare but the lectures and guest speakers are amazing. The study guides are instead a test but they add about 2 extra readings a week that you don't talk about in class (on top of the readings for guest speakers). Grades strangely harsh for a professor that gives a 24 hour grace period. Guest speakers make it worth it.

Super nice, responds to emails / questions and concerns and is flexible regarding attendance conflicts. Study guides are genuinely helpful and seem like a lot to handle, but are more helpful than exams in terms of understanding material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Frederick provides an expected learning experience for the number one public university; simultaneously, he requires you to apply your knowledge. I recommend Professor Frederick if you have a genuine desire to better yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this class! The lectures were super interesting and he had guest speakers, like authors and people in the porn industry, come talk to us. There are no tests for this class but there are two very long and detailed study guides that are worth the majority of your grade, but if you stay on track you should be good.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dave made this class very helpful and easy. He had a study guide that covered the lectures, however I feel like the study guides were a little long and graded a bit harshly. Other than that his class was really enjoyable. He brought in various guest speakers who discussed anything from bdsm to sex toys. I really enjoyed Daves class.

The class was without any exams which is very chill. There were 2 study guides, some discussions, and a final project ( optional). I like the study guides because they made to find out so much interesting information about COVID and other psychological issues, while I was answering the questions.

The lectures were not recorded so attendance and participation are critical. The grading is pretty fair and the rubric is clear. Focus on the Study Guides, start them early and you will be fine. Overall, this was a fun class and the guest speakers were all really helpful and informative for the study guide.

Never have I completed so much work for a class. The class material is interesting and fun to learn about but the amount of work for this class was so draining. No exams, just 20+ page study guides. I would honestly rather take the exams than spend 10+ hours on each study guide. Grade breakdown: 2 study guides 60%, group proj 20%, final proj 20%
Professor Dave is really helpful and attentive to his students. The lectures were always well organized and interesting, but the workload was overwhelming.

The lectures were not recorded so attendance and participation are critical. The grading is pretty fair and the rubric is clear. Focus on the Study Guides, start them early and you will be fine. Overall, this was a fun class and the guest speakers were all really helpful and informative.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Was a chill class in terms of lecture and guest speakers every class meeting. He gives lots of class time to work on group projects during class; however, if you fall behind on assignments, it can be really stressful. He communicates expectations clearly and communicates a lot week to week. Overall, I recommend this class, it was interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dave's lectures and his plethora of guest speakers makes his class interesting and fulfilling to learn about. What ruined the class for me was the heavy load of homework that's worth 75% of our grade. Instead of midterms, Dave gives 20+ page, single spaced study guides. I have never completed more work for a class in my college career...

This class is SUPER insightful and the way Frederick structures his class reallys helps your learning outcome and engagement.The two major study guides that really need attention to detail  so do not expect a regular just fill in the answer but be more indepth with outside resource and person. I recommend this course if you are able to stay on trac

4 Study Guides based on reading, lots of extra credit opportunities, and one final project. The lectures are not only very interesting, but you have the opportunity to meet a lot of other influential psychologists, researchers, and authors. Clear grading structure and instructions for assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Love love loved this course! The summer course was super flexible, and Dr. Frederick is always more than willing to answer any questions you have. Keep in mind you REALLY have to put in some work to get an A; that being said I thought the lectures and study guides were interesting and engaging, and everything was graded fairly.

Professor Frederick is a wonderful professor. Even though the class was very big, 70+ students live each class, he made it very personable and every lecture felt like a group discussion which I appreciated a lot. Personally, I think when professors take this approach, I tend to learn and engage more in class. I highly recommend this class!

Exams are memorization heavy but come straight from the study guides. Also good to know research methods (experiments, correlations). Lectures are fun and engaging (and funny). One of my fave classes.

TAKE THIS CLASS!!! For someone like me who's suffering from crippling senioritis & have tasted the various demoralizing flavors of college courses, Dave's class was a much-needed breath of fresh air. I felt like this class reminded me of how exciting learning can be, and gave students a chance to voice our insights. Inspirational & highly memorable
great class. fun guest speakers. most of grade is short answer exam. learn about different topics in sexuality.
Loved loved loved this class! Discussion and debate in class get to know problem well and great lectures. 2 exams one poster and completing study guides.

Engaging lectures / homework that makes you study for the exam / mostly lecture with some discussion and activities / exams hard but doable / funny and interesting lectures / talk about hormones, brain, sexual satisfaction, dating, trans issues. One of my favorite classes.

Legit class. Exams require lots of memorizing but totally doable. Pay attention to the research studies. Sit in groups of 6 and lots of discussion. Lectures interesting and funny sometimes. Amazing guest speakers

Dr Dave is fantastic. Interesting lectures and all exams are directly from homework study guides. You ha e to memorize a lot and know research methods well. Great guest speakers and lectures. Attendances coutns toward grade.
I love his lesson
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Frederick is a fantastic professor, his lectures are soo interesting and rich of contenutes and discussion moment

I really enjoyed the class, it was a great way to end my last summer at UCLA. The course content was extremely interesting and very informative. In class discussions about topics, and exams are hand written but he gives study guides he makes the exams straight from

The course is entertaining The weakness of the course is the exam format. I agree that the recall test is better. However, his test is just the recitation of the powerpoint. More questions that link or apply the course material to the real world is better because not all people taking this course go to graduate school.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: he is very inspirational

David is an amazing professor that engages with the class to challenge their thought process. The lectures are well paced and students are given enough time to discuss with their peers their ideas. He is very approachable (available through email and before class) and gives the students the tools that they need to succeed in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This class is really great and so is Professor Dave! If you do the study guides and pay attention in lecture you will do great. Study guides are similar to the exams. You learn a lot in a short of the time. Topics were interesting as well!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: given lecture+reading packets. Lecture packets are a little hard to follow during lecture because sometimes the things he's talks about doesn't reflect to the packet. Make sure you do the packets though they're E.C.(3%boost). Tests are hard, but lecture is easy + entertaining. If you study a week before, you'll do pretty well in the class!

Dave was a really funny and engaging professor. However, the tests very heavily rely on memorization. I also wish we spoke more about human sexuality rather than biological and evolutionary studies of why certain phenomena exist. I would recommend Dave for his lecturing and the course readings, but I did not feel like I learned much about humans.
He is reaaaally a responsible professor! Clear study guides help me a lot in the exam, and the lectures are interesting with lots of discussions and surveys.

Dave provides lecture/reading study guides that outline what he expects you to learn from the course, and they are extremely helpful. As long as you are engaged in lecture, which isn't hard with him, and fill the guides out as you go along you will learn the material and do well in the course.

Professor Frederick makes the class interesting with his lectures and constant jokes. His exams mostly require memorization but he gives enough extra credit for those that dont do as well on them. His study guides are very helpful and are almost exactly what is on the exam. Would definitely take again!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This professor makes the class very interactive and interesting through his lectures and his personality. While I personally don't like group projects it was very useful in this class to delve a bit deeper into certain concepts. Overall a very good class and definitely worth learning about.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: David is very up front with what is expected in the course. He provides study guides for lectures and readings that help you know exactly what will be expected of you on the exams, and the group projects he requires are actually fun and helpful in understanding the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: best class ever!  i put off taking research lab until my last semester here but this class was amazing.  we covered research on racism, dating, etc.  weekly quizzes and two group projects.  Dave is funny and cares.  The class goes REALLY fast

Eugene Volokh is terrible. He's just interested in using students for his lucrative so-called First Amendment practice working for big law firms on behalf of big corporations. A phony baloney. He's from the Ukraine.

Volokh is very funny and extremely intelligent. He describes himself, as a "crazy libertarian" and is a bit eccentric. Relies heavily on the textbook but is very easy to follow his class structure in the syllabus and the material is not too difficult to wrap your head around.

Volokh's great. He's hilarious, and he likes the material and makes it fun. Unfortunately, his brain works a little differently than most people and so sometimes it's tough to follow his logic or keep up with his reasoning. But his class is actually really formulaic and easy to follow once you get through all the material.
Brilliant guy. Not always great about keeping the classroom a safe and respectful learning environment.
No Comments

Tough grader, grades on a curve. 
If she is having a bad day then the entire class is having a bad day with her.

Grading is tough, finished on a curve.
Let's just say if she is having a bad day you will too in this class.
This class is very easy overall. The grading may appear harsh, but don't stress about it—she curves everyone at the end, and everyone WILL PASS. But not recomanded if your metal is not strong

This class is very easy overall. The grading may appear harsh, but don't stress about it—she curves everyone at the end, and everyone WILL PASS. 

My course points for the whole quarter are 411/428, which is like 96%, and I got an A easily.
The way she does homework are terrible. You can either get a 0 or 100. If you forget even one in text citation its an automatic 0

She is really nice and also really funny during lecture. For her assignments, as long as you understand the homework structure and follow the guideline properly, you should receive a really good grade.

Not enough space in the text box to explain the pain this class brought me. No rubrics given for the essays, a total of 15 of them. Weekly homework graded on completion sounds nice, until you realize the final exam is two randomly chosen essay questions from the total 12 homework assignments and an additional personal social change one too. Awful

This professor writes vague test questions and expects students to provide ambitious responses that cover a wide range of terms and definitions that are neither listed nor hinted at.  The grading is extremely arbitrary - a 36/120 curved to a C and was considered average.  I am disgusted with this waste of tuition and taxpayer money. TAKE JEPSON!

Her lectures are well organized -- she always writes valuable information on the overhead. Her tests are somewhat lengthy, but are fair. Homework is quite a loadful. Nice professor overall; funny and tries to engage audience.
No Comments

I really liked Professor Mahtash's class a lot. I think the structure of it resembled what working in industry is like the most closely out of all the classes in my Master's program, which I found invaluable. 

Class is not very organized and randomized groups for projects was challenging, but it taught me a lot about improvising and collaboration.

She's VERY nice and very helpful. She'll take the time to make sure everyone understands everything. Her homework is very easy and only focuses on concepts. The only thing I didn't like is that i feel like I didn't learn much.
Great upperdivision Professor!  Care a LOT about student's learning and understanding; focuses and logic and concept, NOT memorization!
One of the better upper div Stats professors.
No Comments

Stats 13 was not difficult (open book and notes), but Esfandiari is a horrible teacher. Lectures are incomprehensible, and her 'concern' for student learning is not really as strong as she wants you to believe.

He mumbles everything but when you can understand him, everything he says is fascist. Not university admin fascist but real dyed-in-the-wool fascism even though he says everyone's anti-semitic. Shouldn't be teaching.

19th century French short stories. The readings were interesting. Friendly professor, very approachable. Mostly lecture, but he encourages students to comment on texts. Grading: 2 five page papers 35% each; Attendance and participation 30%. No tests or quizzes. Recommended. :-)
No Comments
Calculators are not allowed in the exams, good luck :)
Very great lecturer. One of the better Econ professors at UCLA. But the classes are pretty difficult. Notes and practice problems are the best way to study for exams.

Tests are hard and get easier after the first one.  Best lectures, very clear and concise.  Do the questions, they are like the test.  My econ prof now is horrible and just shows slides and doesn't work out the problems.  McDevitt is much better and is the best econ prof I've had so far.  Didn't realize it until now

OK, this guy is one of the best speakers I know.  Very clear, interesting, and easy to understand.  However, his tests are tricky with a crappy curve.  You need to know calculus and obscure econ concepts from earlier classes.  40% of questions he never went over in class or modified extremely.  Always trying to trick you.  Tough graders too.
Great teacher very understandable lectures. Tests are very hard but they are curved and if you study his lectures and study guides you won't be surprised when you take the test.

You need to know your stuff....but study and you'll do well...he's maybe the only FAIR econ professor at ucla, and indeed an excellent teacher...the lectures were interesting and easy to follow, the lecture notes were clear, and the study questions are exa
Pretty hard tests! Know everything for the finals. Study questions help on essay portion but must know general knowledge of everything gone over in lectures because multiple choice is killer!
Easy teaching style.  Tests are straight forward.
No Comments
Very straight forward and never off topic. The study questions are extremely similar to the tests - if you can perfect those, it's not hard to ace the class.
Great teacher.Very structured and organized.He gives you a lot of value.
One of the best teachers I've ever had.  He's very approachable in office hours, and his lectures are extremely well-organized (and taught from memory!)
strict, but very clear, and useful
Cool guy! Very clear explanations, super organized. Best Econ prof I had so far @ UCLA! Make sure to study his review sheets.
No Comments
No Comments
I did not have a positive experience in this class. I would recommend taking 10A from any other professor if available.

This professor accused me of plagiarism because he said he couldn't understand how much paper related to the various prompts we could choose from. He gave me a D. I was able to salvage my grade, but it was not only insulting (I didn't plagiarize) but he was rude about how he approached it and did not provide any sort of constructive feedback.

Prof. Fisher's passion for what he teaches makes his lectures truly profound and thought-provoking. I learned so much more in his class than I ever expected to from a lower division course. The class is challenging due to the sheer volume of material, but he is quite understanding & fair if you need extensions or if any other difficulties arise.

Prof Fisher is intimidating and a bit of a bully. Always cuts students off when they speak, and his responses are mostly judgmental. I tried my absolute best in his class which wasn't enough, and all the help I sought was a waste of time because he's so unclear. He grades with so much exactness for someone with vague essay prompts. Don't take.

I looked forward to a Fisher lecture every week.  Entertaining and energetic. Class workload is a bit on the heavy side but doable. I enjoyed all the readings save one or two. Good class, great professor.

Although Prof. Fisher is extremely bright; I would hold off on this class if your a transfer student attending UCLA for the first time, this Fall quarter.  Even under the best of circumstances this class can be challenging.  I was new to UCLA and took Prof. Fisher's class my first quarter.  Yikes.  I was like a deer in the headlights.

This class exceeded my expectations. Professor Fisher was an absolutely amazing professor. Probably the best English professor I've ever had. He's brilliant! Sure, at times he spoke so fast I couldn't possibly write everything down, but every little thing he said was so great that it got imprinted on your mind anyway.
I love him.  He is bright, emotionally generous and cute.  I feel energized, when I leave his class.

Get a grip girls, Fisher is NOT cute. He looks more like Ross from "Friends" than Prof McDreamy. Also, I got the impression his caffeine-fueled lectures were prepared last-minute, and often ended with him shouting line numbers at students while they left the classroom. I thought the 2 TA 'guest lectures' were far more clearly presented and useful.
Yes, I am aware he went to Oxford. FYI, "Oxbridge" is a valid term used to describe someone of his educational background: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxbridge
Just reviewed but wanted to say to the person below me..it's OXFORD, not Oxbridge! And yes, his English education made the experience all the better. Loved his Canterbury lectures. :)

At first, Fisher really intimidated me. But, once you get to know him, he is really sweet and friendly and willing to help you succeed. I went to every office hours just for the sake of learning-he truly is passionate and brilliant! He is always willing to help.Immense reading load, but it was a great experience. Plus, he is really cute and witty!

Professor Fisher's lectures are challenging, and times, entertainingly digressive, but if you are willing to be  challenged and inspired to come up to his level you will be greatly rewarded . Yes, he does affect on an eccentric, arrogant Oxbridge persona, but I think it adds to his delivery, and makes him... appealing. Too bad he's married.
oh, by the way, my interest level prior to attending class was low.  As of right now, it's off the charts.  The 2/5 "interest" category in the prior evaluation should instead be 5/5--If not 6/5.

It was an honor to learn from Professor Fisher.  Before the class, I chose to take notes in a blue notebook to reflect my glum disposition towards the subject content.  Now, I find myself reading from the text in my spare time.  Never has a teacher so stirred my thoughts and touched my heart.  I'll never forget him.

Contrary to popular opinion, I think Fisher is great!  Yes, he challenges your arguments and has an extensive vocabulary, but isn't that what one should expect from UCLA? Shouldn't English majors welcome the challenge? He doesn't want people to restate his ideas. In fact, he once commended me for disagreeing with him on a midterm. He's just smart.

This child is the epitome of pretension. His lack of clarity or willingness to assist students exudes beyond his ego. As stated before, regurgitation is the best method applied in his course. The smell of coffee and tobacco are not very inviting towards the student body and one can sense he is there only to publish, not teach. How unfortunate.

Started off the course shooting down every argument voiced, and then had the nerve to be annoyed when no one wanted to talk in class. Wonder why that was? Blamed one particularly bad day on trying to quit smoking, and flat-out told me I deserved a higher grade than he'd given me. Not a great experience, overall.

The worst of the worst. Addicted to coffee & interrupts lecture when briefly misplaced it. Embracing his coffee took priority over writing any of the course's incomprehensibly complex terms/concepts. This makes it all the more painful when subject material predates Shakespeare & friends. Tops all off with an insanely rapid speech rate.

can i say that this man loves the sound of his own voice and even though he is obviously brilliant, he is a jerk and a big complainer. he asks grandiose questions and never give the answer. and u better regurgitate all of his personal ideas in your papers. i say save yourself the trouble, dont take him.

Pros: He's energetic, enthusiastic, well-informed, and uses helpful and sometimes funny analogies and anecdotes./ Cons: He paces across the front of the room and it gets distracting after a while; he encourages class participation, but has a habit of interrupting and dismantling the argument before the student's thought is completely voiced.

Socratic method. That says it all. Every sentence he says has to end with a question!! Can't seem to please him no matter how hard you try. However, he's really nice....maybe you just need to truly understand the Socratic method in order to get an A.

He is sweet but sooooo boring. I dropped out because I couldn't take it. Even tho its a 60-person class there is section and ta who has to teach this class because he has NO structure. There is in-person reflections on the lecture that you turn in every class a presentation on the reading and papers. Take if you dont mind sitting thru the lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dropped 139 because it was so boring. Has section. Prof talks about whatever he wants, no coordination at all. Consists of an in person section presentation on one of the readings, written comments EVERY lecture, weekly comments on readings, 4 papers. So much extra work because the professor just can't do his job and we still have to listen to it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Don't take your first qrter. He said first day - its a hard class lots of writing - you might want to leave  The TA I had was tough grader & nit picky. I have never gotten a C until her.  4 800 word papers includ final paper. I think its screwed that a TA decides my fate! that's where they get you have to show up. Trying to drop it to save my gpa.

Pretty good lectures.Interesting material.Grade based on attendance/participation at TA discussions, 3 2pg papers, 1 4pg paper as final.Papers based largely on assigned books but not very difficult or long readings.TAs DETERMINE YOUR GRADE!
picky graders that don't allow individual thought
TAs determine your grade so if they suck or if they are harsh graders too bad
Fiske is a good professor
a lot of work for one class
Great Professor!  Class involved a lot of reading and writing but the topic was so great it wasn't difficult.  He is very easy going and easy to talk to, his class is worth it.

He is a great professor. The topic is very entertaining and intriguing. Will leave you thinking long after the quarter is over. The TA's are not approachable probably due to lack of time. Eye-opening class, valuable lectures but can be confusing at times.
TA's didn't want to help, not enough time for everybody apparently. Go to lecture and skim the readings and you will do fine.
Lot's of reading involved. Go to office hours. TA is fairly  helpful

I'll reflect on him too, since many are. I'm now very successful, took this professor years ago,had 0 impact on my success. Psych is super easy, he just made tests hard. Was not approachable, acted like a God. While I don't want rats/mice in my home, sad he really does unnecessary cruel experiments on rats/mice that should be illegal, Sad! PIETA ?

Took him several years ago.  Now I teach college-level Psychology and I still look back on Fanselow's 110 as the best Psych class that I took at UCLA.  I'm literally passing on tidbits that I learned from him, directly to my own students.  By the way, my GPA was only OK, and I got an A- in this class without too much pain, so he's not that hard.

I has this professor a few years ago- so GPA doesnt matter & its not why I'm complaining- I do want to warn other students that this guy is terrible. You should avoid him at all costs. He cares more about his research than the class- I went to office hours to ask qs and hed get irritated. Worst professor @ ucla.

Prof Fanselow is an old school professor. He uses a chalkboard not powerpoint. I think this is why people think he is difficult. He isnt you just have to be on talk. He is definetly not arrogant or pompous. I have taken two classes with him and he has never been anything but helpful and friendly.I think there are a few bitter lowGPA ppl commenting.

Hard tests, have nothing to do with the book. The book even sucks. Study your but off and you will get a C. Don't take this class if you want an A or a B. Come on, how hard is it to know about ringing a bell and a dog salivating. He makes it hard in his tests!!!!Time to retire buddy!
Very interesting class; it's not as tough as people make it out to be.  Fanselow makes it tough because of his disorganized presentation in lectures.  Sections are mandatory.  Generally good lecturer
Fanselow was great. He tried to make the class interesting, and he'd answer any questions people had. Also nice to talk to outside of class. You will have to work hard though. Take him!
One of those super productive researchers who could care less about teaching.  Gets irritated by less intellectual people.  drop him and take Blaisdell or Balliene.
Difficult course content. He tries to be pretty clear in his lectures. Found that he was helpful when i went to office hours. Exams were hard.
Exams have nothing to do with the materials presented. This guy is the biggest narcissist and could care less about teaching. He's only at UCLA to bolster his research. Total ****!
No Comments

Quite possibly the worst professor I have had at UCLA.Incredibly difficult tests & a disorganized presentation of the material.He was dismissive of some of the students questions in class & struck me as a pompous ass when I dealt w/him 1-on-1.AVOID HIM!
I really enjoyed taking this class. A very interesting professor and very knowledgeable. I learned a lot and know it will help me in the future. Recommend this professor.

This is a professor that goes above and beyond the classroom. Understands it's not just a class but a stepping stone to how you would need to perform within an actual occupation. Is ready and willing to work with you on a myriad of topics not just class related ones. Overall 10/10.

I could go on forever ab how amazing Nikjeh is. The class was very informative for me. He goes through stuff in class thoroughly. He is so kind and makes class comfortable. As someone who feels like passing out while just introducing myself to the class, he made it so I wasn't afraid to answer questions or ask them. I will miss having him.

He was a great professor, and I learned a lot from his class (it was my first calc class). There is a LOT of homework but it makes it so that you know the material. Would definitely recommend this class, just be prepared to do the work and get a good grade!
Prof. Nikjeh is one the best profs. I had in UCLA. You will learn complicated subjects with easy examples. He grades fair and curves the tests if the highest test score is low. 100% recommended.

Professor Nikjeh is the most caring, thoughtful, and inspirational professor I've had during my 3 years at UCLA. He outlines very clearly his expectations, and is always there to answer any questions and be by your side throughout the duration of the course. I would recommend, if take this class with professor Nikjeh, you will not regret it!

Best professor I've had at UCLA. Really wants everyone to do well. 2 Exams and final exam are based completely off his lectures and class examples so be sure to study all of them. If you study it isn't hard to get an A.

Professor Nikjeh is such an amazing teacher. His Calculus class was the best math class I have ever taken! He is very clear and provides many examples. Only makes you take 3 midterm tests and 1 final. Just come to class and do the class activities. Tests are similar to the class examples. Such a great guy!

Prof.Nikjeh is so good at what he does, that he noticed a pattern of mistakes I was making on tests, which later helped my doctor pin a dyslexia diagnosis. This man is the reason I got into UCLA (took his LAPC STAT), is the reason I'm not afraid of math anymore, and I wish I could take his CALC class right now. Take his class, you won't regret it!

Amazing teacher and really helps you understand the material. Dr. Nikjeh made the transition to higher level math very manageable. He gave me a good recommendation letter for my medical school applications. Super nice person.
Dr. Nikjeh reignited my love of math. He made Calculus accessible, even for students who haven't taken a math class in 10+ years. He really cares, he's very funny, and his teaching makes sense.

His lecture notes are easy to look at and take in. He communicates very well and makes sure the lecture that he does make sense. His clicker questions focus more on understanding of concepts rather than understanding how to do basic Calculus (good thing).

Best class I've taken at UCLA. Nikjeh teaches in a way that encourages deep thought, real world application, and rapid individual. Everyone has the ability to succeed in the class & learn so much about Calculus if you really dive deep. I recommend putting more into this class than you need to, I promise you won't regret it.
This was the best course I've taken so far during my undergrad career. Prof. Nikjeh lectured extremely well. I loved the style of the course. I recommend this class if you want to learn Calculus.

He is great! He is a great lecturer and is knowledgeable in the subjects he discusses. He is actively available and is very interactive with the class. He wants everyone to understand everything he teaches. He is the type of professor all professors aspire to be.

Best professor that I have had the pleasure of having meeting and having here at UCLA.
I learned a lot about Integrals and derivatives from his class. Super nice person.

He is just amazing!! Very inspiring and he is always offering multiple times for office hours which is great:) definitely take him next quarter! Best prof ever! I'm glad I'm at UCLA because of him. He makes everything worth it.
The professor was like a magician the way he would solve the math.

I thought the material was difficult, but I really liked how it was organized and distilled in the lectures with engaging examples. I also liked how he demonstrated how to do some of the HW problems during the lecture, it really helped.

Prof Nikjeh is the GOAT. He explains the concepts clearly, and love helping students. HWs grade on completion, and midterms are similar to hws. The only challenge part is the final, which requires deep application of the formulas. However, he curves generously, and you will be guaranteed for an A as long as in high ranking.

Prof. Nikjeh is one of the best instructors in the department. We can learn lots of things. HWs grade on completion, and midterms are testing for concepts we learned in the class. The final is more about derivatives, integrals, and latest concepts, and Nikjeh gives a big curve.

Nikjeh might be the best professor in the math department! His lectures are very good and he grades fairly. HWs on completion while the two midterms are similar to the class examples and HW problems. He curves the grades generously at the end of the term. Highly recommend.

He is overall a pretty good professor. Math isn't my major but he explains the material well even for outsiders. I found he lectured a bit fast for my liking, but he provides many examples to teach the complicated concepts. Tests and quizzes are fair. 100% recommended.

This professor is AWESOME!!! He explains things very clearly and wants to help students succeed he is very accommodating and offers lots of opportunities to get help. If you have any interest in learning Calculus take him!
Nikjeh is an incredible professor. He loves to make calculus entertaining and explains good examples in an easy way to understand the complicated concepts.
I hate math, but this was by far my favorite class of the quarter. Nikjeh makes lectures easy to understand. It's hard, and requires a lot of outside work but his tests are very fair.

Esmaail is honestly the best Calculus teacher! He gives you extra credit for people who need to boost their grades up. He is very helpful, he takes his time to help the students. He cares about his students. You will learn a lot. 11/10

Amazing professor! He is very enthusiastic about the class and does a superb job explaining the concepts. His notes are well organized and informational (you wouldn't need to read the book in this class,) and he is very willing to help after class. His tests are fair.

Dr. Nikjeh is a great Math Professor. He's very helpful and caring. Again study from his lectures, if you need additional help then practice from the PDF book! He gave me a great recommendation letter for medical school. Just take him.11/10

Dr. Nikjeh is definitely my favorite professor from all my classes that I've taken this semester. He allows notes on tests. I would suggest attending his lectures since he will often give 2 free test questions during the lectures before the day of the test. If you have to take Calculus, I definitely recommend taking it with him!11/10

Is this class hard? Yes, I'll say it: this is not a hard class. Prof. Nikjeh makes the Calculus easy to understand. I enjoyed the lecture. He is a fair grader. He gives extra credit. At the end of the class you will be happy with whatever you learned and what you earned. He curves the grades.

Super clear lectures, math is not my strong point but Professor Nikjeh is very good at making seemingly hard math concepts appear easy. Tests are open notes and open book. Weekly homework, just follow the agenda and you can easily get an A.

This professor was so great! The class is really interesting- you don't have to show up every session but it's definitely easier if you attend for lectures & review, though. DON'T GET THE BOOK! All the info needed to study is in Canvas and his class notes:)Take him!

This guy is one of the top professors at UCLA. Doesn't even need any aids to help him present the material. Great personality and fun guy to work with. He is demanding of students (don't miss any classes) but the landed benefit of having taken him for this class is well worth it!
Prof. Nikjeh is EXCELLENT!!! If you have a chance to take him, DO NOT HESITATE!

Prof. Nikjeh is the students' dream instructor. He is very knowledgeable and explains difficult concepts in an easy way. He gives many examples. Exams are similar to the class examples. He curves the grades at the end of the quarter.

Really great and nice professor overall. Makes lectures interesting, and explains complex material in a coherent fashion. Provides plenty of material to study for exams. Classes themselves are very laid back and comfortable. He curves the final grades.
Dr. Nikjeh is AMAZING! Do all the homework he assigns and study the class examples you'll be set for every quiz and exam, sometimes he copies questions directly. He nicely curved the final grades.
He was able to explain things clearly and concisely and was probably the most "down to earth" math professor I experienced. Best math professor I have taken. I learned a lot in this class.

Prof. Nikjeh is an awesome professor. His lectures are simple and clear, though a bit tedious. I think it's the subject and concepts he has to work with since it's a calculus class. Class got more interesting towards the end. He grades fair, tests are fair.

The course itself is very difficult but the professor speaks clearly.  his quiz is about 4-5 questions limited to one hour and the exam is about 10 questions. I like this professor very much because he is very reasonable and has taught me a lot and understands the students.
Excellent teacher, one of the best at UCLA Math Dept. English is my second Lang. but he is very clear on aspects. Very very helpful and super nice. You will like and enjoy the class.

Prof. Nikjeh is a remarkable teacher, and I passed his course with flying colors. He allows note sheets on exams. He was able to explain complicated concepts clearly and concisely and was probably the most "down to earth" math professor I experienced. Overall, wonderful teacher.

Prof. Nikjeh is by far the best teacher for Calculus at UCLA. He genuinely cares about his students and works tirelessly to ensure that everyone learns. Never be scared to ask questions if you're entirely confused about a subject! He'll be delighted to assist!

The written HW was a bit challenge and the exams are fair but honestly,Prof. Nikjeh is now my favorite math professor I've had at this school. He is willing to work with you if you need grace/help. Strongly recommend taking him not just for this class, but for any math class.

I loved every part of this class. Professor Nikjeh makes learning easy. The class is very easy to pass as long as you take good notes and watch the lecture videos. Tests are open book and are based on lecture videos and class examples. Prof Nikjeh is very caring about his students and wants them to pass.

I absolutely loved the class I took with Professor Nikjeh. Calculus ended up being my favorite class this semester. Professor Nikjeh is thoughtful and really just a great guy. Every single expectation was outlined and overviewed. Every single lecture was entertaining. I wish Professor Nikjeh could teach all of my courses. Take this class!!

I would highly recommend professor Nikjeh to anyone! He breaks things down and makes it really easy to understand. He also takes the time to make sure each and every student has a clear understanding of the subject. I was terrified of Calculus because I am not a math savvy person, but he made it all so easy to understand!

He is incredibly smart and actually cares that his students understand the material. His lectures are clear and precise with plenty of opportunities to ask questions, where he gives clear answers. His teaching styles make hard classes seem easy. Take him.

Nikjeh is the best math instructor I've had here. His lectures are pretty easy to follow and he does a good job of doing a lot of examples. He also uploads everything he ever writes out so that is helpful too. I struggle with math and I found him very willing and able to go above and beyond to help students understand. Super nice!

If you have to take MathXL31A this is absolutely the guy you want to take it with. Seriously cannot recommend him enough. He made one of the more difficult classes at UCLA manageable. He's a fair grader on the exams and while there is a lot of homework he'll often let you turn things in late without penalty if you ask.

Professor Nikjeh's "teaching" was simply regurgitating text book material for three hours straight in lecture; he couldn't manage his time and constantly shifted tests/assignments. HIs tests weren't commiserate with material "taught" in class. He didn't return a single graded item (so you never understood what you did wrong). Absolutely dreadful.

He is awesome! Gives great examples of concepts to help students understand. Soft spoken and always leaves room for questions. Work is easy if you try a little bit. Definitely my favorite math teacher. His quizzes are from the lectures (examples). He'll show you exactly how to ace the exams.10000% recommend.

He is a great professor. He wants you to know all detail of the class and gives you enough information to do it. If you study you will get an A or B. Attendance is important. An easy class as long as you listen to him carefully and study. I will definitely take his class again. he knows what he teaches. Super nice.

One of the best math teachers at UCLA and quite laid back. Had him for MathXL31A on Saturday mornings, not the most fun thing to do on a Saturday morning but this professor is great and teaches the material well. He allows note sheets on exams, so take advantage of this however you will. Overall, wonderful teacher.

Incredibly nice and caring guy, wants everyone to do well and understand the material. With that being said, if I could rate his communication I would give a 0. Quiz and homework dates were always in the air and he would take so long to reply to your emails. He's a very traditional, old school teacher and virtual class has made it very hard for him

Does not actually teach the concepts well, rather he just hand writes what the book says. While his quizzes and tests are just reworks of the example problems he gives during lecture, meaning they're pretty easy to pass, don't expect to learn anything. Had to go to pretty much teach this entire class by myself.

Professor Nikjeh is great! His lectures make Calculus very digestible. He does an excellent job explaining theorems and providing example. More importantly, he really cares about his students. He wants everyone to do well in class. He encourages students to ask questions and is willing to slow down if students find the material difficult.

Straightforward class with clear grading criteria. Do homework, pay attention in lecture and take notes. Exams reflect notes exactly! Professor was patient and lectures were very clear! Quizzes, exam and final from notes. Take this professor and class!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Nikjeh is one of the most understanding and caring professors I have ever met. He truly cares about you and wants you to succeed; his lectures are clear and well organized. Super accessible outside the classroom; take Calculus with him. You won't regret it.

Professor Nikjeh is an excellent professor and I greatly enjoyed his course. His lectures are very helpful for understanding the material with exams/quizzes that are very fair in challenge level. He made the course interesting and his lecture notes are very organized! He is kind and genuinely cares about his students.

He was a great professor who cares about you doing well in the class. His lectures are pretty easy to follow and he gives you shortcuts where he can. There is a lot of homework but it is helpful because many of his quizzes and exams are modified versions of the HW questions. He grades your HW by completion. Exams and quizzes are not too difficult.

Professor Nikjeh is extremely understanding and approachable. He truly wants his students to succeed in his course . He also provides you with plenty of examples that will help you with quizzes and homework. He also does not rush through material. . I would recommend this course for anyone that wants to do well in calculus.
Great Professor would take again 10/10. The class was easy if you listened to his lectures and took good notes. Hes more than fair with grading!

You will need to work out problems by yourself since he only explains really basic ones during the lecture. Have the student solutions manual handy. He was too overloaded with work to answer questions or give feedback/ mark questions in quizzes or homework. Really lovely professor but he was definitely overwhelmed by online teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Nikjeh is by far the MOST AMAZING professor I have ever had. I had no prior knowledge in algebra before this class, but with his teaching skills, I was able to end with an A-. He makes it clear that he wants his students to succeed in the class. He is super caring and passionate about teaching. I was rly lucky to have him as a prof! 11/10 :)
Not good professor but very funny and personable. Didnt understand much but he showed up to every class
Ok professor, didnt learn much but its an easy class and easy A.

Best math professor. He really teaches you how to manage your own and when you put in the bit of work that is compiled in the homework and his study guides, there is no way you can't get a B or A. I received all HW credits. You need to have a very good understanding of basic algebra to be successful.

Prof. Nikjeh is a wonderful professor. He makes concepts clear and makes himself available if anyone needs help. This was an easy class for me. I went to all classes and paid attention, and did all practice problems that he would say to look at before a test and got an A+. He is a great guy.

Great professor! Very clear expectations from his students. Is more concerned about students understanding a few solid concepts, rather than jamming a book down their throat. If you have to take Calculus, take him! He will make it interesting and easy for you. 2 thumbs up!

Best professor I've ever had! I am NOT a math person, I repeat I AM NOT. However, I made it with an A in a very short time (5 Weeks). Only 2 quizzes, 2 exams, and a final. His lectures are very straight-forward. His quizzes are exactly the same as the examples he provides in the lectures. He's an amazing & caring person. I highly recommend him!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Nikjeh goes over the material clearly at a moderate pace. You will probably not do well if you dont do the homework or you didnt take Intermediate Algebra before. Homework is not hard at all. You may see class examples on the test. Overall he is very nice and helpful. I will take more classes with him.

BEST PROFESSOR AT UCLA! Honestly an amazing person (reminds me of my dad). He is very sweet. His exams are based on textbook questions & examples so make sure you look over questions and you'll be solid. Honestly a great professor, explains well in class. If you dont understand he is more than willing to explain it again. Truly an amazing prof.

He is one of the best professor Ive ever had in my life. He takes such an abstract concept like calculus and breaks it down fundamentally and really makes you think. Hands down one of the best math teachers youll ever find. I will take him again.

Prof. Nikjeh is great math instructor and I enjoyed and learned a lot. Always kept class active, although we were learning the most boring materials. Fair and easy grader. Tests were a breeze if u study just enough to know how to do the math. No crazy preparation needed. Awesome. 10/10

Professor Nikjeh is the best lecturer I have ever had. His lectures were organized and prepared. They used well thought-out examples that were referenced across multiple lectures. I never felt like my time was being wasted. Also, Nikjeh's demeanor was very polite, funny, and relaxed.

MATHXL1 is the hardest class I've ever taken but prof. Nikjeh was an absolutely amazing professor. His lectures were well organized and full of meaningful content, and he structures his lectures so that his students' focus is maximized.
Not organized at all and blabbers while explaining. I didnt understand much of calculus
OK professor, he can make it boring but you don't learn anything

Professor Nikjeh is a wonderful teacher with a firm, accurate, and understanding grasp of that which he teaches. He makes difficult content easy to master and does so in a comfortable way that makes lectures a joy and not just another class. His fashion sense also speaks volumes about what truly matters to him and I respect him very much.

What to say about Nikjeh? The best professor of my life. In the most different class I've ever taken, Nikjeh made a dent in my education. Nikjeh is just not only a talented professor, but also a great human being who believes in teaching as a life changing job he's up to. MathXL31A is hard, but Nikjeh will make it inspiring and really worth taking.

I was scared to take this class- but Dr. Nikjeh made it totally the best class. He is so understanding, and he is a great teacher. He is very nice and helpful- the ideal prof to take for ANY course- let alone math. I love this guy- def take this class no matter what time it is offered.

I must admit, considering that I am not mathematically-adept, this was the one course I feared taking the most this semester. Yet, Professor Nikjeh's class has turned out to be one of the easiest, yet more informative, class I've ever taken. I highly recommend him to everyone.

He is the BEST Math Professor I have ever, ever had. He really knows how to teach a very difficult subject in such a way that students can grasp and understand. I took him for 31A & 31B and I learned more than ever before. He is very fair and truly cares about his students learning the material. I Highly recommend him for any Math class. Great man!

This class was GREAT. I dreaded taking it, but I was so lucky to have Prof. Nikjeh. He taught it clearly so that non math majors could understand it. I actually understood the material, while friends in other classes struggled. Don't settle for anyone other than him, he is the best and super nice. And I got an A.

He is a GREAT GUY. Reminds me of a high school math teacher. Truly caring, very compassionate, and makes Math genuinely fun. I do not like Math at all, but learning math with him was absolutely great. He made math fun. Definitely one of the nicest professor in UCLA.

Just do yourself a favor and take his class. You won't regret it. Prof. Nikjeh is one of the most helpful and genuine professors I've had at UCLA. And did I mention he actually teaches you the material? And that he cares about whether you understand or not?? Just take his class already.

One of the best professors that I have ever taken. The material isn't easy, but he really prepares you well for the midterm and exam. Super nice and patient, and explains everything very clearly. He is so passionate about his work, he is funny, and fantastic.

Esmaail is one of the best professor I have had at UCLA! I never write reviews about anybody, but he really deserves all the praise he can get. His lectures are clear and to the point. Final exam is super fair, if you study. Definitely take him! No regrets! If you need to change your schedule to fit him in, DO IT.

He was honestly the best professor that I've had since being at UCLA. He really does care about whether or not you understand the material. He is very specific about what you will be tested on and as long as you do the study guide and show up to class you'll do fine in the course. He also offers extra credit for the final and homework.

Accepts late homework and gives very lovely quizzes/tests with some questions derived verbatim from the examples in the textbook and homework problems. Great professor, I highly recommend him and would definitely take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: Best professor ever. Take him if you can. Not an easy class, but you will learn a lot. Five short papers, but he will drop the lowest grade. Two midterms and one final, which are essay format. He is so nice and helpful.

if u a not interested in archaeology, don't take it. not a easy class. he is a great teacher n concern his students, even reply emails on Sat. also gives comments on papers. reading n txtbk help a lots on exams. n he is reli HOT n funny!

Trying to get help in this class was very difficult, every questioned that you ask if answered in a very unclear way so you never know what exactly to study or how to study. Very unclear and all over the place.

Great professor! Very engaging speaker. His lecture slides are avali. to print out. Readings can be a bit long and boring, but important for exams. I didn't read many and I got a B. Could have been an A if I read more. Multiple choice exams and two short papers. Finally, he is very funny and "nerdy" hot. Made me want to go to class everyday.

His lectures are dry and he is pretty boring. He is also very sarcastic which could be irritating. He posts slides online, but you shouldnt rely on that, his lectures are more informative for the essay exams. Papers can be difficult but drops one or two. He makes time for appointments. Overall the class is alright/average.

His lectures are very structured and organized. His grading system is fair (2x 1 page papers, a midterm, a final). There are no surprises in this class, he basically gives you what to study. As long as you do the work, you'll be fine. I ended up with a B+ but didn't do any of the reading, I just attended lectures (which is highly recommended).

Most organized professor I've had at UCLA!  Very clear directions and you know what's expected of you for the midterm and the final! You will learn a lot of valuable information! As long as you work hard and do your part, you should be fine!
Clear lectures, no surprises. Great class to take if you're looking for an Archaeology class from a professor who knows the material very well!

85% of the grade was split between the midterm and final which is brutal, the other 25% is a written report on the weekly readings done in class with pen and paper (you cannot prewrite it). He is an engaging lecturer but please record it because he does not have slides so if you space out and miss what he said, there's no way to go back.
Midterm and Final are 85% in total of your grade, with each 16 points. One mis-step/losing one more point means your grade will be in trouble.

By far one of the best professors at UCLA. Had a disappointing experience with Terri Anderson and he definitely made up for it with informative and intentional lectures. The dense workload and no computer rule was intimidating at first but he was very attentive. This course improved my retention, critical analysis, and note taking skills.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Rossman is 100% one of the best professors available at UCLA. I was so nervous to take this class because it seemed very dense and hard. I am so happy I stuck it out for the long run. Professor Rossman is one of the most intelligent professors and so good at making the information understandable to everyone. I recommend this class 100%
Dr. Rossman is one of the best professors I have ever had. Such a great lecturer on fascinating material.

He was a good professor in retrospect; although sometimes he does talk fast, in isn't that difficult to keep up. Only a midterm, final (each 40% of your grade) and 6 or 7 mult. choice reading quizzes for 20%. It was interesting material and every lecture with never-before-learned material! It REALLYYYY helps to go to his office hours

He was a good professor in retrospect; although sometimes he does talk fast, in isn't that difficult to keep up. Only a midterm, final (each 40% of your grade) and 6 or 7 mult. choice reading quizzes for 20%. It was interesting material and every lecture with never-before-learned material!

The grading is subjective and the Ta's are incompetent. A student had her grade jump over 10 % after getting  the midterm re corrected by the professor.  Though the professor himself is detailed and clear, the only down side is he talks to fast.

Although I thought he was an effective teacher, since grading is primarily based on essays, grading in my opinion was extremely subjective.   In addition, papers were graded by TA's and not the professor.  Despite this, I do feel that the class was interesting only due to Rossman's lectures.

Prof. Rossman is one of the best at UCLA.  Not only is he incredibly smart but he is one of few teachers who can really teach in an effective and clear manner.  There are 3 take home papers and 3 in class exams.  Not very much reading but lectures are essential.  Take his class you will freaking love it!!!
He is awesome
Oleg is amazing.
The Worst professor I ever took, and I had to have him freshman year.

This professor lectures in a thick accent and is unavailable outside of class, does not respond to emails. She sees teaching as a burden, does not care. She also compares herself to Machiavelli and thinks she is a canonical thinker, an absolute joke. Someone needs to remind her she is not a true philosopher but a professional teacher of rhetoric.

She does have an accent and can be difficult to understand at times. However, she is very helpful and he class is not difficult at all. Don't miss lecture because she tests on material from lecture that is not in the readings. As long as you do that and do the readings, this will be a very easy class for you.
Unbelievably useless class. Too much reading required per week. Doesn't put lecture notes online. Unclear lecturer. Overall a disappointing class. I thought sex was supposed to be interesting.
No Comments

Excellent professor. Expert in her field. Loves to help students, takes time to explain subject in clear manner. Her papers are tough but she will help you in office hours. Some people didn't like her but they were just being too lazy to learn

2 hrs. of solid lecture everyday.  The prof talks fast and it's hard to understand her, partially because of her accent.  She asks if the class has questions, but we are too lost to ask them.  Everyday, large groups of people get frustrated and walk out early. She calls us rude. Negative environment
Good Proffesor! Would take again, there isn't a lot of work it may tedious but not diffcult. There was one essay but again not diffcult. Easy GE.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Amazing professor very engaging very passionate
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She was an alright professor. She was very passionate about the material and really wanted you to take something from the material that she taught. Weekly Journal Entries and 4 papers for the entire course. The midterm involved memorization. Final Paper AND Final Exam (which included 2 Extra papers). If you don't have a choice take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--So many papers

reviews.comment: Mamun is an excellent design professor. He is patient with his students, constantly pushing them to give their best and unlock their full potential. He is the kind of educator who inspires his students to appreciate the beauty of design and the vast world of creative exploration it offers.
Cool professor. I learned a lot in his Studio 5 Interior Design course at Woodbury University. He's a very enthusiastic and dedicated educator, and he also has an adorable little cat named Memphis.
Took Prof Hashem's Design Communication class. Super chill professor and a modern thinker. Worked hard during class, but learned a lot. Would recommend for intro to Design students

I feel like the comment below is a bit dramatic, the lectures were interesting and easy to understand. Its somewhat challenging but not super difficult if you do the work and study what he tells you to.The tests are only difficult because the the topic itself is law based so you just have to know the court rulings and apply the same legal logic.

Do not take this class or professor. It appears easy on the surface, but the midterm was awful. He does not give a study guide, and told students to review all class material to prepare. The class avg ended up being a 71 (in a comms class btw) and he was actually mad a students for being "underprepared." Professor is egotistical and unsympathetic.

Amazing teacher. Lectures clear and to the point, with examples. Prof Huppin is very impressive yet very accessible and kind. Will not baby you, but fair and if you follow directions and take the class seriously you will be fine. Learned a lot, which is the goal. Subject matter was fascinating. My favorite class I've taken. No unnecessary busywork.

I love, respect and admire this professor. Always available, clear instruction. She made Stat doable. Amazing! I personally recommend Dr. Hart as professor. Very encouraging and carrying professor. Thank you.
Wonderful human being, excellent professor and very helpful. I'll have her for all my classes any time. Respect!

Easy A! This class is online and lectures are pre-recorded. Her slides are very informative and pretty enjoyable! She gives excellent feedback and responds to all emails! All assignments are straightforward, weekly discussion posts, 5 quizzes, 4 assignments, a midterm, and one final with extra credit overall great professor!
Good class, really easy
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She's assessable to reach through her email, she's clear on what you need to do by sending in announcements. We usually read off the textbook alot for the discussion posts, make sure to take notes on the slides she uploads and on the chapters you read because you'll need them on your exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Doesn't know what he's doing, I'd suggest you take someone else honestly.

He showed up late to class several times and sometimes seemed like he hadn't done the readings that he himself assigned to us. The lectures were ok. He is friendly but really hard to get ahold of if you email. Better to go through one of the TAs. It's an easy class though and if you just want the GE, go for it
More like a high school elective course than a college class. Super easy A even if you don't show up. You won't learn anything new if you do come to class. But Professor Herrera is a nice guy.

his class was interesting, but i think hes busy and its not his top priority, but that worked in my favor tbh it was super easy. had a group project/presentation that was pretty ok too. cancelled a couple classes, got dismissed early for a couple others.

Tyrone Howard is one of a kind. He is more compassionate than any professor at UCLA. He is there for all students that show an interest and will go beyond for those that have aspirations to pursue careers in education, as well as counseling. His lectures are amazing and he engages students with critical discussions on key topics. Take him--trust me

After taking Educ 130 with Professor Howard, I have never felt more determined to pursue a career path in education. He really cares about his student's educational goals and I learned a lot about my privileges and purposes from this class. I would take him for any other class that he offers. He's funny, approachable and someone to get to know!

He is the absolute BEST professor on this campus for ed and otherwise. Such a salient teaching style and so willing to help you in whatever way he can. (Bonus: Build a close working relationship with him and he will save your life time and time again.)
wonderful teacher, made me far more award of culture and race than ever before!

THE BEST professor EVER! I looked forward to every single class of his! 2 page journal every other week, one midterm 5 pgs final 15 page paper but it is sooooo worth it!! EVERY prof at UCLA should learn how to teach from him! you HAVE to take this class, its AWESOME!! HE IS THE BEST!
Take any class you can with him.  He is an outstanding individual and remarkable professor.  He made my UCLA experience very positive.

Extremely strict grader. All his feedback on the papers he will return to you will barely have anything positive in them. I thought I had a good understanding of the material we went over in class. He will absolutely nitpick your paper so make sure you repeatedly go over it and make it the best paper you have ever written.

I have read a couple of posts saying that Huehls is nice when you get to know him. This was not the experience I had. However, his classes are meaningfully challenging and he grades very fair. I would suggest his classes to anyone who has a thick skin and is up for a challenge.

Prof. Huehls is a brilliant professor because he is unapologetic in what he expects of his students. He holds us to high standards and doesn't shy away from complex ideas. But though the material is tough, he doesn't just leave us to figure it out - he is very thorough in his lectures. He's hilarious and both creatively/intellectually stimulating.

This class was by far the most difficult I have taken in my three years of college. I thought I was following along just fine, but my grades in the class reflected otherwise. Everyone in my department raves about him, but I didn't really care for the class, and I will not be taking classes with him in future.

By far the best professor I've ever had.  His classes are not easy by any means, but if you're willing to put in the work you will get back what you've given tenfold.  He's engaging, hilarious, and does the best job possible to make certain his students understand his lectures.

Maybe the best professor I've had. He's funny enough to keep you totally engaged through frequently difficult material, and teaches well enough to justify how tough he is. He loves to engage his students, but doesn't force participation. Wish I could take more classes with him.

This is one of the greatest classes that I have ever taken. He is very passionate about what he teaches. This is a great class and you will leave it wanting to have learned more. Great teacher, great texts, great class. Don't get me wrong, this is not an easy class, but you will love the challenge!

This professor is awful, she explains one thing in class then makes it 10x more difficult on the exams, her practice exams don't reflect the difficulty nor the material on her real exams, I would not recommend taking her for anything, the amount of work you have to put into this class is insane, its more than in pretty much any other class

I hated 100B, she designs the trickiest exams possible..its very hard to get a good score even if you know the material well. Also the grading in this class depends a great deal on your TA, if you get a good TA you will get a good grade, if you get a bad TA then you're screwed. I suggest you take another teacher for 100B, they are likely easier.

It is incredible how you can read the book, do the practice exams, and still do miserably poor on the actual exams because the exams are created to trick you. They want you to fail. Make sure you know how to decipher cryptic messages because there is nothing clear about this class. The professor needs a lesson in proper email etiquette; super rude.

She called me a lady, which is nice.  The class is way better than some other courses I have taken in the psychology dept.  She also wrote a book, which is also super.  The psych major is impacted, so it's rough out there, but I do recall this as a better class I had taken.

I took the 1st midterm yest. & let me tell you it was designed to trick u. She gives a disclaimer saying "students claim its harder than the practice exam and thats bc people take it w/ the key and they are in a different state-of-mind during the real exam," but NO. That was RIDICULOUS. I get its curved but whats the point if she TRIES to trick u??

She wasn't hard! just read summaries and know terms from book. know lecture and be able to apply. Exam answers are not tricky but you really need to understand the question. You do not need to make any assumptions, guesses. If you see the obvious answers or answer then pick it. the paragraph given or the question will include the answer of the ?s

i hope the next time she is late more than 10 minutes for ANYTHING that she stops to reflect on the ridiculous guidelines for the class. extremely confusing exams designed to trick you instead of testing knowledge or learning. most useless class about the most useless subject ever. i hope the TAs continue to do useless research while making 60k/yr
Entertaining teacher.
Wonderful, entertaining professor.
soo nice very clear, love her
No Comments
distant and rude
Nice people, perfect lecture
His exam and final sucks. Does not invloved on the homework he assigned. He doesn't know how to teach. I wondered why UCLA hasn't fired him yet. His class was like milk. It only good for 1 weeks
Hes the worst. DONT TAKE HIM

He was Absolutely HORRIBLE. you cant at all understand a word of what he speaks. and really vain. Very unhelpful. Infact the t.a can teach a million times better than he does. if u ask him quest. he gets all confused and makes u confused. this was the worst class i have ever taken.
Worst professor I've ever taken at UCLA! Avoid at all costs!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: This lady now teaches at Texas State university. She is by far the worst professor I have ever taken. DO NOT take this lady. The way she grades is insane! You must get every part of the work correct to get any credit. To make matters worse SHE CAN NOT SPEAK ENGLISH so understanding her lectures is near impossible you will be teaching yourself the material if you take this course.

Worst professor I have  taken. Her lectures are worthless dont bother taking notes she goes WAY to fast. Her English is terrible. Homework is difficult and practice quizzes, midterms, and final were also hard. She posted the answers but it didn't help because I couldn't find out how the professor solved the problems etc.
Absolutely terrible. Lectures were useless and she gave pop quizzes to screw people over. Tries to catch you at your worst by giving hard pop quizzes and difficult homework.
She is a really hard professor.  Do not miss lecture or the sections/labs.  You will learn more from your TA's IF they know what they're talking about.  I highly recommend a tutor.
this woman cannot speak English.
Dr. Grossman is really knowledgeable and supportive.

Dr. Grossman is one of the most inspiring and supportive instructors I've had at UCLA. He's extremely knowledgeable in dentistry, but what stands out most is how much he genuinely cares about his students and patients. His feedback is clear, constructive, and always aimed at helping you grow both clinically and personally
He is amazing!

Dr. Grossman was a fantastic professor. He expanded my knowledge in both clinical dentistry and the business side of practice. He offered insight into management not often taught in dental school. He encouraged me, helped build my confidence, and gave great advice that shaped how I view my future career.
Great professor!

Dr. Grossman has great confidence in the way he teaches his students as well as his patients. It is extremely helpful to learn to communicate with and care for patients at such a high level like Dr. Grossman.
Great experience with Dr. Jay! Learned a lot in regards to dentistry and life.

While volunteering at Dr Grossman's Homeless Not Toothless clinic, I felt empowered. At arrival I was greeted, given a tour, and was free to ask questions. Dr Grossman boosted my confidence and answered questions I had not completely come up with. During our lunch breaks he even spent time mentoring us. I felt confident during all procedures.

Dr. Jay Grossman is a very good professor. On his lecture about finances and investments in dentistry I was amazed with all his knowledge and how easily understandable he made himself for the students. He is also pretty accessible if we have any questions and always ready to help the students.

Dr. Grossman was an incredible preceptor who took the time and care to ensure that I had the proper guidance working on our patients. I appreciated the way he supported me as I took on a variety of procedures, giving the perfect balance of freedom and direction.
Dr Jay is such a great leader and mentor, I would love to the the boss and clinician he is. He makes everybody around him feel so comfortable and cared for.

Dr. Grossman is not only aspirational for his business and dental knowledge, but the way he works with his patients, staff and the student who work with him is so impressive and compassionate. He is such a great role model and I hope I can continue to learn from him throughout my career!

Dr. Grossman is an immensely insightful, inspiring mentor. His perspective on how to be a good clinician, effectively communicate with patients, and manage a successful practice is invaluable and so vital to our dental education. I learned so much beyond patient care, and am so grateful I had the opportunity to attend his clinical rotation!
Dr. Grossman has such valuable insight and advice not only for dentistry but for real world applications. Highly recommend having the opportunity to work and learn under him!
Dr. Grossman is a great mentor for any UCLA dental student who attends his CBCE. They will be given autonomy that is not possible in the dental clinic and learn the ins and outs of private practice.
A great clinician and leader. Inspires young students and teaches in a practical manner. He is willing to share his experience and different skills in his repertoire.

Dr. Grossman is an inspirational professors that I am grateful that I came across as a UCLA dental student. He was generous in providing advice and mentorship during my time at HNT. Dr. Grossman also provided critical information in regards to various dental and management techniques he utilizes in practice. I definitely enjoyed and learned alot

Dr. Jay Grossman's remarkable feedback, intelligence, leadership, respect, resourcefulness, and patient care contribute to his legacy of excellence in dental education. His influence extends beyond classrooms and clinics, inspiring a new generation of dental professionals, including myself.
Dr. Grossman is not only a great dentist, but a great person!  I loved my time in his office.  I learned so much clinically in addition to patient communication and business tips.
Great mentor and coach!

Dr.Grossman was a pleasure to work with! Not only will he give constructive criticism but will also present the material in a way that makes sense and address the why of a problem. Great bedside manner and overall a great human being.
Dr. Grossman is very thorough and inspirational. He has good chairside manner and treats his patients and staff very well.

I had the privilege of collaborating with Dr. Jay Grossman at HNT. His unwavering accessibility and willingness to provide assistance whenever needed were truly remarkable. Under his guidance, my confidence was boosted, empowering me to effectively treat and care for patients with utmost success.
Gives a great amount of independence but will provide critical feedback when asked for.

UCLA can tend to zone students in on grad requirements, but Dr. Grossman's rotation was the refreshing experience I needed to re-emphasize why many of us chose dentistry in the first place. His variety of cases enabled me to refine my skills while stressing the importance of public service. Would recommend to every student in the school!
