Learning from Dr. Grossman led me to build upon my technical knowledge of dentistry. In addition, he has great tips, tricks, and advice regarding dental procedures, treatment planning, and so much more. He took the time to teach about patient communication, patient management, and practice management, which was very helpful. The real deal.

Learned a lot about the technical aspects of dentistry, patient communication, patient management, and practice management from Dr. Grossman. He has great tips, tricks, advice, and a lot of wisdom to share! The real deal.
Very knowledgeable and willing to take time to teach you!

Fantastic clinical based teaching, patient based treatment planning, Excellent communication skills,
Caring and knowledgeable.
He is so wonderful and likes to teach.
It was very informative and amazing lessons learnt! Learned a lot about running an office and patient flow!

Professor Grossman truly cares about the community & educating the students. He is exceptionally knowledgeable & gives thoughtful advice, offering a holistic & accurate perspective of private practice. Through the invaluable services Homeless Not Toothless provides for the veterans, he inspires us to do more for the community & follow his success.
Dr. Grossman is one of the best professors and dentists that I have ever met! He is incredibly kind, caring and wants to teach you the ropes of dentistry in a private practice setting!

Dr. Grossman was a pleasure to work with during my rotation at Homeless Not Toothless. I was able to learn a great deal about proper treatment planning, patient communication, and more. Not only is Dr. Grossman a caring and respectable doctor in the office, he genuinely wants every one of his students to succeed and learn while they are there.
Dr. Grossman is so approachable when it comes to learning. You can ask for help and he will educate you without looking down on you for not knowing. A rare quality in professors these days!
I enjoyed the rewarding experience of serving people who are in dire need of help: vets, women and children of domestic violence, and the homeless.
Great professor who cares to give back to his local community
Great caring professor who gives back to the community!
Dr. Grossman is so understanding and compassionate. I really love his energy and his determination to do his very best in all aspects of life
Amazing instructor and person. There is a lot to learn from Dr. Grossman as an accomplished and expert dentist and a fascinating human being. He never hesitates to teach and share his knowledge.

I really appreciated the practicality of Dr. Grossmans advice, from real life dentistry to the numbers behind a great practice. We got to see the real side of a running a profitable clinic AND a nonprofit. I loved learning about new technology (lasers, cancer screenings) and about the business side of dentistry from him.

If you're willing to work hard you can get a lot of work done here that normally can't be found as a regular dental student. In particular you can do surgical extractions if you're comfortable, and can do same-day treatment if your schedule permits. The mission of the program is also wonderful, as I saw patients in quite extenuating circumstances.

Dr. Grossman is the best! I learned more about real world dentistry during my time with him than all my years at dental school. He teaches so much about what running a dental practice is actually like in the real world. I wish I could extern with him every week.

Dr. Grossman is always looking to teach you in his clinic, while treating patients he routinely notes why something is being done for specific reasons ... this type of learning in a real private practice is truly unique and not something I believe every dental student can experience.
Excellent professor. Great and Funny Lectures. Easy Class. I cheated on his test by finding answers online, but I'm sure he would never find out.

Dr Grossman and HNT were awesome! Such a great combination to get both hands on experience and amazing real world tips for business management, insurance, patient management, etc. Would definitely recommend!

Dr. Grossman is truly one of the rare dental professionals that is able to impart the same level of excitement and interest in the field as when he first started practicing dentistry. He taught me more in a three-week rotation than most of my other teachers have taught me in months-long courses at UCLA. He's a rare breed of clinician and teacher!

I spent three at this program. The culture that Dr. Grossman promotes at his clinic is astounding and gives a very welcome feel. The clinical expertise Dr. Grossman teaches in a very short time of the rotation was extremely valuable to me. Dr. Grossman always made himself available for questions and was constantly ensuring that we were learning.
Dr. Grossman is a great mentor! He provides excellent care to his patients and shares clinical skills and knowledge with his students. I definitely recommend him and the HNT program.

Dr. Grossman challenges students to think critically and independently during all clinical tasks. Dr. Grossman encourages all students to self-reflect and determine areas they can improve their skills moving forward. Thank you Dr. Grossman!
Gives great feedback and caring. Very kind.

I really appreciated being able to work with Dr. Grossman at HNT. I not only gained confidence in my clinical skills, but was also able to learn about the practical aspects of private practice management. I really enjoyed my experience!

Great professor. Blends the clinical aspects of dentistry to the professional aspects of patient management in a seamless way. Invaluable mentor to dental students; he is one reason that keeps me motivated in the pursuit of becoming a dentist.

Dr. Jay Grossman is an excellent clinical professor at the UCLA School of Dentistry. His ability to mentor and blend the different aspects of dentistry in a seamless way is unparalleled. The patients at Homeless Not Toothless come from a multitude of backgrounds and are all very satisfied with the program and how Dr. Grossman delivers his care.

Dr. Grossman is an amazing mentor! He made sure I took something away from every learning opportunity. Instructors who care this much about student growth are rare, and I know he's helped so many students in their journeys. I felt so supported and encouraged my entire time working with him.

Dr. Grossman combines clinical expertise, business acumen and a deep compassion for people... I feel like I learned so much during this rotation that I couldn't have learned in an institutional setting. Great experience!

Dr. Grossman is an amazing dentist. Not only does he do great work but he is extremely kind as well. I really appreciate him hosting HNT and he does a great job in giving dental students a preview of what private practice looks like. I wish I could've spent more time with him at HNT but I'm very appreciative of the time I got to learn from him.

Dr. Grossman is the most thorough and caring dentist that I have seen and you can really see through the way he talks to his patients. Truly wants the best for them and takes a lot of his time to make sure that they understand what they need. Learned a lot from this rotation and would highly recommend Dr. Grossman!

Extremely amazing mentor and leader. His Homeless not Toothless program is an exceptionally wonderful resource that helps so many people with their smiles. I learned so much in my rotation at his office and know that many students will. He really goes the extra mile in involving students with care and provides autonomy for us to learn dentistry!

An excellent experience all around. Dr. Grossman has crown preps down to an art and his business model is second to none. There is so much to learn and a lot of opportunity to practice clinical dentistry.

Dr. Jay goes above and beyond for his patients and students. He runs a Homeless not Toothless program to provide quality dental care at no cost to those with serious financial needs. As a long time dental educator, he takes time to give students the real world dentistry perspective and encourages us to think critically. I've learned a ton from him!

He truly enjoys his career as a dentist and is extremely knowledgeable. He is always wanting to help out everyone and anyone. Hes caring, compassionate and hes been there and done that. ALWAYS willing to give advice on future career steps and business side of things as well. Love love love him!
Great work!!
Excellent experience
Amazing

Dr. Grossman was an inspiration to observe and fantastic to work with. He has great rapport with his patients and really explained everything he was doing. He also gave great advice not only from a dental but also a business perspective. I wish I could have more time to learn from him through the rotations.
Dr. Grossman is really inspirational and his lessons are really important for us to hear as students. I wish their were more opportunities to hear from him!
Best lecture I've had in quite some time. Dr. Grossman shared his own story and gave us an idea of what to expect after graduation. I'm so glad I attended.

As an international dentist, I was very confused/worried about my future path after graduating from a dental school in the US.  Dr. Grossman really encouraged and inspired me by giving this amazing lecture telling us his own story abt all the options after dental school. I am also very impressed by his passion and hard work.

As a dental student, thinking about life after dental school can be a daunting task. However, Dr. Grossman turned this time in our lives into an inspiration and exciting time. He laid out the options for after graduation so clearly and passionately. I wish we had this lecture earlier on in dental school. I would recommend his lecture to student.

Dr. Grossman is a really inspirational speaker. He opened my eyes to a few option I could choose about dentistry, as well as gave us more information about residencies and other advanced education programs and the pros and cons of a few which I thought was really helpful.

Dr. Grossman's is a great speaker. He was very engaging and spent the entirety of the lecture interacting with the students. His insight on life after dental school gave me a few things to think about. He highlighted his work with Homeless Not Toothless and that really encouraged me to make a difference in what I can do.

Loved him.  Makes his student think.  Is open to true intellectual thinking.  Opinions are not allowed.  This is GOOD.  To many of my fellow student thought opinions are driven by intelect, and not assumptions. Dr. Lovas, was a fantastic teach for Behavior Modification course!
Cranky, boring, narrow-minded, and he expects his substandard word usage to be parroted back on tests. A living demonstration of the limits of Behaviorism as a legitimate field of study.
he is MARVOLOUS !  ABA RULES!

Rating Professor Sempliner a 1 feels generous. His lectures were disorganized and unnecessarily complicated, and were completely different from his exams. I advise reading the textbook, as that offers a much clearer interpretation of the material. He would also often snap back at students who asked questions.
Run. Don't even think about it. Take the class another quarter.
Disorganized lecture and was absent for a few weeks, which was not supplemented by making the course any more lenient

Never recorded lectures & they were disorganized. Never posted any lecture notes, except once. Midterms and exams were not representative of the content we saw in class. He gave a practice midterm and provided no solutions. The midterms felt so long and complicated (5 questions with multiple parts).

Had him for 32A. Lectures were disorganized, at times he didn't know how to solve problems he was writing. Exams were not representative of the homework/textbook difficulty. Didn't provide extra material for practice, we had to ask repeatedly for it. He was insistent on no curve till he realized a good chunk of students would fail if he did so.

Professor Smurda makes Social Influence engaging and relevant by clearly explaining concepts and connecting them to real life. The lectures are organized, interesting, and reflect what actually shows up on exams, which makes the class feel fair and manageable. I left the course thinking differently about everyday behavior and social situations.

This was my favorite class at UCLA. She is a good communicator and her lectures were so interesting because she talks about current events that are happening right now and the research applies to everything in the world. Her tests are not easy, but you will be fine if you study.

Very interesting lectures. I can't believe someone is complaining about having to read an easy book for a college class. It's actually on the best seller list. This class is probably the only useful psychology class that I've ever taken. The professor loves her students and will spend a lot of time discussing the material with you.
This is by far my favorite class. The lectures were outstanding and Dr. Smurda really cares about her students.

Dr. Smurda's class is fun and interesting. It took me a while to actually understand everything, but once I did, it blew me away. She is also a great person to talk to and to get advice about graduate school. I enjoyed her class so much that I ended up taking 2 more with her.
Favorite class
I found this class to be very interesing and informative. Her tests were hard though so you need to study. I like the fact that there was a discussion component so we could talk about the material.

This was the worst class hands down. The readings were redundant. She assigned multiple readings on the same subject and none provide any new information, so clear your schedule because youll be reading A LOT. The lectures are dry and boring. And, the exams are written to confuse you. I dont understand why some professors and TAs do this.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: 135- Social Psych The class itself is cool/interactive. Two Midterms  40-50 MC questions There were 4 books assigned, however you're only responsible for a few chapters/book. Easy read & very interesting. 6 dis sections for this quarter, only accountable for 5. Must submit a post on readings.
I'm in love with her
She's amazing, hilarious, very intelligent. I highly recommend her
By far, the best professor on campus. She has changed my thinking about life more generally.
great teacher and very fair on exams. she is very easy to understand but i would recommend reading the textbook because it goes more in depth. favorite class though.

By far the best science teacher I have ever had. She is clear, helpful and entertaining as well. Her tests are extremely fair. There is a lot of information covered but that is the only reason I'd say easiness is not a 5. I would recommend attending her lectures because she goes into more detail than her slides, but her slides are posted.
Amazing teacher
Epic Professor! Can't go wrong! Highly Recommend!

JUKKA IS THE BEST! His practice exams were so helpful so TAKE THEM! He does give a lot of homework but he does office hours to go over questions on Sundays so if you are lost last minute these are a great resource. He is a great guy and even better professor. He offers so much extra help and he is reasonable in grading. I LOVE JUKKA!
this is a corona time review TAKE JUKKA. HES THE BEST PROFESSOR FOR LS30A. YOU DONT NEED TO WASTE MORE TIME FILTERING THROUGH REVIEWS FOR HIM JUST TAKE HIS CLASS. best, F+P
One of the best professors ucla has.

Hilarious man, funniest professor I've had thus far. Huge goofball. Loves his students and will listen to their feedback. He even lightened the homework load for us when we requested it. Will do whatever it takes to make sure you are prepared for exams. Supplies great resources, and keeps all lecture slides on CCLE.

Double PH.D eccentric guy who is willing to open up any of his specific view on comedy and his teaching is very theoretical. If you take his class be prepared to really study and review the topics. In the future you'll be really well off and you'll also do well on tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough--Would take again

reviews.comment: Jukka is an excellent professor who really cares about students learning. He does a multitude of office hours, particularly before the midterms and final. I even reached out to him when struggling in my next math class and he set up office hours with me. Not at all a dry lecturer which was a huge plus. Very well organized with his notes.

Great lecturer. Very funny. Arrogant but also cares for his students. TAKE ADVANTAGE OF HIS OFFICE HOURS. He gives out so many that you would be stupid not to go, especially during the week before midterms and finals. There, he sometimes gives out hints like "This is a good problem. Maybe I should give it in the test."

Dude is funny, sure. Midterm 1 is supposed to be easy but if you mess up one or two questions you get a C, Midterm is awfully hard yet somehow grades are still the same as Midterm 2 because people study harder based on Midterm 1, and then your final is doable as long as you study but you better go that extra mile for practice or be happy with a C
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Hilarious, responsible, and very helpful. Tests are based on lecture examples and homework, nothing surprising.
Great professor. Funny, clear, and very helpful. There is a lot of homework, but it's not an unreasonable amount. Just more than I'm used to. Final was hard, midterms were pretty easy.

Jukka is an amazing professor, and his clarity and drive to make sure everyone understood the material was appreciated. His review sessions for the two midterms were very helpful, but not the one for the final. The only downside- everyone did so well that because of the department standard, a 95% and below is a B, not A :/
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really good professor, just do your work.
Funny professor, tests are based on homework, clear lectures.. Good overall experience
He's really funny but he'll be laughing when he's grading your papers too.
Amazing.

Professor Johns' class is not an easy A but is quite rewarding after you are able to understand the material.

The midterm and final are 70% of your final grade. Section participation and quizzes are 30%. 

You will be required to read her book in order to do well on the tests. 

independentcampustutoring is my google address if you need help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This entire class is based on memorization. You must remember all of the vocab words just for her to put about  2-6 on exams. She gives no discretion on what will be on exams (exception of 1-2 in lecture).I would not recommend this class unless truly passionate about international law. I would definitely not recommend this professor at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She makes law seem so easy. Lectures and cases are so interesting. She posts all lectures to the class website at the beginning of the quarter, which lets you do things at your own pace. SUPER clear and makes things so easy to understand. ONE OF MY FAVORITE PROFESSORS EVER. Is always free to meet and gives tons of useful feedback. 10/10
Fun lectures, and super easy midterm with a nice curve, but walked out of the final feeling physically assaulted. I assume the curve was ridiculous because I still did okay in the end
Yikes, young and yet pedantic, arrogant, unapproachable. Dry, boring, uncessarily complicated. And the lady is a bit loopy and koo-koo, if you know what I mean.
Ugh, what a waste of time!

The textbook assigned does contain very dense legal material, however, she clears up any confusion with her lectures. I wouldn't suggest missing lectures or you'll regret it come midterm and finals time. If you're interested in international law, this class is for you.

Leslie Johns is an excellent professor, but her International Law class is incredibly challenging. There is a TON of reading and it is very dense legal reading that is required in order to do well on the 2 exams. The subject matter is interesting & lectures help to understand the reading. Make sure you have plenty of time to set aside to study!

Professor Terraciano is a great guy. Super friendly, understanding, and helpful. I won't lie, there is quite a lot of reading but the content is pretty interesting/applicable and discussion sections/lectures helped a lot. Graded on 2 essays, 1 midterm, 1 final, and discussion section participation. Exams were open book and online
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Terraciano cares about his students and the material he teaches them. He graded us based on 2 essays, a midterm essay, and a final essay. The work is fairly easy if you attend lecture or do all the readings. Great professor and class. Definitely recommend.
Great professor; glad he was willing to teach this very important material.

Professor Terraciano is very passionate about Latin American Studies. Office hours are group dialogue format. Reading-intensive course. Lectures highlight major themes and critical thinking points. Grading scale: 40% 2 essays, 10% participation, 15% midterm, and 35% final.

Extremely helpful when it came to finals, essays, and midterm. For the final and midterm, he provided a study guide that explain each part of the exam and even included essay questions that would be on the exam. He really knows what he is talking about in the class because his research is in the subject matter covered in the class.

He is very clear with what he expects, uses lectures to complement/clarify readings (of which he is the main author)...excellent readings...exams are straight to the heart of the subject matter, usually requires a paper on a related topic...fair in grading exams and papers.  Super friendly and is always available if you need him.

I took various classes with Dr. Terraciano.  His classes are very interesting, he is very clear, super student friendly.  Students do well not because he is easy, but because he has clear expectations and is always willing to help!
He is very helpful if you go to office hours. If you attend AAP tutoring, he'll review what is on the midterm and the final. Good Guy. Big Heart. Loves to see Chicano/Latino students do well.

She needs to go back to teaching kindergarten. I don't know who she is but she should try sitting in on her own classes. I guarantee by the end of her class, she will be sleeping because of how boring it is. Warning to students planning on taking her class: there's no ice cream party.
uninspiring lectures, uninspiring professor
Unclear grading criteria and bad lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever had at UCLA. I regret taking this class. Her grading system is terrible.
Very compassionate about her students. She clearly cares she wants all students to pass her class
Most caring and respected professor

Great professor! The workload for this class was extremely manageable and easy to follow as long as you pay attention in lectures. Provided weekly study guides which helped with understanding class material and the quiz questions were based on these study guides! Lectures were also recorded with subtitles and attendance was not mandatory!
The best professor I have ever met. Legit the GOAT.!!!
She is very caring and understanding
Interesting lectures ranging on many topics. Greatly enjoyed the class and hope to be able to take another class soon.

I loved Professor Placek's class.  She is approachable and well-versed in her subject area. Her lectures are interesting and keep your attention easily. She keeps materials current and all of the studies we read were interesting and not too long. Homework assignments were appropriate in size and quizzes were not very difficult.
Caring, compassionate professor, very easy to communicate with fast feedback. I will take any class of hers she is dedicated to your learning. A true value to UCLA.

Dr. Placek knows how to teach difficult concepts in a way that captivates young adults. She provides a variety of different resources like traditional readings, tv show episodes, documentaries, guest lectures, etc. And she really cares about her students. She's very helpful with assignments and even helps us discover things about ourselves!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class is chaotic and you have no idea what the assignment is asking of you. There are probably seven TRILLION instructions and conditions you have to keep in mind per workbook Prompt. You can find 50,000 more on the syllabus if you want to be extra perfectionist. ONE prompt question is a PAGE full of instructions.

Best professor I've had at UCLA, she makes learning so fun! Don't be scared of her quizzes, I watched the lectures + went over the study guides and got an A or B on them all. She gave a generous 7% of EC. Follow the rubric and you can get a perfect score on her papers. She's so cool & accommodating. I would take all my classes with her if I could.

Great Professor who gives you plenty of help for the weekly quizzes. There's a study guide for it every week that has everything covered for the quiz, so even if you don't want to go through the weekly reading then you can at least grind the study guide.

I enjoyed taking this class over the summer sessions. The course content was very interesting which helped with staying engaged in an asynchronous class. There is a quiz every week regarding that weeks lectures/readings. There is also 2 workbooks that are straightforward and relatively easy if you paid attention throughout the course.
This is a very balanced and informational course. I took it asynchronously and you will be able to obtain an A as long as you watch the lectures fully through. The content is also really interesting!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Wonderful course. Was very interesting and engaging. Clear that the professor loves the topic she teaches. She is very helpful during office hours. If you remotely pay attention to the lectures, she tells you the answers to most of the quiz content during those lectures. She offers a few opportunities for extra credit also ! great class !!

Professor Placek doesn't just teach what is traditional, she digs deep and it will blow your mind!

Class isn't hard but quizzes can be confusing with the wording.  She's understanding but don't take advantage of her kindness.  Don't expect to get an extension on an assignment if you don't show up to class/never make comments or ask questions.

Professor Placek is very nice and her lectures are actually fun and interesting. Usually we're done a little early too, which is nice. Personally, I thought the class was very easy, but that's because I attend lectures and studied for the quizzes for a few hours. Would take her again, and I probably will in the fall.

Prof. Placek is incredibly engaging. The content material provided is exciting and relevant. She has fascinating guest speakers for both courses. She is very accessible via email or office hours. She records the lectures, is accommodating, and does not count attendance. Stay on top of workbooks and review material! The quizzes require studying.

I love Professor Placek. Her lectures and material are super interesting. She is accommodating, caring, and considerate of all students. She wants everyone to succeed. I've already taken both classes but would immediately take her courses again. Quizzes can be challenging. Make sure to review lectures. I would highly recommend!

Lectures were super engaging. Prof. Placek really cares about all her students and creates a welcoming/safe learning environment in her classroom. Only thing I didn't like all too much were the quizzes. I don't know if I was the only one who struggled that much, but I never did well on them despite how much I studied. Workbooks were good though.

She has one of the clearest grading criteria and the course is about probably something that you never expected to be taught in a university classroom. Do take this course if you are interested in such topics and do not if you hate readings.

Professor Placek is one of the most caring teachers. - Cares about our well-being and grades & always provides additional instructions on assignments. -The lectures are to the point -Tests are a little confusing because of the wording, but if you study the lectures & readings, you are good! Caring, respected, and interesting professor!

Good professor and very caring, the TA's kind of graded harsher than I'm used to. Quizzes sometimes were confusing. On the study guides she gives there's a correct answer and then on the exam when that question is asked it's missing the correct answer but has all the other options. Attendance wasn't mandatory and online recordings were provided.
Class seems like it will be super easy in week 1, but the grading is really rough.

One of my favorite professors! She chooses really interesting topics and makes lecture fun. To do well on quizzes, you just need to go to lecture and listen/ take notes. She really cares about what she's teaching as well as the well-being of her students. Thank you Prof Placek for a great quarter!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: OUTSTANDING PROFESSOR EVER! She is generous and friendly if you face any hardship in your quizzes or assignments. I appreciate her caring for a whole semester, I definitely would take her courses in the future again!

Dr. Placek is one of the most generous, understanding, and engaging professors I have taken here at UCLA. She was an amazing lecturer, and had quite a few guest speakers to break up the monotony of school work. She is easy to relate to and fun to listen to. I highly recommend attending lecture... it will benefit you for her tests.

Dr. Placek is tremendously understanding and approachable. She cares about her students, gives extensions on assignments w/o taking points off is very accommodating during exams, and lets you reschedule w/o a question. I took soc. 132 w/ her and it was very interesting. her exams take a bit of getting used to but she applies a curve if needed.
Really enjoyed taking this class and learning about the material!

If you can take any class by Prof Placek pls do! They are so interesting and bring in a good amount of guest lecturers to break up the coursework and keep things fresh. I always felt engaged during class and enjoyed speaking to them after as well. The graders were a little harsh but there are several extra credit opportunities.

Professor P, is one of the most caring professors UCLA has to offer. She is very understanding. Be transparent with her if you are facing any obstacles. Be prepared for weekly monitored test. Do the study guides and take GOOD lecture notes and you should do fine on the test. She grades on a curve. Start your workbooks early.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Socio - 145This course is structure primarily with a study guide review that are useful for the weekly quizzes and for following lecture. Lecture can cause for you to be distracted since the professor might go on tangents but overall good professor.

Placek has probably been my least favorite professor at UCLA. The way Placek lectures is very unorganized and goes on random tangents. Almost everyone did very bad on the midterm. Grades are based off of participation(10%), 2 workbooks(50%), a midterm (20%), and a final but you can opt to do a 3 page single spaced reflection paper instead (20%).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Placek made my first quarter at UCLA amazing. When I first began her class I was genuinely uninterested in the course material and by the end of the quarter, I became well-invested in the course material looked forward to her lectures. Be prepared to write a lot for the workbooks though and start early.

Dr. Placek provides an incredibly supportive classroom environment, which was especially conducive to my learning for this Sociology of Sexuality class. Her responses to the research articles presented in class are critically kind and compassionate. She is an excellent facilitator of discussion and respects her students unique perspectives.

Professor Placek is very caring and accessible. The course is well structured with very interesting topics. There are guest speakers, and the lectures are very inspiring. Would definitely take another course from Professor Placek again.

She gets the struggle of being a student & provides all materials for free. Its clear she's rlly seen it all, nothing phases her &she rlly likes to open up the lecture into class discussion.. Even tho she is super chill the work should be taken seriously. She gives specific rubrics & lecture attendance was optional bc she provided a recording

This class is very fun and interesting. Workload is manageable if you get started early. Amazing guest lecturers!!! Only downside is lots of references to popular media so if you don't watch TV it's a little hard to understand at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Placek has honestly been one of (if not my favorite) lectures @ UCLA. Take any of Dr. Placek's classes. Her lectures helped me learn more about myself & conversely, what I've been taught about conventionally stigmatized or deviant communities.  Dr. Placek was extremely understanding & extended deadlines when necessary. We Love Dr. Placek !10/10
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Amazing the lectures were always interesting and we had guest lectures. The class has 2 workbooks, midterm quiz, extra credit, and the final was either a quiz or essay. Everything is interesting. The TAs also help a lot with understanding topics. She is also super funny.
very good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: Hate hiim
Lousy teacher
Such a jerk
Excellent lecturer and researcher. An top-level expert in his field.

Korf is awesome.  He has extremely high standards, but he is clear and precise in lecture and cares about students learning.  I have since gone on and become a professor myself, and I try to pattern my AI class after his.  I hope I do it justice.
No Comments

The professors passion towards physics is contagious. He explains complex concepts clearly and uses visual examples, making lessons easy to understand. I loved having him as a professor, he's
a fantastic instructor- made for teaching.
Dr. Park, made learning physics as interactive and understandable as possible. He made me fall in love with physics again!!

The professor Park was engaging the whole time. I particularly like how he showed us models during lecture and explained how the models worked. The professor was very detailed in his explanations. I
also enjoyed doing the Exit Activities, I like the videos he created and the polls in the videos to keep us engaged while watching the videos.

Dr. Park relates classroom material to real life examples, which greatly helps to better understand concepts. Dr. Park's teaching method encourages students to go beyond memorizing, and pushes toward applying your understanding beyond the scope of the classroom.

The course was taught well, professor thoroughly explained the concepts. The lecture notes and check your understanding handouts were extremely helpful. The exit tasks and weekly homework
assignments also helped reinforce the learning.

Dr Park is an excellent instructor. He cares about his students and makes sure we understand the content. His lectures are very interesting and thanks to the exercises we were able to commit the
content of the class to long term memory
it was an amazing course, i really enjoyed it. Best Physics class I've taken in my life. And I've taken about 6 course and I finally understand through his teaching methods.

Dr. Park says he will change the way we look at the world, and he was right. I learned so much!!! It's not just for my career but for my life. I wish I would have had Dr. Park and this class knowledge long ago in my life. Incredibly practical and applicable in so much of life. You really do understand so much more about how everything
Dr. Park is good at engaging the students in his lectures. Each lesson is fun and refreshing. He ismeticulous in his syllabus, so students know what to expect.

Dr. Park was a great instructor to have. He was very informative in every lecture and brought a sense ofhumour that put a smile on my face during every lesson. I had little interest in physics before taking thiscourse, but Dr. Park's teaching completely changed that in my and now I feel passionate about thesubject. This has been a very enjoyable co

I was always interested in Physics, but Dr. Park really gave me a new view on it and taught things I didnot know existed. He had already said the class would change your view of the world, and I really didnot expect the experience I got. He taught the course very well. I would only say that it would be morebeneficial to students if he taught the le

Dr. Park is a wonderful instructor. He is both knowledgable and an excellent educator. I felt like he wants his students to succeed and gave us every opportunity to do so. He is a passionate teacher and you can tell he cares about the subject and his students. He doesn't let remote instruction limit his ability to provide demonstrations to class

Overall I really enjoyed this class! Probably one of the lowest stress classes I've ever taken at UCLA since Dr. Park prepared us exceptionally well for his midterms. He NEVER tried to trick us on tests, but instead asked questions that directly related to material he covered. His method of teaching was very successful in helping us students

Dr. Park is brilliant. There was never a moment where he didn't have an answer, or a clever way to convey a complex concept to someone who doesn't understand. He's funny, he has great demonstrations, he's always prepared, and he is always willing to answer questions

I have learned a lot and had a great time in his class. Although I am not pursuing a career in physics, this class sparks my interest in learning more about our universe and gravity. It's truly my pleasure to be in Dr Park's Physics 10 class.

Dr. Park was extremely knowledgeable and entertaining. He kept the class engaged while making sure we learned all of the core physics concepts. I will Dr. Park was teaching more courses at extension because I would take them. Overall, Dr. Park is an excellent professor and I would recommend this course to everyone interested in taking a physics

Dr. Park is a very entertaining professor, in a sense that he keeps his students engaged with the topic at hand. He deliberately asks questions from students about certain topics that have been previously mentioned, which allows him to gauge where the class is with the material and how well they understand it. I have enjoyed my time with Dr. Park,

Loved the class! Made it super enjoyable to come in on my summer and take this class. He was a pleasure. I didn't like physics before and now with his class he made it super fun and enjoyable. Loved it! Wish he taught all my physics classes.

Dr. Park is probably one of my favorite professors. I wish we had more like him. He makes learning very fun. He has a great sense of humor, energy and wonderful teaching style. I learned a lot of interesting concepts and ideas in our class.

I thought Dr. Park was amazing. I learned so much from this class and his tests were very fair. He was very serious and expected us to take this class seriously as well. He was very patient and made sure we fully understood the material. I would definitely recommend him to others/ take another class with him! Probably one of the best Professor

Dr. Park is what you would call "one of a kind". He makes the class so very interesting and makes sure he implements real life examples that make the class better. The class isn't boring or hard, as people would usually say, because of him. He makes it great, and he is a very good professor.

I thought doctor Park did a great job of explaining concepts and giving us real life applications of them. The course has made me think about the basic aspects of my life in greater detail, and that has made it very valuable educationally.
I learned a lot and got a much better appreciation of Physics !!!

Professor Park made this course a smooth ride. I was worried about doing a physics class, but the professor made sure to keep a slow and steady pace of each topic so we could fully master each topic. His check your understanding packet is amazing because it helps be review important topics and feel confident in knowing each topic in the course. I l
Easily the best teacher I've ever had and the best class I've ever taken. His lectures went by quickly and were always interesting. I would take this class again.

A fabulous instructor in my opinion. He makes the course fun, interactive and the little introductory toastronomy has made me want to go into that field of study. He tries his best to answer questions for students and makes himself available as possible for his students. His passion made this course fun to be in.
The strengths of the professor include his humor and enthusiasm in teaching introductory physics to non-science majors. He is both an amazing professor and person. Thank you

I love what I have learned in this class, I only wish there was more time to cover concepts in more depth and learn other topics. Dr. Park is a great teacher who brings his energy and enthusiasm for physics to the classroom, and keeps us on our toes and engaged.

Dr. Park is by far the best professor I have met at UCLA who changed my academic performance. He creates the class atmosphere to be very exciting and interesting to students. He has a powerful communication skill that draws all students' attention and he definitely is tremendously humorous.

I really appreicate the class has such aa knowledgable professor. It surely broaden my interpretation at the whole Universe. Professor Park explained the material brilliantly. He shared his enthusiasm and knowledge of the subject with us very well
Super Course, Great Professor,Very knowledgeable,Great powerpoint, Very through!!!

Dr. Park is by far one of the best professors in terms of keeping students engaged. Physics can be a difficult subject matter but his demonstrations make it very easy to understand. I don't think a 50 minute class has ever gone by as fast as physics 10 has.

Dr. Park was an enthusiastic, compelling, and hilarious instructor! I am so glad I got to take this course with him it was definitely worthwhile and he made it an excellent class! I learned a lot of material in a short time because Dr. Park presented it in a very effective manner.
Dr Park holds class attention extremely well. He is very entertaining and effective. I was highly satisfied with this course!
Wow... yeah, I'm taking his class, and he's a great Professor, just too smart
He is fantastic. I loved the class. He is very knowledgable and super good.
Great demo and always helpful slide ! Very satisfied  At times a bit difficult to understand but I still learned a lot  professor makes dry physics materials fascinating.
I really enjoy professor teahcing. He has a lot of energy keep material simple and  also maintain classroom attention thoroughout the class
Fun Professor.Know what he is doing. I really enjoy every meetings.
HE ROCKS !He covered all modern issues in Physics !! very Funny Professor I love his class

He is an awesome professor. I was terrified to take Physics and now I actually really like it!!!!! Professor Park is fun, incredibly kind, funny, helpful and engaging. He shows a lot of helpful animations and really makes the class interesting. I would HIGHLY recommend taking this class with him. He makes it interesting and fun!
Explain difficult concept with easy step by step method. Learn a   lot He also teach astronomy parts that I like most
You should take this course animation, demo, movie clip so so fun I learned a LOT
Best Professor ever!

Took his class during the summer, the biggest issue is not really an issue at all, is that he is korean and has a heavy heavy accent. But no worries he has power points, and the slides that are plain whitish/yellow with multiple choice questions are directly from the test and make up about 90% of it
Great class full of fun

He was definitely a fun professor. Quite the opposite of Brent Corbin in terms of difficulty, Prof. Park still makes room for demos and comprehension in simpler terms than most other profs. However, this is a review for Physics 10, which isn't even university level, so disregard this review... entirely.
excellent class with lots of demo
He is really nice.  Explains things wells.  Has great examples and in class demonstrations.  He makes a tough subject easy.  Best to take his class through extension.

Extremely easy class. All the questions on the test are from the so called "quizzes," which are basically questions we go over as a class and don't even count as our grade. When I took this class in the summer, the class average was an A. out of 75 people, I believe only one person got a C, a few more got B's, and the rest got A's.

I throughly enjoyed taking this class because I was able to learn a lot. While it was a difficult class, Professor Lee was always clear about what she was looking for in Memos and graded fairly based on the criteria. There were no midterm/final exams, other there were biweekly argumentative memos and one final group project (you pick the topic).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: DO NOT take this class if you need any accommodations. Professor ignored my needs, made up her own rules, and I spent the rest of the quarter fighting for my accommodations with the sociology department and CAE instead of doing homework. I am not the only CAE student who struggled immensely because of her, JUST IN THIS QUARTER!! :((((

She definitely was passionate about the class and wanted the students to learn. Most Soc classes focus on expanding ideas for the exams, but not this one. You have to more or less memorize. The good thing about this is that the exams are based on the slides. Use her lectures to expand on those ideas for the essay questions. Do the readings or gg.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: This was a history of contemporary china class rather than a SOC class.  her class was well organized and interesting, but her exams were horrible..she made you remember all the facts rather than use your soc. imagination to understand Chinese soc. system..she was very emotional, she can't stand any  absquatulation during class

Class was actually interesting. However, it is not really a Soc class at all. Before the midterm she tried to say "remember this is a soc class, so know concepts..." So I studied all the concepts and bombed the test because it was ALL facts. There are a lot of little assignments though, which are easy and help your grade.
He's very hands on and what he teaches is what we have on the tests. Very easy to talk to and get help from. Over all a really fun class.

Overall I had a good time in his class. The only homework we were ever assigned was readings and 3 essays. He make philosophy fun and engaging. He never brought his own opinion into the lecture. His lectures expressed both sides equally and fair without bias. You can really see the passion in his eyes when he teaches. 10/10!!!!
Have no idea what he was talking about

If you like logic to begin with, this class will be great and he will make sense. If logic isn't your thing, it will be confusing. But he is always there to help via email and consistent office hours. Textbook is overly complicated and therefore useless, but you learn everything you need to know from lecture. Good class overall.

Luca Struble gets easily distracted, and sometimes doesn't explain things in a straightforward or clear cut way, but if you ask for help or clarification, then he will always find a way to restate the ideas. Very passionate about Logic and Philosophy.

Used an outdated program as the primary class material. Easily flustered, lectures change direction rapidly and can be very confusing, will answer questions in a way that can make the entire subject more confusing.
Luca is very hard to follow, all over the place, and disorganized. He often went wait way too quickly through material and was very unclear. SAVE YOURSELF. do not take him if you can avoid it.
Solid teacher.

Was really kind throughout the course. You could tell how much he cares about his students succeeding. I know online learning isn't for everyone, but this was definitely one of the best online classes I've taken. He has you apply the concepts to real world scenarios in a relatively low-stakes environment that allows you to learn from your errors.

He never responded to a single request for help, neither through class e-mail, nor work e-mail. Nothing more than a proctor, enjoying benefits of online instruction compensation with close to zero effort. This instructor is not instrumental in providing differentiation for the institution and you are just as likely to succeed anywhere else.
Great on-line class experience if you need to get a stats prerequisite out of the way!

Professor Siskaki is a strong professor. Her lectures are clear and she's always open to answering questions. But, the LS30 series in general are some of the hardest classes that I have ever experienced in my whole life. Pay attention and try your absolute hardest because this series will test your sanity.

Siskaki is such a clear and concise lecturer, the material is pretty easy to follow if you've taken calculus before. Paying attention to lectures/not skipping them is crucial for your understanding of the course material. I honestly could've put more effort into studying, but even with the minimal effort, you can FOR SURE get an A in the class.

I actually really enjoyed taking this professor's class last quarter. She is very knowledgeable on the content of this course, despite it being her first time teaching it. She allows time for questions, and gives direct feedback when requested. The effort you put into this course, equates what you will receive from it.

It was easy to pay attention to her lectures, but I also felt like she didn't teach the same content as the other professor did, or at least to the same extent. We at least got points for trying, which lowkey carried my grade.
The professor is very harsh and doesn't explain the material at all. She expects us to ace finals without knowing the subjects.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took her for LS30A for the fall quarter and I think this was one of the first times she taught this class because she was TERRIBLE. She never finished her lecture slides, she always answered all the questions during the lecture which took up time and she never got to the important information until the end which caused her to rush.

It is clear that she has taught this course for multiple years but not always in a good way. She is very vague about what content will be tested on and gets frustrated when the class underperforms due to this. Much of the lecture emphasized why certain materials were ordered for the lab which is nice to know but irrelevant for the course.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: hard. took the class fall 08. so much memorization and the final was killer. the midterms were easy compared to the final. i had an a-/b+ going into the final and got a B. i really studied a LOT for this class.  i have no idea how anyone managed to get an A.
Uses someone elses notes on a projector. Just reads off of projector and underlines words. Not helpful at all. Lectures are essentially useless.
No Comments

I think the average test grade in our class was like 25% or something. He would then bell curve to between 70-75% which ended up being really bad because even if you did significantly better than average (scoring perhaps 30% higher), it wouldn't be reflected after curve (curving to around 10-15% better than average)

3 midterms with 20% each and a final worth 40% of total grade, which means 100% testing. He asked us to drop midterm grades and cumulate to final before the final exam, and there is no limited midterms to drop. SO MUCH PRESSURE!!!! I have friend who have their final worth 100% of their grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His a good person since he is willing to help if you have questions. However, his lecture is hard to understand since he can spend 20 minutes deriving equations and you have no idea what he is doing. the moment he finish his logic, you'll definitely forget where he starts. He doesn't curve a lot and grades harshly. All in all, just RUN!!!!

3 midterms and 1 final for entire grade, but allowed us to transfer midterm weights to final exam. Tests very difficult, averages in 50s, but curved because the average student was failing. Provided little resources to prepare for tests. Grades def worse than other lectures. Granted, first quarter teaching, but I'd avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's nice and answers questions! That said, I don't think he's a good professor. Admittedly, I could've gone to more lectures, but I do think we got dealt a bad hand with his difficult syllabus. If you learn more-so through proofs as opposed to visual diagrams then this is your guy: otherwise, search up Brenda Edmonds' yt series, she will fs help!

Yes this guy is HARD, but he teaches you grit and how to figure stuff out when things go bad.

Your GPA will tank, quality of life will plummet exponentially (heh, welcome to engineering), but he will set you up for success in future classes which will inevitably get a lot harder.

Safe to say, I can open tests from MIT on multi and understand it.

Tests are very difficult. Much more content is covered than in other sections.
That said, his lecture notes are informative and good for review. At the end, he also curved very generously. Taking his class may not be a bad choice IF you are comfortable with math.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Your grade will depend on how good you are at teaching yourself the material: my B is from going to as many office hours as I could + doing every single problem in the textbook + taking others' past midterms/finals + personal tutor. Those getting A's likely pre-read material for the course.. I'm screwed for premed now, even tho I understand it all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Whoever said he curves generously, that is not true. Class average failing on midterms, he only increased scores by 10%: STILL failing avg. 

1. Heavy accent, can't hear
2. Hard to see (leveled chairs), uses lightest markers, can't read
3. Absent TA's in discussions
4. 1/2 pts/question docked for arithmetic error on exams
5. 60% final, 20% midterms
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Sihao Ma is typically helpful as long as you take initiative to ask him questions. His tests are difficult and challenge your thinking, but he curves the final grades generously. Overall I feel like I learned a lot, although it was not always fun or easy.

Professor Ma's class is hard, but he curves final grades. The last question on midterms is usually a difficult, proof-based problem. He puts effort into teaching, but he can sometimes be hard to understand due to his accent. Overall, the class requires a lot of studying, but if you do the homework and lots of practice questions, it's manageable.

The class isn't as bad as others are saying if you have a good foundation, study well, and attend lectures. The lectures lean theoretical. He expects you to know foundational things like trig identities. The last question on exams is typically difficult and proof-based. However, he curves generously at the end and adds extra points to midterms.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things
Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Why lecture and give homework if it has nothing to do with exam questions? Even his TAs did not understand the homework or questions and were confused.  No one could help us- not tutors, not TAs and definitely not office hours with him.  Don't take his class even if it is the only one available.

Wow where do I even begin. This class just isn't worth it, I came to UCLA in order to further my education but, this class genuinely opened my eyes to how badly college courses are structured. The fact that one professor can ruin my experience says a lot about the school.

I think this class made me smarter because it was so hard, but Sihao Ma is really hard to understand and his test questions are as far removed from the homework questions as possible. The final test was truly a masterpiece of difficulty. Sihao really outdid himself.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would rather get run over by a truck than take Sihao Ma's class again. Never in my eighteen years of existence have I felt so miserable seeing someone's face. It's like he get's off seeing all his students suffer. Don't take this class unless you're telepathic, because I have to tickle Einstein's balls to know what he wants us to do for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Shihao Ma might as well have bent me over and started spanking me with a paddle. My dreams of having a successful engineering career has single handedly been smushed by his exams, my future is done for. I have sacrificed everything and everyone in my life in order to get a C+ in the class. I wouldn't wish this class on my worst enemy.

Sihao Ma is the professor of doom and despair. Forget sleep and joy. There is no hope. Just give up. His tests were very difficult in comparison to his lectures. He showed no mercy. I wouldn't wish this professor on my worst enemy. My soul was crushed by him and it genuinely made me stop caring about my grades. My outlook on life has changed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor's lectures are pretty difficult to understand due to his thick accent and poor style of teaching. In my experience my TA for discussions was also pretty bad, would show up late atleast 30 min or more to eat 50 min discussions and pretty unclear with his explanations. Midterms all average around 50%, strongly do not reccomend
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Ma's instruction lacked clarity, responsiveness, and awareness of how students learn. He ignored feedback and made the course far more stressful than necessary- even the TAs couldn't solve his problems  I strongly advise avoiding his classes. The Math Department should do better. Worst UCLA experience!

Heavy accent + zero conceptual teaching = useless lectures. Tests nothing like practice/textbook, no practice exams. Average: 65%. Rare, arbitrary curves that barely help. Minimal partial credit, incompetent TAs. Other sections average 90%. Said: "I make exams extremely difficult or I'll get in trouble giving too many A's." Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Sihao Ma is the worst professor I have had to take at UCLA. Professor Ma decided to make things extremely difficult. Unclear grading criteria, difficult exams, unrealistic/unsimilar homework. He said in lecture "I make the exams intentionally difficult because I will get in trouble for giving out too many As." I would rate lower if I could.

His explanation in class isn't very clear. His exams are difficult since some of the questions don't really match the homework. For midterms, if you don't use the methods he preferred, you'll likely get no credit for that question. But he's accessible and replies to email quickly. I would suggest taking 32A with other profs if possible.

His accent is hard to understand and he grades extremely harshly. He asks vague questions and gives no credit if your method isn't his preferred one. Test averages are in the 50s, and homework doesn't match the tests. Office hours aren't helpful because his explanations are confusing . This class has been very disappointing.

By far the worst professor I've ever had. He was rude, ignored emails, and made exams purely memorization-based. I learned nothing in this class. He requires you to pay for materials and bans laptops in class. His ratings are terrible, and it's baffling he's still employed—nobody seems to like him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Poor communication skills, lack of self-awareness.

Worse professor I have ever had! What is the point of memorizing the reading materials word by word? And what is the point of learning by mere memorizing? And why are p/np students required to get a B to pass the course? Could have been a useful course, but unfortunately a horrible professor.
sad af wefr wefrw wefrw

Just awful. Doesn't seem to want to even be in class. Barely knows material. If you can work the homework problems, the old exams, and TA review problems then you should do well on the tests. avoid her like the plague.

Professor Annabi is the best,knowledgeable,patience and She is one of the highest honors in the world.
We all know who are those who make negative comments and who are they from and what is their intention, they are not more than two people, it is better to seek more effort in education instead of envy.
Recommend
she is a very easy professor. The class was very difficult to not get an "A" in it. However, that does not mean it is a useful class or that you'd learn very much from the experience.
No comments, just bad.

HORRIBLE!!!
You could skip class since everything she lectures are in the book and the powerpoints based on the book.

Annabi is nice, but she is very very difficult to understand. She can't answer questions well so her lecture is next to useless. Count on using the textbook and practice exams to learn the material on your own. I don't want to completely bash her though because she tries hard to do what she can for her students.

I'm 100% sure that she knows nothing about Bioseparations! NEVER take her class! She answered almost no question! She taught wrong formula! She deliberately forget extra credits for course evaluations! No more Annabi for me
Highly recommend this class and prof.Annabi. She is knowledgeable and caring.

The TA was more help than her by far. Don't get me wrong she seems like a nice person but i didnt learn anything in her class. When we asked her for help she simply said "please ask my TA". At first I thought she was strict but quickly realized she had no idea what she was doing.

Best course& instructor. Prof Annabi is great. Course was well organized.Really liked the course structure, lectures and invited speakers seminars. We worked on a research project with our team members and gave a group presentation during the last week and liked doing research on topic related to medical applications of materials. Def recommend her

Enjoyed C124, also took C125 with Prof Annabi. Very knowledgeable and an expert in biomaterials. With no biology background, I learned many new concept in biomaterials and tissue engineering, great lectures, also posted lecture notes/recordings soon after the class. assignments were journal club presentation and paper summery. Highly recommend it!

I enjoyed this course. Surprised by all the negative comments that is obviously repeated by few disgruntled students. prof.Annabi knows course material well. organized lecture notes ,knowledgeable and expert in her field and more importantly caring. Definitely recommend her.
Amazing prof. cares about her students and their success. Highly recommend prof.Annabi.
She is unprofessional, does not prepare for class, and her class is somewhat uninteresting.

Annabi is quite possibly the worst professor I've ever had. She teaches so much off her slides it seems like she doesn't even know what she's teaching. She was very condescending when students were confused and had to ask questions. She refuses to be accommodating to students, and failed to organize her course in any way that made sense.
she is awful

This by far has been the worst college course I've ever taken. Professor Annabi is not helpful. She is constantly corrected by students and is very unclear when trying to teach. She also seems to get bothered if you ask for clarification. If you have the book you are better off teaching yourself. I wish I had something positive to say, but I don't.
worst ever

Nasim Annabi is a very nice lady. Unfortunately she does not have a firm grasp of the bioseparation course and should not be teaching this class. She is disorganized and changes due dates at the last minute. It appears the students know more about separation strategies and UO problems than she does. But getting an A wasn't that hard.

The problem is trivial. When she does answer a question, she says an answer that is completely unrelated to the question. In addition, during most classes, she does not know how to solve some problems in the textbook. She would put an equation on the board, spend at most 10 minutes on a problem, and this is not an exaggeration, and then erases it.

Prof. Annabi is by far one of the best teachers at Chemical Eng Dept at UCLA. You will notice her dedication towards her students and enthusiasm to teach. She shows how theoretical materials relate to real life industry problems. She has an accent ( common among professors in engineering majors) but speaks clearly. many share my opinion!

I got an A in this class and still say that she is one of the worst teachers I have had. It is possible to get an A in her class, but she is not a very good teacher and ruined a class that I was very excited to take.

She is barely qualified to be a lecturer. After spending an entire class bumbling around writing arbitrary equations and becoming confused by her own algebra and logic. Poor teacher, poor understanding of subject matter.
If anyone want to learn something please dont take her class. She is unbelievable. My advice ( save your money ).
It was okay. Prof. Annabi was not very organized but she is kind and caring.

Dr. Annabi is without a doubt the WORST professor I ever ever had in my academic career. Extremely inconsiderate person. She never answered any of questions. I taken two classes with her and in both of these classes she promised extra credit but never kept her promise. If you can, DO NOT take a class with her.
Just No!

Dr. Annabi is kind and caring and easy to communicate with. Course is easy and you can get an easy A if you have already pass reaction engineering course. Final project was fun, and she gave an award to our group.

I highly recommend taking this class for an easy A. Prof Annabi is a very generous grader and gives lots of food/drink during the course. I don't think anyone learned anything from this course - Prof Annabi is not a natural teacher. And she won't be able to answer homework/test questions 99% of the time. But, it will bump your GPA

Just okay! Exams are technically the same as last year, but she was not helpful at all if you had any real questions or concerns. Most of my emails were forwarded to the TA, and questions after class brushed off.

reviews.comment: The course is very easy; the lectures are basically the text book (same and even easier than 106 Chemical Reaction Engineering); Just attend the class and get an easy A; Professor Annabi is kind and bought us cookies and coffee :d, but she is not very helpful, like she doesn't know well the stuff and can't properly answer the questions!

Professor Annabi was one of the dumbest people I ever met. She made several mathematical errors on the board while doing problems. If students had any questions, she had no idea how to respond, instead asking the TA. I asked her a basic question regarding the homework and she told me she never looked at the problem before, and referred me to the TA
this was for anth001, wasn't listed. easy class, didn't attend anything for lectures. just do the work and you'll get an A.
Very easy class, I never went to any lectures and there are only essays. There is no final.
a great prof
Worst teacher ever

This class could definitely be more interesting if he was not the professor. He expects you to read so much yet when you come to class he goes off on tangents that do NOT relate. The course is half way over and i only have 3 pages of notes. He doesn't explain anything very yet but expects you to know everything. avoid this class for sure!
im in the class right now. and its alright. just gotta keep your mind interested in the subject. i feel like its gonna be a fun class.
I like the class. the last comment was uncalled for!

avoid this guy, completely unfair and uses the television to teach the class, might as well stay home and watch the discovery channel and national geographic. He expects ALOT from students as if it were a upper division course. He will make you hate studying about asian culture. Enough said~
he thinks he knows about asian culture, he knows as much as we do... basically if this guy can teach this guy you can also teach it. Basically the book taught the class, not the teacher.

Not a good professor. Bad textbooks. One of the books was only used 2 times for the entire quarter. He doesn't really teach, he just makes us do presentations on Tuesdays and then show us films on Thursdays. I'm a very good note taker, and only got 5 pages of notes this quarter. Bad exams! Not recommended!

Horrible professor, incomprehensible, hard for no reason.  Example-he told the ENTIRE class that he would fail us on a project BEFORE it was even turned in.  ALL the students hated him, and he even reduced a girl to tears...one of the worst classes ever.
He is so unlcear, he tells you one thing and then expects another thing.  he doesnt know how to explain and he is so freakin boring.  good luck.
He is so boring. He tries to make funny jokes that relate to class but then you get even more loss. Recommend not to take this class or if you do take it with another teacher

reviews.comment: I took this class Fall 2009, so I am posting it a little late. Prof O'Neill is awesome. It is impossible to get anything less than a C+. I have taken him twice and have received 2 As. His class is pretty straight forward. He actually has a pretty awesome sense of humor. Take him, trust me.
Easy A. I went to probably five classes, did half the work, and still nailed it.

He is not a hard professor he just doesn't really know how to run a 300= class. I enjoyed the class but it was hard. Go to class and pay attention. Do the readings and study you will do fine. He doesn't give less then a C so how bad can he be.
Seriously one of the hardest classes I have ever taken.  But I found out later that he doesn't give out anything less then a C.  Just be prepared to do a little math even though its a poli sci class

His lecture is full of trivial steps in those simple proofs, over and over again, and thus you will find it really tiring to take notes. Homeworks are not so hard but with a lot of problems, that requires you for hours on it.
Clear instruction

Professor O. definitely cares about his students, but he is a strict grader. Depending on your math background, this will be easy or hard class for you. I got an "A" in the class, but I felt like this class was VERY time-consuming. There is weekly homework AND quiz due. To prepare for exams, study your homework/quizzes/lecture problems.

Math has not been by strong suit. I even hired a tutor for this class and I still didn't pass. He goes over the material in class but it can get a little confusing. He just isn't that clear in his method of teaching.

I think since he's a friendly person, some assume he's also a good teacher.  Not so.  It's a rushed, confusing, and intense class.  Easy quizzes and brutal exams.  Do not trust the study guides!  The exam will be much harder.

Not the greatest instructor.. The lectures consist of you copying work from the board that looks similar to your homework. So basically not a lecture you just copy his work and try to apply it to your homework. I felt this instructor really didnt care about his students AT ALL. Would not recommend unless you already know precalc and need the credit

I am an engineering major I love math. This guy is REALLY amazing teacher. one thing i would say is WATCH OUT FOR THE HOMEWORK!!!! because you WILL get marked down. He takes the logical approach of teaching, shows what & y an equation works. Love this guy if you have taken algebra you will learn what i mean by 'logical' approach vs. your old prof.

Poor experience studying precalc for the 1st time. Granted the material would be difficult regardless of the instructor, Prof. Ouelette is more of a mathematician than a teacher. Classes consist of him copying his homework solutions to the board with little or no interruption. Bit of a show-off, I learned more about his calc skills than my own.

I never had as good of a teacher before and years from now, I will look back and think I never had as good of a teacher since.  He has been an inspiration and rejuvenated my self-confidence that was cracked upon transferring to a university.  Great man.  I hope he has a great life.

I don't know why he teaches at UCLA. when he makes examples in class, he is not able to solve them, but then he gives those things in exam. I do not recommend him. his lectures are terrible. BE AWARE!! His exams are EXTREMLY HARD!! today I had my midterm and I got a B, although I studied like hell. I'm going to get a unfair B in his class :( :(

He doesn't care about his students at all. He doesn't  know how to teach. such a waist of time, but if u have to take pic20A or 40A u have to take him. he doesn't replay to emails u send to him or when he replys, he replys after a week. he looks like a nice person, but I personally don't like his class, since he doesn't know how to teach!

Great person and passionate about the subject, but their teaching style isn't conducive to truly understanding the course material on a level that is required to succeed in the class. reviews.comment: Easily the worst Professor I've ever had, her exams are extremely difficult and aren't correlated with the lectures or reading at all. Not a single person in our group chat liked her class

the prof herself is nice but only grades on the midterm (40%) and final (60%). Read off the slides about random facts about monkeys and talks about her past research. The readings, option homework, nor lecture help on the exams. Seems like you have to major in this specific field and have years of experience in order to get an A on the exams.
Literally the worst. Worst lecturer, literally just reads slides. 40% midterm 60% final. Curved but who cares. Likes monkeys too much.

reviews.comment: This is the first time any of her courses has had a TA, which makes the class way better.
