Very helpful in office hours, easy grade as long as you go to discussion and skim.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Your final grade is determined by the Midterm 40% and the Final Exam 60%. The exams were extremely difficult and not representative of the course material (lectures, notes, videos, readings) I attended all lectures and studied 10+ hours for each exam, I passed the class with a C. I am extremely disappointed in this class and would not recommend it.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: #1 turn off she speaks about the material as if looking in the mirror, so it's easy to see why most of the students cant stand this course and are failing, we are not PhD-holders! Its mostly irrelevant facts about monkeys that have no grounds in the real world, in addition to advertising her pointless book, she has no TA! You're on your own in this

This class is hard! The reading is a lot and doesn't really help answering questions on the exam. Her lecture is very important, because she will be asking a few question straight from it.This class also have a research paper which is quite annoying, unless you enjoy doing some kind of research. Not recommended if you want to take it as an elective

She is very knowledgeable. You gotta be aware that you read the material & understand the concept b/f lec. so you can follow up with her lecture. If you don't do the reading and seek for help and expect to do well on the exam, there's no way. You have to study, but the material she makes you read is extremely relevant to a lot of subjects

actually she is not that bad. class was quite boring n unorganized. handouts are posted online, but u still need to go to class as she gives more info than the notes. she curves a lot, i got D n C on exams, but end up with B+.

This was the single worst experience of a class and professor I have ever had! Horrible teacher, unreasonable grading. Judgmental and rude when approached at office hours. For the final she had 200+ students single-file into an auditorium, waiting for 1.5 hours before starting the exam, then didn't even give enough time to finish. DO NO TAKE!

awful. awful. awful. boring lecturer; clearly she'd rather be doing research than teaching.  she's not available or approachable and her exam material include way more than just course content/lectures.  i'm an A student and i have never worked so hard for so little reward in a class before.  stay away!
Just no
Class is easy and nice for GEs. All quizzes are open book so that makes them a breeze.
When you look into his eyes, he looks like he's dying inside because he knows how easy his class is. Yet, he decides the material!
Patrick Wen was a very clear professor and made lectures interesting.

I honestly looked forward to coming to this class.  I had zero interest in the subject beforehand and finished it absolutely fascinated.  The best thing about this class was how much enthusiasm he had for the material, which made me enthusiastic too.  Who would've thought? Grading was reasonable and the exams were straightforward. The best GE ever.
Professor Wen's classes are always interesting.  His lectures are insightful and mind-expanding - and well prepared. His assignments are creative and interesting.  I highly recommend this Professor!

Pat is the hottest professor on the face of the earth!!!!   BUT... he is also the most interesting, interested, organized, and informative professor ever. He teaches you everything there is to know about Scandinavian culture, and his selection of books, which he requires you to read, are also great.
My favorite class I've taken so far!

Don't have the words to describe how great he is! Above all of the other professors, I think I would recommend taking him the most. An amazing person with clear passion. It also doesn't hurt that he's a pretty lenient grader, though I wouldn't slack off. Overall couldn't recommend him more, especially for first quarter transfers.

He's that rare combination of brilliance and kindness. His teaching style is excellent. He'll always stick out in my mind as a professor I was incredibly lucky to have. You'll learn a lot about literature, the United States' history and society, and yourself in a class of Yarborough's. Do take one if you have the chance.

My fav His analysis of the societal implications of a work, its symbolism, and the stylistic maneuverings of its author were drawn with most powerful and interesting care. My brain would brim with thought and was impressed by the great emotional charge of each piece. Long final, if you have an LD, contact OSD for proctoring. A note taker will help

After you go through the 10-series, the reading in this class seems so light. Passionate, articulate, understanding, educated, etc. Great individual, no negatives. Apart from reading, lots of history; make sure you don't get lost remembering all of it since ALL you need to do is identify 5 passages and analyze them in one paragraph. Great TAs too.

An infinite amount of superlatives do not even come close to how great of a professor he is! He is passionate about the material and it is infectious. As long as you read and think critically, you should be fine. He is also very approachable during office hours. He is one of my favorite professors!
Dr Cook is all inspiration. Thank you Professor!
Took Anthro of Protest and Activism with her. INCREDIBLE teacher. Amazing topic - she is very passionate and wants you to succeed. Hard class - lot of work and lots of readings but SO worth it.
WOW
Professor Cook doesn't teach every quarter. Take her when you can.Your world will change. She's rare as a human being and a teacher -:)

Dr cook doesn't just teach how to research change. She creates the change. Her course on protest and activism changed my life. This is what a human teacher with crazy smarts looks like. Take note UCLA.
The best course on activism and protest. Dr Cook is a force of nature:D
I'm still trying to figure her out as a person but as a teacher she's terrific. Her anthro of protest is the hardest course i've taken at UCLA and I'm not complaining
Wow Her anthro of protest course's revolutionary.It isn't for the faint hearted. Dr Cook's demanding instructor but she's in it for the students. Respect.

Probing performance has taken over my life after this Perf studies class and I didn't even want to take it at first. I'm considering grad school in this field now. Dr Cook is intense but clear thorough and fair. She gets everyone excited with her.
I really enjoyed this challenging course. This was my first real anthro experience!
Best instructor I've ever had at UCLA and i'm graduating this weekend
Dr Potuolu Cook can be scary because she is big on challenging studnents but she's a better tecaher for it
anthropology and performance studies
How can she have so much energy?
Tough as hell but amazingggg teacher.
I;m taking her anthro of performance.So far her lectures are engaging and helpful.
the best
Extraordinary course on the middle east
I miss Dr. Cook's class.Everything about the ME course. crazy pace crazy amount of info. She pushed us.She cares.
Kindest person ever. She takes her students on a challenging journey but i learned more in this class than in my 4 years at UCLA
Spectacular course
Great teacher and a greater person!!!
P. cook's honesty's refreshing. Think dat is why she's an amazin prof. super caring and tuff. Interesting preson.
Culture area of Middle East
Brrilliant!!

Very disappointing experience. I enrolled late due to waitlists and was met with immediate frustration from the professor, who said she was unhappy and suggested I shouldn't have joined. Great lecturer. And she's very helpful during office hours, but beware...if you are talking nonsense she'll call you out. She also teaches at the Law school.

Professor Shiffrin is one of the most intelligent people I have ever met. While this sometimes makes her intimidating, she truly cares about her students and makes every effort to make sure they're understanding the material and are engaged. Excellent professor.

Shiffrin can be cold.  However, I've also experienced how helpful she can be.  I don't know what I did or didn't do to receive the help I did from her, but she's actually quite compassionate.  If you want to be challenged, take her classes.  I took normative ethics and Rawls.  She is definitely something else.

terrible disposition. She is condescending & rude to students,outside and inside class. However, her lectures are crystal clear and the subject material is fascinating. It's a crime her personality isn't as remarkable as her pedagogy.  She is quite brilliant. Genius, in fact. TERRIBLE PROFESSOR AS A WHOLE!! TERRIBLE!!
great teacher
Hes absolutely awful. He talks really fast and expects you to comprehend everything
Lightest class of my life, would 100% take again for the free 100, Terrance Tao ngl the GOAT.
The lectures are pretty difficult but interesting
Overall not that great.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Best professor i have ever met.
Amazing Prof, was an absolute pleasure to work with.
I love this guy.
Just feels like he's spilling his knowledge out rather than teaching. You can be a genius and all, but that doesn't automatically make you a great teacher.
Great Prof.
AMAZING
this guy is the best high school teacher of the past 4 centuries.....i kid you not
It's just plain wonderful to be taught by the smartest, most amicable mathematician alive! Truly amazing.
Amazing professor, this guy is literally a genius. It's amazing to learn from him.
Great
No Comments
He is sweet, personally I found the exams pretty challenging though. They are open note so that is a plus.
Honestly, if you ARENT taking Sherrer, I don't know what you are doing. Dude literally lets you use hand-written notes on tests and curves his exams. PS he is also rlly good looking

Dr. Sherrer is honestly a great professor who understands students have a lot on their plate. The class consisted of two homework assignments, a midterm and a final which are open notes. Attendance isn't mandatory and he is super helpful and posts recordings and slides of all of his lectures to reference. His class was an easy A and he's great.

I hated this teacher, he was not only boring but he took very simple ideas and dragged it out to the point where I was confused and didnt even know what we were taking about. He also can seem to humiliate his students. He also took roll 5 minutes before class started and for all of us coming from other classes were late though his watch is fast
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Sitting through his boring lectures are beyond painful.

As a high school summer studies student I really enjoyed the class. I found Prof. Urdank's lectures interesting. Although depending upon which midterm and final essays were chosen, there was no need to buy all the texts and this should have been made clearer at the start of the quarter.
had this guy in late '80's-early 90's and he's actually quite approachable and helpful and he made me realize that history is a rigorous discipline.

Prof. Urdank took a jockish, boorish, apathetic student and inspired a love of History.  That was me back in the late-80s. He's really laid back, but his methods are Socratic, so be prepared when you get into class or be prepared to shrink down into your chair.  My favorite prof. at UCLA, and still going strong

One of the best and nicest professors at UCLA.  There's a midterm, a short(5-7 pages)"thought piece" paper, and a final.  All the questions are straightforward; there are no tricks.  Lectures are very informative and interesting and the books he assigns very interesting.  Highly recommended.
Love him. Insisted we call him God.

Professor Ruiz is one of the funniest professors I've had in UCLA. He enjoys teaching and engaging with students, which demonstrates that he cares about student learning .One of his key messages is for us to enjoy life.
Such a great lecturer, I would take him every year if I could. He was helpful, considerate, and gives us the midterm and final weeks in advance.
Amazing performer! Memorized verses of Classics of Spanish Golden Age! Overall great class! BEST TEACHER, caring , understanding and a bit revolutionary which makes him even better of a person!

The first lecture I attended Freshman year. After stepping out of Moore, I thought all professors were as enthusiastic, bright spirited and passionate in what they taught. He has a great sense of humor and truly cares about his students. I regret not going to office hours, yet I will definitely take another class with him. Take him! Easy A as well.

If there were words to describe Teo! He likes you to show up to class but I wouldn't necessarily call it mandatory, but it gets to the point where you WANT to show up to class. He really is a storyteller and sucks you in in lecture to the point where you don't realize you learned anything until you write a paper. Incredible teacher and human being!

In short, their is nothing NOT to LOVE about Teo.  A great professor, probably the best UCLA offers.  Fun, engaging, incredibly knowledgeable.  Teo is what I will miss most when my days at UCLA are done.  HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!

Dr. Ruiz has been in National Geographic television for the show "TABOO" and was a Princeton University professor in his earlier years. His lectures are so moving, he will almost bring tears to your eyes. He incorporates history and life as a human being into great depth, touching the heart of a student who truly loves knowledge and learning.
COOL PROFESSOR. SUPER FUNNY! KNOWS HOW TO GET HIS STUDENTS' ATTENTION WHILE TEACHING EFFECTIVELY.
Love him, you'll really miss out if you don't take his class. One of the best and most caring professors I've met!
Awesome prof- best ever.
amazing guy :)

He works really hard at appearing to be friendly, but he doesn't teach history.  Rather, history is a platform for him to preach his message of hedonism and revolution.  That's all he cares about and you'd better be able to write about it on his tests if you want a good grade.
No Comments
Best history professor/history chair ever!
Awesome teacher, do your self a favor and sign up for his class.
teo is the greatest! do urself some good and take a class with teo. went to paris with him this past summer...a truly amazing experience!
One of the best teachers ever!
Best. Teacher. Ever.
Don't graduate from UCLA without taking a class with Teo... your tuition is a waste without it.
Teo is the man.
Amazing is the only word for Teo
I love this man...
I love this man...

ive taken most of teo's classes. his 121 series, his religion & magic class, etc. and i even did a research project for him. the one thing about teo that stands out above his colleagues is the amount of caring he has for his students, their well being, and their educational experience.
Teo is so much fun. He is an awesome professor.
What a remarkable, interesting, caring professor.  His lectures are like being transported to another time.  Best professor in my years at UCLA.
My favorite professor at UCLA. Very passionate about teaching and an expert in medieval history.
He is a great professor and advisor.
WOW!! absolutely the teach I had at UCLA.
No Comments
SIMPLY THE BEST TEACHER AT UCLA!
Absolutely the best professor! I love him. He is just great. He is so passionate about the material that there is no way this is boring. He also relates the importance of history now.

It is important to address the issues that are affecting our community today. Our parents and grandparents worked hard for us so we can enjoy the privileges we have today in the academic world. 
Some profs. were given a chance under the Diversity, Equity and Inclusion program. Charlene was one of those bc she is a Chicano female of indigent roots.

She really cares about the chicano community.  We are sure that she will take a strong leadership role in  addressing the challenges in our city. Our parents and grandparents have worked a lot for their children and grandchildren and deserve the support of those who by pure luck are doing better and making a lot of money.  Please talk to her asap.

When we come back in the fall, Charlene will discuss the  issues affecting the Los Angeles community these days. She is the leader of The New Aztlan and chair of Chicano Studies. She is giving a voice to the oppressed at UCLA
 Please support her brave endeavors

I agree with all the students who find inspiration on Charlene's projects. I will also apply for the Woodrow Wilson scholarship, because the President's legacy is the foundation of the Chicano/a movement and the privileges we have today. Like Charlene, many of us come from the lower-working class.

I agree with the opinions of fellow students. She is one of the best teachers in the department.
It is a role model for many students who come from disadvantaged backgrounds. The thing to do these days is to apply for scholarships with major foundations like Rockefeller, Ford or Wilson. Those are the best avenues to undo the oppressive thoughts.

Liked the course very much, though it is a lot of work. Charlene's projects are motivating. She is involved in the history of art and the interpretation of decolonizing the minds of the Chicana /o people. Please follow her lead and apply for the Woodrow Wilson Foundation scholarship. It is the accurate path to decolonization of the oppressed

She is excellent in her field. Would take another class with her cz' she is a model for Chicana / Chicano students who are entering college these days. Charlene is admirable, particularly if you check her humble origins. An inspiration for all of us.
Lectures are always packed with info, do not miss!!! She is very nice and very approachable outside of class. By far one of the best professors you will ever take.

Bascara's writing prompts are REALLY confusing. The papers are all pretty long (6-7 pages) and the prompts can be a full page long without clearly stating what he wants or asking a direct question. He also sprung a last minute paper on us in wk 10 with an unclear prompt. The readings are great & hopefully your TA can decode the prompts in section.

Prof. Bascara is a real gem. He keeps class interesting with a variety of teaching strategies, films, and active-learning activities. He inspires us to think critically about the world and make the change that we want to see. He is accessible outside of class and gives feedback that is both helpful and motivational. Hands down the best!
Prof. Bascara is hot!! But yeah, a good teacher also.. take him. you'll enjoy him.

Although Prof. Bascara has a very different style of teaching, he is a fantastic professor nonetheless.  There is a lot of reading but also lots of group assignments and stuff.  Papers are definitely doable and actually interesting.  I would totally recommend him and I'm not an Asian Studies major.  He's incredibly nice/helpful! Take him!!!
good stuff

Brilliant Professor with deep knowledge 
Easy to understand and learn

Professor Lal's lectures are engaging, weaving in his opinions (w/o leaving out other POVs), anecdotes and interesting insights into how the material is relevant today. History, as he says, is the study of the future. You'll study history for ideas, not rote facts. The readings may be a bit heavy esp. if history is not your favorite subject.
He's pretty cool. He's a genius.
Not good for beginners need lot of extra reading,but course highly rearding

His lectures are very fast. He doesn't allow time for questions. His grader is ridiculously unfair. Lal claims there's a 10% portion for attendance, which he doesn't take, yet I never missed a class and my final grade never changed. Be prepared to read several novels and an excruciating amount of dense material. It's an unrealistic load of reading.
amazing guy . must watch his lectures

Very interesting and informative. He recognizes his own bias, and attempts to give both sides. Discussions are mandatory and TAs check to make sure you go to lecture. You should go to lecture because that is the main source of information. Midterms alright, final is difficult but doable. Skim the readings, but GO TO LECTURE!
lectures are very interesting, his opinions and annecdotes are fun to listen to, but work load is heavy
great teacher!!! the best

Profesor LAL is one of the best History instructors ever. He is an intellectual and challenges you like mad balls. He expects his undergrads to work like graduate students. If you are a lazy loafer, don't take any of his classes. However, if your up for a

Lal is a passionate man who really captivates you with the ancectdotes and stories he tells.  He's very approachable and interesting to talk to in and out of class.  Very supportive of student learning.

Brian a smart guy, and his lectures and written homeworks force you to learn a lot. Unfortunately, the written homeworks are a lot of work, and they generally don't match what you do on the quizzes. He also doesn't give clear feedback on quizzes, and the class felt disorganized. Our grades were not posted online, and deadlines were always changing.

Professor Van Koten is a young, new professor. He is clear with his examples and he's always willing to answer questions. His exams, however, are very difficult. He's more focused on proofs . His class is kind of demanding since he has quizzes every week. It was the first time I struggled in a math class. I worked very hard and ended up with a B.

He teaches well and has good examples. The problem is his tests. The av. on the midterm/final was about 50%. I got a C+ which is super disappointing because I've never gotten below an A in math ever before. His tests are SUPER hypothetical, confusing, and downright almost impossible to pass. May the curve be ever in your favor.
Van Koten is a new professor, and he is very clear with his examples. He focuses on theory and proofs, rather than routine calculation. If you do the homework in his class, you will be fine
Hes a very good professor! Amazing class! I feel like I am learning more than being stressed about silly exams.

His accent is fine, the subject is demanding so don't no slackers need apply. The more I read up in this subject, the more I find his name cross referenced everywhere! He's an expert for sure and can answer any question your throw at him provided it's relevant. Difficult, but you'll learn so much. That's why were here after all right?

He is extremely well-educated and provides ample background on everything he teaches, going beyond dry facts. His attention to detail and knowledge of entertaining tidbits of history make for a very stimulating class. You write a lot but he is not a hard grader at all, and very encouraging. He is enthusiastic + informed. He made me a better writer.
Obviously LOVES what he teaches!
Very knowledgeable and helpful.
Personally found the subjects provided mind numbing.  Maybe it is his heavy accent, but I didn't feel like he engaged the class to participate in conversation/discussion of presented material

Some students felt that the thick German accent and esoteric nature of his teaching were difficult to understand.  But he was one of my favorite teachers, who got beyond the boring art-historical information and went into symbolism.

He gives 6 long tough assignments instead of a final but you're allowed to work with others and get help during office hours. If you like the material and are comfortable with proofs then it's gonna be a great time cause Liam is very enthusiastic about the topic and it shows.

Very chill teaching style and super reasonable guy. Frequently checked in with the class to make sure things were going well and that he wasn't going too fast or too slow. We had 6 assignments that made up our entire grade which was a welcomed change of pace for me. He gives lots of help on the assignments if you ask for it.
Professor Watson is at UBC Math dept now and is an excellent professor. Great humour, very polite, quick to get back to you. Just all around excellent asset for UBC.
Professor Watson is really talented at presenting lectures. And his exams are nice and closely related to the course materials. I had a great time having his class.

I don't know what the other comment is talking about I don't think i've ever seen professor yoon be anything other than friendly. The class was a good pace and the lectures were always interesting because she's good at adding personal anecdotes here and there. The only thing is the tests are a bit hard but straightforward if you study everything.
Mrs Yoon is nice but sometime very strict and cranky or moody. This class was all about memorization. If you are a native Korean speaker you will do fine. Overall it was fun.
Very helpful.Willing to listen and work with you.Highly recommend.
really good professor
Incredible teacher. Take this class. So willing to help. exams are not bad.

Best class I have ever taken in USC. He was so enthusiastic about what he teaches, always willing to help his students or to discuss anything with his students. Exams are writing essays based on readings and lectures. there's no right/wrong answer but you need to develop your arguments logically. Pay attention to his lectures and do the readings!

Professor Von Blum is one exciting, inspiring and intelligent man. He is extremely engaging with his students, managing to get impressive participation in a class get participation in a 120 person class. He encourages you to think about apathy, inequality, racism, war, patriarchal society. Yells at first, but honestly cares about his students!

Took his course at USC on censorship and the 1st amendment. Would definitely recommend him to any student. He is a very passionate educator with lots of life and educational experience. He talks loud and is bold. Midterm and final only -- essay style. Loved this class.

Professor Von Blum is by far my favorite professor at UCLA. His stories are great, he challenges you with intriguing scenarios, is extremely passionate about his subject, and truly cares about his students. I really have no negatives to say; I am even pursuing an individual studies course with him in the fall!

I took courses with Paul Von Blum at U.C. Berkeley over 35 years ago.  He was a highlight of my college experience.  I still think about the books we read (The Plague, Miss Lonelyhearts, The Loneliness of the Long Distance Runner, etc.) as well as the artists we studied (Hogarth, Goya, Daumier, etc.)  Thank you, Paul, for everything!

I took several of his pol sci classes at UC Irvine 20 yrs ago and thought so highly of him that I recently looked him up to see if he was still at UCLA.  He challenged us with moral and ethical scenarios which made us think and some didn't like it.  I loved it and thought he was very brave to push the envelope on taboo subjects.BEST Teacher EVER!

I took Paul's class over 13 years ago.  He helped shape my understanding of the world.  I learned a lot about Paul Robeson, who continues to inspire me, and about public art.  Good teachers question the way we think about the world and they force us to ask ourselves questions far after the exams are over.  I recommend that everyone take him.

Anyone reading this....I took two courses with Paul in the mid-70's at Berkeley.  35 years later, I can tell you he changed my life.  What he teaches is how to think and communicate.  Whatever you do in your life...those are the most important tools.

Itrs professors like Von Blum that make UCLA prestigious and worthy of anything. He's been in the UC system I believe for over 20 years begining with Berkeley.. part of the Civil Rights movement.. I cant say more than passionate & experienced to describe him. You must take a class with him to make your education worth anything!

At first I did not want to go to class during the summer because basically I did not have to for the grade was based on two essays. But the passion and quality of this teacher made me want to go just to hear him talk and engage in the interesting conversations. I RECOMMEND THIS TEACHER!

This class was soo long that it could get kinda boring, but professor Von Blum is the type that will go out of his way to help you understand and grasp the information. I recomend him just another course.
EXCELLENT PROF! MUST TAKE AT LEAST ONCE WHILE AT UCLA!

I would not call this man a teacher, but more a speaker. He is a very passionate speaker. However, I had no idea what he wanted us to retain. I did not know what to take down in my notes. I think it is completely unfair that some TAs grade harsher than others. It is an easy grade if you have easy TAs. If you are a great writer, you should be fine.

He's an amazing teacher! The discussions in class - the way he leads them w/out you even noticing it -, his points and questions, the topics, the works, etc. were excellent. AND he's  there for his students anytime.

I disagree with the previous review. My discussion sections with my TA helped glue everything from lecture and readings together. I did very well in the class and found my TA to be very accessible and helpful. If you write well and convincingly, participate in discussion, and attend sections, you'll get an easy A.

He is very passionate about his teaching, but the grades are not in line with what you learn.  Poor TAs that lack communication skills.  He is a great guy with interesting lectures, but the graded system is all subjective and is not objective.
No Comments
EASY B HARD A
very captive speaker with lots of interesting stories.  class is just two papers and a quiz, class can inspire you.  is always open to help students after class
he's one of the best professor here.

Von Blum is by far one of the best professors at UCLA.  he give his lectures with much energy and her sincerely cares about his students.  he has a passion for what he does and what he teaches- and it shows.  if you haven't taken a class with von blum, you've missed out.
After graduating from UCLA, Von Blums classes are probably the fondest *coursework* memory that I have.  He really enhanced my college experience.

Who takes you on a field trip to LA county Jail for a Speech class - Prof. Von Blum. This man poseses a gift for teaching. He inspires and excites his students through the greatest lessons of all, that which come from real life experience. I'll never forget some of the discussions we had in and out of class. How many teachers truly make that kind of an impact? I hope he'd take pride knowing he's partialy to blame for the passion (and stories) I bring into my own classroom.
Amazing professor. Amazing man. Like no professor you've ever had.

One-of-a-kind professor-- Exemplary man. The most profound and influential teacher i've had.  I'm now a professor in San Diego and I continue to reference Von Blum as an inspiration. Unfortunately, the UCLA administration fails to recognize that it's the people like Paul who make the university honorable, respected and prestigious.

Thrilled to see the students at UCLA recognize quality - had Von Blum over ten years ago at UCI and we all worshipped him.  I am now a professor on the East Coast and he is still the best I've ever seen.  He is a living legend, so make your time with him count.  He is a rare gift.

I love this professor, his classes are interesting, and he brings together a wide range of knowledge and integrates them into his classes. and he teaches everything! I especically liked his women's studies and art history courses, and I guess the comm classes were awesome tooo. You rock Paul!
well he is my dad so I guess you can say that he's also my professor. If any of you students have heard any stories about me in your class, remember that it's just a story.  love you dad
this professor is excellent, and unlike another reviewer's opinion, I like his personal stories because he is actually interesting
anybody like hearing stories?  because that's what his lectures are.  very opinionated man, prefers to share past experiences rather than teach anything.
we loev you BLUUUUUUUM!!

This professor truly cares about his students - like no other I have met at UCLA or anywhere. He even spend his off hours helping students, taking students on field trips (four field trips in one quarter!) The best teacher I've had!
No Comments
Professor Von Blum is quite refreshing and he doesn't believe in final exams.
i hate her she is awful
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Yan lao shi is amazing! She is so caring and helpful. Her lectures are all in Mandarin and she pushes you to speak as much Chinese as possible, so you have to study a lot in order to keep up. Exams are difficult but not impossible, and quizzes are the hardest part of the class, but she drops your two lowest quiz scores. Amazing teacher overall!
Shen laoshi is an inspiration. She cares about her students more than any teacher I've ever met and is so wholesome and kind. I learned so much and never felt that I wouldn't be able to suceed.

Lovely and caring lady but very intense Chinese course. She teaches the class almost entirely in Mandarin and covers a ton of material. It's very fast-paced... You will learn a lot but will fall easily behind if you don't keep up. The exams are brutal. Make sure your schedule is flexible and dedicate much time to studying otherwise you will burn.
I know that Prof Goyal yells at department staff, showing elitism and poor character.

She hate females. Incredibly boring and opinionated. Many of us met after class for "therapy" to decompress because she is an awful person. Thank God I will never have to take her again, could not pay me enough. Her criticism is not helpful or contractive. Does not lend itself to improving. Take only if you are a masochist.

This class was taken on Zoom due to the coronavirus. Professor Goyal was very understanding and nice considering everyone was quarantined during class. We read a lot of books and had mostly lectures/discussions each class. If you do the work on time and put in effort, you'll get an A. She is not a hard grader. I recommend her
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Goyal is extremely knowledgeable about African American History. Always available during office hours, she genuinely cares about students. She is a fair grader and gives great feedbacks. She is brilliant and her passion for the topic is contagious.I loved her class and style of teaching.

WONDERFUL! Clear, organized, prepared, she makes class a breeze. The books were great and her lectures were heavenly. Study guides match the tests so easy As. Great to talk to in office hours, great to do honors with, GREAT PERSON. She was concerned for our learning and the whole class felt appreciated and respected by her.
She is a great, extremely intelligent teacher.  However, it is essential one keeps decorum and writes well in order to succeed in her class.

The readings were very interesting. That's about it. The seminars were super boring- half of the class would fall asleep. I found more amusement watching people's heads bobbing then I did trying to decipher what she was saying.

Yirush is great! Super helpful in office hours. His enthusiasm for early American history is infectious. Get ready to read a LOT every week, 150 pages a week of dense primary source material is not unusual. But if you enjoy American history you will love his class, one of my favorite profs at UCLA
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: You will do a lot of work (if you want an A) and learn alot. Great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Yirush is very challenging, but he makes you a better writer while teaching history in an organized, logical, but interesting and fun way. His use of primary sources is incredibly beneficial, and he essentially provides you with all the evidence you need for your papers. Again, he is tough, but his criticism of your writing is truly a gift.

One of the toughest classes I've ever had, but it was worth it because it made me a better writer.  It also made me better at analyzing primary sources.  He pushes you to give him your very best, although if you're looking to help your GPA more than your writing skills take someone else.

I enjoyed this class alot. Professor Yirush really knows the subject and is passionate about it. There is a lot of class discussion and he likes to call on the unsuspecting (always be prepared). Lots of memorizing dates and reading from primary sources. I would definitely take his class again.
Yirush is an excellent lecturer - kept things interesting and fast paced.  He is tough but fair & consistent.  I would highly recommend this Professor.

He was very excited about the material, which makes the class more interesting. Make sure to do all the readings. He gives a helpful study guide a few days before the final so you can narrow down your studies. Very friendly guy.
Really knows the subject but could  lighten up on the primary source readings. He seems to take this a little too seriously. Attendance is mandatory.
lectures made me so sleepy lol just make sure you're at discussions and listen to prof Yans examples in lecture
Lectures were really interesting. Exams are straightforward. You HAVE TO READ ALL THE ARTICLES! He uses them for the exam.
Fair
Very kind professor, inspirational and dedication. Though his class is dry and textbook poorly organized.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: I enjoyed his class, although probability is a pretty uninteresting subject. The prof. explained everything clearly and in an organised manner.
best professor at UCLA!

Did not teach towards the bar at all, but taught only what he was interested in. Oftentimes made offensive or insensitive jokes that he thought was funny. Extremely disorganized and would often digress to make jokes or tell stories that were not relevant, which often disrupted the flow of his class. Poorly constructed final and MC questions
Very engaging lectures, pretty fair with cold calls. The class covers a lot of ground that you are expected to be responsible for.
Terrible Professor.

He's pretty fantastic. Hilarious to say the least, always keeps class interesting. He does focus quite a bit on policy, which some like and some don't. His final exam was incredibly fair. Overall one of my favorite professors at UCLA.
Well good. Funny lectures and good relationship with students.

Nice professor in teaching GEOG XL5, has learned various perspectives about the Human and Nature Relationships. Even though the final exam was hard, the class is still inspirational and meaningful to take.

My first course taken in Senior 2 for UCLA Credit, great professor! It is easy to follow through classes but it is also necessary to study hard according to the study guide provided before the exam, but it is easy A with enough preparation!
Amazing Professor! Very interesting lecture and very helpful in responding to students' question. Not hard on grade. Friendly to students. Fast-pace lesson but always easy to understand.
Very good personality and amazing lecture content. Dr. Zackey draws excellent geographic graphs to make sure that students understand clearly. His grading is fair and just, but never too harsh.
Always engage students with fascinating topics in the lectures. Fine homework and almost no quizzes. Appropriate exams! Generous curve and grades.
Very nice professor, and I took 3 his courses in China. Hard exams, but interesting lectures.

Prof. is a great Prof overall. He has passion for his field and he explains course material clearly. He keeps the class engaged and lifts the class spirit up from time to time with his jokes. Prof. Zackey is very approachable after class during office hours and he's a friendly guy to talk to. An eye candy too, but TMI.
Nice teacher.
love this professor. very helpful.very fun class!
Tougher class than I thought. Exams are pure memorization- unfortunately, they do not test whether or not you know the material and can put it in your own words. Write down everything from lecture.

He's awesome. He's the teacher for our international program. I took two of his classes. He's really easy-going and humorous.  I'm not a native English speaker, but he can explain everything clearly. He gives a lot of help out of class.
This guy is awesome,he is one of the best teachers that I have met! Don't hesitate, join his class, be a friend with him, you will enjoy it.

He is one of the sweetest guys I've ever met. Humorous, easy-going, helpful, responsible...He also gave me a lot of help outside of class. Don't hesitate. He's the right guy to go to whenever u have problems.

Had him for class in China.  Gets to know students, approachable, fun.  Easy class, but I learned a lot. Willing to work with you if you don't "get it" right away.  Lectures didn't always seem to have a theme or point tying them together, but were usually clear.  Professionalism not his strength.  Study guides for tests, straightforward assessment.

An awesome professor! Very clear, helpful, and knowledgeable! I would highly recommend taking his class! There are 2 exams, somewhat extensive but extremely fair. Loved it and can't wait for him to come back to take another class with him.

Great professor! Very clear, helpful, and a joy to listen to. Explains things well and is always available during office hours for any questions. Many of my classmates and I were very upset that he didn't teach Geog 2 during Winter 2009. UCLA needs more professors like Dr. Zackey. I highly recommend taking classes with him!
Great professor, will make material very easy to understand and is very approachable. Highly recommend him, he is no easy A, but if you put in the effort it is very easy to get a good grade.
Cool guy and interesting mateial. Exams are mostly memorization and labs are really easy.

During the COVID-19 era, since everyone is remote, this professor is extremely obsessed with the assumption that all students cheat and that makes him paranoid. Consequently, he makes the quizzes for our readings so difficult that even after thoroughly reading, despite multiple ways to answer, he's very picky and will mark you down. Don't take him
Great class. The easiest poli sci i have taken thus far. Do the readings, 5 quizzes, an in class essay for the midterm, and then a take home final. You will not regret.
Very helpful, very informative, a brilliant guy.

very enjoyable guy, has a hilariously dry sense of humor and will definitely make you laugh at times. very helpful, always responds to questions through email, which he will respond to whole class. fair quizes and midterm, final yet to be seen.  highly recommended

Adam Roberts is extremely nice. You need to go to every lecture because if you do not I guarantee you that you will not get even an A-.  You should always bring his lecture power points  to class and also record him. His tests are ridiculous: multiple questions, fill in blank and stories. I would not take any course with him. Don't count on a curve
Very good professor, knows what he is talking about, very friendly and didactic.

Dr. Roberts used his good sense of humor and his bio/neurology stories to keep the class interesting the entire quarter. He was good at reading when the class was overloaded with info, and would answer students' questions with tons of enthusiasm. He's excellent at explaining tough concepts, and genuinely wanted us to do well. Learned so much!

Dr Roberts is a dream. His lectures are hilarious and informative. Additionally, he  has a great personality and is extremely caring and  approachable. I would call his tests tough but fair. Extensive studying is required to get a good score, but all the relevant info is covered during lecture and review aka there are no curveballs. Absolute gem.
Dr. Roberts is awesome. He really cares about trying to make sure that students understand the info and will go over the tough information multiple times in order to do so. He's great!
reviews.rating_tags: CARES ABOUT STUDENTS--Respected by students--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Truly cares about students and wants to make sure each of them learn the materials.  He is absolutely helpful, amazing teacher, always there for you to help you throughout the course.  If you want good grade you must attend all the classes.
Lots of chance for improvement. Hard tests. Explanations are more like tangents, long and at times unorderly. Doesn't use textbook and lectures slide notes aren't all the material thats on text.

Roberts is THE BEST, most adorably amazing teacher. I am touched to see how far above & beyond he goes to help his students learn. I hope he knows his time & effort (& humor) are deeply appreciated. This class is quite challenging. I worked harder & smarter than before. I learned so much cool stuff, and I am now more inspired than ever.

Best anatomy professor at GCC. He takes so much of his OWN time to have review sessions For his students. STORIES are KEY! Be prepared to Study! If I passed you could pass! You won't regret taking this professor. he is funny and makes lectures fun .. Good luck p.s Gcc needs to hire more professors like Dr. Roberts
Roberts definitely has passion for teaching this course.  Lecture slides and stories are key.

Roberts definitely has passion in teaching this course.  Basically study what he tells you to.  If you are unsure of what to study, go to his office hours and he might even give away a few questions on the exam.

Honestly I've never had such an amazing professor before, especially for biology. He was supper involved with us, and tried to help us succeed more than any professor has ever before. He held tons of office hours, where he would go over many topics that he would tell us would be on the test. Very passionate and amazing professor! TAKE HIS CLASS!

Crager was really interesting. His presentations were always clear & organized. He took a lot of time to answer questions, even the most obvious, insipid or downright stupid ones he addressed with total respect & seemingly genuine interest. I find that really admirable and unique in a prof. Even the nice ones tend to condescend at times. Good guy.

Crager is not only competent in his field, but also great at teaching. His lectures are exceedingly clear; if you take good notes, class assignments (3 total) will be straightforward. The papers are not full-length argumentative essays. Rather they comprise 4-6 questions wherein you explain arguments from the texts to show comprehension.

He's a great lecturer who really demonstrates the importance and nuances of ancient Greek philosophy.  Extremely, extremely intelligent and always made time for me when I had questions on the subject. He devoted a lot of time to make sure I understood the answers to the questions I had.  Thank God he teaches at UCLA.
Super smart, approachable and nice, he's a clear and funny lecturer who will help students if they need it with patience. We need more professors like him around
Took Ancient Philosophy with Crager. Great lectures, and I hear he was good in office hours. T.A.s did the grading.
Really helpful and his lectures are clear and concise. Gives you plenty of time to finish assignments and lets you know way ahead of time. Actually, pretty funny and easy to listen to!

PR is a stupid class.  He makes it kind of fun but at the end of the day it is just a mandatory class that neither he nor the students care about.  Take his class when you are taking the MPRE.  Study ahead and take MPRE mid-semester. Review for final. Gives bonuses for participation.  Don't buy the textbook.  You hardly use it.  Buy suppliments.
Oh man - he is not very good. He thinks he is funny but he isn't.

Winkler becomes a better teacher each year.  He is extremely clear, especially for Con Law, which is notoriously ambiguous.  He is also an easy guy to get to know and is willing to take the time to get to know you.
this guy sucks. bad
Professor Saguy cares a lot about her students and is challenging but fair

Professor Saguy is an excellent professor. Her lectures are engaging, and they're organized and easy to follow. This was one of the best classes I've taken at UCLA. Professor Saguy is interesting and informative, and while the class is challenging it's definitely manageable.

if you are not gender major, do not take the class. it wont do any good for you. professor has very narrow minded in terms of taking students idea. she does not answer you with your question, but reiterate her ideas.
Nice guy but don't feel like I learned much it was mostly busywork.
Nice guy but does not teach that well. Class was often boring but chill. Read the book and you will be fine.
Great prof, no problems
Totally nice guy, but i didn't really learn anything about Torts in his class...

I had the misfortune of having Prof. Abel's torts class at CUNY Law School. While I came into class really interested in Torts, I left completely disinterested, disappointed, and felt that I learned absolutely nothing. If you want to get a decent grade, use outside material, i.e., Gilberts outlines. For policy, use the article he published.

Abel is really nice, but he is so focused on policy that it is hard to learn the rules.  If you have him, make sure to check out a sample exam early on -- it is nothing like what you discuss in class!
A raving socialist in my opinion.  He didn't teach the subject as much as he taught his socialist worldview.  It was annoying.
The final was tough but it's no different from past years.  I really liked Professor Abel.  He's a cool guy.

Prof doesn't believe in the tort system. While you're a first year, it's irritating (because you don't learn the black letter law), but later you pretty much realize he had it right all along. Read the papers he's published to better understand his p.o.v. for the test.
Richard Abel's exam are a crapshoot.
A Marxist at heart, his teaching of the subject tends to be ideologically driven, and he does not give enough time to alternative perspectives.
1st year School of Law Torts class.. very good teacher

Overall, this class was really easy, There's no discussion and it's pretty straight forward. The midterm and final are both multiple choice, 103 questions each. The final only covered material taught after the midterm, so that was nice. You shouldn't have a problem acing, no less passing, a class with Abramson. (Check out his band Crying 4 Kafka).

Nice prof but the material can get very boring sometimes. If u read his book and attend lectures the good grade is guaranteed. I got 50 out of 100 of midterm and the final and ended up with the B in the class. Curve is only to help you. Highly recommended

Dr.A is pretty straightforward. There are lots of movies and 4 books to read. 2 are case studies...easy reading. 1 book is about Pleasure and its dry because its very theoretical...and last book is a novel. He repeats himself, so it might be good if you take notes slow...but dreadful if you feel he repeated the info already.

Very cool teacher! fair and balanced, has strong opinions, but is not preachy, respectful and curious about others points of view, text is a book he wrote. If you do the readings and use the study guide you will do well
She focuses A LOT of small details. totally would not recommend. Chose another professor if possible
She is a tough grader, but her office hours are very helpful. This class is a lot of work, but overall I still enjoyed it.

Very bad teacher. Focuse on details and not on important subjects. She expects you to know every word of every reading. Very difficult tests. She choose images we have not seen in class. And she is very severe. I regret to have taken her class.

Very new teacher. Expects you to know every single thing she assigns as a reading or discusses in class. Does not always ask test questions on important concepts but uses non-central topics to insure you read. Photo ID's can be on anything shown in class or on readings. Nice but give her a few years to figure out how to teach before you take her.
lame
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
loves those cadavers a bit too much

Professor Acabado is an amazing lecturer! I had heard amazing things about him prior to the quarter so I was excited to take his course while also bummed that it would be online. There are weekly quizzes, bi-weekly precis, and bi-weekly peer reviews. Both tests are based HEAVILY on the quizzes and textbook so it's easy to study for! Great course!

A subject like Archaeology definitely isn't my cup of tea, but Professor Acabado definitely made it painless for me. His lectures are dry but he cracks some pretty good jokes now and then. Just fill out the study guides, participate in disucssion, and you'll get an A. Would definitely recommend taking this course as an easy GE.

He tells us to do group projects because he doesn't like to grade papers. He is unclear on deadlines and paper rubricscriteria. Better be ready to check in every day to see if he's changed the syllabus and requirements without telling anyone. His lectures are unorganized, unclear, and skip over the actual facts to focus on unimportant things.
Hal was everything an arts professor should be: creative, helpful and supportive.

Hal was an amazing Prof. He inspired me to continue writing, and he is one of the best at UCLA. He cares about the process rather than the end goal, which makes for a great Prof. He also tells amazing stories that will teach you more than any book will. Take any class that he teaches. You will not regret it.

Very intelligent and challenging professor. Doesn't sugarcoat when it comes to feedback or notes on writing. Sticks to the facts about the process of creating a screenplay and getting it sold. Pushes students to really consider the positives and negatives of a career in screenwriting. Those that are driven and have the talent will succeed.
No Comments

UCLA consists of a variety of challenging courses. Of course, this is one of them that is full of content, yet if you do your part you are bound to pass. Agnew is very straight forward and if you pay just enough attention you are bound to pass this class with flying colors since all the content he discusses is in the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I like Agnew but hate the way grading was set up for this class. You have a midterm & a final, plus some points for participation. That wouldn't be so bad except that the tests are essay format ONLY, short and long answer - without the option to choose between questions. You have to answer every single one, so you have to know ALL material. Avoid!!

He is one of my favorite professor. His class is easy to follow along.  I guess he was up sad about people didn't show up, so his exams questions are topics and ideas  mentioned in lecture. 1 midterm and 1 final , both take home and no homework. Go to his office hour, he would help you.
One of the best geography professors in the U.S.!!!!

Doesn't teach Gov. He's mister geography! His overheads leave something to be desired (some are brown around the edges!) but he's very focused and explains things clearly. he's a nice guy and open to different opinions which is good when the course has a lot of politics in it.
He's great, super helpful and British

Prof Agnew is a really nice guy, he keeps lectures pretty interesting, shows lots of pictures and shows videos. Grade is based on 3 5-page essays, which are based on lecture material, no surprises at all. Grades fairly and is easy to approach. Only problem is he doesn't post any lecture outlines or anything online. Overall Id definitely recommend.
He was by far one of my favorite professors at UCLA!

His lectures are interesting, even though much of the subject matter is pretty dry.  He has a sense of humor which makes lectures even better.  Agnew posts "practice" midterms, but they are practically the same as the real midterm.  He does assign lots of reading, but i really enjoyed his class.
I loved this professor. I took the class for fun and he even posted the previous midterms online and the questions on mine were the same! If you put in a little work its an easy A
No Comments
this per-v left the 4 comments below and talks about how hot he is. again he left these comments.

he came in the middle of the year so he tried the best to catch up. i was unable to go to office hours cause his times conflicted with my schedule. overall he's a pretty fair grader.  word of advice is to go to as much office hours possible. i heard it really helped.

Didn't think he knew too much cause he seemed young and inexperienced.  Turned out to be wrong.  Ended up getting a B+, but I felt I learned alot. The only thing that bugged was all the girls sat in the front.
pretty demanding and expects the best from students. great lectures for an early morning class.  the cutiest, youngest prof i've had, but still good caliber teaching. too bad ladies - he's taken.
very informative instructor. uses real life case scenarios that strengthen his lectures. extremely good looking, making it hard to concentrate, however. ladies watch out, he's a looker!

Difficult to understand at times, but definitely worth it. He knows exactly what he's talking about, but he's not looking for you to mimic his own opinions. He wants you to expand your own thoughts and give what you believe is the true answer. He doesn't believe in grading and is very open to all ideas. Great human being, overall.

Incredibly brilliant, incredibly kind, and incredibly cool. Prof. Julius is one of those rare professors who truly cares about not just research or the material, but encouraging students to learn it rather than regurgitate it. In other words, I learned to do actual philosophy as a result of him.. Basically this man is a genius.
He is very smart. But truly not the best explainer. I didn't enjoy his class

The man is so genius it may be hard to follow him. But you will have a great experience from taking his class, and it will make you think harder. on a one to one basis he is approachable and a very good dude. He doesn't believe in grades (competitive learning), he's very easy going (approaches the class with ease), & loves philosophy/economics! A+
AJ is the BEST! Every Philosophy student MUST take him before they leave UCLA!

Was extremely intelligent and belonged to 'net generation' before that generation came into existence. For that reason several students somewhat did not understand him. He was great and I learned a great deal from him.

INF STD 30. DONT TAKE HIM. HE IS LAZY, VAGUE, and BORING. THE ESSAYS HE ASSIGNS ARE VAGUE AND HE DOESN'T ELABORATE ON WHAT HE WANTS. THE CLASS MATERIAL IS ALSO USELESS IN WRITING THE ESSAYS. HE NEVER RESPONDS TO EMAILS AND IF HE DOES ITS ALREADY BEEN A WEEK OR TWO. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.
nauseating
Ugh! The Worst! He's so antagonizing and unhelpful. He doesn't always know what he wrote in his syllabus and he's often unclear and un-constructive.
Smart guy, subtle humor. Classes are interesting and well prepared. Seminars have great discussions.
No Comments

Honestly, took this class as the art history classes I've taken have been v chill/accessible. It just feels like this Prof takes the class WAY too seriously. The did not have the correct class code, but the class I had her for was Chicano/a art and artists.. Literally so much reading, and a lot of it is written by her, such dense writing. v mid
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Profe is one of the best professors I've had. You MUST attend lectures, because she uses clicker questions to check attendance, but the lectures basically provide everything you need to know for the tests. Be sure to do the readings or else you won't pass!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: She's brilliant. One the best professors I had at UCLA. She may not be super friendly, but I liked that she was strong, intelligent, and passionate. I loved her.

She is a good professor and will make you see Chican@ studies from a different point of view than Americans are used to. However, she is very obviously biased because she is a lesbian feminist who grew up in El Paso. Some of the stuff she says can be radical as hell.
I took a fiat lux on the Women of Juarez. Really informative!
I took various classes with Prof. Gaspar.  Yes, she is not always friendly, and yes she is hard.  However, her readings and lectures are awesome, and you will learn soooo much!
She is strict, not always friendly, BUT you will learn so much!  Great selection of books, powerful lectures!
Wow, one of the best prof. in the Chican@ studies department
No Comments
Excellent professor, tests quizzes everything the whole package.
Great professor! Funny accent, but clear lectures. Tests highly based on lectures. He provides a study guide and makes tests super easy!

I have to disagree with the comments. Yes, he is boring. Yes, it would be useful if he could put some visual presentation in class other than just talking (he doesnt allow recorders either) but the readings he assigns are interesting also the movies. Just pay attention to his lectures and you will walk out with an A like I did. It's not hard!
Awful tests. Blew my GPA.

This teacher is horrible. The class would be one of the easier classes if it were not for the curve. I aced the midterm and i'm pretty sure I did well on the final but enede up with a B-. Do nopt take this class if you care about your GPA!!!

The most annoying class I've taken. He is very incomprehensible, unavailable, and clearly doesn't care about his students. DO NOT TAKE HIM! Grades are based off a midterm(40% of your grade) and final(60% of your grade), but he grades on a curve, so beware since majority of the class will get over 90%. If you plan on taking him...GOOD LUCK!

Gupta is not even an anthropologist. His lectures are dry, boring and incomprehensible. The class is based on two exams and he uses graders to grade them and they are VERY unfairly. DONT TAKE THIS CLASS. He's a bad professor who didn't even show up for our final! He just doesn't care about his students.

Going into this class I was really excited. Then he started to talk...boring! he made the subject of food boring. Futhermore, his grading style has no logic. He had all of the graders grade our exam and he did not even look at them. He won't even regrade! He exam questions were worded in an incomprehensible manner.

He is one of my favorite teachers! He gives us interesting readings. I like how he doesn't stand there with a powerpoint but lectures in a discussion-like style.  He's also fun. His class is based all around great discussions. I find his approach to anthropology refreshing as he comes from a different field. Want to learn? Take this class!

Completely boring.  Adheres to a classic anthropological approach.  Very formulaic.  Definitely not meant to be a professor.  Should have found another niche.  Readings are good but he has not figured out how to moderate a good discussion.

Pretty good class. Prof goes on a lot of tangents and is always late to class so lecture isn't worth it. Lectures get recorded on Zoom so you can 2x speed through. Topics are interesting and give a very wide view of all healthcare-related subjects. Assignments aren't too bad, just take a little bit of time every few weeks.

Dr. Fluitt is one of the best professors here at UCLA. She made my first quarter as a transfer student wonderful and her class was extremely inspirational. She was always available for extra clarification and helps you in a way that really makes you think and learn the material. I highly recommend this class and denounce the previous review.

This class sucks! The class focuses on 2 guest speakers who present on topics that go over everyones heads. Then Amy deconstructs their research, each for 5 weeks. My main problem with Amy is her impossible problem sets! She comes up with questions unrelated to lecture and when you ask questions about your work, she becomes snarky and wont help.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He is very knowledgeable about the subject and very caring!

Amr is amazing!! I had a lot of professors & TAs during my time at UCLA but he was my favorite. He was able to help me understand nebulous religious concepts of Ancient Egyptian religion with more clarity, as well as articulate my own ideas in papers. Unlike many other professors/TAs, Amr encouraged me to think outside the box. Very supportive!

Amr is so knowledgeable about the material and presents it in an incredibly engaging manner. He is very accessible for office hours, and he provided me excellent feedback on my papers. I could tell he cared about me and was invested in my growth as a student. Because of him, I found a love of ancient Egyptian history I didn't know I had!

Amr is amazing!! He was always available for office hours where we had insightful discussions about the lecture and discussion material. He provided feedback on my essays and had great suggestions. Amr does research directly in Egypt's catacombs so he's extremely knowledgeable about the material and has a sincere passion for it.

If you take ANNEA 15W, I would definitely recommend taking it with Amr! He was one of the most engaging TA's I had at UCLA, and he was always available to answer our questions and work with us on our final projects outside of class. His discussions also expanded well on the course material, and I could tell he had our best interests at heart.

Amr is one of my favorite TAs at UCLA! He is obviously very knowledgable about the topids discussed in the course, but he is never condescending and always helps his students understand. He is accessible outside of class and I love going to his office hours!
Professor Amr is amazing! Easily one of the best teachers I have ever had, and he is extremely knowledgeable about everything, not just Arabic (he is of course an amazing Arabic teacher as well).

Amr is one of the kindest professors that I have had. On top of this, he is competent, well-rounded, and is always engaging students. Amr really cares about each individual student, and customizes his lessons for each one. He only wants the success of his students and their mastery of the material
Amr made this class so much less stressful for me, especially during the current times. I found him to be understanding and supportive as well as very knowledgeable about his subject.

Amr is easily one of the best professors Ive had at UCLA. He is exceptionally caring and goes out of his way to provide our class with resources and extra help when its needed. With courses transitioning online for spring, Amr has effectively adapted & taught us well. I would love to take more advanced Arabic & ancient near East courses with Amr!

Amr is easily one of the kindest and most patient teachers Ive ever had, and possibly the best language teacher Ive ever had (linguist speaking!). He genuinely cares about his students interests and their success in the course. I would take a class with him again in a heartbeat!

Amr is incredibly knowledgeable about the language and able to communicate so effectively. He was always ready to give us logical answers for things we found difficult. His patience was unmatched & it was so apparent how much he cared for our wellbeing. I love the atmosphere he makes for the class; he is one of the best instructors I've ever had!!
He is amazing!!!
Most caring professor ever!

Lectures were disorganized and she was very rambled with some of the things she said.  Difficulty wasn't horrific, and on the plus side you have like 30 practice midterms from either her or Rubin (which she pulls a lot of quetions from) if you do your research (online).
Easy grader..lectures tidious.  Grading: Hw: 3....Tests:4
Worst lecture ever.  Easy grader.
Very clear, very organized. Take her if you value your education. Especially if your field is in the sciences. Drops the lowest scored test. Interesting lectures.

McWhorter is a young professor whose lack of skill shows in the way she manages her classroom. She has little enthusiasm for the subject being taught and her contempt for students is easily evident. She is more subjective in grading than a true scientist should be. Enrolling in this class puts you at her mercy which would not be a wise move.

It was McWhorter's second year teaching Anatomy and she did fairly well. You can usually tell the new teachers and McWhorter was no exception but I definitely see her developing into a pretty good lecturer.  She was also easy on the eyes which made the lecture go fast.  Test and practicals very fair and mainly taken from the powerpoint notes.

One of the best teachers I have ever had.  She gave insightful and interesting lectures. Very knowledgable, organized and helpful. Her problem solving based approach made the material more interesting and helped me know what material I needed to study harder. Take this class with her if you can.
The best professor I have had at UCLA. Many of us have a lot to thank her for.
Ugh. Professor needs to get over herself.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
Everything is on quizlet
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Anderson was awesome!  I took Accounting 1 with him at UCLA extension.  You will KNOW basic accounting if you take his class.  He gives great packets which he works through during class.  These are really helpful.  If you want to learn accounting, take this class.  He's very caring and very funny too.  Great teacher overall!

Anderson creates his own packet for students to work with. He works through problems all class, it is up to you to read the book and learn theories. He is very interactive and tests are easy, they are like the problems from class. I love working through problems and understanding how to do them. I'd rather do that then be lost during a lecture.
Anderson is great teacher. I have taken him several times and would take him again if I could. He has a way that makes you really understand accounting.

Very good teacher, very patient. Makes accounting seem like fun. At then end of this class, you will know basics of accounting, book keeoing and how to interpret Financial statements. No home work. Easy exams. Little repetitive, but thats how accounting is.

He teaches UCLA Extension accounting classes at all levels and he also teaches at Azusa Pacific University. He's got a twisted accounting sense of humor, but he also knows his stuff. Accounting isn't the most exciting thing in the world to do after a day of work, but he got to the point fast and we got out of class early 50% of the time. He always returned my emails and phone calls for help and he was very flexible. Exams were computer-based and handwritten take-homes, ok to work in groups, no homework.
Hands down, the best professor at UCLA. Brilliant; passionate; and really makes sure that her students understand everything. Brilliant.

Professor Mellor is a great teacher and an expert in her field. If you take good notes and keep up with the reading you will do fine. She really brought the Shelleys alive to me. So if you love Romantic literature and are an Austen/Keats fan then definitely take her!

Prof Mellor is a wonderful teacher. I loved her Late Romantic Literature class. She really brought the Shelleys and Keats alive and if you're an Austen fan, definitely take her. There is a ton of reading though but if you keep up-to-date you'll be fine.
Her lectures are very interesting but because she has A LOT to say, it is a little hard to keep up with notes. Otherwise, she is a great professor.
heavy reading load

Professor Hsu is a nice teacher, but the only thing I got out of his lectures was becoming more confused. I found that when I studied on my own and participated in the discussion sections I learned much more. His lectures have no structure to them and he often goes off-topic, as well as entertaining bad questions and acting like they are good.

Prof Hsu seems to be an incredibly intelligent man but the lecture position doesn't seem to be made for him. Although I found his accent to be rather interesting during week one, I quickly discovered that it made it extremely difficult to focus in class. Also, his handwriting is incredibly illegible and lectures are a bit bias.

Okay seriously. This guy is really really sweet and nice, but he is the worst professor I have ever taken. His lectures don't go anywhere, there is no train of thought and he lectures without a clear plan. He wastes too much time just talking to students about something thats completely off topic, poor syllabus, and poor structure to the class.

He's very verbose, which took some getting used to. Lectures are dense, but he tries to be as helpful as possible. Thankfully he offers more than one way of explaining most concepts, so if you still don't get it after lecture you need to try harder. He's very passionate about Philosophy, & soft spoken. Don't skip lecture! The recorder failed a lot.

Hsu was one of my favorite professors at UCLA. I feel his grading was overly harsh in his Wittgenstein class though. As a phil. major, I struggled to get over a B flat in his class even though he told me I was one of the top students in the class and wrote an excellent midterm

Professor Hsu is AWESOME! Philosophy 185 was probably one of the most difficult classes I've taken. YET, he was extremly helpful in unpacking the extremly difficult work of Wittgenstein's Philosophical Investigations.

He's a real nice professor but with that stated, he has been the worse philosophy professor I've taken. This is due to the fact that the subject is not the easiest and his way of lecturing is hard to follow. The problem is his monotone voice in combination with a overly thorough explanation Of EVERYTHING. I refuse to take another course with him

He is speaks as if he is writing. Means well and is a sweet guy. I would recommend turning on your recorder for his lectures cause, since he is very detailed oriented you may loose track of his syntax, a subject which he address in his Wittgenstein class as well.

The professor is pretty clear. He does excellent reviews of the content--reiterates content to make sure everyone understands it. I have no complaints whatsoever. I learned the content and did well in class but could have done better if it weren't for my lack of philosophy writing skills. But, he convinced me to major in Philosophy!

Most of the class slept during most of the lectures even the RAs would sleep. Hsu is a nice guy and means well. He is approachable and answers too many questions during class. The lecture started to get around 8 week. I was planning to minor in phil but this class changed my mind. Do not take this with him unless you really have to.

Alexx is the sweetest professor I have ever had. Alexx always makes time to help their students. I learned so much from them! I recommend Alexx for anyone because they makes the subject of film so fun and accessible.

By far the best TA I've ever had. Alexx made a large class feel small and comfortable. Very knowledgable about the films we studied. Alexx is passionate about film and truly wants her students to have fun. She wants you to succeed and provides feedback on your rough drafts. I wish I could take this class again!!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Alexx is an excellent TA. I cannot emphasize this enough! Very accommodating and gives feedback on rough drafts.
One of the best teachers I have ever had. Understanding, engaging, passionate, knowledgeable - I couldn't ask for a better learning experience.

Apter seems like a really nice guy and of course he's very intelligent...I just couldn't believe how horrible his class was. It was terrible. He never introduced a topic. He was the only one who understood what he was talking about. The readings were incredibly difficult- graduate level. Almost impossible to learn. He seems very nice though...

I took Professor Apter's class for my senior thesis, I had to write a 20-page paper at the end of the seminar. But the class was definitely one of the most unique and eye-opening course for me. I was afraid of speaking in class at first, but he is super friendly and helpful, so that helped me overcame the fear and really began to participate.

He is a very helpful teacher. The lectures are the most important and sometimes he talks fast, but if you type rather than write by hand it is very helpful. I recommend  him, the test are fair and mostly based on his lectures.
No Comments
His vocabulary is very advanced as is his passion for his work and for his students learning and teaching. I highly recommend the experience.
Lectures are hard to understand sometimes. It is important to do all of the reading. Makes it easier by telling you exactly what will be on the tests.
No Comments
Horrible professor
he's just awful.
Dush bag, thinks he walks on water. Puts students down , horrible lecturer and rude. Does not explain criminal procedure clearly, also spits when he talks. Class of 1996 UCLA LAW

Visiting professor at University of Washington School of Law. Not a good experience because the course materials were presented in such a disorganized manner ("The way I do it makes more sense", he arrogantly announced during class one day). I don't see what the other raters see in him, to be honest.
Arenella is kind of old and crazy, but a good instructor.  If he's in a bad mood, though, you should lay low unless you're sure of your answer
Including instructors in the LLM degree program (obtained later), I've never had a better instructor.  Brilliant, entertaining, organized, challenging, you name it.

students who dont like to be intellectually challenged really dislike him but if you have any genuine intellectual curiousity about the subject he teaches, he is incredible-charismatic, articulate, and able to offer competing perspectives on any subject-to describe him as hot makes sense only if you are into older guys with saltpepper hair
tough, demanding, but brillant
undeserved reputation as someone who enjoys to make students suffer, he is very demanding and requires students to think critically in class-something many students dont appreciate
not good

Awesome professor - very clear powerpoints that he's happy to post before class if you ask. I downloaded them and took notes directly on slides so everything was very clear. I loved his class! (I took Philosophy of Biology with him!)
Excellent Professor and really cares about his students. I would take another class from Josh Armstrong in a heartbeat! I encourage you to take one of his courses as you will not regret it.
Do yourself a favor and take a class from Armstrong.
Amazing professor. She makes the class fun and interesting and knows what she is talking about. Definitely take her if you can. Not an easy A, but if you study you will do well.
