Awesome teacher. She's young and really relates to the students well. Take her if you can. I would't call it an easy A because there are presentations to do and a long term paper, but she's definitely fun, informative, and super smart.

One of the best professors I've had at UCLA...It's too bad she is only a "visiting" professor and not going to be around full-time. Material can get a bit dull at times, but she makes it interesting. Take her class if you can. She truly cares about the students and wants you to learn something, not just write papers for a grade and then leave.
Prof. Little gives a great class, engages the students, demands participation and pushes the class to work out ideas.  Great class and I'm glad I could attend.

I've taken Professor Little twice and wished he'd teach more classes. He's absolutely full of knowledge and so incredible. He's very engaged with your learning and loves to lecture. He's very theatrical, you can't be bored in any of his classes. I learned so much form him. If you can take him, TAKE ALL OR ANY CLASSES HE TEACHES.
I wanted a college professor who shared knowledge, not one who gave over every class discussion to students who seemed to be quite opinionated, but didn't seem to know much.
He's brilliant! Take any class with him.

I don't think there was a single day I didn't laugh out loud in this class.  Made shakespeare fun.  He is also a LBGT studies professor I think and it showed a little in the class, but it's shakespeare...its jam packed with LBGT and sexuality.  Really open in office hours.  Helpful, plays favorites (but your TAs grade you anyway), fun.

I had Little for M101A and B and also for his lower division Shakespeare class and absolutely loved all of them. You HAVE to do the reading, but in the M101 classes, if you read exactly what you're supposed to and do well on the midterm and papers, you can get an automatic A without taking the final; I did it twice, so it's possible!
Great professor. Very accessible and helpful.
Absolutely the best professor of my undergrad experience.

The class is tough. Some people say that you must be LGBT to get an A...not so. In fact, many of the straight kids in the class got the top grades. I'm straight, I got a B, and it was well deserved. This is not an easy A class, and I don't regret taking it. Prof. Little is absolutely brilliant. You will look at literature in a whole new way.

Prof Little is hands-down one of the best professors I've ever had.  Lots of fun in class, very engaging as a lecturer, and really knowledgeable -- plus, super available outside of class if you have any questions!  If you love Shakespeare, you need to take his class. :)

thinking outside the box and reconsidering your most sacred assumptions isn't always a comfortable experience, but if you came to college to do just that, then take shakespeare with prof little. he's brilliant, engaging, hilarious, and devoted to his students. what more could you ask for??

Been in grad school for three years, and in all graduate and undergraduate courses I have taken, Arthur is the one professor that I felt taught me something I would not have otherwise figured out on my own.  He made me work my ass off, and he gave me all the support I needed in the process.  He is as brilliant as he is generous and humble.  Buy his book:  Shakespeare Jungle Fever.
No Comments
He's completely immersed in the subject matter and his lectures are usually amusing. Class discussions were usually the most interesting.

Professor Little is an awesome professor. Who knew taking Shakespeare cold be so interesting, englightening, and ultimately radical?  It speaks volumes to this mans intellect.  UCLA should be honored to have a scholar such as Professor Little

He is one of the best professors at UCLA.  I really had to work hard for my A and I actually enjoyed working for it.  He makes you really think outside of the box and he makes you want to do your best.

Shakespeare: the best course in Shakespeare ever. A brilliant man. Provides fascinating context that aids understanding. He's out-there. No cookie-cutter stuff. Tough exterior; but win his respect with enthusiam and hard work and he's a doll.

I have him in USC for calc3 and I love his enthusiasm for the subject and willingness to help.  Makes the class fun but his class is definitely too difficult. You must understand the concept ahead before attending the class or you'll get lost.

The hardest class I've ever taken, and his teaching style did not make it any easier. He goes crazy about the syntax of his sentences. His quizzes consist of just 1 question to be done in 20 minutes. Then, his tests have 4 questions, which are harder than the quiz, to be done in 50 minutes. Impossible. Makes you feel bad for not understanding.

He doesn't know what he is teaching at all. Just copy every word from the book on to the board. Unreachable. Do not want to communicate with students. Tests are super hard. He tests on things he does not teach.

He assigned a book he wrote, but it was totally useless, and never used, for the class.  The tests and assignments are vague and opaquely worded and no matter how you answer, you get points taken off.  All in all, a typical Political "Science" class where nothing useful for any kind of relations, political or not, is taught or learned.

Excellent Professor. If you are interested in international relations, I highly recommend this guy. Sarcastic, cynical, and funny. Im a psych major and took his class out of interest and now i am minoring in poli sci. It is a difficult class, but dude, this is UCLA.

Unfortunately I have to agree with the previous comment- Prof. Stein did not make this a fun class- he never answered questions (clearly), nor did he or his TAs answer e-mails, which, in my opinion, is a must.The subject matter is great, and the text was interesting, but I would suggest a different prof. if you are free to choose one.

What makes this class hard is that he and his TA's are disinterested, smug liars.  They will tell you that the tests are one thing, but on test day, you'll discover that the test is actually too long, too complex and not clearly worded.  They also refuse to be clear about what they expect on assignments and then, of course, deduct points.
She is very intelligent. And cares about her students.

She is hard, I will admit that. But she is very helpful and she wants you to learn. She is so intelligent. I have her in both PIL 270 and 566 Laws of war and terror. In 566 the reading is a lot, but the class gets better when you get in to it.

Worst class that I have taken in law school.  Professor Bali is an amazing woman, but has no clue how to teach.  I learned barely anything and law of war was my life for the semester.  She has unreasonable expectations, and taking this class was a horrible choice on my part.
No
He can't teach at all! Thick accent - boring.
Easy class, taught directly from book, he wasn't very helpful but didn't impede learning.

He's a pretty good teacher. His lectures are pretty good and his accent makes them fun. Getting a good TA is probably the 2nd most important thing. The most important thing is how hot Aschenbrenner is. He wears the tightest jeans and has the nicest ass. I go to lecture just to stare at him, while sitting in the front row. Aschenbrenner is a hottie!
Great teacher, awesome accent, tests are reasonable, and hws aren't too hard and help you study for the tests. Definitely try to get a god TA, it makes a big difference (Sherman is awesome).
love the accent. fair grading and homework is helpful. get a good TA, though because they're the ones who do most of the real teaching
Best of the Best!
Sophisticated; Very Attractive; Knowledgeable of the field; Articulate; Professional
Best Professor Ever; Very Personable; Knows Her Stuff; Highly Educated; Highly Gifted
Just a bit difficult, he cares for his students. He tries to make his his tests are fair as possible.
Does he speak Spanish? No.  The least of his problems.
great class.  lectures extremely interesting and well put together.  if you're looking for a class with fresh perspective and insightfull observations, take avila for anything.
No Comments

Avila is great! The best professor at UCLA. By Fall 06 I will have taken every single class he teaches. There is a reason he keeps students coming back for more. He knows his stuff, is engaging, and interesting. Avila's goal is to make his students critically think. His classes arent always the easiest but the knowledge you gain is life changing.
Very interesting course content.  Clear, and eager to hear what students have to say.
He thinks he's all that, but he's arrogant and rude and God help you, if you need to talk to him, he won't give you the time of day!
If you like history. You will love this class. He is amazing. He is hard to approach, but is lectures are fresh, dynamic, and enthralling.
The most arrogant and full of himself professor ever! Uncaring and dismissive. Awful

Dr. Basarudin is a great professor. I had never taken a women's studies course until this one and I had a great experience. Readings can be heavy and tough to read sometimes but she does a great job at explaining them for better understanding. She checks up on each of her students and seems to really care about each of us. I definitely recommend.

She's dope. I thought this class is all about feminist stuff but I guess not. It's more than just about feminism. It's about how gender is in every level of life. I like the contemporary topics she tackle and showed how gender works through these topics. Superb lectures, easily accessible outside of class. The TAs are amazing too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Basarudin's syllabus is current and interdisciplinary. I became a Gender Studies major because of her. She is an inspirational and compassionate teacher. She uses a lot of media and keeps the topics current while sticking to fundamental theories of gender. Super accessible on email and outside of class. She is young and hip!

Prof. Basarudin injured her leg but came to class in a wheelchair and had crutches for good measure so she can stand up. When UCLA classes had to be cancelled (fire), she finds an alternative day to schedule a make-up class and has extended office hours. If that is not dedication to knowledge and love for teaching/students, I don't know what is.

Ever heard of a Professor emailing students to check in on how they are doing in the course? Ever met a Professor who knows your name/remembers you in a class of 200 students? Me neither until I took a class with Professor Basarudin. She is all that and more. Want to be inspired and really learn? Take her. Mind blowing. I can't stop thinking.

Where do I start? She made me think of so many things. Made me re-evaluate my life and the lens from which I view social, cultural and political issue. Basarudin redefined simplistic understanding of feminism and gender studies. Many have said, take her class if you are ready to be enlightened. Work hard. She is worth all the work you put in.

Caution: Take Prof. Basarudin's class if you want an alternative way to understand the world and you don't want to be a generic person. After taking her class, you won't even be able to buy strawberries or underwear without thinking about the footprint and its political implication. I adore her. BEST teacher I ever had in my entire academic career

She will change your life. Her lectures are amazing, information and relevant to everyday life. She is smart, caring and overall awesome. Cares about student learning and will take the time to get to know you individually. Should be poster girl or UCLA and Gender Studies. Take her if you want to revolutionize your thinking.

Basarudin is such an inspiration. Shewill cultivate your interest in social justice even if you have none. She is that remarkable. Energetic, brilliant and compassionate, she is why higher education should not be an option. They don't make professors like her anymore. She is level-headed and talks to students. Actually cares about student learning

She is THE best professor I have ever had at UCLA. Inspirational, kind, smart, caring and hella funny. Her lectures are mind blowing and the way she teaches is sure to grab you attention like no other. Take her class if you want a life changing experience and a solid understanding of gender and social justice.
The queen of UCLA AND instagram. Professor Tox is the greatest as always
I loved taking her class she was always caring and shes the reason I passed she wasnt hard on us which made it easy and she made class easy

Professor Tox kept it real when nobody else wouldn't... She saved the community and I believe she is the best psychology teacher on the planet... She cussed out a few students but she is THE BEST teacher in the world
Awesome
Amazing
"Some of y'all about to be real mad at me, but it must be said." lol
love her! very sweet and really engaged with her students. it's a little odd to have her with her social media presence but she is just so impressive as an individual.

Dr. Sanjhra Banks is a real gem in the classroom. She never allows us to leave class without laughing and learning, and her dedication to the course really shows. Her office hours are very accessible and she responds to emails quickly.
She is very inspirational would definitely take the class afain.

One of the best professors I have ever had! Really fostered my interest in psychology; her lectures are always intriguing and engaging. Not to mention she is hilarious! She is a fair grader and always available to give help & general life advice outside of class.

Professor Tox was super fun and keeps her lectures interesting. She brings in treats every once in a while and has an fresh perspective on every topic she lectures on. Her class is difficult but if you work for it she is a very fair grader. She is well respected but is also very relatable and accessible.

I really hope I never have to encounter a professor like her again. She is the worst. I have never had a professor so out of touch. Most of the class has been doing bad and she tells us to try harder. She really needs to take a step back and self reflect. She has me contemplating my time at UCLA. My depression came back because of her. Thanks Daria

She is not a sociology professor, beware! Shes a linguistics proffessor and you can tell! In this class you will not learn anything might as well not go and teach yourself. When you ask for help she will be very vague and not give you an answer. She doesn't care if you aren't doing well either. Study guides are NOTHING like exams. Tests are HARD.

DO NOT TAKE HER. She does not care about her students the slightest. She is the worst professor I have ever encountered at UCLA and is such a harsh grader. Not only can she not teach but she is not even a sociology teacher. She acts nice but does not care if you fail. When you ask for help on a question she answers everything but the question awful
Dr. BBJ is an amazing Prof. It's not an easy class, but you will learn a lot. She has energy for daysssss and is extremely passionate about her lectures. I would take her again for sure!

Can be  rude and jaded. She often yells at students, forgetting we are all adults.  I got a B+ in her class  but it wasn't easy. She weeds a lot of people out. An A is possible with lots of hard work and study time. I found her to be very unapproachable. My advice, take the class, study study, pass the exams and move on with your life. UcanDoIt

She does not respect your privacy as answer scantron, paper,office door is open for others to hear:so you can not address your specific needs. Talks incessantly about her extracurricular activities-- gives more time to that then her students.

Even a fully online class, this the hardest Professors, EVER! Exams are fully proctored with Webcam and Microphone! If your eyeballs move to the side your exam will close and fail you. NO excuse email will help you! First thing he will say your CHEATING!! The worst Chem ever, and he makes the material even harder ! Avoid at all costs! Super HARD!

Avoid at all cost! Taking his Chem Lab at ELAC rn. Super tough grader! All lab reports worth 80 points and the class average are always only around 40 points. The lab report guideline are extremely confusing! Even enough I did what he asked, I still got average points.

The lab instructor went out of his way to say that Bacher is a great chemist with impeccable lab technique, but strangely had little to say as to whether Bacher was good in the classroom. He's absolutely terrible. Being a good chemist does not make one a good chemistry teacher, and Bacher is simply not made to teach. I dropped the course.

Cant understand accent. He's the chem professor at csub. Hardly does examples he just reads off slides that he already posts on blackboard. Intelligent man but not a good teacher. Not everyone is made to teach

If you're a biochem major prepare to SUFFER. The hours in lab among toxic chemicals are hard. Getting product with good purity is even harder. That said, this class will teach you a lot and if you're going into any sort of research later on, the skills you learn here will be useful well beyond what you can comprehend as an undergraduate. Good luck!
The worst professor at UCLA, fails at least a quarter of the class.  Didn't learn a thing about chem, and yet it is still a required class for the major.  Useless and rediculous.

take it at some other JC he wants to help but these classes are graded by the TAs mostly and he comes in at the end. Your life would be a living hell for the entire session and you still find yourself incapable of getting a decent grade. He is the worst O chem teacher yet.
The worst professor I have ever had.  Fails about 30% of the class.  Very confusing.  Class would be fine if there was no final.  Beware!
No Comments

Alfred Bacher was a tremendous help and motivator for me.   He is challenging, but extremely knowledgeable and is very concerned with student's success, more than most teachers I've had.   I may not be taking the MCAT's if it wasn't for his way of "pushing me" further!!
No Comments

master's program here, took his 215A class. The best professor I have ever had, his teaching is the best, his explained it so well, take his class was the best choice ever. He curves a lot at the end, so don't worry. If you keep your grade in midstream, even lower that mid, you will get at least B-.
Goat
God of microelectronics. You MUST take his class if u majored in EE
Simply, his way of teachingexplanation is too good to be true !
Best prof in the UCLA EE dept.  The guy is amazing.  Has great insight, can explain things clearly, and is very helpful.
No Comments
No Comments

This class is HARD for a 1000 level. SO much reading and lectures are boring. He does care about the topic but doesn't understand this class is 1000 level. Midterm and Final are ID/essays. Tough grader! Takes FOREVER for grades to come back. 8-10 page research paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: His first year teaching at SMU. The class was super unorganized/ chaotic, we had two quizzes before the mid term and one paper due all semester. He also is super boring, but does really seem to care about students/ what he is teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Berenji is great at breaking down overarching theories in understandable terms. He's very relatable, tying classic concepts to today's tv shows and ensuring that students have time for Q&A. Very approachable, offered test study guides that were clear and helpful, wonderfully marked readings that were "optional" as such.

I took Bjork's Psych 100B class back in 2015. Mileage will vary in terms of final grades but it seemed like a pretty stressful class for all. I was pretty mad about my grades at the time, but looking back, Bjork was one of the more caring and good hearted professors, both on and off campus. You can do it as long as you put in the time and effort.

Despite getting an A, she's one of the worst tchrs at UCLA.Her lectures are extremely boring, her tests are unfair.  I got an A is bec I had to take this class twice. My GPA- 3.8. What do you expect,the professor teaches one thing and then tests you about another, duh,how am I supposed to know, worst teacher and her ta's suck too, boring and hard.

I'm beginning to think a lot of the students on this website, do not put in the work that is required for an A. UCLA is a top ranked school - it's not supposed to be easy. However, it is mostly fair. That being said, Professor Bjork was extremely clear and caring. Psych 100B is a challenging class but you learn a lot and it can be fun!
She has no idea about what's going on in class.  Her lectures are boring. Her master TA's are the worst on the planet and her regular TA's don't even know the material.

Wow. That is what I have to say. She was one of the worse professors I have ever had. She was not clear in her lectures and, sadly enough, I know more about the birthday cards her sons had sent her than the actual research methods needed to do proper experiments. My advice to you reading this, take Geiselman. He was a great professor.

Like the person said below, she doesn't matter all that much. It's more dependent on your TA. Make sure to understand e/t she lectures about, which is pretty clear and gone over again in section. And work really hard on your paper. There really isn't anyone else to take for this, so take her. I doubt anyone else would make this class any better.

She was no help at all.  I found her to be one of the worst professors I have ever had, biased opinion or not.  Even the TAs were no help.  She was not clear on the material, she would stray from her lectures and when she presented material, it was only half finished!
Material on the exams is very tricky.
Average teacher. TA matters more for this course. Exams were pretty clear.
She got confused during her own lectures.  The exams are tricky!  You need a good TA to help you through, and also there is a long report you have to write.
Very engaging and knows exactly what he is talking about!!

This was one of the most challenging classes that I've taken at UCLA, but also one of the classes where I've learned the most. Professor Madley really takes the time to go over essays and gives amazing feedback. He surely is one of the best professors on campus.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: He's incredible!
He is a remarkable professor and very ready to help. Very engaged in the subject youll enjoy! Alot of reading and work but do-able!!

Dr. Madley is a true inspiration.  As a professor, he is engaging, entertaining, and very informative.  Even though he assigns a substantial amount of reading, it is all relatively fascinating.  His class is not easy whatsoever, but hard work does not go unnoticed and is rewarded generously.  One of the best professors at UCLA, no doubt.

Really amazing professor. Makes the subject interesting for someone who might not be. Extremely well done lectures and well thought out. This professor is brilliant. Great study guide for the final (50%) and he allows you to choose 2 of 3 essay options to write 2 5 page papers about (25% each). Would definitely recommend him.

Awesome professor, one of the best at UCLA. He really helps students write well thought concise essays. He is very passionate something that not a lot of UCLA professors are. His lectures are well planned and engaging. Whoever takes this class will gain an immense knowledge of American Indian history. He also gives a study guide for the final!

Awesome professor, one of the best at UCLA. He really helps students write well thought concise essays. He is very passionate something that not a lot of UCLA professors are. His lectures are well planned and engaging. Whoever takes this class will gain an immense knowledge of American Indian history.  He's also quite handsome; I'm jealous.

Professor Madley is passionate about what he teaches which makes class all the more interesting and fun. The assignments are crystal clear so that you always know what you need to do to get a good grade. If you have any questions don't hesitate to ask him! He is very approachable and ready to help you succeed in this class.

Professor Madley is incredibly sweet and knowledgeable. I'm so thankful I took his class. He's a fair grader, his lectures are always interesting & accompanied with pictures, & he is eager to help anyone who needs it. I hope to take as many of his classes as I can! Even if you aren't that interested in the subject field, he will make it fun!

Doesn't care if he messes over a student's future, even when true effort was given for the entirety of his class. It would be overall better for everyone if he just advised his graduate students to drop instead of failing them, but no thought is given towards his students. Thanks Biskup.

After spending the entire quarter doing all of the homework, which there was simply way too much of, attending lecture, and studying for the final, I was given a C in the course. What this means for a graduate student (myself) is that not only did I waste my quarter TRYING to pass and NOT getting credit, but now I am a class behind w a lower GPA.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: He's an engaging lecturer and provides good feedback, but his class is incredibly difficult. The quarter I had him I ended up spending more time on his homework than on that of all my other classes combined, and my grades in general suffered. I barely even managed a decent mark in his class. Honestly regret not dropping.

The homework is very challenging, and all the problems are special examples, but if you take the time to understand the problems then you will learn a lot.  If you can understand why behind the problems you will do very well, but be prepared to work to get to this point. I highly recommend taking this class if you plan on using the material later.

You practically need a PhD in math to pass his class. The homework is near impossible and requires you to go to office hours where you can't even take notes anyway. The TA's even had trouble solving some of the homework. About 2/3 of the class dropped before the final. I passed AP Calc BC and still failed this class, even though its the same stuff.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: No Comments

class was super hard. but he really wanted you to learn. hw counts for a lot, so do it. his office hours are helpful too. all in all, if you want a better understanding of the subject, take his class. but he prepared to work your ass off

lots of work, but the class was extremely well taught.  professor biskup is a terrific professor who expects alot from his students although at the same time he spends alot of time trying to help students really learn and understand the material.

Grading criteria for WRIT courses are tough, but this is a department-wide characteristic. Professor Black is arguably one of the best professors at SC. She is truly knowledgable and cares for her students. Highly recommend!

Honestly, can't believe I enjoyed coming to Writ 340 everyday at USC. Dr. Black made it fun to come to class. She grades kind of hard but you get what you put into it. She cares about your school work and also life outside of school. The GOAT.

Professor Black is one of, if not the best teacher I have had in my schooling experience. She insanely smart and well-read but is still a young, cool gal who works to make her class engaging and fun. She will be available to meet with you almost whenever and gives super helpful input during the writing process. You will be happy you took her!!
She is a great teacher to meet with one on one. she is very kind and helpful, but do not expect to get As on papers consistantly
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: She is so kind and caring! She gives amazing feedback and your grade will get better as time goes on.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: I loved Dr. Black so much. She sincerely cares about her students and what they are taking away from the class. Although I wasn't thrilled with my grade, I learned a lot from her and would definitely take her again. If you actually want to LEARN how to be a better writer, take her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Dr. Black is amazing. She really cares for the students and is always willing to help. I would say she is the most caring professor I met at USC. She is a tough grader, but writing 150 is a course to get an A from. There are frequent 1:1 conferences. 4 papers due with 2 portfolios regarding last paper. Extra credit offered for attending events.

Dr. Black is far and away the best professor I've ever had without a doubt. She has a phenomenal understanding of every subject. Her feedback on papers and her care for each of her students is unrivaled. She is so incredibly kind, and sweet. Writing was never my strong suit, but Dr. Black always made me feel accomplished! Lots of work, but worth it
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Really awesome and you'll get the grade you work for

Dr. Black was awesome. Highly recommend for Writing 150 classes, considering how some other professors I've heard are very strict on certain things like participation and stuff. She can grade a bit tough, but if you put in the work, you should be fine. Class was pretty fun and she was pretty funny, cool, and nice.

The best professor ever! You will be cheating yourself if you don't take her class. I was so sad when this class ended. She is so helpful. The work isn't easy, but if you go to lecture (which you'll want to because she's great) then you'll know everything she wants from you. I wish I could have taken more classes with her before I graduated.
Does a good job explaining things. Do the work and get the A. Its very straightforward and very interesting.
Really smart, has a good speaking voice.

Blank is good but the wife...prof: Atherton (sp?) who also teaches classics, is horrible! I had the sincere displeasure of having her as an instructor for the 2nd part of Plato. Absolutely horrifying experience. On the plus side, she's a Brit; awsome accent!
Take him...do the work and its an easy A
demanding but really smart and really clear.
Nasty.

She's very anal. She practically delegates all the work to the students so prepare to present on the reading material which is more than enough. She's not very receptive to others opinions and she'll also tell you to be creative with your final and at the end become very pushy with what she believes is the best way to present it.

I don't recommend this class if you are not Chicano/a because all materials are about Mexican American, and some readings are included Spanish, so if you don't understand Spanish, it's hard to understand. Also, if you don't have background, you have to read all of readings. I liked this course, but it was hard...

Very difficult and rude. She thinks she knows everything. Despite that the class is on understanding oppression and social movements, I find it hard to believe that she actually listens to grassroots people. She gets fidgety and attacks anyone who disagrees with her. take the class if you dare. just read a book on the syllabus topics instead

she's ok but her lectures are based on stuff you can easily find on the internet.  would suggest she bring in guest speakers who know something more about the movies and tv she talks about?   she only has an MA but is teaching a course professors usually teach.  tests easy.

Class readings, movies.  Clementine is helpful but she doesn't  really know the subject too well.  she has strong opinions on Indian images but doesn't  know what  they mean.  she should give a context about these images and some of the history so we can understand them in their own time period and not just today.  Really, we've come a long way.

She seems to have little teaching experience.  Her knowledge of film and media is limited to her opinions, which anyone can give.  She has no historical context and she doesn't understand how movies work visually or the filmmakers behind them . i wish this class were offered through Film and Theater instead.

this teacher is amazing and so incredibly smart. I admit he goes on tangents during lectures and it can be confusing but he really knows what he's talking about and is willing to help. there's only a midterm and final and he also provides study guides for them. so if you do those, your certain to at least get a C. take him!! :)

Professor Boyd is awesome! The lectures are all powerpoint and most of them he posts are online. Also, all of the lectures are on bruincasat so it's way easier to study or if you miss a day. Due to budget cuts, all exams are multiple choice and have a really good curve.

great professor. wrote the textbook so lectures pretty much follow the book. he's super nice and doesn't care if you show up or prefer to stay home and watch lectures on bruincast. due to budget cuts, he had to change the format of the class: one midterm and one final (both scantrons).

Easy A. Not demanding and and only midterm and final in class multiple choice q & a. It was 30 questions and he gives you the Study Guide before. If you go to lecture, complete SG, your an idiot if you can't get at least an A-. The curve in this class rocks!!!!
I really liked that his lecture was available as a podcast and bruincast. The lectures were kind of dry so it allowed me to listen to them in the car and whatnot.
No Comments

I took his class in 1998 and I can honestly say he was one of the most memorable profs I ever had. I also know a hell of a lot more about retroviruses and such--the knowledge has definitely been retained. That's a good prof.
He was one of the most amazing biology teachers I have ever had. I loooved him
No Comments
One of my more interesting profs at UCLA...definitely an experience - take at least one of his classes.  Never fell asleep in them ^_^.
Horrendous.  TA's are awful and grade poorly.  Waste of time. I could have done an independent study.
The scientist from South Park is based on him!
the easiest and funnest class i've takin my whole UCLA career...every lecture we brought people who wasn't in the class just because Prof. Bohman was the****!!!!
No Comments
great teacher. great class.  highly recommend this guy.
Probably the best professor I ever had at UCLA.   Took as many classes from his as possible.....and learned a TON.    He has a unique teaching style that WORKS.
he's hilarious.  enjoyed his class thoroughly.
hilarious, lectures with his eyes closed
possibly the greatest teacher i have ever had. Thanks for everything - arash hakhamian
an excellent lecturer, and very helpful during office hours; takes a bit of time to understand his rantings and ravings, though
asfd
He is still wearing those Hawaiin shirts?  Pretty cool prof., but didn't really teach much.
cool dude, K.I.T.
No Comments
No Comments
at least he stays off your back
Unbelievably cruel to students.

She is a no nonsense, intelligent woman.  I appreciate that all her assignments were specific and straight forward.  If you do the work and show up for class you will do well.  There are not a lot of assignment, but they are a bit involved.
If you want up to date, current information on user interface, searching, and automation you could find no finer professor. I found the classes and work totally fascinating.

The worst professor I have ever had. Her classroom environment is stifling and it is hard to find one’s voice. She obviously plays favorites among her students. She’s rude and lacks social mores and cues. She sticks to a rigid class schedule, even if you have questions about the course materials. She has a way of belittling people and a huge ego.
No Comments
No Comments
Adored this professor! Caring, helpful, and makes class interesting; knowledgable in the field and often spoke from experience with poets/authors/artists. A LOT of reading, but all enjoyable.

OMG I'm dying in this summer cover 19 era. He expects us to write so many papers and the reading is exorbitant. I wish so badly somebody would have written a review letting me know these things. It's so unrealistic, the workload. He's super cool though and the articles, books and movies required are stellar.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Stefans is one of the kindest,interesting professors I've ever come across. His methods for teaching are relaxed and he encourages participation from everyone. Lets the class build off of what other students contribute rather than just focusing on lecture. Books are the most unusual but really intriguing. Lots of film analyses with lit. Easygoing

Took his Visual Lit class because it sounded interesting. Total waste of time (and money for all the books he asks us to read). Very disorganized, and outlines what is expected of you very poorly. Each lecture he expects you to be an expert on subjects he doesn't bother to explain. Only one paper and no midterm or final, but still can't recommend

What a complete and total waste of time. Stefans never explains anything, adds a ton of reading to what's already assigned, shows up late, and doesn't say anything about his grading policies. I'm not afraid to work hard, but after  week two, I was over this guy;  this class is a major outlet for his pretentious, hipster poetry. Worst class taken.

Without a doubt, the worst professor I've come across so far at UCLA. His grading is based on 3 papers and 1 exam, which is fine for an English class. The professor is funny during lecture, but it doesn't matter because it won't help you understand his assignment. The majority of my classmates agreed that we don't know what he wants, TA's sucked 2.
I found the subject matter for this class painfully boring, but he was an entertaining lecturer. I felt his tests were more than fair.
Clear and often funny, Bristow is very easy to listen to and his lectures are fairly well-organized

Not a great professor.  At first I enjoyed the class, but then after the midterm he yelled at me for the way I was writing, gave no constructive criticism, and then told me to get out of his office.  And that was for getting a C on the exam.  Dont screw up in his class or else you will get chewed out.  I enjoyed the class till that point!
The really enjoyed his class. His accent and British humor enhanced the novels that we read.

He lectures very well, his exams were very difficult, we read some really good, and bad books, he is very serious at times, and doesnt like distractions and if you sit in the front make sure you have your books! He is very organized and knowledgable and sometimes funny....I liked his Brittish accent!

I have heard great things about this professor before, unfortunately I think I caught him on a bad quarter. He is obviously very knowlegeable about the subject matter, but tends to come off rather haughty and reads straight from a script (which I'm fairly sure is a good summation of his published book on Victorian poetry)
Prof is very interesting. Material equally interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: A great professor! He explains things very clearly and with great knowledge. He is always free to meet and discuss.

Great Professor. Very approachable and is willing to answer any of your questions. However, lots of reading and study guides are very lengthy but do them as they will save you! Attend the review sessions as it will help you with confusing topics. But dont get lazy because a lax attitude in this class can quickly turn to a D or F.
It was an interesting class. It may seem like a lot of concepts to grasp, but if you keep up with the readings and go to the review sessions, you should be fine.
not a good teacher at all will give you an f even if you try an come to class and to much info in so little time so you are warned bruiners seems nice but doesnt care the TA are a pain

great educator, and immense knowledge of his field.  lectures are at times surprising, or funny, and always interesting.  Prof. Bradbury has a very personal, open, friendly, and clear style that makes the material easier to learn.

Try to attend lectures! Book gets VERY confusing but he breaks down the topics and makes it easy to understand. Take good notes during lecture so you can use it when you're doing the homework. I wish he taught MGMT 124B. I would take his class again
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Tran is really great at lectures. Accounting is not my favorite subject but he make it enjoyable. He connects his real world business experiences to the lectures and makes it interesting for the class. Lots of material to take in but manageable. He also wrote me a letter of recommendation that helped me give a scholarship!

ALWAYS ATTEND LECTURE! Prof Tran's lectures are amazing. The material in this course is difficult to understand, but Prof Tran breaks it down and relates it to real life. He gives the best acronyms! Class has one final exam but he tells you what to study. Hw isn't bad, usually consists of 2-4 problems. No projects. Will take him again!
Yes, I must say that because I had a good standing and experienced in her class.

So, she is now teaching English at Durham Tech. She came in after our first teacher was fired halfway through the semester. It was a hard transition cause the past teacher was not following curriculum at all. Yet, she still managed to get us up to schedule without overwhelming us. She is open and caring and made the whole class so enjoyable

she is really hard to understand because her levels of sophisticated vocabs, she speaks really fast too. The reading is hard to comprehend and not very helpful for students to form a thesis. She is not open to interpretations of literatures too.

Prof. Burge taught some of the most difficult classes I took at UCLA. I took him a second time because the difficulty was a challenge, not an unnecessary obstacle. He respects his students and encourages growth. If you take his class trivially, it will be horrible. If you actually want to grow, then you will be rewarded beyond your expectations.
Best Professor in Philosophy department at UCLA, words cannot describe how brilliant he is
He is detailed oriented and a clear lecturer. Every word is efficiently used and examples are from and outside of the text book. If you like Kant and epistemology, take him. It was a privilege.

CRAZY BRILLIANT! Tyler is truly unique in that his teaching style and his personality make him a  perfect professor. Hands down best professor in UCLA's phil dept. Super fun in lectures and super helpful in OH. DO NOT LEAVE UCLA W/O a CLASS BY BURGE
This guy is obviously brilliant, sometimes too brilliant and it gets a little confusing. But he is really helpful with study sessions, and really knows his philosophy.
No Comments

This guy is brilliant - absolutely brilliant.  One of the brightest and most thorough minds in Kantian philosophy.  Made Everything much clearer than I've seen before - though the material is still (obviously) difficult.

Enrolled course three days after session begun, I asked Prof. Cameron the deadline for assignments I missed via email, but never getting a single word back. A week later, he treats my catch-up assignments as 'regular' late work, by giving them all half credit no matter I turned them in ASAP. Where is all my effort putting in? What a waste of time!
Boring unenthusiastic lectures. Could almost ruin an interesting subject

Good professor, I liked the way he lectured, always very clear and organized. The class material itself was not incredibly interesting to me personally but it was taught well. Parts of it were pretty interesting and enjoyable but it could get really dull at times. Section attendance is mandatory, lecture is not. Overall pretty easy.
No Comments
No Comments

I didn't know anything about historical archaeology before this class, but Monroe totally taught us about how important a field it is.  Great lecturer.  Tests are not too hard, he gives the questions out in advance.  Be prepared for a lot of reading.

Stay away! I dropped week 6. Carson makes no effort to teach to an undergraduate, lower-div level. He has clear communication, but grades all assignments harshly. Each homework (one/week) took me HOURS to complete. He is frequently late/absent for office hours and doesn't care if anyone succeeds. I will retake with a prof who supports my success.

Professor Schutze is one of the best professors I have ever had at UCLA.  If you study everything carefully on the slides and treat the homework sheets seriously, you will likely end up doing pretty well. And when you have things that you really don't understand, either email him or go to his office hours. He is always willing to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lecture slides and homework are very well organized

This was the most frustrating class I have ever taken. Its meant to be an intro to linguistics and yet he spoke to us as if we had been studying the material for the last four years. Even students who had previously taken this class at the community college level struggled to understand. He made everything WAY more complex than it needed to be.
not helpful, difficult exams and hard to get a good grade
He is extremely hard on his students. He is not generous with his curving at all.
i completely disagree- carson was wonderful, very willing to answer questions and help during office hours.
Horrible and boring. Had him for Syntax and thought this class, Language Processing,  would be better since this his field, but I was so wrong! The class was SO HARD and POINTLESS. Avoid if possible.

John is incredible. He made philosophers whom I had no appreciation for beforehand among my favorites, he is open to help during personal trials, he is a great professor and an exemplary person. If you are serious about furthering your philosophical education, then you will regret having not taken him.

He's a good professor and extremely nice to the point were people take advantage. He Does have a monotone voice but he does record his lectures. Yet he has a way of explaining difficult information in a more simple manner. Attendance is a must. And he does expect students to have a high commitment to the course but it isn't overbearing.

Professor Carriero is tough. He has strong material knowledge, but he doesn't always convey it in a clear manner. I found him to be more helpful in one on one office hours. He discourages class discussion or questions during lecture and I found that disheartening. All in all I'd take him again because of his keen knowledge of philosophy.

He's amazing at getting difficult comcepts across very clearly. He's helpful, but he will not do your work for you, and demands ahrd work from his students. Do not expect to sakte in his classes! FYI: his nickname is John Aristotle, if that gives you any clue!
No Comments

Calvin is among my favorite professors. I had him for multiple classes and he was one of the reasons why I transferred to UCLA instead of Berkeley. He finds the profundity in "dumb" questions, he rarely offers his own philosophical sympathies in (what I take to be) an attempt to give credibility to opposing views. Character limits prevent full grat
Totally brilliant and really interesting to listen to. The prompts for the essays were straightforward and I would absolutely take him again.

Normore is a competent professor. However, the class I took with him, Philosophy of Mind, was not engaging. I must mention that Normore answered any questions I had and was very patient with others. I do not recommend the class, but perhaps other classes with this professor would be more interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I loved prof. Noremore BUT his grading was excessive in relation to how unclear his lectures are and in relation to his lack of OH + email communication. I loved his lectures but they did not link up with the assignments at all. Id love to take him NOT for credit but if you are serious about having a high gpa, avoid him.

Professor Normore's class was one of the most frustrating and confusing classes I've ever taken. By week 3 more than half the class didn't come to lecture because you gain more by not being there than by listening to his scattered thoughts. He says the same things each lecture and his slides are either just a title or just a block of text.

A truly amazing Professor. He knows what he's talking about and he really knows how to relate it to his students. He's very approachable and fun. I wish he taught more philosophy courses. He does go off on tangents but it is not detrimental to the overall understanding of the materials he records his lectures so you're not required to attend them

Outstanding professor. I took Phil 100B & Phil 154B with him. Extremely knowledgeable about the topics in his courses. Provides his own, creative examples. Doesn't read off of boring PowerPoint slide shows. Able to keep class interested & doesn't bore the class with monotony. Incorporates good humor into his examples. Highly recommend him!!
Excellent energy. A bit disorganized but a great communicator. He is passionate about medieval philosophy and it gets passed on. Learned a lot about how similar Descartes and Ibn Sina are.
Amazing.  Can give a comprehensive history of philosophy in just one lecture.

Everyone knows Calvin is an amazing philosopher. But as a professor, he sucks. His lectures are ahrd to follow, and he is the most disoprganized man you've ever seen. He didnt answer emails, would never have his lectures ready, went off on tangents, and is not good at lectureing. If you need help, go to office hours-- hes better in that setting.

I had him for a metaphysics class and then took an Independent Study class with him at O.S.U.--of the professors I met there, he seemed to me to be the brightest, most curious and most humble of the pack.

Prof. Normore is a true genuis. he needs no notes to lecture and has a true love of Philosophy. Atypical of the professors in this major he is completely approachable and is not condesending at all. He loves students and embraces their questions. A WONDERFUL CLASS AND WONDERFUL PROFESSOR!!
Brilliant professor. A true genius! Taking his class is an experience that every philosopher should have at least once.

He is about the Best Professor any one could have. He is above all, dedicated to his students. He is not easy, but more than willing to give as much help as you need. He has integrity and he is hands down brilliant! A very fair grader. Which is important because many of the TA's are problematic, some are good.
Seriously, just take a class with him. He's awesome. Actually I want to be his friend. He looks like a magical creature.
awesome prof!
One of those professors that reminds you why you're studying Philosophy.  He's a Philosophy god.
No Comments
Since its ancient philosophy, probably harder to teach, and Normores idiosyncrasies make it harder to understand. Extremely knowlegeable about subject matter and doesn't need notes to lecture!
You missed out if you don't take at least one class with him.
Normore is the best thing that can happen to any student in UCLA!!!

best professor in the department.  brillant man, the classes i took from him were the best in my college career. very patient, takes time to make sure you understand and entertains your thoughts as well.
AMAZING professor. One of the most learned men on the planet.
Best professor I had in college.  Brings cookies and juices to the final exam as well.  Take him and him only for 100B.  No one better to teach Aquinas than him!
Good teacher. Can be advanced & hard to understand sometimes. Has study sessions
Really caring, a gentleman, and rather good looking. I received a good amount of extra credit and he is always open to answering questions.
Super funny professor. Goes into great detail about the novels being discussed. Wasn't my cup of tea but if closely analyzing novels is what you're interested in, it's a great class.

He is a solid professor, a bit weird, but okay. He is very helpful if you go to office hours and really wants to speak with his students. In terms of grading/workload, make sure you have a good TA, like I did, or else this class can become a true pain.

Not an affective teacher: there was no lecture or proper materials for us. He was goofy and fun and class was a blast but he didn't help with the reading material or come prepared to class. If you're used to "normal" professors, don't take him. You'll be  having a good time but HE IS A HARD GRADER; you will suffer at the end with the final essay.

I took two of Professor Mott's classes, and was thoroughly engaged. I am an AP English Language and Literature teacher now and use so many of his strategies still. How great of a professor is he? He had a full house the week of the Northridge earthquake and we didn't skip a beat during an aftershock. Okay maybe I screamed, but we kept at it still!

I used to hate american novels before i took this class, but now i love them! Mott is hilarious, so his lecture are interesting, but he can sometimes be hard to understand because he doesn't really tell you what he's explaining. but that's ok because you don't REALLY have to go to lecture to do well in the class. just read and know all the novels!
I love him!
No Comments

Just a great, great guy. Made lectures a ton of fun. Never fully clear about what he wants from the papers, and a difficult class to get an A in. However, I would still recommend this class highly as a GE that I enjoyed.

American Literature and Seminar: the Politics of ****graphy.  Plus 199's. He is fabulous: innovative, edgy, great sense of humor. Not an easy A but you learn and are inspired. He guides with a light touch. Some wanted more specific guidance. I didn't.

Pros: Smeenk is passionate and knowledgeable about the content and explains it well. Interesting and well-structured course content. Tested on understanding

Cons: Lack of timely communication and feedback was frustrating given how tough the course content is. Large time commitment. Unclear grading criteria at times (marking was fair though)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very nice, very responsive professor. However, won't recommend the course philosophy 2032 to anyone. Too much reading, so many dues, HUGE workloads, difficult exams

Great professor! I took his class at western university. It was a difficult course, but an amazing learning experience. Professor Smeenk was always available via email and even met with me to discuss material questions I had. It is clear that he really cares about his students.
This professor deeply cares about his students. He always is available to students and stays every class to help any student. He is a very caring teacher would recommend.
Great professor

Took his intro to stats class through UCLA Extension. His lectures are very engaging and helpful and clearly explain topics covered in the textbook. It's very clear that he's passionate about statistics and teaching. I really enjoyed his class and would highly recommend!

Yeooo Professor Chen is the best. So organized, so clear, so concise. Every week there will be an in-class quiz, textbook readings, and assignments, but the assignments will be good practice problems for the following week's quiz. You can tell he really loves teaching too. AND he tries really hard to be politically correct, it's so sweet.

I was nervous since I hadn't taken math in ages, this class is designed like a map to guide you through learning the content from start to finish. It's meticulously organized, lectures are recorded for you to review, practice problems solidify the concepts and help you succeed on the tests. I wish all professors were this good!

Really cool guy and is willing to help all his students understand statistics. Im not a math person and was nervous about taking this class but he really helps you understand the material in a smooth process to learn. He gives us everything we need to learn the material and pass the class, everythings online and can make cheat sheet for the tests

Miles is extremely interested in the success of his students. I took this class as my last GE and he certainly made it worth waking up for the  9am class. The Quizzes  are super easy and made up the same amount of the grade as the midterms. It takes the pressure off of the bigger tests. If he keeps the same setup you'll easily get an A.

Miles is a caring professor, who explains the material carefully and efficiently. He provides extra credit, and the quizzes and labs aren't difficult if you go to his lectures. He is accessible if you need help with either the class or if you have any concerns going on in your life. Simply put, Miles is awesome.

Professor Chen is such an amazing professor who clearly cares about student learning and does everything he can to help students get a good grade in his class. Exams, quizzes, and labs are all very fair, and he provides practice quizzes which definitely help a lot. His amazing personality made the class even more enjoyable!

One of the best professors that I have ever taken at UCLA. He is so kind and caring. He made the class so interesting. I actually understood what was being done in class despite hating math! He might have even inspired me to attempt the Statistics minor. I know most majors require Stats 10 as a pre-requisite and I would highly recommend Chen.

Professor Chen is one the best professors I've ever had. He really cares about his students and is very helpful. His exams and quizzes are very fair and if you study you can do well in the class. He is a really good lecturer and covers the material in a way that makes statistics seem easy! I would definitely recommend taking his classes.

Professor Chen is the best professor I have had at UCLA so far! I have never taken a stats class before and I thought I was going to hate it, but he makes the class understandable and enjoyable. He truly cares how his students are doing and makes sure every subject is clear.

Professor Chen really does care about your well-being, and simply wants you to learn and enjoy Statistics. He's very clear about what to expect, and there really are no surprises with him. He understand we're struggling students and takes that into account with the amount of work he assigns. Lectures were really enjoyable.

Dr. Chen is amazing!!!!  I wish I could take him for every class.  His class is well organized and he is really straight forward.  Fair grader and extremely helpful.  Midterm and final are take home.  Everything is submitted online.

Dr. Chen is really cool.  Recent grad student and is really down to earth.  Explains well and is very clear with his expectations.  Getting an A is super possible, but you def have to do the assignments and look at his code.  Teaches you a lot.  I wish he could teach all my classes!  Take him.
WOW! This guy is AWESOME! One of the best professors you'll have.

This guy is supreme! Excellent professor. He outlines what he expects of you and gives you a plethora of tools to meet his expectations (such as study guides, videos, etc.). He also has a nice sense of humor! Awesome guy.

Professor Chen is amazing! He is 1 of those professors who is very caring and wants to see his students do well, but that all depends on if you're willing to put in the time and effort to understand the material. Textbook use is low- only need for hw, this is an example of what a fair teacher, grader, and class should be like, thank you professor!

Professor miles Chen gives you the tools you need to succeed in the class ! I have not taken a math class in years but was able to pass this class. Professors Chens video lectures and tutorials on homework problems will save you in this class ! I found the text book to be confusing and useless however it is required to do homework assignments.

Anyone who doesn't do well in this class is just lazy. He has YouTube videos on how to do all of the homework, he records every lecture, and he makes stats very relatable and engaging. I haven't paid attention in math in years and I still understood the material. One of the best professors I've ever had!

25% of the class dropped by the time I did. I assume the good reviews are from the ones who were able to "get it".  He is somewhat helpful, but he won't review anything one is already supposed to know (i.e., algebra). His quizzes are too long for a 30 minute slot (specially after working all day).  From 3rd week on, most people looked desperate.

In my wildest dreams I could not have imagined that I would fall in love with statistics.  Took as a prerequisite.  Its been a while since math class, so I was intimidated and thought Id have to drop out.  Dr. Chens enthusiasm, creativity, clarity and availability to answer questions motivated me to try, and I learned so much.  A great teacher!

Miles is a great teacher!! I absolutely hate math, I'm not good at it at all, but worked very very hard and got an A! He gives you a lot of hmwk, but trust me you will do very well in the class if you just keep up. He really wants the class to succeed and even posts his lectures and hmwk answers online. I recommend taking this course with him!

Stats requires at least a few hours of homework each week. You will do well without spending this time. That said, Prof Chen is a very good teacher, VERY fair grader, and offers more than enough opportunity to do well in his class (especially toward the end). Don't skip class or homework, pay close attention to lecture, and you'll excel with Chen.

He made math seem so easy and fun! and i hate math. I went with the mindset of just passing with a C but I ended up getting A. that says alot about him as a professor, he really care about his students, he is by far the best professor, because he really enjoys his work. the videos are a big help, so is the note card he lets you bring.

MC is a great guy! But this isn't a popularity contest. Goes way 2fast& doesn't challenge students answer ?s &get involved. Class full w/spoiled kiddies whose mommy/daddy paid 4them. They talked, mocked students asking questions the ENTIRE class. MC doesn't know how to control his class @all impossible 2focus. Work hard 4my$ 2babysit.2distractiing!

Professor Miles Chen is a beyond professional passionate dedicated and an extremely true professor. hard math course however it's doable if students thrive to succeed. I learned SO much by giving 250 percent. Must do Office hrs attend every single class and practice. wow Amazing Professor God Bless Miles Chen.

He is just too good to be true. Extremely organized. You really can get an A if you do the homework pay attention in class.He video tapes his lectures. Don't miss a quiz he gives 4 quizzes and take only the highest 3. each quiz has 5 point bounce. Best professor I have ever had in my life! he just great if you willing to do the work. DO THE WORK

I did not pass this class, but no fault of professor. He is a great instructor and does everything possiable to help you understand and pass his class. I highly recommend him for any student who is serious about learning and willing to do his/her part. The professor does his part 1000%

i would of failed if not for every homework problem and lecture being uploaded on youtube. its the most helpful thing in the world. you don't need to be great in math to get an A. you are allowed cheat sheets on the test which is so cash. I got an A and haven't taken a math course in 3 years. this is the guy to take. best ever.

Miles Chen is a superb instructor - he made the course challenging,engaging, interesting - I loved his real-world world examples--truly brilliant.  Also exceptionally patient and helpful in office hours.  The course was perfectly balanced. I wish I could take another course with Professor Chen!
Great instructor who manages to keep faster learners engaged while not leaving slower students behind.
Really great professor! veey patient and clear. Made stats enjoyable :)

Professor Chen made stats palatable. He is extremely nice and conscientious. Also, he makes himself very available to students via email and his website. I absolutely recommend taking stats with him. I only wish more professors were as inspiring as him!
Sickest guy, really good teacher. Makes everything clear and his youtube videos really help.
Great teacher.  Passionate about the subject matter and great resources available on youtube for both lectures and homework solutions.

Knowledge o/basic math & algebra needed. Excellent instructor that genuinely cares about students' learning. Online vids so helpful for those that work full-time but go to class when you can. Subject matter is not easy so take time to do hmwk and learn material and you'll do fine. Office hours & accessible by email. Highly recommended teacher!
Brush up on your basic math

Great instructor. He posts lectures and HW solutions online. Very accessible but be prepared to work. If you don"t do the work you will not pass, this material is complex. Follow his CARD tips and you can get a good/great grade. Brush up on your elementary math skills, decimal/fractions, simple linear equations. Only need reg. calculator.

Attendance is important to her. She is an incredible person who really guides you to develop a love of learning Dutch.  This professor has a good feel for class temperament and knows when to push and when to back off. She's very compassionate. Just make sure you actually show up!
No Comments

Very talented at explaining the language. Fully bilingual. Often took detours, talking about humorous personal stories and examples of Dutch culture, when she sensed the class wasn't in the mood to learn Dutch. Cares immensely about student success in the class. No textbook. You learn to converse in Dutch from Day One. Attendance IS required!
Best of!

If you haven't had Ciprian for any Math class at UCLA, you missed a lot... He leads you to understand Math concepts in fascinating way...The downward of having him as a professor is you end up comparing every other professor with him and gets disappointed. His tests are easy if you understand the concept. Final is little harder, but still manageabl
The best teacher ever
He is so nice! He really tries to make you understand math, poor guy. I understood all of the content, and whatever I didn't, he was there to help. His class was definitely not what I expected!
HE IS SO CUTE WITH HIS ACCENT AND HE IS SO NICE. I JUST LOVE HIM. GREAT TEACHER OVERALL.
Most helpful teacher ever, more of a friend than a professor.
take class with him, and you won't regret
The best math professor I have ever had. He teaches you to "feel" mathematics.
Claire was a phenomenal teacher.
Great teacher!

Rating cont. as it was cut off. She is passionate about this wonderful philosophical man, and I felt my own faith stirred after taking the class. She does talk fast, so I would sit up front, come to class early and contact OSD if you have a learning disability to ask for a note taker. She is very understanding if you have an LD. See her in o.h.

Professor McEachern is a splendid professor! Her Milton lectures were like heaven to me. The first day of class she told us Milton was her favorite course to teach, and the last day of class, I cried because I could feel her love and passion for this amazing man. Milton was also a philosopher, and after this class, I felt more inclined to return to
She is unclear in her teaching. Half the time I did not understand what she was saying because her thoughts were scattered.

She is great. Encouraging and practical. Milton is a bore but she actually makes it fun. She is easy-going and intelligent but she doesn't take herself too seriously. This is a required class for ENG majors so take it with her! But you have to do ALL the reading or you won't pass.
No Comments

The course material was easy to understand and the professors lectures were great. However, there is a lot of work, homework twice weekly, labs, test, & journal reviews. The test questions are very confusing and answers, it seems like she goes out of her way to make the questions confusing so people can't cheat but it hurts the grades.
incredibly sweet and a great professor
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Courtney was very kind however I didn't like how she set up her midterm/final. Some questions seemed kind of vindictive as they were meant to trick you or were things like "A, B, C, A and B, A B and C" etc. I studied extremely hard for the final and thought I got 100% but ended up with a mid B. Just don't get lulled into security. NOT an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Awesome!!!!!

Boring, but easy. Basically the class boils down to this: diversity is a good thing, let's unproblematically celebrate mulitculturalism. In other words: Little or no sophisticated conversation. She is extremely generous of students' comments, no matter how silly, thoughtless, or off topic.
As ever, Clara is great! Very helpful!
Easy, fun.

Easy, clear instruction, and you'll actually learn something about research methods. Sometimes it can get a bit boring, especially when she reads from power point, but overall I liked the class and the professor.

She's totally kind, easy and informative. She doesn't really challenge the opinions of her students - no matter how silly. Conversely, she encourages everyone to chime in. Sometimes class discussion can lag while other times it can be lively. Some of her assignments - like service learning - can be lame, but she also gets you thinking.
She was a really dedicated prof. who was fair with both students and faculty. We learned a lot from the area fields in which she had expertise, Spanish historical linguistics and Spanish syntax.
Amazing Caring mujer, she is really knowledgeable on the field of linguistics. She is quite flexible and humanizing.
She has a different image now and started anew.  Very soon she'll be just fine!
No big deal; it's actually basic.
It is useless to take a class with her.  Her material is outdated and she is not on top of the cutting edge of Spanish linguistics.
I'm not impressed with this instructor.  She talks too much in class but has no direction.  Grading is always a problem, and she won't listen when you make her see her own mistakes.
She's not bad teaching all the courses of the section. Grading is always a problem; expects too much and does not add all the grades correctly. Same old problem.
Not too bad. She's been teaching around here for a while. She is not too strict.

THE FINAL IS CUMULATIVE, which is very difficult given the extensive amount of information covered.  make sure you stay on top of the readings. they are lengthy, but you WILL NEED THEM. Sections are REQUIRED and you will do well not to miss any of them because there is information given in them you cannot get anywhere else.

Lecture rooms get half empty after a few weeks.  However, she is a very intelligent teacher and is very clear.  It's not necessary to go to all the lectures though.  The midterm and the final weren't cakewalks because of the amount of material but it's easy to get an A or B because she gives you the IDs you need to study.

Dr Rapp is a wonderful professor.  She is infinitely patient and helpful.  Her insights were extremely valuable for my work - a very decent human being too.  I recommend her very strongly for graduate study.
Insane. Teaches feelings over facts. Surely, UCLA diversity hire and nothing more. You'll learn nothing here
She can't even proofread her own work. So won't even bother to read yours.

Dr. Cooney has a really easy lecture style and she's very funny, which helps things stick in your head. She knows her material and is really willing to help, returns emails and will make flexible office hours if needed. Great teacher. Take any class with her.

She is rather a good professor. The courses are well-organized and she is helpful. But during one course she said something bad about European people which I did not appreciate at all as an international student. By this, she disappointed me a lot.

Great teacher, easy to follow. She gives you all the information you need and grades liberally IF you can show that you understood the concepts in your essay's. I enjoyed her so much I took a second class with her.

I would take her class if she was teaching the history of toilet paper. She is so fun and interesting that even though I'm not all that into Egyptian art history, I've taken two classes with her. She's hilarious and inspiring, and I totally recommend her!

Great professor! she's a lot of fun... very dedicated, loves her job and her students. Very helpful. Doesn't give a straight answer, "may be this, may be that... we don't know". If you love art, take her class. you won't regret.
Smart and articulate.  The class was challenging but she made me want to work very hard to do well.

You've got to be kidding me. Cooney was fantastic-- smart, relaxed, great sense of humor...only a midterm and a final and that's it! It's been refreshing to see a woman so young and so driven do well as an art historian and archaeologist. She totally changed my opinion on Egyptian art. She is not to be missed.
One of my favorite professors at UCLA! I learned so much from her lectures. She's awesome. :)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If you get the chance to take a class with Mary, jump at it. She is one of the best professors I have ever had, a true delight to learn from, passionate and quick-witted. She thrives on teaching and it shows. She does assign a lot of reading, but as long as you're engaged you should be fine. Also: GO TO OFFICE HOURS! You'll miss out if you don't.

Mary is very straightforward on what she expects, and she rewards class participation. I highly recommend visiting her during office hours. Mary is brimming with intellectual energy, and her enthusiasm for history rubs off on students. She is also very humorous--one of the my top three most memorable instructors.

She is the best but she is not an easy grader.  Contrary to what people will tell you, you do need to attend lecture and office hours.  Isn't that what separates the C students from the A students anyway?

She's the best!  Leans left, but this is to be expected from a professor.  She gives brilliant lectures and her office hours are some of the best times I have ever had in school.  If you do the readings, go to lectures, and to office hours then you will (probably) get an A.  I cannot say enough good things about Prof. Corey

Either do the readings or go to lecture, depending on what you like. I did almost no reading and just went to lecture; got an A-. Final and midterm were take-home, so make sure you flip through the reading to find quotes for the papers at least, if you read or not. That's almost the entire grade for the class, aside from maybe 10%.
She is an excellent instructor with entertaining lectures and mostly interesting textbooks. Attendance is not mandatory if you do all the reading. All exams are take-home.

Midterm & Finals are take home papers. She loves to teach US History of the 1960's. Not a Communist, but a Social Justice educator. Great lecture and videos (Harvey Milk & Vietnam Documentry). Also, she's very youthful & full of life experience. I always recommend this class to all my fellow Chicano student friends

Great professor. Both the mid-term and final were take-home. "Museum" report was also required, and was about anything you wanted to do it on. Attendance not mandatory if you did the reading, but her entertaining lectures make it worth it. Do not argue about your grades or you'll be sorry.

Professor Corey is very passionate about the 1960s and the civil rights movements that emerged in that era and had a major impact on US history. The class I took dealt with 1950s - present. The assigned readings were informative and fun. Best part of the class was the wide variety of essay questions to choose from for your midterm and final.
Extremely interesting course content plus her bubbly personality makes this a class worth taking.
Very fun lectures and topics... take the class you wont be waisting your time
This teacher is a communists who berates the US in many ways - one wonders why she still lives in the CA capitalistic system rather than in some socialist backwater country.
Cool as hell! does not hold back, take good notes and you'l be just fine I got a B- and I would still reccomend her

She is one the GREATEST professors of history I have ever taken.  If you are having trouble, she takes the time to help you out!!  Her class is awesome!  I promise that you will not fall asleep, she captures her students with her great lectures.  I totally recommend her!

Mary was amazing.  Note to students - take notes voraciously...that helps a lot with the midterms and finals...regurgitation of lecture + your well formulated and intellectual opinion of the subject matter = A...don't be brain dead and you will be fine.  Also, take advantage of office hours...this is where you will get some of the most intellecutally stimulating conversation she has to offer.  If you want the chance to speak with a very accomplished and interesting Prof, this is her.
amazing mind... in spite of her 'aural hallucinations'...

Mary is hilarious.  She has a passion for the subject matter as well as an opinion.  the class is fairly easy if you write descent.  Despite her relentless attacks on white people and her liberal politics...she is pretty cool.  She might be the only liberal on campus i can actually stand listening to.
Best professor I had in my freshman year at UCLA, engaging lectures and extremely helpful review sessions. Can't recommend him enough.

Conley is great and really cares. You should study before quizzes and tests. As long as you put in a little time you will experience the rewards. Amazing professor and really explains everything so that everyone cane have the opportunity to understand what he is teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Conley is one of the best professors I have ever had. He cares a lot about his students and will voluntarily take time out of his schedule to make sure that you understand everything. While his tests are challenging, they really test students' knowledge and understanding. Take Conley if you're looking to really learn!

The other two reviews are inaccurate. I am a 3.5 student and have gotten an A- in Precalc in High School, and Conley is one of the worst teacher ever.
