I got all As and B on the quizzes.He has questions on his Midterm/Final never covered through lecture or HW. Exams are designed to trick you and ruin your grade. Learn some fairness, Conley!

He is extremely helpful in helping with homework and studying for tests. Very nice teacher. High recomend him. His quizzes are fair, but tests can become difficult, especially in the final which caught many people off guard. He explains things very well and makes attending class worth it.
he's a very nice teacher, pretty easy lectures and straightforward. He's very helpful! definitely recommend him! HW is not as bad either and quizzes are pretty fair.

Really loved his lectures but you must do the reading in order to follow along otherwise good luck! He gives quizzes but 2 are take home. He gives you study guides for the final and a sufficient amount of time for essays. Easy grader as long as you put in some effort. 20% of the grade is just showing up to class! I think I'll get an A!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Colacurcio was really nice and funny. He gives easy quizzes--he basically hand a quiz to you you a few weeks in advance and you just have to listen for the answers. He is an easy grader too. I got an A in his class.

He is cynical and shocking but he is 100% involved in the learning of students. There are handouts, a reader and practice quizzes. He tells you what he wants for the tests, does close analysis on the texts and engages the class. VERY GOOD PERSON, very intelligent and very efficient. Straight forward class!
Professor Colacurcio is a man of staggering genius. If you really want to learn anything in an English course and you are not afraid to work, sign up for one of his courses.

You will read more than you ever thought possible in his class. He has no problem insulting people & saying crude things about them. BUT he is an absolute genius! If you do the reading you will do well. It's hard but if you do the work you'll get your A & more. The material is dry but he had many great lectures, especially  on Hawthorne and Irving.

Prof. Cola is a very intellingent man.  He does not tip his hand when teaching.  He expects you to learn, do the reading and participate.  I really enjoyed having an intimate seminar with one of the world's leading Hawthorne experts.  He is eager to see his students do well.

Didn't necessarily feel like the lecture was all that valuable to the class. I did fine by spending that time reading the textbook rather than attending. He tells you the concepts for the exams in advance, though. Midterms are fairly simple- ranging in easy to somewhat hard questions. There's no homework or quizzes, just two midterms and final.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Coroniti is an awesome guy that is genuinely interested in and knowledgeable in his field. There were no homework assignments in the Astronomy 5 class, and it was very interesting.
very caring
why did you take my rating down Mr craig what's up with that
Sigma Sigma on the wall, who's the rizziest of them all.

Quiz every single week. Doesn't matter if it's midterm week or the week before finals. You're still going to have a quiz. But don't expect to get these quizzes back because that would be ridiculous. Started doing better in the class after I stopped going. 10 week quarter, haven't been to class in 6 weeks.

Professor Crimi is awesome. You can tell he really cares for his students and wants everyone to succeed. He is flexible and very accessible outside the classroom. I loved taking Formal Logic with him and would recommend taking his class!

Milo is very caring and is willing to work with you if you are struggling. The class is difficult if you struggle with math, but Milo will help you every step of the way. All the exams are take-home, and all the homework is collaborative. There are plenty of extra credit opportunities. Great class!

Milo is incredibly kind and an amazing, engaging teacher. He is super knowledgeable about the material he teaches and is willing to thoughtfully answer all your questions. The grading is very much based on the effort you put into his class. The homework is relatively light, but it's important that you do it. One of my favorite professors so far! :)
Michelle Craske announced that she herself is a white supremacist twice in front of a group of students each time so I suffer feeling terrorized a year later.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Not really supportive. Got nowhere with her after a long time.
Extremely knowledgable about anxiety disorders-if you show willingness to learn and grow-she will support you all the way!

I am now in grad school in another state but I still use the text and remember what a great prof she was.  She is not boring, just low-key.  Approach for help and she will. Loved the class. Taking a grad version of it this coming Fall.
Dr. Craske may seem hard to approach or too quiet and shy.  But approach her any way and you'll find that she's amazing!
Dr. Craske my seen hard to approach, but when you do, you'll find that she is amazing.
needs to improve communications skills
Nice enough but so boring
This rating is for his SCAND R5B at UC Berkeley. Amazing professor and engaging lecturer. Such a chill class, and favorite class I've taken at Cal so far. Low stress and you learn a lot.
Love this class

Professor Crawford's classes were a highlight of my time at UCLA. Probably his best quality as a teacher is his desire to make Old Norse literature and mythology accessible and interesting to laypeople. At least with me, he definitely succeeded.
Wow, amazing course, amazing professor. The most fun you'll probably have in a UCLA class. Handsome too, that never hurts.
Makes the language come alive.

Crawford is a good teacher. The class was VIKINGS and he presented the availible (scattered) material in a really cohesive way. This is a great class, by the way, if you need a GE. The material is a trip and Crawford has a great dry sense of humor. This class was a blast.
He's a great professor and really wants students to care about the subjects as much as he does. I've taken a few classes with him because it's either fun or interesting or both!
He really cares about what he taught and made sure everyone understood it.
He knew he was working with difficult material and tried his best to make it clear and concise.
Absoloutely horrible. A complete waste of time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: All this professor's classes are useless and it's clear that she is only in the Department because otherwise she would be unemployed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This professor demonstrates a lack of care, and constantly tries to attempts to indoctrinate students with her personal agenda in a way that is so obvious, everyone is left very uncomfotable. Additionally, she shows clear preferential treatment towards some students over others.

This professor demonstrates a lack of intelligence, evident in her assignments of useless content and attempts to indoctrinate students with her personal agenda. Additionally, she shows clear preferential treatment towards certain students, which undermines fairness in the classroom.

This professor lacks knowledge and tries to indoctrinate students with her political agenda. The assigned materials are poorly written and valueless. Critical thinking is not encouraged; students must regurgitate her views. She makes illogical connections, creating an uncomfortable atmosphere for open discussion.

Even though she's clearly got her hands full, Dana always makes her seminars interesting and demonstrates a real interest in the work her students are doing. She's especially good at getting you feedback on your writing--that does mean she'll end up pushing you to produce good work, but at least you won't be left guessing how to do that.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Had a very pleasant overall experience with Dana in her seminar on utopian fragments! The subject matter was fascinating, and despite having a TON of things going on, Dana seemed very personally interested and invested in the work each student did throughout the quarter.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Pseudo-intellectual whose research is as unresolved and flawed as that of a High School paper.

Sam was great and I highly recommend him to future students. There were three 4-page papers, and participation - each accounted for 25% of the grade. He is a very forgiving grader and he gives very helpful feedback on papers. Class discussions were great. I really appreciated how he treated students. He genuinely cared about our opinions/ideas
Super cool. Special mind
Not that interesting and not a very good teacher overall.

He is such a sweet teacher and exceptionally intelligent. He just recently published a great piece of philosophical work in 2011 and really shows true potential as a professor and mentor. Plus, he's super attractive and from Australia! Too bad he's married!
Rutgers guy and very smart. He is young and a bit unseasoned but overall a promising teacher and researcher. I left being very interested in philosophy of language and communication.
Awesome dude. Super sweet and helpful!
He's such a sweet guy! Very interesting lectures, just really hard to follow--you need need need to ask a lot of questions. He's really smart but kind of bad at explaining things orally....

he's great at explaining things in office hours, but his ramblings during class are really hard to understand....pretty much the only thing that keeps you attentive is the fact that he's really hot and has a sexy accent!

This professor builds his whole course around a personal opinion, seemingly based in little or no research.  Because he so hates getting feedback of any kind (his articles in WSJ are not subject to reader comments) he has tried to vilify any docrine which advocates for feedback as a means to improve performance. Teach, don't preach!
Smug; indifferent toward his students; and a poor teacher, that is overly impressed with himself.
He is the ultimate jerk. He is in love with himself. You learn lot of dogmas- not functional at all

This professor enforces mandatory lectures that primarily consist of her personal conjecture on political events. She will rarely if ever, delve into the required course readings that are both excessive and frequently irrelevant to her actual assignments. Her student responses range from passive-aggressive to outright rude. Do not take her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: 2 stories: 1. A student asks for advice and is told to just drop the course. 2. A student asks for clarification and is accused of not attending class. Enough Said.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: On the first day of class Lebow said her class might not be for you if you have a "boyfriend or a job". I literally have to work to survive. The work load from this class is unmanageable, you can only keep your sanity if this is your only class for the quarter. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Lebow can be best described as fair. I have taken 3 of her classes and in all three of her classes, there has been a lot of reading and a lot of content. If you are willing to put in the effort, you will get an A. she is not out to trick you. Professor Lebow is also always willing to help her students.

One of those classes that are hard to get through but once you have you walk away appreciating it. The vast amount of knowledge you will gain about the Court system, major cases, the federal government, and law terminology makes this class worthwhile. Especially if your planning on law school. Lebow is attentive,clear and ready to help in any way

If you are interested in the supreme court and possibly going to law school then I recommend this class.  There is a TON of reading but if you go to all the lectures then you can get by by just skimming the book.  Also be sure to go to office hours because she's happy to help you out if you just make an effort.

The Supreme Court and other classes: This instructor is difficult but excellent. Lectures are very clear and are directly related to the cases and textbook readings. Go to her office hours if you need help. Speak up in class and do the readings and you will be fine. She is always willing to listen and help students succeed. Recommended!
Tough, but very informed, knowledgeable, clear, and helps her students grasp the material.
The worst teacher, and the most hardest. She is very tough, if you don't comment in class. She will mark you down. Don't take this class, unless you have to
Great!
Decent prof, but I don't think the board is what the girls in his class stare at during his lectures.

He's a nice enough guy and he wants to help but his French accent is extremely heavy and if you're not already good at calculus, his lectures and even office hours can be of limited help because of his limited English.
What can I say? Smart + attractive guy= great prof!

Prof. Houdayer is a great lecturer! It is clear that he knows the material very well- he lectures without any notes at all. I thought that the midterms were fair and the final was hard, but manageable (had some interesting problems).Also, I absolutely love his French accent!
This professor makes things much harder than they need to be. If you can avoid him, I would.

I did not enjoy this class at all. Was disappointed due to my original liking on the topic being changed because of his teaching strategies. He is extremely knowledgeable in every course he teaches, but expects you to be on the same expert level as he is. He is also unclear on his expectations, then gets confused as to why they were not met.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Very knowledgable about subject matter and dedicated to his field. Always up for out of class discussions about philosophy and behaviorism and very reachable when needed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Personally knowledgeable about subject, terrible teacher who assumes that you too are a learning expert.Exam questions did not fairly represent the material learned in class, responsible for information in text even if it it not covered in class. can tell that he didn't know how to write a good exam yet...maybe in 10 years he'll be a better teacher

This class was a struggle. The material is difficult to grasp and it seemed like he was not willing to help at all. He does not care about the students learning at all. His first test is very easy but the remaining two are very difficult especially the second one. If you want to have a enriching course where you will succeed don't take him.
So basically there was litewrally this one time where hew as like teaching and then he gave a test. SO literally gotta study like literally
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Inspirational--So many papers

reviews.comment: He was a good and funny professor but the material for the class is sooooo conceptual and its all theories...its a very hard class over all but he is not all that helpful when u ask questions he is not good at explaining...very nice guy though..awsome australian accent ;)

The prof. gives an A to only 10-15% of the students, so it's really hard to get an A in PSYCH 15 which IS a lower-division class while competing a lot of smart ppl. And there are many tricky questions in exams. If I had known this info, I wouldn't have taken it.

His lectures relate very little to the textbook and homework problems. He only teaches things that he finds interesting. He started off the General Chemistry course talking about quantum mechanics rather than actual general chemistry. He is lazy and makes his TAs do all the work for him.
Took him for 113A Quantum Mechanics!! He is really bad at teaching  a. if you can avoid him specially for such a hard course like this.

Worst "teacher" I've ever had, period. He cannot teach to save his life. Studied my a** off and it did nothing for me. His test questions were completely from out of left field, and his lectures make no sense whatsoever. The little that I did learn was all thanks to my TA and myself. Take a different professor and save yourself the stress.
Terrible teacher, he teaches at a level far above the students in Chem 20A. His tests are unrelated to the assigned readings in the book, and only slightly related to his lectures. Avoid if possible.

Boy, where to begin. Goes off onto tangents, he is an amateur in chemistry, and not a professional at teaching. You might as well take Chem 20A later unless you have to. AVOID BAUGH! You will be happy that you did not take chemistry with this teacher!
Terribly professor, smart guy. That's it.

WORST CLASS I'VE EVER HAD!!! i went to class everyday and took very good notes and read the book, first off, the book is terrible and hardly makes any sense and his lectures make even less sense. after receiving a D and F on the midterms and a D on the final somehow he curved so much because everyone must have done terrible i got a C- in the class

A good chemist but a bad teacher. The tests are entirely from lecture slides, but the slides have nothing to do with the book, and the book is so God-awful it's entirely unhelpful. You can tell he knows chemistry, but he cannot convey his understanding to students. It's a difficult class as it is, but having Baugh made it that much harder.

Dr. Baugh is a really nice person, but he has a hard time teaching his students. The finals counts for your entire grade, one page of note for each midterm and two pages on the final is allowed. Go to your TA's office hours, and Kenny is the most beautiful creature on earth, oh and he's an amazing TA too, if you gut him you should ask him for help.

The exams were easy, if that's what matters to you.  He went on weird tangents, stuff like Africans in ancient Egypt and claiming that whites stole all their technology from Africans.  Lectures were ok, although he sometimes got confused by fairly simple concepts.
he is very bright. explains concepts well, even though he often goes off on tangents.

He had no interest in teaching this subject, and it showed in his lackluster performance.  His TA's did a better job.  He is only interested in his own research.  He never once gave an interesting lecture, his lectures didn't connect with the book info, and his tests were out of left field.  I think he should go back to USC.
easier once you know what his tests will ask. off topic alot.

he knows his stuff, but hes rather incompetent as a professor.  lectures are useless and really slow. material seemed to be entirely disconnected until the final review session when things were somewhat explained. overall, not a great professor

Terrible Professor.Talks to the board and talks funny too.Tests on things he never goes over.All he does is derive stuff.Take Scerri instead if you can.If you absolutely have to take his class, be sure to get tutoring in covel.

He's probably one of the worst professors I've had the displeasure of having to take. If you value your mental health, then please don't take this class or the professor. He claims he lectures, but most of it is him giving one opinion and opening to discussion. Speaking of opinion, if you don't share his opinion, you prob won't get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you have anxiety, this class is perfect for you. You never get called on unless you volunteer. With that being said, if you are not an auditory learner, it'll be a pretty hard class for you. He goes off book or notes he writes and it's hardly on the board (just record the lecture on goodnotes and go back as many times as you want).

The subject material is fascinating. It revolves around author Henry James, his life and major works. For this class, you'll have to read five or six of his books and the way he writes can be very confusing. The professor is not extremely helpful in this arena, but his lectures are interesting.
Only got a "B" but learned a hell of a lot.
Fantastic professor. Knowledgable about literature, art, philosophy, history, and even popular culture. Loved this class.
He's a very scholarly type, and one of the last true gentlemen in America. Loved his course on James, even aced it!!! I recommend him to everyone.

My writing improved a hundred fold after taking this class because the guy spent a lot of extra time giving me and the other people in the class lots of helpful comments and advice about their papers.You can tell he loves what he does and he doesn't like BS!

The most articulate, interesting, and caring teacher I have encountered in college so far. I can't believe what a huge Henry James geek I have become because of Dr. DiMuro. Oh and his tests are not bad at all.
Such a wide range of knowledge that I loved coming to class to listen to him. Fair grader.
His lectures are interesting but he provides a lot of superfluous information. He used a lot of obscure and indistinct quotes to identify on the final, but he grades the papers very easily.
Brilliant, outstanding

Bad teacher, but not terrible. He gives the impression that his class is hard to pass, but he want simple and easy to read essays. WEIRD coming from a UCLA professor. He will grade you down if you use big words. Overall, try to avoid his courses.
Awesome instructor - DIFFICULT, but the class is WELL WORTH THE EFFORT!
Too lazy to give lectures, lets students carry on mind-numbing discussions instead. Doesn't make an effort and criticizes theories he doesn't understand. Very disappointed.
awesome prof but lots of reading and hard exams. witty and nice
terrific prof--brilliant, gifted teacher. opened my mind. handsome!

Very fun and engaging lectures, as well as the Discussion Sections. The homework is easy and straightforward, but not overwhelming and also relevant. The class does start out a bit slow, but the articles you read and write about are all interesting and fun to discuss in class and in Discussion.

Diamond is, well, a gem. He imparts soooo much incredible information, and you remember years (and I would assume decades) later. I had him for two classes, both of which you must write an essay just to get into, well worth the effort. I feel fortunate to have been in his classes, he won't be around forever! Pay attention in class and you do well!
He's amazing. So nice & insightful

Extremely intelligent and insightful man.  Can be a little hard to get a hold of given his research and other obligations. Lectures can get a little dull, but overall a very informative and interesting course.
Yes, as an avid reader of Diamond, and a faculty member at another institution, I too am surprised to see no ratings on Diamond. He is like a real Indiana Jones. We need more like him in higher ed.

I can't believe no one has rated jared Diamond. He is one of the most famous professors in the country and a legend at UCLA. When you enter a mind like that you don't come out the same person. You will have a very different view on humanity and you can die knowing that you were taught by one of the greatest mind of century.
great teacher
He is a very good professor and makes the material understandable. Not a very easy class, but you will learn a lot, and with effort, you will earn a high grade.

Dienstag just plain rules. He's not by any means easy, but you will learn, you will laugh, and you will earn the grade you put into it. Having a good TA really helps, but I have yet to meet anyone who regrets taking him.
Modern Political Theory - tough class.  The Professor and TA's however, are great. Highly recommend this Professor.
he's amazing. honestly he changed my life

I was an Anthro major many moons ago at UCLA and Dr. Hollan was one of my favorite. I found his course challenging, entertaining, and he was a very approachable Professor. I never met a student who did not like him! He's a wonderful human being. Period.
Great lecturer. Quite a bit to read but assigns interesting books/articles. Is very clear and willing to help to make sure everyone is on the same page.

My experience with this teacher rates lowest of all my experiences with teachers at UCLA. He leads the anthropology departmental honors, and in my opinion, is better suited to teaching kindergarten. In a program designed to give the most interested and advanced students in the department an advantage, he insisted on mindless handholding and busywor
Professor Dolovich is hands down my favorite law school professor, and I've had some great ones.  She is brilliant, passionate, and comitted to her students.

Professor Dolovich is brilliant!  Had heard mixed things about her entering the class, but she really surprised me with how interesting the class ended up being.  What really held the discussions back, at least in my section, was actually the class' unwillingness to oppose "PC" opinions, for fear of offending someone.

Amazing. Absolutely brilliant, and even though she talks REALLY fast (!), she is very willing to work with you outside of class if you don't understand the issues. She focused on very interesting topics within Crim. I came into the class not interested in the subject and now I'm absolutely in love! Dolovich was the best prof I had this semester.

Professor Dolovich is wonderful. Yeah she talks fast and encourages discussion, sometimes at the expense of reading cases, but this is partially because the doctrines are unclear and disputed and it's important to learn why. She encourages difficult discussion and critical thinking, and I've always felt respected in and interested by her class.

Do attend class and try to stay somewhat engaged; she tends to call on people that appear to be zoned out. Write well and edit your exam; there is no time pressure and the issues are easy to spot, so clear writing sets you apart.
Sometimes hard to follow, but she is brilliant and very interested in the subject.
She was pretty bad.  One of the worst I have ever had.  Rude, unhelpful, unfriendly, close-minded.  I could write much more...
Such a brilliant legal mind.  She talks fast, so you'd better be a fast typer, but everything she says is totally worth hearing.

This woman had managed to completely turn off any interest I might have had in criminal law. She was more interested in dragging out of us what she thinks the policy should be than cases and law. Too bad as 1Ls people don't have much choice to avoid her.
Her exam is pretty easy - 8 hour take home.  A lot of smart people did poorly in her class. =)

This was the worst class I have had thus far at law school.  The woman is brilliant, but I feel CHEATED out of my the crim law experience.  She was more interested in how my fellow students felt than in teaching us the state of the law.  Terrible, terrible, terrible.
fascinating.  I hung on every word.
No Comments

People with anxiety listen up: as someone with severe anxiety this class wasn't bad. He doesn't expect people to share out or call on people at random. He does do popcorn reading through each row sometimes but the lines are never long and no one looks up from their papers. he's really nice and it wasn't a bad experience. *Join the groupme it helps*
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Excellent, able to invoke magic in the classroom.  Has a real passion for Shakespeare.
Great class
Took this for the Literary GE and I don't regret it at all. A very engaging lecturer and encourages students to speak up in class, but doesn't put any pressure. Very funny.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you can write well, I fully recommend taking ENGL 91A (Intro to Poetry) with Dr. D. Preferably, take it your first quarter at UCLA, because you can really get a chance to meet people and interact with the class. Sometimes lectures are slow, but this class is 100% what you make of it. Read poems, write a paper, and take two exams (test bank!).

AMAZING instructor. His sense of humor and personality shine through in his lectures. Definitely my most highly enjoyed class, ever. He makes learning fun and therefore easy to remember. He is so passionate and knowledgeable--  I could not imagine being taught Shakespeare by anyone else...see for yourself what everyone is raving about!!
Best Professor I have ever had.
Had him for three classes 20 years ago. One of the few profs I remembered. Smart and compassionate.

Professor Dickey has been by far one the most amazing professor I have had. He is very inspiring, knowledgable, and passionate on what he teaches. His engagement with his students is remarkable and he is always happy to assist student. I cannot emphasizes enough how much I appreciate to have had the opportunity to be taught Shakespeare by this guy!

I've taken him for two quarters now. He's probably one of the most inspiring, passionate professors at UCLA. Even if you aren't an English major, I highly recommend taking one of his GE classes [95A/B]. He's helpful, approachable, and really funny. His exams are very fair.

Professor Dickey is my favorite Professor at UCLA.  I have never met anyone who is genuinely interested and knowledgeable in so many subjects.  If you put in the effort and get to know him, you will not only leave the class with lots of knowledge, but a helpful and kind friend.

Prof D is one of a kind. He is a teacher that is not only inspiring but also a friend. He really seems to care about his students and wants to see them succeed. If you get a chance take him for Shakespeare or his Beat class. Dickey Rocks.

Prof D is one of a kind. He is a teacher that is not only inspiring but also a friend. He really seems to care about his students and wants to see them succeed. If you get a chance take him for Shakespeare or his Beat class. Dickey Rocks.
I really enjoyed his class and learned a great deal about the plays, as well as, the poet. I loved how allowed students to "creatively" present the play rather than having to write another paper!
Mr. Dickey is an awesome instructor!
Truly talented, performative professor.  Will read drafts of essays, and helped me write one of my best papers.  Simply fabulous.
No Comments
He's a fascinating lecturer and a real nice guy. Loved the class even if I thought I deserved a better grade on my one paper.
the most approachable smart guy that i've ever met.

He really got me excited about shakespear, what a great profes. amazingly intellegent, funny, enthusiastic. He really made me enjoy a subject that I didnt think I would ever really enjoy! If you enroll in his class you are sooo lucky
I had him for 2 shakespeare classes.  He is the nicest guy, funny, and not pretenious but really smart.  I wish he taught at the grad school I'm going to go to next year.
I had Professor Dickey for 3 courses at UCLA.  He is the best especially Shakespeare.  Even if you're not an English major take him.  He just brighten up my day.

Many graduate level music classes for conducting majors at UCLA.  Be prepared!  Do your best!  Soak up all he has to offer. Even if it seems extreme (perhaps?), you will be able to apply it in the future. No nonsense. Full of artistry, talent, and methods that WORK!

An inspirational professor who is a breath of fresh air in this world where society beats around the bush, gets nothing accomplished, and then congratulates themselves.  Neuen is the real deal with the experience and international accolades to prove it.
Worth living in LA because of him.

brilliant... one of the most interesting and insightful professors i've ever encountered... always has useful and pertinent commentary on almost every topic a student could come up with... an extremely talented conductor and evangelical teacher...  one of shaw's most brilliant protoges.

He is really knowledgeable. He lectures with passion and enthusiasm. There is a couple of assignments but he does give clear instructions on what he wants and what he looking for. Will fail students for not citing correctly or at all.

One of the best professors at CSULA. He is too smart for his own good. Extremely passionate about his lectures and gets students engaged. Talks fast as hell so try and keep up. The readings are complicated and semi-boring but once you hear him explain it, it all makes so much sense. No text books, everything online.
Be on time!

Duhart is one of those professors that reminds you why you're majoring in sociology. He is engaging during lectures, and clears up any difficult concepts. He assigned one midterm, final, and weekly memos from the previous weeks  readings. Material he utilizes is very educating.

In a field of study that has a tendency to be confusing more often that not, it helps to have a professor that is willing to explain things until everyone has an understanding. Professor Duhart knows how to communicate ideas,command a classroom, and be approachable at the same time. He encourages questions and creates a noble learning environment.

Duane Champagne is a supa'star in the AIS field. Try to read any work pertaining to Natives without a citation from this man. If given the luxury of taking a class of his, I would highly suggest it. If you could care less about AIS, find another professor in the appropriate field. But as far as AIS goes, this is the dude you want to learn from!!!

He is a great professor and though some people apparently find him boring, I find that his lectures are extremely clear and I can take good notes. I guess if you're not interested in the subject, you shouldn't take the class because you'll find him boring, but he cares a lot about his subject and it shows in his lectures.
BORING AS HELL

Mr. Champagne is incredibly well versed in the subject. His lectures are extremely boring. I take rabid notes and type out everything he says (which is a ton) just to stay interested. He talks at great lengths without notes, as he wrote two of our three textbooks and doesn't really need notes. Tests are all take home essays with clear directions.
Boring Boring Boring Boring Boring
Gives a new meaning to the word "boring". Unbelivably bad lecturer

Prof. Read is an extremely intelligent professor. However, his lecture are somewhat dull and dry making the material hard to grasp. Books are optional, all the exams are based of his lectures, which can be somewhat confusing sometimes, but he is always in his office hours to help.
he posts lecture notes online and the tests are not too bad.

He sucks,  this graphs are crap and  he uses math to explain cultural anthropology . Smart guy bad teacher. Mult choice test ,t/f , essay. Class is curved test can be tricky. If you can put up with not understanding anything  then take this class
Great lectures! The assessments were very relevant, no trick questions. Very efficient and dedicated professor. Definitely learned a lot in his class!

Geiselman is one of the friendliest, most helpful Psych profs at UCLA, hands down.  I took 187A, 187B, and then TA'd for him in 187A.  Just an awesome class and prof.  I regularly tell people about things I learned in 187A/187B, and it's years later; that's how interesting it was.  Whether you're psych, pre-law, or poli-sci -- take it!!

Great class. Although I received a C, I still think this was the most interesting class I took at UCLA. Professor brings in Police Officers, judges, etc. to speak. The material was so interesting. Although the profesor often expressed his liberal views, he was very knowledgeable and laid back. Be careful tho, there's only 2 tests, 50% each.

Overall good prof. Had trouble staying awake for some lectures. One midterm and one final, 50% of your grade each. Make sure you do well on BOTH. Material relatively interesting, even if you're not going into law or forensic psych.
Great professor! He was fair with the material.  I had him for 100B also and he was the best!
The good: knows his stuff, offers help The bad: class kinda boring, can't hear his voice

Econ 101 is meant to weed out the unprepared, so I dont mind that this class was a little tough. Prof Kung is clear, and that's what matters, so im glad its not a cakewalk class! If youre not ready for UCLA, too bad. The one bad is that his voice is too soft and passive and may put you to sleep in class!

AVOID THIS CLASS!! This class was the hardest class I've ever took in UCLA. He certainly does not understand that he is teaching undergraduate students. He expects graduate level performance from all of his students. DO NOT disregard this comments and regret after taking his first impossible exam. I am expecting to get my first C in UCLA.

take Kung if you want to boost your gpa. I took sproul for 1 and 11 and loved him and did well, but Kung is even easier than sproul. Two midterms and a final. He's a little quiet when he lectures but always willing to help during office hours
Edward W. Soja exemplifies what it means to be a professor. He is intelligent, enthusiastic, well-published, and interesting. He is a legend in Geography. I love this man.

This is an interesting class that makes you think.  Soja is quite bookish and theoretical, but presents the material in an interesting way.  Mostly lecture, but I could listen to him lecture for hours without falling asleep.

Ed is a living legend if you follow postmodern theory, especially as it relates to geography, urban planning and architecture.  He will challenge you; he will make you read; he will demand solid papers.  But it's all worth your effort.  You will walk away from every lecture feeling enlightened! Take his course and do well!
Incredible instructor. One of the most intelligent and kind professors I've met at the graduate level.  He is very supportive of student's work and projects.
He is what higher education should be: knows the subject through and through, important figure, boring and incoherent at times, great person, amazing intellect, rambles, enthusiastic all the time.

Exams were very difficult.

Expect homework to take up to 30+ hours each.

CS97 is something new each week, covering everything from Emacs to Python to React/Node. It's a lot of material for a short quarter. Everything is interesting and everything is worth learning. I really liked the class. Note: if you are not Linux friendly it will be difficult. But maybe it's time to learn Linux. Totally liked the class.

The class was so different from the assignments that I really wasn't learning anything. His lectures were all over the place. I don't think I've met any student who has taken this class and not hated it (or themselves) by the end of it. Run.

Well, what can you say about him? His tests have no answers. Even if Einstein was whispering in your ear, you couldn't be sure if that was the answer. The specs never change they remain convoluted and full of typos. They are more confusing to understand than inception's plot. They have no relation to the material taught in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor is extremely difficult, as if the material were not difficult as it is, his assignments are very challenging and time consuming. Very smart and knowledgeable, if you are up for a challenge be ready, i mean it, are you top 1%?
It's a hard subject, so it's gonna be hard to teach. He's extremely knowledgeable though.

I'll admit, Eggert isn't the easiest professor, but you actually learn your stuff in his class.  He has an excitment and a passion for what he teaches, and that translates into a class that students actually enjoy and pay attention to.
He is a very difficult teacher

Prof. Elieraas is a one of the best professors I've ever had. She listens, she cares, she is passionate about learning, and what she teaches you in class is very near and dear to her heart. Everything she says is worded so beautifully, she'll be lecturing, weaving her tale of truth and justice, you're just sitting in awe like 'YAS TELL 'EM SIS'.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Professor Eileraas has been, HANDS DOWN, the greatest professor at USC I have had so far. Everything that comes out of her mouth is intelligent, sharp, and clearcut. Her lectures are long and she does assign a lot of dry readings (the ones I read were v interesting.) Have to be interested in the subject or she will bore you. PARTICIPATION MATTERS!

Dr. Eileraas is a wonderful professor with an amazing ability to articulate her lectures clearly and challenge student's generalized thinking. Her lectures satisfy many different learning techniques as she incorporates music, quotes, videos, additional literature and great insight concerning the subject material. The absolute best professor ever!
amazing and inspiring.

An extremely helpful professor. Cares about learning. Seems somewhat unapproachable but very pleasant if you do. Teaches all sides of feminist theory without personal bias. One of the best WS professors at UCLA.
very interesting and different kind of class.
One of the best GE I took.
Great class. I recommend it.

MUS HIST 5. Easy class. Online quizzes with 2-3 attempts. Need to go to class to get the lecture notes which is what you're tested on. But, easy information to take in and understand. However, Prof Eidsheim is difficult to understand and she babbles a lot.

Great and passionate professor. Lectures are information heavy; however, he lets you know exactly what will be on the exams and quizzes. I changed my vacation plans and course schedule for this once in a life time opportunity to learn from his class the one time he is on the west coast. Passionate in helping students succeed to medical school.
Heavy lectures but very passionate professor! Well qualified and respected.

Dr. Dauer does a great job teaching this course. He is very straight forward about the material that will be on the test and what won't. What makes this course very intriguing is that he is very excited about what he is teaching. Would definitely recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Dauer has so much life experience and I felt very lucky to have been able to learn something from him over this course! His lectures can be pretty heavy and include some techniques/procedures with immense detail that he breezes over though. However, I appreciated him letting us know exactly what we needed to study and his straightforwardedness.

Great Professor, makes course material really interesting. Very qualified, over qualified if anything to teach. An honor to learn from a man who has so many degrees. Caring, willing to offer help and advice. Really interesting to learn course material from the various perspectives he has to offer. Sympathetic towards student learning and obstacles.

Great Professor, makes course material interesting. Very qualified, over qualified if anything to teach. An honor to learn from a man who has so many degrees. Caring, willing to offer help and advice. Really interesting to learn course material from the various perspectives he has to offer. Sympathetic towards student learning and obstacles.
Love Dauer!! Super kind and knowledgeable man. Lectures were dense but very interesting
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr Dauer is a really good professor, he is clear on what he includes on the exams; lectures are fast paced but he is very considerate of everyone's standing on the class. Must-take!

Dr. Dauer is really laid back and chill. He's not working against the students and it seems like he's just there to help us learn about bioengineering (and brag a little bit). He's very clear about what will be on the exams. However, sometimes he lectures kind of quickly. You have to go to class and pay attention to get the most out of it, imo.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Dauer really cares about his students. He wants everyone to succeed and will make sure everyone feels comfortable with the material.
Does a really great job keeping the class entertained and interested in his lectures.

Definitely one of the best professors I've had at UCLA. He cares about the class and student learning, but he's also just an interesting person with a lot of life experience and a lot of stories to share. I highly respect him and am very thankful I decided to take this class.

Super knowledgeable man. Very intriguing and interesting lectures!! Very fair with material and lets you know what topics to focus on. Tests a little tricky; make sure to read carefully! Overall very respected and noteworthy man! I love his class!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dauer has a very charismatic personality which helps keeps the students engaged on the material. The material is very interesting and he does a great way of explaining things

I took Dr. Dauer  for Physiologic Sciences 122 in summer session A at UCLA during summer 2017. I really cant say enough good things about Dr. Dauer.  He is kind, knowledgable, funny, and very fair as a professor.  You will learn tons of physiology as well as essential lessons when applying to medical school.  You are the best Dr. Dauer. Thank you!

Thank you, Dr. Dauer for a great class! This class is one of those few classes where what you learn you use for life to help yourself and/or others. Dr. Dauer teaches extremely well, so hard concepts are made clear. He also takes time to speak with students and help them on their career pathway.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I had to work hard in this class, but I honestly enjoyed it.
Great professor. I would definitely take this class again.

Definitely recommend this class for anyone interested in medicine or biomedical technology. Dr. Dauer has studied both medicine and engineering so he bridges the gap between the two excellently. He always keeps lectures engaging and his class is fair.

Dr. Dauer really cares about his students. He is a world class, diagnostic radiologist that facilitates a great learning experience and never fails to prompt interest. I would highly recommend this class to anyone interested in biomedical science.

Wonderful professor who really cares about his students and wants us to not only do well in the class but do well in our future and will take the time to give us advice on future careers in the health field and how we can succeed.

This class is a must take for anyone interested in biomedical engineering or preparing to prepare for medical school. Dr. Dauer gives students interesting lectures and has clear expectations. Put in the work and you will get an A.
Great prof. Sometime goes on random tangents but they are almost always entertaining.

Professor Dauer is by far one if the best professors I have had at UCLA. Not only are his lectures extremely engaging and very interesting, but Dr. Dauer also truly cares about his students' well being and makes himself very available to answer questions. He strives for his course to not provide extra stress. I would highly recommend this course!

Dr. Dauer's lectures are not only informative but also engaging. It was especially so because he would tell us about his clinical experiences with patients. Although I had no interest in biomedical technology initially, I was definitely swayed by the end of class due to how he presents the information. Definitely take his class if you can!

Dr Dauer is a great professor. He genuinely cares about his students and wants to see all of them succeed. HIs grading is very fair and he isn't there to trick you on tests. They are very straight forward. Anyone should take his class. There is never a dull moment.

Dr. Dauer's class teaches very relevant and fascinating biomedical topics, like MRI machines to stents for coronary disease to bone marrow transplant. Dr. Dauer also tailors the class for pre-med students, which is a plus for many. He's willing to meet outside of class and is very approachable. However, he goes off on tangents about his hobbies

Dr. Dauer cares about his students and their learning. He is readily available outside the classroom. The class itself is interesting and offers valuable and relevant information for pre-medical students. I highly recommend taking his class.

His lecture presentations are always interesting and keeps me at my toes. Never felt like sleeping in his lectures and that says something considering the fact that I am heavily prone to sleep in classes. Truly a respectable scholar and teacher. Please take Dr. Dauer's classes whenever he is available!
Dr. Dauer is absolutely amazing, take him if you get the chance.
Professor Dauer is awesome! He is an interesting lecturer, clearly knows his stuff! Very friendly and approachable!

The professor puts a lot of work into his presentations. He drops hints on what will be on the exam, and if you catch them, you will do well in his course. His course is a difficult not just in his teaching style, but the material he covers is a big variety.  He is very dedicated to this subject, and can make the class entertaining. Awesome guy!

Professor Dauer connects concepts to real world application, goes above and beyond outside of class with regards to availability, and is an expert in a lot of material, making class really interesting.

It was a pleasure taking Dr. D's class this summer quarter. The course content was interesting and relevant, especially for aspiring doctors. Dr. D was extremely clear in grading criteria, presented engaging lectures, and made himself accessible to students outside of class. One of the most helpful professors I've ever had--take this class!
Awesome professor. Dr. Dauer teaches us a lot of important concepts. He is a very dedicated professor. I would want to take him again.
helpful professor

This was my first physci class and I actually loved it. I honestly learned so much and I'm definitely keeping the powerpoints for future reference. Dr. Dauer is a great professor with great stories and he makes every subject exciting and simple. He personally gave me great advice regarding my future in science. I high recommend taking this class

Professor who is dedicated to teaching and dedicated his students. Dr. Dauer is not only a great professor but a great mentor who is willing to help out students and talk about their future endeavors.He is very approachable and wants his students to do well. All professors need to be more like Dr. Dauer!

Doctor Dauer brings a lot to UCLA. He has high expectations for his students and motivates his students to live up to them. I have learned and have been able to retain class material and I credit Dr. Dauer's passion for teaching.

This guy really knows his stuff. He looks like he just graduated himself. I'm assuming he specialized in Kant because he just goes off and describes every thought that Kant ever had. Reading the textbook was a waste. I survived by going to lecture and taking detailed notes on everything he said.

I really liked him and thought he was fantastic "Ethics" teacher. Additionally, he didn't come off as agendized, which is uncommon for liberal arts professors at major research universities like UCLA.
No Comments
I have far too many years of education, and Dr. Keeler is one of the best professors I've ever had.

Dr. Keeler was a great professor. His experiences at RAND and real-life examples made this class very applicable and useful training for the real world! Highly recommend taking his course. And, he is super nice.

Professor Keeler is very approachable and asks for feedback after every class. He responds promptly to student questions and suggestions and try to be helpful by providing more explanations. Sometimes the economic/math concepts are difficult to come across to health science students. Many terminologies take some time to get used to.

Dr. Keeler is an excellent professor. He uses a variety of methods to ensure that you learn the material. He is particularly good at teaching math and economics concepts. He uses a lot of real-world examples. One of the things I appreciated the most is how he asked us repeatedly to give him feedback on the course so that he could improve it.
I would not recommend this course.
This course is boring and more of an undergraduate course that should not be taught at a graduate level
Course is appropriate for undergraduates rather than graduate students. It was not intellectually engaging for me.
Noel Enyedy is my favorite uncle
Noel is my hero.

I did not enjoy taking this class. In fact, if you're a poli sci major (or any major besides computer science) I would highly recommend taking just a regular stats class to fill your requirements. Professor Hartman places so much emphasis on learning coding that the part where you actually learn stats gets neglected and must be self taught.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Unless you have taken statistics before, or just enjoy teaching yourself material; don't take this class. Hartman is smug, and makes a clear effort to be condescending especially to those who are clearly struggling with the material. She's a smart person, but she absolutely should not be teaching an undergraduate lower division class.

You get whatever you put into this course. Keep up with the weekly reading, and you will do fine on the simple online quizzes. Honestly, do not miss class and do the reading and you should be fine. Learning how to code is hard, but that's why she puts you in groups so you can help each other out. Midterm and final are fair.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Don't take this class with her. Worst professor I have had. Not a good instructor; visibly annoyed when you ask questions or help solving a problem. Lectures from slides and textbook, does not walk you through anything. Group work, worksheets, academic papers. You know it's bad when the TAs don't know how to answer the questions on the worksheets.

Professor Hartman did an absolutely fantastic job of creating an environment where students from all ethnicities, languages, and backgrounds could excel in. As a Latina student who has always had much trouble in mathematics, science, and statistics I was worried that this course would be too difficult, but she made sure we excelled.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Stay away from this class. She does not care about her students and is truly the WORST teacher I have ever taken. She does not know how to teach and is too lazy to explain anything to her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Expectations are waaay too high. She kind of rushes you, which is not conducive to learning such hard stuff in a thorough way. Professor kind of expects you to be a coding whiz and about 90% of the material will go over your head. Most of class is spent asking for help and the readings are never clear & go over your head, which makes quizes hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: AMAZING PROFESSOR! He makes medieval literature very interesting and also quite modern for us millennials. He's also very accommodating and helpful whenever you approach him for any help or feedback. I definitely recommend this professor because he will challenge you while enriching your literature learning experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Jäger is a very passionate professor. Jager's lectures are always entertaining and his energy makes you want to participate even if you're usually the "quiet" one. Jager is very clear on the grading criteria and as long as you do the reading, you'll be fine. Definitely recommend Jager for Medieval lit but I heard he is great for Chaucer too.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Unlike some other professors I've taken, he seems to realize that he's dealing with full-time students and so doesn't assign a ridiculous amount of reading. Everything is manageable. You DO have to keep up with the reading, though, but overall he's a great professor who's really fair and considerate.
Jager is both very knowledgeable and entertaining. His grading seemed fair. I would definitely recommend him for Chaucer.

Jager displays a deep interest in his course material and can really suck in those who have had limited experience previously with Anglo-Saxon literature.  His course covering Beowulf is extremely interesting.  His requirements for letters of rec are bogus though, as he tends to make up ridiculous reasons not to write them for straight-A students

Prof Jager is a charismatic, inspiring teacher who brings Chaucer alive to his students. He really cares about student learning. He even gave an invaluable workshop to help improve our writing skills. If you keep up-to-date with your reading each week then your Middle English will really improve, plus you will be well prepared for the final.
I took his class during the summer.  He was an awesome professor who made Chaucer understandable and interesting.
A wonderful teacher who encourages you to think outside of the box.  Taking a class with him is a great experience.
Awesome! Best Professor at UCLA
No Comments
Professor Erwin makes no effort to help her students. Her lectures take interesting topics and make them boring. She has completely tainted my love of theater.

Very clear and straightforward teaching style. Also very easy to communicate with. Although the subject matter can seem redundant, as it is a survey course, Erwin does make an effort to integrate good visual aids(films). For my qtr, she got really great speakers... really nice when lecture is 3 hours long on fridays

Professor Erwin gives all the answers to her tests in her powerpoints. She is always helpful, but this is a class of history of theater, not practical theater (although the extra credit requires you to act).

I have taken 3 classes by Dr. Esdin, and I look forward to taking all his other classes! I took Endocrin, Pulmonary Physio, and Cardiovascular Pathophys. Very caring professor. He takes the extra time to make it seem more like an in-person class rather than online class. This rare esp in other UC online classes that I have taken many of.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Enjoyed the class. Take notes on lectures then read the assigned reading and you will be fine for the exams.

Lectures are amazing but the textbook should always be your tried and true. His exams are very tough; so be warned.  Dr. Esdin seems to get a lot of hate but I will say you will get what you give to the class.  I was out of school for 7 years so I'm haply grade wise.  10/10 suggest. I plan to take all his classes he offers via extension.
Best professor at UCLA.

Seems to be running courses only for $$.  Literally copies/pastes the same responses into his online classes. Doesn't respond to mandatory discussion posts or engage with students in any way. Quizzes are random multiple choice questions from the reading, hyper-specific and not applying any information. Exams have typos, misspellings, and errors.

Don't bomb a single test because your grade will never recover. Not very accomodating to students or passionate about teaching. Really his class is just the textbook. He didn't add anything to it. Also, tests are very specific to random details from the textbook, so knowing the materials isn't a good measure of how well you will do.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Great orator. I always left class feeling very excited about the material and inspired. But the exams showed that I did not fully understand the material. It is NOT enough to rely on his lectures. You need to study on your own. 5 years later in med school as I am now studying the lungs, I am careful not to make the same mistakes again.

Professor Esdin is amazing. Genetics is a difficult subject but he made it more than bearable. He explains everything step by step and goes through practice problems in class which is extremely helpful.

For Physci 5 you have to sign in for participation during discussions. My lectures were 50 minutes three times a week and section is once a week for 2 hours(lab).  The class was manageable and Professor Esdin was a great lecturer. He sometimes makes silly comments.  It's easy to keep up if you make sure to read along and go to lectures.
Best Professor ever

Course is very straightforward: read the pages assigned weekly, and be expected to know the material inside out for the exams. 3 multiple-choice exams, with some questions you'll see before in open-book weekly quizzes. Esdin isn't great at responding quickly to e-mails, especially when you need him the most...

The class is well structured: 3 exams with 3-4 chapters of material each, with open-book weekly quizzes and discussion responses. Dr. Esdin's lecture slides are helpful for review, but you definitely need the textbook for often detailed exam questions. You won't need much helpclarification from him, but he's still responsive to all questions.

He is very punctual with E-Mail replies. Also, he is very clear about his expectations and of the material that students need to know for class. Do not wait until the last min to study. Study as planned in syllabus, you will do great. Buy the book, worth it. He gives review sheets, very helpful. Weekly quizzes, easy. Exams:use review sheet as guide

He must be writing his own reviews on this site. I took an online Endocrinology class with him. Basically, he assigned you a bunch of chapters from an Endocrinology textbook and gives you multiple choice tests that could cover anything from the textbook. Gave very limited handouts and minimal guidance. Instead of paying tuition, just buy the book.

Dr. Esdin is superlative. His lectures are perfect. He is very aware of himself, the students, and guides the class masterfully and seamlessly. His class is truly a masterpiece of teaching. He is clearly passionate about the genetics as well as teaching, a great combination. One of the best professors I've ever had the pleasure of learning under.

I was dreading the fact that I had to take Genetics as a GPA booster and heard how hard the class was overall. Dr. Esdin is not only one of the best teachers that I've had but one of the most helpful teachers that I have ever had. He will go out of his way during lecture or after class to make sure that you understand it prior to leaving class.

Dr. Esdin is an EXCELLENT professor!  I took Genetics this summer and I can't say enough good things about him.  He cracks jokes which makes class enjoyable and he does practice problems in class so you know how to set up the homework problems.  Tests were challenging but it's clear what you need to know.  Oh, and the class is curved!

Excellent professor. He goes out of his way to ensure that his students receive the education befitting a school like UCLA, and goes beyond that. I'm taking him for genetics this Fall, too. I can't say enough positive things.

(This is for LS 2) Dr. Esdin is so helpful.  His tests are exactly what he teaches from his lecture notes.  The book is helpful on confusing topics, but Dr. Esdin does a really good job explaining most of them.  The best professor I've had yet.

Randy is very different than other English college professors. The fact that we can call him "Randy" instead of Mr. Fallows is awesome. You get graded on participation and it's great because it's not intimidating to participate when the class is a small group. The essays he makes us write were related to things happening in our world too.

not your average class. I took Prof F. for an SMC extension class. I may only be a freshman, but so far he's much different than the other professors. Never condescending and genuinely tries to help you out. Just read the plays, write commentaries, write a play/essay and you'll do fine. Easy and Interesting =best class so far
Professor Fallows is amazing!His classes are fun and he is very mellow. He lets you make up work if you miss a class, and his main focus is to get you to think about the text you read! take him!

Contrary to what the last person wrote, Randy does not have a large ego.  He asks to be called by his first name, elicits students for their opinions (rather than pushes his), and admits that he doesn't know all the answers,  His main goal is to get you to think for yourself, rather than repeat his opinions like most professors ask you to do.
Nice guy, but has a large ego.

Awesome professor and extremely helpful. But don't expect to turn in a paper and get an A right the way. He will make you revise it a couple of times. But you have control over your grades since he lets you revise your papers til you get the grade that you want.
No Comments
No Comments
Really thoughtful and sweet.  Good writing assignments, good amount of reading, always interesting classes.
Cool, funny and great looking guy

Best Spanish Professor at UCLA! Everything you cover in the class is on the test, so there are no surprises which makes the class easy if you just pay attention and do the work! I have no complains about her teaching method.

Amazing teacher! would recommend her to anyone. We should have more teachers like her. She is so helpful, makes you work to ensure you are learning. Genuinely cares about her students and takes time to get to know each one. Makes sure you are on track and more than willing to help. Very smart, funny, charismatic and all around wonderful!

The structure of her class is simple and interesting. She is a family person that always has a new picture of her kids as her computer background. Although she is busy by being in charge of the TA's for every lower division course in the department she is surprisingly very accessible either for office hours in her office or for virtual office hours
Lectures are boring. It's pretty much the same as him reading the book. Only way to pass is to go to sections and try to teach yourself.
AVOID FELKER AT ALL COSTS

Just a terrible lecturer. Puts you to sleep the second you walk into the lecture hall and what he says has nothing to do with the homework or the midterms and finals (which were next to impossible). They often say that open note tests are harder than usual... well try open textbook with felker. That just gives you a feel for how tough his class is
just AVOID Felker

Lectures were convoluted and did little if anything to clarify material, relying almost exclusively on derivations. Rarely took questions and was very unapproachable, seemingly annoyed if anyone had questions. His tests were entirely mathematical, a direct contrast from his derivational lectures. A mess of a lecturer.
His lecture is very bad. His tests are extremly hard....If you don't want to ruin your gpa or suffer from his classes, please stay away from him!!

one of the best Pchem profs at UCLA. He is extremely knowledgeable and confident in Physical Chem. His lectures are full of excitement since he usually derives formulas. He will really make you learn Pchem. His open-book exams are amazing as long as you understand the concepts. Overall, he is one of the best Pchem profs with strong Math background.

Prof. Dr. Felker is an extraordinary teacher. Actually, he is the hero in Pchem. He is extremely confident about his knowledge and can make the most difficult subject become applicable and enjoyable somehow. But, his tests are killers, you must understand the concepts to do well on the tests. Take him if u want the challenge in Pchem!!!!
If you aren't stupid and go meet with him during his office hours he is very helpful and you will do well
Writes extremely hard tests. Not very friendly to you or your GPA.
not a good speaker, isn't always there for us
She is not only brilliant, but she is able to teach and help students understand the brilliant concepts she conveys.
She is not only brilliant, but she is able to communicate her briliance to students in a way they can understand.
She is an amazing Professor! Not only was she interesting, but she was clear and brilliant!
She's professional, she knows her stuff, and she even has a sense of humor to go along with it.  Her tests weren't overly difficult.  Good lecturer.
nice you need to work hard

Great professor. Very helpful outside class. Tests are pretty hard, focus on concept more than mathematics, the best ways to prepare are take previous year tests, do OWL questions and go to his office hours. Lectures are understandable, sometimes confusing. Stick to your course reader at all costs.

Easy if you study his old tests with the correct solutions. He provides some past exams and some are available at the UCLA test bank in SAC.  Not recommended because the material is  not taught well and the course reader is disorganized. But if you choose Lavelle over Scerri for 14A/B, be prepared to study hard.
Definitely recommend over other 14B teachers: not too much hw, lectures relatively easy to understand, and you can prepare for the midterm and final by looking through past ones.

Scerri is very interesting... he's kind of snarky and asking questions in class is a little intimidating, but on the whole, he's actually a good teacher. Chem itself isn't easy, but with Scerri you focus more on concepts than math so depending on what kind of learner you are, that may be easier or harder.
He's absolutely hilarious. Tests can be tough sometimes, but if you live Chen, he's gonna be your motivation. He's very intelligent and knows all his stuff. I'd recommend that you take him.

He is arrogant and brash but he is a good professor. He speaks well, is not boring, and his course reader actually makes it easier to study for his class. A lot of people underestimate his difficulty. He is not hard, but his class isn't a cakewalk either. Do the practice exams, go to discussion, and watch his podcasts.

What I don't like about Prof. Scerri is that he makes you buy his expensive course readers. You can't even use the old ones because he switches the pages. This makes it mandatory to buy one and i did not appreciate it as a cash strapped student. I thought he only cared about making money off students.He doesn't explain most concepts well,
GOOD PROFESSOR

Everybody complains about Dr. Scerri's snobby demeanor, but honestly, I found it hilarious. Apart from that, he's a really good professor. He WILL lose you at times, as his course reader becomes hard to follow in some places. But if you pay attention, seek help when it's needed and do the practice tests in the back of the reader, you WILL pass.

Awful teacher. Incredibly disorganized: makes mistakes 10+ times a lecture despite the fact that he's been teaching this subject for years. His course reader was like Egyptian hieroglyphics I had to decipher, and sadly his tests are entirely based on the ridiculously confusing course reader. Does not seem to care about his students, just his ego.

I don't know why everyone complains. He's funny and snappy to students who waste his time by asking stupid questions but nice to people who genuinely need help. I got a 57/58 on the first midterm (though the class average was like a 62%). Its really not that bad. Oh, and every lecture was podcasted- yay!

I enjoy Scerri. He's really snarky, but very helpful if you approach him outside of lecture. He doesn't like answering silly questions mid-lecture though. He's extremely knowledgeable, the class is straight forward, and he's also funny. The chili pepper is retroactive speculation...I mean he used to be in a band.
<3 sexy beast

So he was better than the alternate teacher.  His classes were incredibly boring! And it was a struggle to get up to go.  His midterms were horribly graded, mean was 40! If you dont have to take this with Scerri, DONT.
Ok, not the best or the worst. I took his class because the other professor was even worse.
Just memorize the reader and you should be fine. He does not use the textbook at all so don't even buy the book if you plan to take him for Chem 14B.

Scerri's class can be fun with the demos. he is really good at the guitar. Make sure you get 100% on the repeatable quizzes. Know the reader and you will get a good grade. Don't read the book or anything else. You also don't need to goto office hours. You can actually understand scerri and the pace isn't too slow/fast.

I feel like I'm always interrupting him when I visit Prof Scerri during his office hours.  It's like he quickly wants to get rid of you and go back to whatever is on his computer.  In class, you'll notice that the more attractive a girl is the more attention she gets.  He seems very cool and aloof in his European sort of way.  Can't figure him out.
look him up o YouTube and see videos of his lectures !!
i have no opinion. memorize the reader or you're dumb.

Yes he may seem arrogant and stuck up but he's a good lecturer.  His accent actually kept it interesting for me.  I give him an A for effort for trying with his jokes.  Overall, besides personality, I learned a lot from him.  He's good at teaching chem.
awsome!!!!!!!!

Scerri is overall not bad. His grading policy is decent, and the guy curves the midterms. In addition, his office hours do help a lot. However, his practice midterms are misleading. Don't ever base your upcoming midterms on the practice ones.

Scerri's an arrogant british boob.  don't bother going to lectures because the guy doesn't even teach.  only redeeming aspect of the class was his head TA Gagik who spearheaded ALL of the review sessions (read: real lectures where you actually LEARN!!!)  yeah...but, he does play the electric guitar. but still, BAD PROFESSOR!!!
No Comments

Scerri wasn't that bad of a teacher. You do well on his tests if you memorize the reader he gives you-especially the graphs. He's not very approachable, but he's concerned about how much his students know the material. Last quarter he thought my class wasn
He is full of crap with his english accent. He doesn't give 2 shiets about his students. The only good thing about his class was his damn experiments and even those weren't that interesting.
He's alright compared to other professors just know reader inside and out and you should do alrite..
Great class, great teacher. Made complex concepts easy to grasp in an entertaining way. Loved his experiments, guitar, and British accent.
No Comments
nice accent, as long as u can understand him

He SUCKS and oh yeah, he's really stuck up. He lectures like it's an audience of biochemists and he's rude and he's not funny.  Also, his exams are SUPER hard. He deterred me away from the biological sciences--my experience with him and his class was just horrible- maybe it'll be different for you/others!

my first impression wasnt very favorable, but after taking chem with baugh, scerri isnt a bad professor at all.  his tests are fair and although lectures are a bit dry, theyre lightened by his demos.  The course reader became my best friend for this class.
Very arrogant and difficult, TA couldn't speak english either unfortunately.
He is very arrogant and unclear and boring during lecture. His accent is annoying. HARD grader.
No Comments
His class was pretty entertaining. Although he makes jokes that arn't so humerous, he teaches chemistry really well. It solely depends on your TA for the further understanding in chemistry.
No Comments

just go by his reader, forget the book (he doesn't even know what the book is saying), use the Munowitz book instead. Everything he knows is in the reader, outside of that, he really doesn't know much chem. Fun demos. Can be arrogant.
Very good at explaining himself
Very helpful. Must take!!!
Professor extremely hot
No Comments
No Comments
Very articulate. His lectures are easy to understand and help very well on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I was not a music major, but was able to take his amazing class on current music and its roots. Totally amazing. I would listen to him read the phone book! I learned a ton. Probably the best class I took at UCLA.
though only music students will probably ever have him, he is a great professor.  His was one of my favorite classes at UCLA
He is strong on the constitution and encourages an open mind. Welcomes diverse opinions and ideas.  We need more professors like him.

Keith Fink is one of the best Professors I've ever had at UCLA. It is troubling to me that at this time Fink is being forced out of UCLA. I don't like what I'm hearing or reading on the subject and this prevalent fabrication of evidence. He has a mastery of the material, makes lectures interesting and interactive&makes himself readily available
This Professor cares about all of his students. Takes time to answers your questions.  Excellent class discussions.
Incredible professor
great proff, amazing lectures
We need more professors like this.  Providing the facts for all involved. I want to know my rights, not just "theirs"... whoever "they" are.

Great guy. Teaches in a very direct and easy to understand method. Loves to debate interesting hypotheticals. Fair warning though, he is politically incorrect, so the school will probably try to get rid of him soon, so sign up for his class NOW

One of the top professors at school. Dr. Fink gets beyond the doublethink and newspeak prevalent on campus and analyzes first amendment rights in a clear, compelling, and interesting way.  Sooner or later, administration will likely figure out a way to get rid of him, because he's not politically correct. Take his course now.

Took him in the summer and he was fantastic . Don't leave UCLA without taking a course with him. He truly cares and cases our really interesting. He goes over the cases in class. The class wasn't difficult and very enjoyable.
super easy. attend class and its an easy A. Midterm and Final are multiple choice. Professor is really entertaining

Professor Fink's classes on free speech in differnt settings are amazing. The class lectures are entertaining with the students having to answer hypotheticals which keep changing. Professor Fink is very challenging during class discussions and has a masterful style based on his debate and liitgation background.This was my favorite class.

Best class I have had. Professor extremely dymamic and smart and teaches in a unique pure Socratic style. Professor Fink went to UCLA and clearly loves the school and teaching. Very interesting material covered and I came away with a great understanding of the First Amendment.
