Great teacher! Really loves his students and will do anything to help them out. He will take care of you if show respect and interest. Go to class and speak up! It will make a world of a difference if you are involved in the discussions!!!

Prof. Fink is a UCLA alumni; he clearly loves the school and has respect for the students there.  He has the typical tough lawyer persona because he is a practicing attorney, and though he can seem intimidating,  he cares about his students' understanding of the material.  This class definitely broadened my knowledge of free speech and the law.
Great lectures, interesting readings, and extremely knowledgeable. His class was one of the best UCLA courses I've taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Flomen is extremely knowledgeable, and it shows in his lectures. He is passionate about what he teaches. Lots of reading, many papers, and participation is important. I learned so much from him.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: We were graded on two papers. For the first assignment, we were required to use our lecture notes and the four to five sources he provided. Since Max's lectures are filled with information and his slides are 95% photos...I would recommend recording him, if possible. I enjoyed the subject and material, but I hope he changes the course in the future.

Susan Foster is the best teacher in the department. She genuinely cares about her students. Is the class easy? No. But like any other class, it requires work. She helped me become a better dancer, writer, and artist-scholar and I know many that feel as I do. I highly recommend her class to all!

The other raters are right. Stay away from this woman's class. The grading is based on sucking up. She doesn't seem to prepare lectures, just randomly talk on the topic, beginning discussions of tough theoretical topics not at the beginning by explaining baseline info, but halfway through. Also, very rude to students who disagree with her opinions
One of the most arrogant and insecure human beings you will ever meet.  Veneer of manufactured personability covering for a lack of actual concern for students and impossibly self-centered behavior.
If you want a good grade, better agree with her, no dissenting opinions tolerated.

This was a terrible class. I love anthropology, but I hated this class. her lectures are horrible. She drones and is so boring. try to take another class instead of this. if this is an elective, do NOT take it.

There were certain things that were a plus about her as a professor and certain things that were a minus - HATED that she viewed our assignments as a contract and was rigid in this regard but she was very engaging and equally as fair when it came to grading.
WORST TEACHER!

Fonarow is a kickass Professor. Her lectures are so engaging- I always looked forward to her class. She's funny too and very eloquent. She cares about her students and even likes hanging out with them. A year after her class- I'm still on her emaillist.

Professor Ford is exceedingly knowledgeable, and it was an honor to learn from her in a small 15-student seminar. (She's also very kind!) If you take Paris: Biography of a City, be prepared for 200-400 pages of reading a week, an 8-page midterm paper, and a 12-15 page research paper. It is A LOT of work, but it's worth it.

It seemed evident to me the Ford had interesting analysis on the topics, but they were presented adequately. Lectures are monotone and can be hard to sit through without getting distracted. In my section I was assigned a 500 word response to readings every week, there were two map quizzes, and two essays.

While the class is easy, the lectures are unbearable. Professor Ford is very descriptive, but often includes unimportant details. She is also extremely boring. While reading the textbook the material was rather interesting but when I got to lecture it was a struggle to not fall asleep.

Best  class ever. 10 percent map quiz . 2o percent participation.  20 percent short paper. 30 percent final and 20 percent midterm. Very straight forword class.Alex is a highly recommended ta. Easy A if u try. professor is a sweetheart!
Cannot recommend this Professor.  Highly inconsistent grading standards - petty and conveniently subjective.

Ms. Ford is all right. Overall, I hated her TAs cause they're the 1 who ruined my grades. When i went to see her during OFHr, she changed my grade, and i felt GOOD! Someone can say she's subjective & favor some students to others. But she's overall fair; what's NOT fair are her TAs, other insolent losers!
He's a friendly professor who gives solid lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Really good professor. He is not intimidating to talk to and is very willing to listen to his students during office hours or after class. YOU. MUST. DO. THE. READINGS. The class is a breeze if you do. Easy grader as long as you don't summarize the content and have some idea what you're talking about. Lectures are very helpful, just pay attention.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Constantly and desperately seeks validation through contrived jokes, will openly disparage a class non-complicit with this vanity. His powerpoints seem custom-made to detract from lecture cohesion and confuse students. Not a bad person - Just a bad professor.
Lectures are a bore. Reading is plentiful, and the midterm was HARD. Stay away from this professor/class if you can.

Very well prepared lectures.  The material was an excellent selection of primary sources -absolutely facinating information.  I LOVED this class and Professor Frank - looked forward to classes every week.
boring, the TA was very helpful though

give me a break! this clown is teaching is like fk hell! he's such a fk joker! yeah, take his arrogant stepping on the fk lacture and saying"so Peter the Great is 1st imperial czar of Russia. Look, he's like 12'6'" PATHETIC INSOLENT REVOLTING FOOL! Yeah, AN EXCELLENT CHOICE FOR YALL TO BLOW UR GPA! Hurry & get him!

This guy seriously doesn't know how to teach. Might as well go party and @#$% Russian chicks. He nearly never reply to our emails and always acting so arrogant when teaching. His lectures r BORING AS HELL!! Many people even slept in class. He NEVER engage the class to the materials were learning! So useless to even take Russian History with him!

It's a challenging course for those of you that, like me, struggle with writing. She assigns long papers and is a tough grader. However, if you ask for help, she's always nice and willing to help. If you're good with writing and reading you'll be fine; I could've gotten an A. I wouldn't take it again but it was definitely a nice experience.
TOO much work. Her projects are interesting, but assignments are due every other day! Let me breathe, lady!
Way too much work bruhhh. Its not worth it.

Dr. Fretz comes off to me like a genuinely nice lady. There's a LOT of work and she's VERY tough when it comes to grading, but she gives thorough feedback on how to improve. There's also the nice thing of being able to drop your first essay if it tanks your grade (You write 3 in the quarter and the first one's 30%)
Don't do it.
No Comments
His lecture are not hard to understand and have key information on the core of the class and tests. However, there are just TOO MANY THINGS to memorize, and he does not care.

Very unorganized. Doesn't stay on track in lectures so hard to follow. No homework, no attendance. About 6 under 5 question quizzes. Not worth much in the grade though. Grade based on a midterm and cumulative final for the most part.Tests are mostly essay.

I took him for Physiology and learned so much. Keep your head down, listen, read, study. His lectures are great and overwhelming, just know that is your 2nd time hearing the material (read chapter before class) and once you review the lecture again it will all set in.  He can seem a bit standoffish but that is just him being awkward science guy.

i took 183A and 183B. the Man is brilliant and extremely insightful. 183A is easier than 183B. Great courses. Attendance and taking good notes is mandatory. Get to know ur reader since they read ur exams, NOT Friedlander.
A world renowned historian on the holocaust. His background is also very interesting: Read When Memory Comes. Helpful man, really.

I took his Holocaust class (183B), got an A- and loved it. I thought it'd be great to take his history seminar, but it wasn't nearly as good. 5 of the 15 students ended up being grad students who got a lot of the focus and changed the whole dynamic. He assigned a lot of reading and then never really had us do anything with it. 183B was GREAT!

I am a Psychology major, interested in the Holocaust & decided to take his class. He has changed my aversivess to learning History. He tells History like a story-- he is factual & encompasses all antecedents to understand outcomes. I wish I could learn more from him. Fascinating class and an honor to be taught by this man.

Friedlander is brilliant. If you are going to learn Holocaust history from anyone it should be him, there are few that are better qualified. I don't really inderstand the full of himself comment, I suppose if someone knows what they are talking about then they must be full of themselves.
very interesting but a little full of himself!
I don't recommend him.
No Comments

Great class: the subject was fun (in a word:3d), and the teacher explained it phenomenally(blatant sycophancy). He doesn't curve his tests because he doesn't have to: you'll most definitely get at least 90s on his tests because he has practice exams, review sessions and good lectures. Smith is the best TA around so if you fail it's your damn fault.

Park really cares for his class to learn and do well on the tests. He thoroughly goes over the material and his lecture notes facilitate note-taking. His tests are fair, but not easy. Everyone does well though.
No Comments

Furner is one of those rare professors who both cares about his research and very much wants you to care (or at least think long and hard) about it too. I thought he did an excellent job of facilitating class discussing and curbing it when it was getting too heated or going astray.
Friendly, clear, interesting, funny. Really enjoyed his class.
Really good.  Covers a lot of material, but keeps it understandable and interesting.
amazing
He is a very easy going guy, although this does not deter him from giving constructive advice. Note: You need to be self-critical because he usually won't do that for you.
What a joke. Transfer to USC and study with a real clarinet teacher, please.

This whole class is one in-the-field research project ending in a 15 page paper. Very fun, lots of independent work, and amazing discussions.

10/10 Professor. Not too hard but a lot of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Grade consisted of two things: participation (doing weekly reading assignments, attending class, engaging in class, a powerpoint presentation) and a final paper. The final paper carried most of the wight and was centered around data analysis. This is a writing intensive class. She is a good professor who is clear in her expectations.

Grade based on: Participation (which included turning in a question at the end of class and doing weekly read and response assignments), midterm, and a final paper. She is an engaging lecturer and very clear in her expectations.

She is a grad student, but still totally inept to be teaching a college level course. Her lectures were all over the place, the exam questions were on things that were nowhere in the course material, and she was extremely pretentious and unapproachable. I managed to get an A in the course, but really wish I'd taken it with a better professor.

Gives literally HUNDREDS of pages of philosophical hard-to-read material every week. Tests are 6-10 page essays that you only have 24 hours to do. We had to beg for 48 hours. Yes, you read that right. Lectures always go over by 30 minutes. Was supposed to be a fun elective, and turned into my hardest class. And I'm in Pre Law, so that says a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lectures and scholarly articles are everything. As usual w AH courses, profs give more articles per week than is humanly possible to read. But why I'm really here is to talk about his lectures: listening to him talk is like trying to decode a cipher. His convoluted verbiage overcomplicates topics and comes off as pompous. Skip this prof if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He says a lot during his lectures so it's sometimes hard to keep up with what he's saying and taking notes, but he is clearly brilliant at what he does and you can tell he really cares about it and is enjoying himself which makes the class a lot more interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Every lecture is amazing! However be ready to put in a lot of reading hours! He is not one to hand you an easy A, but he will make class extremely interesting!

Professor Baker is really one of the most amazing teachers at UCLA. I've learned so much from his lectures and he has really inspired me to think more deeply about art. He does however have a terrible habit of Duchamping people. The Duchamping is a bit much, even if he follows up with the appropriated laughter meme. Fair grader, Good commenter.
bad

His lectures are full of energy but like most large classes he uses a TA which are NOT as intelligent and no where near as exciting to listen to. He is a little on the pompous side. I enjoyed the class but won't choose him again as an instructor.

Baker is wonderful.  I suppose most of the girls in my Art History class were enthralled with his dress and shoes, but boy was the guy eloquent.  Not only has he really met Rauschenberg and other artists he lectures on, but he also edits his own magazine.  His lectures are so passionate that his voice changes.  However, he does lack clarity.

He is so smart and into art history, which makes his lectures really interesting. But some of the concepts he talks about are hard to grasp quickly and he talks fast, making note taking sort of hard. And there was SOOOOO much reading for this class. He seemed nice but intimidatingly smart and somewhat condescending. The grading made it hard to tell

Without a doubt the most thrilling lecturer I have ever experienced.  He is a rare, rare breed.  A little heavy on the phallic references, but hey, this only makes him more attractive.  Beautiful shoes, beautiful mind, but ugly course load, oh my god did we READ.  Very challenging studying for his tests: you WON'T be able to predict test questions.
No Comments

The most unprofessional encounter in my academic career was with Professor Laviña. I came to his office prior to beginning my MSW for help with an issue concerning my placement. Mr Laviña made accommodations but also, without good reason, made it sound as though my ability to continue with my MSW more broadly was at risk. I wish I reported him then

Gerry is popular with a lot of students - but mainly with the ones he's cool with. He has his favorites in the department, and often funnels them into the better internships/fellowships. He has also shown himself to have pretty questionable boundaries with students, often drinking with them and flirting with female students. Don't believe the hype.

Gerry is an exceptional professor, mentor and social worker. He is an amazing asset to UCLA, the Social Welfare department and this profession. He is a genuinely caring individual that goes above and beyond to support his students.
I love Lavina!

Gerry is just awesome! He cares a lot about his students. We love him so much his office hours are always booked because of students wanting to get some time with him. I've cried a few times during this quarter and Gerry was always there to encourage me to keep going. He is also a pretty awesome lecturer. Better than the rest if you ask me.

Gillespie was excellent. He's laugh-out-loud funny, very down to earth and super helpful. He really wants everyone to succeed and pass. You MUST attend lecture AND read the book. About 30% of the material on tests was from the book and the rest from lecture. Everyone should take this class (and be glad there is such a great professor for it!)
Professorn Gillespie was fun and interesting. He is hilarious and explains everything. He is able to have almost all 300 people there because he is just that interesting.

Pretty fair teacher, seems like he knows his stuff but also expects that you do, even though this is an intro class! Anyway gave him a Try and the class was interesting although the project was horrible. Also, almost always has vpl, if you are into that...

I have taken four classes with Prof Gillespie and he is by far my favorite professor at UCLA. He's incredibly easy, but he also makes the material interesting and applicable to learn so you're not just blankly memorizing stuff to later forget. I've taken 5, 111, 113, & 169 and received A's in all of them and I am certainly not an A student.

prof. gillespie is super nice (and, yeah, hot)- in office hours before the midterm and final he'd go over questions that ended up being on the test (like the ones the tas made). his lectures are really intersting & pretty organized. it was an easy class if you put in any effort.

A lot of memorization and a lab poster project that was too artsy for my taste.  Lectures were great, though-Professor Gillespie is funny and interesting.  He really makes you realize how much is out there to experience.  Also makes you wish there was nothing against professor-student relationships.  He's hot and his personality just adds to it.
Good
No Comments
Dr. Shaffer is a great teacher!!! I highly recommend taking her.
good professor-very helpful

Professors really cool, the workload is fair, 3 papers 4-6 pages he mainly cares about what you want to say and your own opinion which is really cool and nice. I like how you go with the flow and breeze.

He's engaging and really funny; he can be very sarcastic. The TAs for the quarter were also really cool and section is pretty important in understanding the material. A lot of writing, but overall worth it for a really cool class.

Hands down the BEST HISTORY PROFESSOR at UCLA. I wish I have taken more classes with him. He is brilliant and hilarious, absolutely a legend in his field. His lectures are concise and on-point, I went to his class every lecture and scored A's on all his papers. If you pay attention when he is talking, then you won't have trouble writing papers.

Am taking a one unit, SU undergraduate seminar on ISIS and the Islamic State.  Great class.  Gelvin started by giving background, then we read and discuss several articles each week. Gelvin guides discussion lightly and tries to get everyone involved.  Lightens the mood with humor--the subject is stressful.  Take him--you'll learn a lot.

Funny, smart, approachable.  Teaches ME history from a world history viewpoint ("In the 19th century Ottoman sultans looked at France, looked at China, and decided it would be better to a strong power like France than one picked apart like China").  More interested in the big picture than factoids.  Went to class, took notes, and did fine.

I enjoyed this class alot.  Gelvin's lectures are clear and he numbers everything (there are three reasons for...).  Used to hang out during his office hours, where we talked about course stuff and current events.  He does seem to like undergraduates, but cuts off conversations when you whine about grades.  Overall, I learned alot.  Will take again

Extremely knowledgeable guy, you will definitely learn a lot.  Problem is he doesn't really care about the teaching aspect of being a professor, so he isn't very helpful.  Also, his lectures can be all over the place at times.

Gelvin is fantastic.  We used the book he wrote on the Arab uprisings, supplemented by videos and articles.  Map quiz, paper, and final.  Overall, his lectures were crystal.  I didn't know much going in, but followed the news.  He helped me put it all together.  Jokes are good too.

Gelvin is an expert in his field. Lectures are very engaging but are essentially a regurgitation of the textbook.There are no tests in this class, just take home essays. His outlines are basically an outline of what he expects in the papers. I didn't read the book at all and got a B+
Awesome prof. He really knows his stuff and makes it so much easier to understand to everyone who doesn't! He's witty and really relaxed. TAKE HIS CLASS.

Took his graduate seminar on historiography of the modern Middle East.  Although I planned to be a historian for years, I never knew history was like this.  Made us read classics in ME history, along with classics in the discipline (EP Thompson, Foucault, Quentin Skinner, Tilley).  You should hear him on Marx, Hegel,Foucault, etc.  Now I know!
One hour p/f seminar on Arab Spring (he hates that phrase, btw).  Took it because I needed the credit, but began looking forward to it every week.  NY sense of humor and he DOES look like House.
The guy already has a class about the Arab uprisings.  Blew me away.  Gives us chapters of a book he is writing on the topic chapter-by-chapter to get our opinions.  OUR opinions!!!
totally awesome, even at 10 in the monrning.

Loved this class.  Loved this professor. I like the way he didn't talk down to undergraduates, but at the same time was able to explain complex ideas (totalization!  integration and peripheralization!) in a way undergraduates could understand.  Take him.
Really smart, really funny.  leaned alot.
Really smart, great lecturer.  Course was on Israel and Palestine. Professor treated the problem fairly.  Also told funny stories.

I cannot say enough about how nice this guy is and how beautfifully organized his lectures are.  One should take ANY class he offers.  As for the "bias" comments, unfortunately they are true.  Snide comments,  highlighting certain information while downplaying other info.  It is minor, but it is certainly present.  Still, you've got to take him!

I have one gripe. He does not grade fairly imo. As and Fs are absolute and everything else is on a curve. So you can get a C, like a 72, and it is still a B. But if you get like an 87, that is a B+ and will not become an A. I do not think that is fair. Everthing should be on a curve.
i take his grad seminar and he's the best teacher i think i've had at UCLA.

Hilarious (and he knows it too), confident, a genius of middle eastern history. Gives absolutely non-biased views. I took his Arab-Israel class, and didn't know anything about the issue, but he opened up my eyes. I attest to him not being biased, but merely giving the facts (and pissing some ppl off), facts some ppl don't want others to hear.

The best professor of Middle Eastern history around. He is a balanced and truthful professor. Those who say he is biased are those who(especially in regards to the Israel/Palestine conflict) take his class, refuse to listen to anything that does not conform to their viewpoint, and then scream "academic dishonesty." Take him if you can.
No Comments

Prof. Gelvin is by far the BEST at UCLA. His lectures are incredibly easy to follow, as are the readings. He has an awesome sense of humor and will keep you entertained. There's usually a couple 5-page papers and a final (this does not apply to the seminar)and neither are that difficult. Totally approachable and genuinely cares about his students.

The class is discussion based so you really need to show up and talk to get a good grade. No tests, just papers, 3 in total. Lots and lots of reading. It's what discussion and papers are based on so the more of it you do the easier the class is. He really leads you to paper topics and answers in discussion if you pay attention so take notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The class is really interesting for those with an interest in Chinese history. The professor explains quite well, and he gives out detailed outlines and even sample exam prompts so that it is easy to prepare for exams.  There are two papers, a midterm and a final. While the papers are graded strictly, the exams are easy indeed.

Tedious class because no one cares about participating in the seminars...but Von Glahn was a good prof. in a discursive way. Overall, he can be tedious because he'd wait 'til someone speaks up after he asked a q. But I'm ok w/that cause i'm the MOST ACTIVE STUDENT IN HIS CLASS! If u hate discussions, then bring a pillow!

Professor Goldberg really cares about her students. The reading is tough, but she is more than willing to help you master it. She taught me how to read academic papers with ease, which helped me tremendously in other classes. Just do the work to the best of your ability, and everything will come together

This class and professor is a joke and a waste my time. Considering the circumstances that has came during our quarter, she has nevertheless made it more difficult for not only my peers and I, but also her and the head TA. With 1 out of the 2 TAs at our disposal going on strike, she has decided to up the final material to be a redundant amount.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Goldberg does not care about her students at all. Despite the challenges we faced during this quarter (TA strike, protests) she has not made any modifications to the course, but in fact she made the final more difficult. She basically made the final an AP test worth around 60% of our grade, with 2 timed essays, and a paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Goldberg is a great professor who truly cares about her students. The class itself consisted of weekly readings and writing assignments, 1 essay, and a midterm+final. The class was fairly easy considering history requires a lot of memorization. I came in with an A+ before the final and I am south campus major.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Class was online for the fall quarter. Lectures were tenuous and very difficult to sit through. The class consisted of a midterm, research paper, and a final exam. The communication between the professor and students was lacking entirely. If this class is the only one available in myucla, look again because this is not a class worth taking.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Took this class as an upper division elective, after having the class go online and strike through the second half of the quarter. Did not make study guides accessible to anyone who was not in the lecture, made final 1 hour time limit online for 55 questions. Essay prompt was broad with little guidance given by prof. Class needed a TA desperately.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: never again
Jamie was great, but this is pretty difficult material. Wasn't happy with my grade. Expect 30-100 pages of reading per week. The final paper was pretty demanding too.

Very clear and tests are not too tough if you show up to class and skim readings. Gives extra credit on tests which are multiple choice. Would def take again shes lovely. Would recommend to any geography or non-geography major for an easyinteresting upper division.
Love her! She's smart and sassy - great lecturer!
knowledgeable professor. Very approachable and helpful. As long as you do the readings and assigned work you will get an A.

Nice lady, very knowledgable, but a bit ambiguous in her teaching style. To give her credit the subject itself is very fluid and hard to understand, but I had a hard time following her at times. Class was a midterm, 5 page paper and a final. I think I am getting a B. Just do the reading and pay attention and you should be fine.
Wonderful professor.
Test questions will come off his powerpoint notes. I only made a low B on one test and still passed.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He needs a massive raise from this joke of a university right now

Wonderful professor! His lectures move quickly and he gives extra time for those who need help. Exams are based on the book and lectures and he's always available during office hours. His emails, contrary to what BLM is saying, are funny and very well thought out. Hope he stays!
Great professor! Love the way he teaches and goes about his students! Anyone who begs to differ is lying! His style is not for everyone but he is an overall GREAT person!
awful
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Excellent professor who makes you work for your grade. He is also a recent victim of social justice activists getting him fired for not being racist. I appreciate that he is not racist. Thank you professor Gordon for adhering to the principles of the great Martin Luther King Jr.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Klein makes you earn your grade but is fair. Don't expect to be lazy and get a good grade as you will earn what you put in to the class. It shows that he cares about what he teaches and about student learning. I would highly recommend him if you are ready to work and learn!
Great job

Klein is NOT approachable at all. He wud set his OH at 7 and you have to meet like a lot of requirements to be permitted to even email him cuz his personal and professional time is much more important than teaching or helping students. Barely any practice questions for exams other than 2 Qs from the course reader, which is not like the actual exam

Klein's style isn't for everyone but he was my favorite professor  bar none (and yes, I do know Ravetch is amazing). Humorous anecdotes convey core concepts and keep his lectures easy to follow and at times, even *entertaining* to follow. Don't expect details.  You'll understand; you won't memorize. Do NOT buy textbook; course reader  mandatory.
Teaching is relatively straight forward.  Sort of likes to talk about himself.  Unresponsive and unhelpful outside of class.
Superb. Great real world knowledge and a very concerned person who goes out of his way for his students.
Good instructor to take if you are preparing for CPA exam. Study course reader and read text for your success in his class.
No Comments

Professor is highly qualified to do research and knows the material. It is possible to learn a lot. However, he is condescending and projects his anger at the atrocities of history on his own students. His biggest turnoffs are his rudeness, anger, arrogance and lack of clarity. Sad that UCLA has allowed him to disrespect students for over 40 years.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: As a U.C.L.A. alumnus and prior student of Dr. Juan Gomez Quinones, his intellectual prowess granted me a quality academic foundation. There are millions of intelligent educators in the world, yet very few intellectuals, such as he, whose demanding instruct helped expand both my academic potential and love towards my Chicano culture in Aztlan .
The man knows his stuff.

He is a very important figure in the Chicano movement. But thats not what this is about. His lectures are disorganized. Notes he writes on the board are barely readable. He assigns a ton of books, which he is the author. He is an easier grader though. AAP peer learning is a must for this. Midterm and Final were both take home. 1 term paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Worst class I've ever taken at UCLA. The professor speaks so slowly and quietly if you're not sitting in the first row, you'll hear about 40% of what he says. If you're farther than that? Zero percent. So much reading. If you want to include the rev commended readings, he asks you to purchase 14 books for his class. FOURTEEN. Utterly ridiculous.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is the "God Father" of Chicano Studies. Be sure to kiss his hands...Much more intellectual and sophisticated than Rudy Acuna hands down.  A great man w/ great work!!
One of the OGs... he is very good
He's good with limits, but he isn't a professor.
memorize the lecture note.

Great professor if you go to office hours. You get to go over way more stuff than the other math 132 classes, and it makes what they go over look easy. Greene's class gets you to the like three theorems that tie the entire subject together in my opinion. You just have to ask questions when you have them, and then in office hours use the board.

I didn't like his style of teaching. He referred to integrals as "junk', "trash", etc. Maybe he thought he was funny, but he was funny in his own eyes only. He does not use correct mathematical terminology & he just laughs & looks happy. There are much better professors at UCLA than Robert Greene.

This review is for 169 because it's not an option but basically I am frustrated and disappointed with this class. It's supposed to be about political corruption, which we did not look at for the entirety of the quarter except for 2 weeks and it's very R Studio/stats heavy. I spend more time coding than learning anything useful about the subject
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Uses outdated resources & just rambles on. Exams are very poorly thought out. She?s a waste of time & you will learn nothing from her class except not to take her again. If you want a better grasp of the EU take Tsebelis not Golden. UGH!

This teacher is horrible! the subject is pretty cut and dry-but somehow the average grade in her class was a 70%. the curve was so bad that a 60% was a C+. She gives a study guide, but the test is totally from the readings. She does not care about student's grades.
A man with a mission!
I've been taking Dr. Graffin's classes now for like 20 years. Best professor I've had.
Wicked cool.
He was awesome, very available for questions
Awesome teacher!, and a great singer
Badass professor
coolest man alive
great teacher and punk rocker
Amazing, I really loved whole experience! And he is frontman of great band. Just wow.
Best teacher ever
one of the best!
Awesome Teacher.  Not an easy A, but still work taking.  Also fronts bad religion
agreed. frontman of an amazing band AND professor? thats insane. the man does everything.
awesome, great, fun
Totally Cool!!!!!
Wow. I was hoping for so much more. But he is the most boring lecturer I've come across my college career. AVOID AT ALL COST!
great teacher.  knows everything
Genius.

Prof Sonali teaches with passion. I love her teaching method and love for archaeology and anthropology. She inspires her students to learn rather than just attend lectures. I recommend any class she teaches.
Dr. Sonali Gupta is amazing! I would take her classes again and again. She is so inspiring and makes you fall in love with the subject due to her passion. I wish I had taken more of her classes.
I love Prof. Sonalis approach. She engages with students and wants them to learn actively. I wish we had more classes like the one she offers. I think she is one of the best professors at UCLA

Sonali is a great professor. Very warm, experienced, and takes a multimedia approach to her archaeology classes. I have taken her twice and I really like her style. She doesn't make you memorize material endlessly, and gets students engaged with the subject matter through creative projects like short films and portfolios. Very thought-provoking!

I really liked Sonali & felt she cared about how I did in the class but the study guides and tests she gives out are insane. She also scans all her pdf's to make sure you cant use control find and she gave out the study guide for the final 2 days before not that it mattered ,study guide was not specific whatsoever. Again shes really nice but beware
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: This man is an absolute gem.
He's a star.

Professor Gunn makes student growth a priority and encourages them to seek help and training whenever possible. He is happy to go over choreography multiple times, and will compliment a student's technique and offer criticism to better the student.
No Comments
I don't like him. I asked him to regrade my paper and he didn't look at it and just said that whatever my TA said goes and wouldn't help me learn

History teachers can get bad. He, abeit a bit boring, was really dependable and nice. He gave study guides, posted his lectures and even planned voluntary class field trips. Although some of the lectures were off topic and not interesting (he doesn't have the same interests as students...) he was a good professor.
Wonderful.
Very nice guy.
Professor Gurval brings Roman history to life!

do well on his papers and you'll be fine. i took the summer course, never read the books, and got a 'B'. watching all the movies is very important. extra credit assignments are available. he hands out outlines of his lectures (helpful for the final) and gives you possible essay topics that will be on the final.
Very thorough. Nice guy. Knows his stuff
very snotty

Professor Greene is nice, but the homework is really unnecessary and many of the problems are not reflective of material learned either in the book or the lecture. Also, the lectures really do not match the textbook well, and you will have a hard time reading her writing during lectures because she moves quickly. Good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Admittedly I had experience with AP Calculus in high school making the class easy for me, but Prof Greene makes the class easy for anyone. She's clear, not too fast, accessible, helpful even over zoom - she answers all your questions, and her homework isn't busywork: each assignment was about 10 problems. Absolutely take her for calculus!

Her lectures only provide examples and rarely ever go into depth on laws, definitions, or rules that you need to know in order to do the work. I do not recommend having her as a professor unless you have a good foundation of calculus (not pre-calculus, but calculus).

If you are an incoming freshmen who needs to take math I highly recommend professor Greene! Her lectures are well organized and she has long office hours so you can always go in if you need help on homework. The class is a little homework heavy because there is book work and online HW, but you will be glad to have taken the class with Greene! Easy!
Even now after I graduated she remains as the standout professor to me. She really cares about her students' education and holds plenty of office hours. 10/10 recommend.

If you need to take Math 1 or the Math 3 series do yourself a favor and TAKE HER CLASS! She is super sweet and so nice. She genuinely wants students in her class to do well. Her midterms and final exams are very reasonable. There usually is weekly written homework due on Fridays and online homework due as well but it helps you understand.

Professor Greene set the standard for all of my other professors. She is detail oriented, and will carefully go over examples from the textbook in class. Homework is always doable, and if it's not she has great ta's. No surprises on the exam. She gives practice exams with solutions. Wish I could take her again! (took 3a, 3b, and 3c with her)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: She's great, literally the sweetest. She is there to help you whenever you need her.

Professor Greene is the BEST professor out there. Super sweet. Midterms were similar to the practice midterm she handed out. The work load is doable if you keep up with them. She gives a reasonable amount of time to finish everything. She provides a PDF textbook which means less money.

She was an excellent professor. She was super kind and always really helpful. Her exams are straightforward and she has many office hours that can and should be taken advantage of. There is no reason anyone should get below a B in her class.

In my personal opinion, Greene is not a great teacher. She rushes through topics in class and the lectures are pretty much pointless. The homework helps a lot with midterms, but there is SO much of it, it's hard to study for tests because you have so much material to choose from. Also tests are out of like 7 questions, miss 2 and you fail.

She seems sweet at first, but in all honesty, she's not. Terrible teacher, not clear at all, and rushes through everything. Lots of homework, because you're covering a whole chapter in a week without really covering everything in the chapter. Be forewarned: you'll end up teaching yourself.

hey so this is for all the math 1 people. I just took her test 1. To do well, study the HW inside and out. Also the practice quizes she gives you at the beginnign of class are important, questions from them were practically all on the test. this test was easy. Dont freak out, just make sure you get the practice exam and her practice quizzes

Mrs. Greene makes Math so much fun! Like mentioned before there is a significant amount of homework to do per week, but she is so sweet and welcomes you for help with open arms. Tests can be quite challenging but if you study all the homework and practice tests she provides, then you're fine. I definitely recommend her! Best math teacher I've had

Professor Greene is hands down the most helpful professor at UCLA.  I consider myself very lucky to have been her student.  She is so kind and caring, and like she mentions many times in class, she truly wants you to succeed.  If you have a chance to take any class she happens to be teaching, I highly recommend you take it!

Professor Greene has a fun personality and is very helpful. I fell asleep in her lectures many times though (8am classes suck), but still managed to get a A+. Homework is very long and tedious, but it makes good practice. Her midterms and finals are similar to her midterm/final review worksheets so be sure to do that and go to the review sessions.
She clarifies her ideas very well. Like the other people mentioned, she is very approachable and helpful. I recommend taking her class. It will make you like math a lot more than you did.

She is very interesting when she lectures and throws in cute little jokes. I would recommend this teacher a thousand times. Some of the things I learned in high school were taught with more clarity in her lecture and helped me understand so much better.

Goes through all of her progressions and thus helps you understand how she got from one place to the next-- doesn't jump around. She emphasizes the homework a lot, because it definitely helps on the tests. She's one of those professors with a really cool personality and that you would like to go to her office hours to just talk about casual things

Great professor, definitely recommend anyone to take a class with her. She loves teaching and does it well. Very funny and helpfull professor. This class was too easy..=A+  HW is a bit lengthy , but its a great practice  :D

class is catered to those who already took math analysis..calculus in high school aka ALMOST EVERYONE! therefore, thats exactly how she teaches, rushing thru things as if its just review. so i skipped pointless lectures and just did the ENDLESS amounts of hw and only wound up with a B. for me..it was terrible.... HOWEVER,EXTREMELY helpful during OH
Hands down the BEST professor at UCLA. Enough said.

Gussin is the Man! I learned a ton, class was somewhat fun, and he was funny and a genius. Can't really ask for more. I made a study group for the midterm and final, which helped a lot with splitting the reading, I would highly recommend doing that. No surprises, he gives an exhaustive study guide. If you don't ace this class.... you suck at life
A is attainable if you go to class, go to office hours, build a study group.

One of the best professors you can have at UCLA very down to earth and extremely fun class if you get a chance to take his class put everything aside and register 1st or you will regret.... go to lecture, go to office hours, get to know him awesome professor.

Really straightforward class. As long as you show up, take notes and prepare well, you'll do well. He's not boring, very down to earth and he'll test you only on what he covers. I have taken four different courses he's taught and he has conducted all the same way. I like many others who have taken his class, recommend him highly.

Lectures are organize, ur given an outline to fill out. test are based on lecture, some book material is also included but mostly lecture. professor is willing to stop and make sure info is understood, very approachable , truly cares about students. holds online review night before exam along with complete study guide. you have to try to do bad

This professor really cares about you understanding the material. He gives you outlines of the lecture so you just have to follow along and insert info. His lectures are also podcasted. The essay portion of the test is lecture based, multiple choice is book based and worth only 20% of test, so go to lectures! good lecturer

You have to be kidding me. He worked at UCLA once and then the class complained because he failed the class so he was pretty much history after that.  His class was useless in the end since you have no strong evidence about the campaign process and his class(and Prof.Hussey) proved that. What a waste of time. NEVER TAKE HIS CLASSES!~~~~~

Professor Gussin's class is not all difficult as long as you do the reading and attend class on a regular basis. Everything he tests is covered in the reading and lecture. He even conducts online review sessions before the midterm and finals. His lectures aren't long winded and boring. He is a very likeable, down to earth professor and I would high

Phil is a great guy. He is still figuring out how to teach, but is continuously improving. He genuinely cares about student learning- to the extreme of sacrificing his personal time to hold online review sessions before exams. I highly recommend taking classes with him and attending his office hours.

time and time again I have met various former students of this monster in Los Angeles. all say the same sad things... basically he was abusive to the point of absurdity. gets red faced and mean whenever he speaks. always on the verge of an aneurysm. saw 2 actors walk out of his instruction and tell him to go F himself after he pushed too far

Such a knowledgable man.  It's a shame he is such a negative communicator.   I dread his harangues.  For all of his experience you would think he would be a better leader.  He expects us to read his mind. Terribly uninspiring.  Inefficient conductor.
Great teacher.  Very passionate.  Makes people feel a little uncomfortable by his enthusiasm and intensity at first, but he's great!
Thre is a method to his madness!  And he truly cares about his students.
A snob. Teaches with his nose in the air...ive never had a professer sound so condescending when answering student questions. His finals are torture as well.
I took his theater history class almost 15 years ago, and I still remember what ekkyklema and periaktoi are.  A terrific, impassioned teacher.
so fun and so passionate about performing arts. awesome and caring guy who plays the drums like no other! take his classes!
Amazing, take her if you can
Professor Harris is a brilliant scholar, and an engaged and thoughtful teacher.
No Comments
AMAZING lecturer. She makes everything so clear, and can make a century-old case relevant to the moment. One of the best professors at UCLA Law

The reading comes in small doses but is very heavy and you need to dissect it. Take notes on reading. She is very approachable and knowledgeable in the subject. Does not allow computers. Lets students go on pretentious rants sometimes. Overall, knows her stuff and cares, but it's a tough topic. Don't be afraid to ask questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: The best philosophy professor I have taken so far. She has a great way of putting complex material into an easier context making out more enjoyable and understandable. She's funny and extremely knowledgeable on ethics and life overall.

perhaps it was because I loved the subject, but all my senses were focused completely on her and her words when she lectured; a very engaging, calculating lecturer, and always was concerned with student questions
The foremost leading authority on Kantian Ethics and Moral Philosophy, AND a very good teacher; a must have as a student of philosophy.
Really fun teacher. Easy to understand. Really fair. Really nice.

The class itself was very interesting and I really enjoyed it. However the pop quizzes were brutal, mostly due to the difficult wording. Your grade consists of 4 pop quizzes (drops lowest one), 2 essay, and participation.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Good teacher.
Simply she is AMAZING

-Get ready to read in this class. -Expect to participate because it matters. -She gives excellent feedback and is always willing to answer questions and/or clarify. -She is definitely there for her students. -Very supportive and patient. -I would with out a doubt take her again!
Wonderful professor!  Cares about students and engages them!
Prof. Heilemann is dedicated to excellence in education. She teaches in a culturally sensitive manner and really encourages the potential of each individual student.
prof is very dynamic and teaches and awesome class
awesome!
No Comments
SHE IS REALLY NICE AND TRIES REALLY HARD TO GET THE INFORMATION ACROSS. I RECCOMMEND HER.

One of the BEST history professors I have ever taken at UCLA. His lectures are in the format of story-telling, never missed a class because he is that inspirational! I learned so much from him, and his class truly changed the way I viewed the world, and the race. Our entire class gave him a standing ovation at the last class. He is truly amazing.

Took his African American Studies/History Introductory course.  It was eye-opening. Prof. Hill is passionate, articulate, and intelligent.  Does a wonderful job of also linking readings to everday life, history, and culture.  One of the best courses I took as an undergraduate atUCLA.
intense, amazing and incredible.  Changed my view of the world, of race and of history.  Still resonates 13 years after graduation.

He seems to be an extremely knowledgeable professor who speaks with an often poetic precision, choosing his words so carefully sometimes that they come out haltingly.  The material he presents, however, at least for 150E, was worth less than 50% of the grade--student presentations and class discussions filled up, for me, an inordinate proportion.

Great Professor. Probably one of UCLA's best. His knowledge of historical subjects is truly inspiring. All you have to do is read and write your papers. You may have to present in class. But you will really learn a lot from him.

Professor Homann is wonderful. She's a fast speaker and covers a lot in lecture, so you really need to go. But her passion is so overwhelming that it just excites the whole class. I am saddened to know she won't be teaching next quarer.

Her class was okay, even though there were some works (two essays). But you don't have to read the course reader at all if you just pay attention in class and take notes for everything she'd say. Just show up in class and take notes. And meeting a great TA is crucial for getting A.

Prof Homann is very knowledgeable about African arts & her passion for it it's transmitted in her lectures.Though material is A LOT & she speaks really fast so be ready to be overwhelmed & writing notes like there is no tomorrow ,good notes=crucial.Tests are ridiculous! over 80 images to memorize, 1 full essay and 2 mini compare & contrast essays

This is by far the best Professor at UCLA.  Although his grading may be difficult, his presentation of the material is absolutely outstanding.  Thank you Professor, for telling the Armenian story with such vigor.

Don't be fooled by the other comments. This class is not difficult at all. If you don't do well you have nobody to blame but yourself. If you do the readings and attend class regularly, you will do fine. No prior knowledge of Armenian history is needed. Everything you get tested on is in the book and lecture.

Yes, he it is a hard class, but he is a fair professor. If you do the required readings and attend class, you are pretty much guaranteed an A. There is nothing on the tests that is surprising or has not been extensively discussed in class. He is a great professor and you end up leaving the class having learned tremendously. I recommend him.
HARDEST FREAKING CLASS I HAVE EVER TAKEN.
Simply the best !!!

Pretty much the best professor.  Very intelligent and articulate.  Dr. Hovanissian has a wealth of knowledge at his fingertips.  I took his Armenian History classes as an elective having only heard of his incredible reputation as an academic.  He told the Armenian story with verve and dignity.  Well done professor.
No Comments
Dr. Hovannisian is the finest intsructor I have had at UCLA, both in terms of his mastery of history, and in terms of his dedication to simply being a good teacher.

My idol.  Pretty hard to keep up with him or even try to impress.  Even after graduating I would enjoy his lectures and conferences.  I was raising my two children, working full time and taking care of my disable parents while taking all of his classes.  My newborn took all the energy I had during the night and I was sleeping during his class even I was sitting just in front of him.  I used to record his lecture and listen to it every day while driving to school, work and back home.  He is the one and only!
Hard class... but you feel like you've learned so much when it ends.
The Professor was very knowledgeable...great lecturer.

One of the best professors I have ever had, Dr. Hovannisian is an encyclopedia of information about the history of one of the oldest cultures in this world, the Armenians. Not an easy grader, but an A in one of his classes is an honor.

It is impossible to believe what a great instructor Dr. H. is, his understanding of the details of history is astounding; especially when combined with the genuine almost fatherly interest he takes in his students.  Thanks for an awesome quarter of learning professor.

I took an Honors Collegium class on Genocide with Prof. H. A LOT OF READING!!! But it was totally worth it. Class discussions were great, and the TA rocked (Go Jake!!) Grade based on two papers - 10-15 pg, and 25 pg. First one, based on novel on the Armenian genocide. Second, you pick your own topic, research it, write it. Great class, great prof.

Professor Hovannissian is the best teacher I have ever had in any subject including high school.  He has a stunning overwhelming grasp of history.  I highly recommend any class he teaches as a class that should be taken.

20 yrs. ago I took 3 quarters of Armenian History from Dr. Hovannissian.  I was a math major but Dr. Hovannissian's classes were the 3 best classes I had ever taken and have ever taken since. Thank you Professor for teaching me what it means to be an Armenian, thank u for giving me back my culture.

This man is incredible--he is a walking history book. His sarcasm is also entertaining. The tests are mostly short answer/ids/essays--none of that tricky multiple choice stuff. Take him--you'll learn a lot.
No Comments

Completely inaccessible outside of lectures, NO discussion is encouraged, the only feedback is given is a question mark with no further context of insight. It is a cruel punishment to be a student in this professor's class.

She is a tough tough grader, watch out! The course only has a few assignments so one screw up and your grade is finished. I found her a little hard to understand, especially because the lectures are slow and difficult to follow as she jumps around with little organization. I would avoid unless you've got a lot of time, patience, and work ethic.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Lee is a very intelligent and kind person who knows her material in and out. I wasn't especially interested in Chinese art before I took her class but now I find it fascinating.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Lee may be a bit hard to follow and she may grade hard, but she is an extremely kind woman and if you go in to talk to her she is more than willing to help. She really cares about Chinese Art History, and is clearly well versed in the topic.

I am going to flat out say, expect her to grade you hard. She is expecting you to be top of your game and expects you read everything before coming to class. I am alright with her lecture though it does repeat a lot on aspects she wishes you to know. Don't hesitate to ask her for help on finer points in the essay, she is there to help.
I lost my interest in modern Chinese art after taking this class.

She approaches the materials thematically, versus chronologically, which might be difficult to follow if AH isn't your interest. She was pretty clear about what to expect on the final, and repeated those points often. Her lectures weren't the most thrilling, but you can tell she loves Chinese AH and tries her best to share what make it special.
Her lectures are very difficult to follow.
Class is lecture heavy, but very thoughtful and interesting to learn from.
Great professor! Lectures were amazing

Lectures sometimes drag on and go in random directions, so it can be hard to pay attention and be engaged. however, he has a wealth of knowledge and believes in the work he does. His honesty and blunt nature can be confused for rudeness, but he is harmless.

The HARDEST class I've ever taken! So glad it's over bc the stress of it all was not healthy. Transferring in from a CC I was used to much smaller classes & more Instructor interaction 1-on-1. The LAs were just as lost as we were. No support. He was not helpful in lectures either.. was harsh to most that asked Qs and set the mood: intimidated..
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof Pham has been my favorite teacher at UCLA! With that said, the class was still one of the hardest I've ever taken. He actually made me enjoy Ochem (can't believe i just said that!) Attendance at lecture wasn't mandatory but missing class WILL hurt you. I had to dedicate hours on end to this class but ending with an A- was worth every second!

Very clear on what he expects and why. He makes lectures interesting, and it is obvious that his first priority is that his students actually learn the material. However, don't think that just because he's a nice professor that the class will be easy. Study consistently, and aim to understand concepts as opposed to memorizing things.

Dr. Pham definitely made 30A an enjoyable course.  Many students are apprehensive about OChem but his lectures, although fast-paced, were clear. To do well, do all suggested hw problems, go over lectures, and understand mechanisms. He makes himself available and has open door OH before all midterms/finals. Definitely recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: My favorite professor so far at UCLA! I was pretty scared going into OChem, but Pham was awesome! He offered so much help, and always cared about all his students. He even gave us candy while we were studying! I hope he comes back to teach 30B. Let's rally together and get him to come back!
One of the Best teachers so far I have studied from. Really explains the stuff well.

Not recommended. Tough tests and barely explain anything. Requires the homework to be done in advance for the next lecture that we haven't even opened yet. participation only to a few people that are funny in the class. the rest are just sitting there having no clue what's goin on

The class is test heavy.  The exams are long, a system whereby the sheeps are separated from the goats, and most people are in one herd.  You have to perhaps do each long problem five times at home to be able to finish the long problem without error on an exam.  That's 120A.  Not sure how 120B or 120C is like.  Felt lost on exams    lots of hw.

Tony's class is very challenging at first, which makes everybody nerves. But later, you will find that the class becomes easier, because he will repeat the most important points several times and let you understand thoroughly, which is actually good for you to process. In the final, he will give you an amazing extra credit to raise your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Tony is a very challenging instructor. He makes you work for it. Definitely do the homework! This class is the basics of accounting so he is hard on everyone for a reason. He is a real jokester, very sarcastic which I enjoyed. Don't worry if you don't understand a concept because he is willing to break it down in class step-by-step so go to class!
very good professor, very nice, explained clearly.

TERRIBLE teacher. he expects you to teach yourself the material.  class time is wasted with him quuuuickly going over homework and playing favoritism.  he offers extra credit on the final exam so focus on that and kissing up to him.  he expects you to remember A LOT of prior material and has very hard exams.
He has a big ego and is of no help when it comes to answering questions.  I suggest taking any other teacher unless you want to have to teach yourself the material.
No midterm, no final. Only papers - can rewrite to get an A if you get a B at the beginning for the midterm
Super easy and chill, but so boring.
No Comments
He literally doesn't care about students' performance. He would purposely make the exam tricky and not makes the practice exam helpful
Not good

he didn't respond to my emails. don't take this summer class. he wasn't even there half the time. he would be good teacher but the summer class was a full time job for more students than he could handle. it wouldve been a very doable class if it was at a reasonable pace with enough depth into each topic.

I got super sick during the first quarter when I was taking this class (surgery level sick), Maloy was super patient with me and helped me out so much to make sure I passed the class with a high grade- A KING. Definitely cares about his students more than anything - also brings his dog to class! 10/10 - the dog (Toby) 100/10

- I actually took SOC 101 but there isn't that option to choose from
- Super easy workload: only 3 assignments per quarter which are the midterm, final exam, and final paper
- exam questions are really similar to discussion questions 
- if you don't read the textbook, attend lecture and you'll be fine
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: 1 is too high for him. Test on really detailed things that only mentioned once in class, and be ready to read 40 more pages per week.. He never has a slide or something to teach in class but only sharing his subjective opinions. No idea why does an introductory class need to be this hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Not a good professor. Has no respect towards students. Has terrible etiquette when students share vulnerable and valuable information in lecture halls. Discussions are mandatory. Readings for class are extremely dry. Would not take this course with this Professor again. Bluebook testing and no notes allowed for the dense, not-so-engaging material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As someone who has heard this professor say really unkind things about his UCLA students outside of class, I cannot recommend him to any student due to his lack of professionalism, integrity, and narcissistic attitude. Outside of the classroom, the only things he talks about is how "entitled" or what "brats" UCLA students are. Very disrespectful!

Jepson's exams are based on readings in discussion AND lecture. Even if he doesn't go over it in lecture yourself, he still expects you to know it because you should have read it. Thankfully, he gives a study guide and the exam is based on it. Just make sure to attend lecture in order to understand and receive all information
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: All the questions on the test were based on the course reader, not on his lectures, which was kind of annoying.  He's a nice person with interesting opinion, but going to his lectures were only useful for the movies that he showed which would be on the test.

I thought he was great. I loved his lectures. There is some opinion mixed in there, but it is educated opinion. Usually the ones getting offended are the students with the uneducated opinions they still hold onto. Jepson is a smart mofo and has made a lot of connections that most people never become aware of.

Professor Jepson is a joke.  I am an English major who took this class as an elective and regretted it.  His lectures are nothing but a comedy routine & all he does is spout his personal beliefs for the entire class.  The topic (collective behavior) is interesting, but I would not recommend this professor.
loves what he teaches. lecture is actually interesting to go to. grading is really difficult though. this was the one class I didn't mind getting a C though because it was really interesting.
No Comments
Love him! Such a great professor--such a great lecturer. Take him take him take him! Really fun/easy class that's super interesting.
He's great.  Nice and patient.  Really wants students to understand the material, not just memorize. Mostly conceptual.
Lectures can be kind of ineresting, he doesn't do a lot of problems in class, mostly theory, but you have to memorize formulas for tests. Have to study a lot. Nice guy!
very bad professor

CHAPMAN!
JoRo is one of the best professors at this school. Such a compassionate guy who knows so many students by their name and cares about their futures & well-being. He's also extremely knowledgeable about the business and invites some really great guest speakers to class.
swag

Joe is the best of the best. I learned so much about the film industry; it was the equivalent, in some way, of being at a Hollywood agency. Joe brought industry leaders in to lecture about various areas of the film/tv/digital industries. He gave succinct and insightful talks after each guest visited. It's no wonder he's now heading up the TFT dept.

idk where these professors get off forcing us to buy these electronic textbooks. yeah dude i totally have the spare 80$, not like I had to fight my financial aid at every turn, lemme fork over almost 100 bucks just to be able to participate in the class I ALREADY PAYED FOR. two separate "courses" for the lab and lecture too btw

Dr. Phelan is great. His lectures are really easy to understand, and their quality is impeccable. He is dedicated to his work and students. If you watch his posted lectures and go to discussion there is really no reason that anyone should get less than a B. Awesome teacher, but also inspirational and interesting. Would recommend!

I really enjoyed Dr. Phelan's class. His lectures were interesting and he really engaged with the class. Although he posts lectures online, there are clicker questions so you have to attend. He isn't boring and you really do learn from him. He's very approachable and honestly one of the best professors I've ever had. Highly recommend him.

Professor Phelan has been one of my favorite and one of the best professor I've had so far. He makes the class topic interesting and relatable to everyday life. You are not going to want to miss a class because it is fun. I actually looked forward to going to his class as opposed to most of my other classes.
He was great. His class is engaging. If you do the work you'll get a good grade. It's a pretty easy GE.

Professor Phelan is a great professor, probably one of the best I have had at UCLA. This class helped me realize how exactly biology comes into play in my life, and that's something I appreciate. He really cares about student learning and takes time to help you.

I loved him even though i'm no longer at UCLA. Very creative way of presenting science material. You could tell he takes the time to prep his lectures.  His tests are challenging, but he curves so it's okay in the end.
Best!
Professor Phelan made me love science! he is an awesome, smart, and funny professor that really cares about his students learning.

Honestly, one of the worst professors I've ever had.  He name drops constantly in class and really doesn't clearly explain much of anything, but then expects you to know every minor detail on exams.  He's also full of himself and pretty useless if you ask him any questions.  He makes LS 2 much harder than it should be.

Hands down the best professor I've ever had. He teaches with such enthusiasm and applies all the concepts to real life situations to make it easier to understand. He's also very helpful, approachable and eager to help his students succeed. His class is one you'll actually look forward to. Material is tough but it's definitely possible to get an A.
One of the best professors at UCLA

I love Dr. Phelan, his lectures were always incredibly interesting lectures. Unfortunately even though I studied as hard as I could, I barely passed. (I must add that I was in the top 10% of my graduating high school class at a highly populated, competitive public hs in So Cal). The class is in no way easy what-so-ever.

Whoever studied their butt off and still thought the exams were hard must not be very bright. Go to class, do the homework, and the class is pretty much a breeze. An easy B, a little bit of work for an A.
His exams were really hard no matter how much more I studied and he is not that helpful when you ask him questions.
Great communication skills, but if you don't have an ok background on biology, you have to study

One of the best science professors I've ever had. The class is catered to humanities majors and is relevant to their lives rather than just another stupid bio class. He's dynamic and fun and makes his lectures easily and readily available. It's not an easy class but it is a good one.

Great professor. You can really understand what he is teaching, keeps the class interesting. Tests are hard and not enough time, but if you keep on top of the material and practice on the problems, shouldnt be that much of a problem.

He's funny.  Connects lectures to common experiences.  Excellent communication.  To understand lectures, stay on top of the reading.  Prepare to MEMORIZE.  Exams are long.  Don't procrastinate on reading - it will mess you up.
Fantastic professor.
BEST SPANISH TEACHER!!!! I highly recommend you take her class. She is the best spanish teacher I have ever had because she is super knowledgeable in her subject.
Very caring person, by far is and will be my favorite teacher. She is very knowledgeable in her subject, and if she doesn't know an answer completely she'll have an answer by the next class :).
She is a great Professor and she knows well how to teach us!
This course is really cool. I love Spanish so much. Gracias.

Professor Rowe was my favorite professor at UCLA. Her class was difficult, but it was only difficult because I had never really put effort into developing my writing skills before taking this course. I will never forget the butter cookies provided during our final exam, nor the hours of help she provided in office hours to make my essays readable.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Oh the memories... OCD is the best word to descrube her. Took her in 1997, ans would type or rather re-type my essay on the back of my paper using an old type writer. It was insane. But i really enjoyed her lectures. She interviewed me for almost two hours just whn i asked her for a letter of rec. Too funny.. she is nice though.

I took Milton with Dr. Rowe about 25 years ago, and we would giggle about the handouts and chalk drawings even back then. They seemed infantile to those of us who were accustomed to Dr. Allen's or Dr. Braunmuller's esoteric lectures (also wonderful). But we LEARNED. I think she was a highly effective teacher.

Though a compassionate woman, her lectures are haphazard!! Yabbering at lightning speed, she spews information & expects automatic absorption. With chalk etchings ALL OVER, her writing is disorganized & unreadable. Using outdated references & allusions, she litters students with meaningless handouts. Her tests focus on most minute & random details.
Loved the seminar. Teacher very gracious with comments.
She's utterly useless and trapped in the past. She's competely out of touch with reality. Her class is painful and pointless.

Oh, please...those who complain about how hard she is or how much work there is in her class (esp. Milton) really don't belong in college! You ARE supposed to work in class, and Prof. Rowe makes you work! She expects a lot but she rewards hard work - you need to be on your game! Those who moan about it - what are you doing at UCLA?

I have taken 3-4 of her classes.  Professor Rowe is one of the best at UCLA--smart, hardworking, very intelligent.  And, she cares about her students.  If you're lazy and want an easy A, you should go somewhere else.  If you want to learn, are serious about literature, and don't mind reading and writing (A LOT), you'll LOVE her!!!

Prof. Rowe is one of the best professors at UCLA.  She's funny, smart, and diligent.  She spends a lot of time making sure her students learn.  The serious English major will love her.  Those who are there to mess around won't do too well.  Her classes are challenging (lots of books, many papers, etc.), but those who work hard will get a lot out of

You people need to grow up.  This woman has a lot of knowledge to share.  If you actually care about your education, you will find her class stimulating and informative.  And if you work hard, you'll get an A.

If you want a quarter filled with femi-nazi rhetoric and opinions, stay.  If you want an unbiased class geared towards fostering individual growth DO NOT take this woman's class.  She is arrogant and uncompromising.  She is a great writer and will give you good suggestions on papers, but that does not outweigh the overall experience.

I don't know why people are so down on Dr. Rowe. She's tough, but that's challenging. She's very fair, and unlike some profs, she really takes your work seriously when she grades it. She's not very smiley, but so what?

Look for an alternate class. I found her to be a tough grader, I don't know if it has anythingto do with having a ****. She has taught for a long time, I heard she helped build UCLA (100 years ago) and started the women's lit program but I can't confirm it.
RUN, RUN far away from this teacher.  She's a horrible lecturer...horrible grader...horrible reader...horrible everything.
Even with an interesting topic and decent material, this teacher was terrible.
No Comments

Horrible, unclear. Attacks students personally in notes. This class in tourture to sit through with this professor.  She reads from a note card the entire class, and is unapproachable. Students also have to endure her shrill voice during readings.

If you truly want to grow as a creative writer, then Professor Kevorkian's poetry workshop is the one you need to enroll in. She teaches and guides in the fundamentals of poetry. The weekly homework consists of writing one poem, alongside various exercises designed to help strengthen and develop specific poetic devices. She is awesome!

Karen changed my writing in so many amazing ways. I feel like she's the hidden gem of UCLA's creative writing staff. She has a very dry wit and sense of humor and a kinda heart to boot. Take her class and be challenged and intruduced to so many wonderful poets work that are wwriting today. (I swear this isn't paid lol)

One of the best classes that I've taken at UCLA. She is very helpful and gives a ton of feedback on your writing. Learned a lot from her. She can come off as a bit weird at first, and she doesn't like to get intimate with the students, because she says that it makes it harder to grade them, but once you get to know her better, she is very kind.
